meanings.definition: (etc.) A set of keys used to operate a typewriter, computer etc.
To type on a computer keyboard.

examples.en: Keyboarding is the part of this job I hate the most.
examples.zh: 敲鍵盤是這份工作中我最討厭的部分。

meanings.definition: A hit or strike with the leg, foot or knee.

examples.en: A kick to the knee.
examples.zh: 一腳踢到膝蓋上。

meanings.definition: To strike or hit with the foot or other extremity of the leg.

examples.en: Did you kick your brother?
examples.zh: 你踢你弟弟了嗎？

meanings.definition: The crime, or an instance, of kidnapping.
To seize and detain a person unlawfully; sometimes for ransom.
An organ in the body that filters the blood, producing urine.
Race; family; breed; kind.
Related by blood or marriage, akin. Generally used in "kin to".

examples.en: It turns out my back-fence neighbor is kin to one of my co-workers.
examples.zh: 原來我的後籬笆鄰居是我的一位同事的親戚。

meanings.definition: A type, race or category; a group of entities that have common characteristics such that they may be grouped together.

examples.en: This is a strange kind of tobacco.
examples.zh: 這是一種奇怪的煙草。

meanings.definition: Relating to motion
A male monarch; a man who heads a monarchy. If it's an absolute monarchy, then he is the supreme ruler of his nation.

examples.en: Henry VIII was the king of England from 1509 to 1547.
examples.zh: 亨利八世是 1509 年至 1547 年間的英格蘭國王。

meanings.definition: To crown king, to make (a person) king.
A small enclosed structure, often freestanding, open on one side or with a window, used as a booth to sell newspapers, cigarettes, etc.
A circular wooden vessel, made of hooped staves.

examples.en: 1961 18 Jan, Guardian (cited after OED):
examples.zh: 1961 年 1 月 18 日，《衛報》（《牛津英語詞典》後引用）：

meanings.definition: To assemble or collect something into kits or sets or to give somebody a kit. See also kit out and other derived phrases.

examples.en: We need to kit the parts for the assembly by Friday, so that manufacturing can build the tool.
examples.zh: 我們需要在周五之前準備好用於組裝的零件，以便製造商可以製造該工具。

meanings.definition: A room or area for preparing food.
To do kitchen work; to prepare food.
The act of kneading something.
To work and press into a mass, usually with the hands; especially, to work, as by repeated pressure with the knuckles, into a well mixed mass, the materials of bread, cake, etc.
In humans, the joint or the region of the joint in the middle part of the leg between the thigh and the shank.

examples.en: Penny was wearing a miniskirt, so she skinned her exposed knees when she fell.
examples.zh: 佩妮穿著迷你裙，所以摔倒時裸露的膝蓋擦破了皮。

meanings.definition: To kneel to.

examples.en: 1605: I could as well be brought / To knee his throne and, squire-like, pension beg / To keep base life afoot. — William Shakespeare, King Lear II.ii
examples.zh: 1605：我也可以被帶去/跪下他的王位，並像鄉紳一樣乞討養老金/維持卑鄙的生活。 ——威廉‧莎士比亞，《李爾王 II.ii》

meanings.definition: To rest on one's bent knees, sometimes only one; to move to such a position.

examples.en: She knelt the doll to fit it into the box.
examples.zh: 她跪下娃娃，把它裝進盒子裡。

meanings.definition: A young servant or follower; a trained military attendant in service of a lord.

examples.en: King Arthur and the Knights of the Round Table
examples.zh: 亞瑟王和圓桌騎士

meanings.definition: A knitted garment.
To turn thread or yarn into a piece of fabric by forming loops that are pulled through each other. This can be done by hand with needles or by machine.

examples.en: The first generation knitted to order; the second still knits for its own use; the next leaves knitting to industrial manufacturers.
examples.zh: 第一代針織定做；第二個仍編織自用；接下來是針織業的工業製造商。

meanings.definition: A rounded protuberance, especially one arising from a flat surface; a fleshy lump or caruncle.
(of a man) To have sex with.
Sudden fatigue as a result of glycogen depletion from not having taken in enough nutrition.
To strike for admittance; to rap upon, as a door.

examples.en: Don’t knock it until you’ve tried it.
examples.zh: 在嘗試之前不要敲它。

meanings.definition: A looping of a piece of string or of any other long, flexible material that cannot be untangled without passing one or both ends of the material through its loops.

examples.en: Climbers must make sure that all knots are both secure and of types that will not weaken the rope.
examples.zh: 登山者必須確保所有結都牢固且不會削弱繩索的類型。

meanings.definition: To form into a knot; to tie with a knot or knots.

examples.en: We knotted the ends of the rope to keep it from unravelling.
examples.zh: 我們把繩子的兩端打結，防止它散開。

meanings.definition: Knowledge; the state of knowing.
To perceive the truth or factuality of; to be certain of or that.

examples.en: He knew something terrible was going to happen.
examples.zh: 他知道將會發生可怕的事。

meanings.definition: The fact of knowing about something; general understanding or familiarity with a subject, place, situation etc.

examples.en: His knowledge of Iceland was limited to what he'd seen on the Travel Channel.
examples.zh: 他對冰島的了解僅限於在旅遊頻道上看到的內容。

meanings.definition: To confess as true; to acknowledge.
A small ticket or sign giving information about something to which it is attached or intended to be attached.

examples.en: Although the label priced this poster at three pounds, I got it for two.
examples.zh: 雖然這張海報的標籤標價為三英鎊，但我卻花了兩英鎊買了它。

meanings.definition: To put a label (a ticket or sign) on (something).

examples.en: The shop assistant labeled all the products in the shop.
examples.zh: 店員給店裡所有的產品貼上了標籤。

meanings.definition: Effort expended on a particular task; toil, work.
To toil, to work.

examples.en: I think we've all got the idea. There's no need to labour the point.
examples.zh: 我想我們都明白了。沒有必要費力說明這一點。

meanings.definition: A room, building or institution equipped for scientific research, experimentation or analysis.
A light fabric containing patterns of holes, usually built up from a single thread. Wp
A defect or failing; moral or spiritual degeneracy.
To be without, to need, to require.

examples.en: My life lacks excitement.
examples.zh: 我的生活缺乏興奮。

meanings.definition: A frame, usually portable, of wood, metal, or rope, used for ascent and descent, consisting of two side pieces to which are fastened rungs (cross strips or rounds acting as steps).
To arrange or form into a shape of a ladder.

examples.en: Oh damn it, I've laddered my tights!
examples.zh: 喔該死，我已經把我的緊身衣拉起來了！

meanings.definition: To fill or load (related to cargo or a shipment).

examples.en: to lade water out of a tub, or into a cistern
examples.zh: 從浴缸或水箱中汲水

meanings.definition: Weighed down with a load, burdened.

examples.en: His comments were laden with deeper meaning.
examples.zh: 他的評論充滿了更深的含義。

meanings.definition: A deep-bowled spoon with a long, usually curved, handle.
To pour or serve something with a ladle.

examples.en: One man ladled molten steel into the shot sleeve.
examples.zh: 一名男子將鋼水舀入砲筒中。

meanings.definition: An aristocratic title for a woman; the wife of a lord and/or a woman who holds the position in her own right; a title for a peeress, the wife of a peer or knight, and the daughters and daughters-in-law of certain peers.

examples.en: Sir John Smith and Lady Smith.
examples.zh: 約翰史密斯爵士和史密斯夫人。

meanings.definition: A gap, a delay; an interval created by something not keeping up; a latency.
To fail to keep up (the pace), to fall behind
Late
A small stream of running water; a channel for water; a drain.
A young sheep.
Of a sheep, to give birth.

examples.en: The shepherd was up all night, lambing her young ewes.
examples.zh: 牧羊人整夜沒睡，正在給小母羊產羔。

meanings.definition: A stupid or undesirable person.
To cause (a person or animal) to become lame.

examples.en: 1877, Anna Sewell, Black Beauty: And if you don't want to lame your horse you must look sharp and get them [stones stuck in hooves] out quickly.
examples.zh: 1877 年，安娜·休厄爾《黑美人》：如果你不想讓你的馬跛腳，你必須目光敏銳並迅速將它們（卡在蹄子裡的石頭）取出。

meanings.definition: Unable to walk properly because of a problem with one's feet or legs.

examples.en: a lame leg, arm or muscle
examples.zh: 腿、手臂或肌肉跛了

meanings.definition: A device that generates heat, light or other radiation. Especially an electric light bulb.
To hit, clout, belt, wallop.
The part of Earth which is not covered by oceans or other bodies of water.

examples.en: Most insects live on land.
examples.zh: 大多數昆蟲生活在陸地上。

meanings.definition: To descend to a surface, especially from the air.

examples.en: The plane is about to land.
examples.zh: 飛機即將降落。

meanings.definition: Of or relating to land.
To descend to a surface, especially from the air.

examples.en: The plane is about to land.
examples.zh: 飛機即將降落。

meanings.definition: Corridor.
An object that marks the boundary of a piece of land (usually a stone, or a tree).

examples.en: a landmark paper in neurosurgery
examples.zh: 神經外科領域的里程碑論文

meanings.definition: To officially designate a site or building as a landmark.
A portion of land or territory which the eye can comprehend in a single view, including all the objects it contains.

examples.en: The software patent landscape has changed considerably in the last years
examples.zh: 軟體專利格局在過去幾年中發生了很大變化

meanings.definition: To create or maintain a landscape.
(used in street names) A road, street, or similar thoroughfare.

examples.en: And it's Uncle Mo in front by two as they come to the top of the lane.
examples.zh: 當他們來到巷子盡頭時，莫叔叔在前面走著兩個。

meanings.definition: A body of words, and set of methods of combining them (called a grammar), understood by a community and used as a form of communication.

examples.en: Deaf and mute people communicate using languages like ASL.
examples.zh: 聾啞人士使用美國手語等語言進行溝通。

meanings.definition: To communicate by language; to express in language.
A case of translucent or transparent material made to protect a flame, or light, used to illuminate its surroundings.

examples.en: the lantern of the cupola of the Capitol at Washington, or that of the Florence cathedral
examples.zh: 華盛頓國會大廈圓屋頂的燈籠，或佛羅倫薩大教堂的燈籠

meanings.definition: To furnish with a lantern.

examples.en: to lantern a lighthouse
examples.zh: 燈塔燈籠

meanings.definition: A salmon.
Arrangement or relationship; layout.

examples.en: the lay of the land
examples.zh: 地勢

meanings.definition: To place down in a position of rest, or in a horizontal position.

examples.en: A shower of rain lays the dust.
examples.zh: 一場傾盆大雨，揚起塵土。

meanings.definition: A single thickness of some material covering a surface.

examples.en: After the first coat of paint dried, he applied another layer.
examples.zh: 第一層油漆乾燥後，他又塗了一層。

meanings.definition: To cut or divide (something) into layers

examples.en: Layer the ribbons on top of one another to make an attractive pattern.
examples.zh: 將絲帶一層一層地疊起來，形成一個有吸引力的圖案。

meanings.definition: A structured arrangement of items within certain limits.

A heavy, pliable, inelastic metal element, having a bright, bluish color, but easily tarnished; both malleable and ductile, though with little tenacity. It is easily fusible, forms alloys with other metals, and is an ingredient of solder and type metal. Atomic number 82, symbol Pb (from Latin plumbum).

examples.en: This copy has too much lead; I prefer less space between the lines.
examples.zh: 這個副本的鉛含量太多；我更喜歡線條之間的空間較小。

meanings.definition: To cover, fill, or affect with lead

examples.en: continuous firing leads the grooves of a rifle.
examples.zh: 連續射擊引導步槍的凹槽。

meanings.definition: Any person that leads or directs
The usually green and flat organ that represents the most prominent feature of most vegetative plants.

examples.en: gold leaf
examples.zh: 金箔

meanings.definition: To produce leaves; put forth foliage.

examples.en: The lettuce in our burgers is 100% hand-leafed.
examples.zh: 我們漢堡中的生菜是 100% 手工生菜。

meanings.definition: One of the components of a compound leaf.

examples.en: The new leaflets at the end of the branch were a lighter shade of green than the mature leaves.
examples.zh: 樹枝末端的新小葉比成熟的葉子呈現淺綠色。

meanings.definition: To distribute leaflets to.

examples.en: A sidewalk preacher gave an impassioned sermon while an assistant leafleted those who stayed to listen.
examples.zh: 一位路邊傳道人熱情洋溢地講道，而一位助手則向留下來聽講的人散發傳單。

meanings.definition: A group or association of cooperating members.

examples.en: the League of Nations
examples.zh: 國際聯盟

meanings.definition: To form an association; to unite in a league or confederacy; to combine for mutual support.
A crack, crevice, fissure, or hole which admits water or other fluid, or lets it escape.

examples.en: a leak in a boat
examples.zh: 船上漏水

meanings.definition: To allow fluid or gas to pass through an opening that should be sealed.

examples.en: The faucet has been leaking since last month.
examples.zh: 水龍頭從上個月開始就一直漏水。

meanings.definition: Leaky.
(of an object taller than its width and depth) An inclination away from the vertical.

examples.en: The trees had various leans toward gaps in the canopy.
examples.zh: 這些樹木向樹冠的縫隙傾斜。

meanings.definition: To incline, deviate, or bend, from a vertical position; to be in a position thus inclining or deviating.

examples.en: She leaned out of the window.
examples.zh: 她把身體探出窗外。

meanings.definition: The act of leaping or jumping.

examples.en: It's quite a leap to claim that those cloud formations are evidence of UFOs.
examples.zh: 聲稱這些雲層是不明飛行物的證據是相當誇張的。

meanings.definition: To jump.

examples.en: to leap a wall or a ditch
examples.zh: 跳過牆壁或溝渠

meanings.definition: (calendar) Intercalary, bissextile.
The act of learning something
To acquire, or attempt to acquire knowledge or an ability to do something.

examples.en: learn from one's mistakes
examples.zh: 從錯誤中學習

meanings.definition: (chiefly dialectal) To gather.
Something of the smallest possible extent; an indivisible unit.
Used for forming superlatives of adjectives, especially those that do not form the superlative by adding -est.

examples.en: It was the least surprising thing.
examples.zh: 這是最不令人驚訝的事。

meanings.definition: A tough material produced from the skin of animals, by tanning or similar process, used e.g. for clothing.
To cover with leather.

examples.en: He leathered the ball all the way down the street.
examples.zh: 他一路把球帶過街道。

meanings.definition: Made of leather.
To have a consequence or remnant.
A spoken lesson or exposition, usually delivered to a group.

examples.en: During class today the professor delivered an interesting lecture.
examples.zh: 今天上課時，教授做了一個有趣的講座。

meanings.definition: To teach (somebody) by giving a speech on a given topic.

examples.en: The professor lectured to two classes this morning.
examples.zh: 今天早上這位教授給兩堂課講課。

meanings.definition: A book for keeping notes, especially one for keeping accounting records; a record book, a register.
To record (something) in, or as if in, a ledger.
The left side or direction.

examples.en: The political left is holding too much power.
examples.zh: 政治左派握有太多權力。

meanings.definition: The opposite of right; toward the west when one is facing north.

examples.en: The left side.
examples.zh: 左側。

meanings.definition: On the left side.

examples.en: Turn left at the corner.
examples.zh: 在轉角處左轉。

meanings.definition: Money or property bequeathed to someone in a will.

examples.en: John Muir left as his legacy an enduring spirit of respect for the environment.
examples.zh: 約翰·繆爾留下了一種持久的尊重環境的精神作為他的遺產。

meanings.definition: Left over from the past; no longer current.
The legal department of a company.

examples.en: Legal wants this in writing.
examples.zh: 法律要求以書面形式。

meanings.definition: Relating to the law or to lawyers.

examples.en: legal profession
examples.zh: 法律職業

meanings.definition: An unrealistic story depicting past events.

examples.en: According to the legend on the map, that building is a school.
examples.zh: 根據地圖上的傳說，那棟建築是一所學校。

meanings.definition: To tell or narrate; to recount.
Clear enough to be read; readable, particularly of handwriting.
The act of legislating; preparation and enactment of laws; the laws enacted.
A person born to a legally married couple.
To make legitimate, lawful, or valid; especially, to put in the position or state of a legitimate person before the law, by legal means.
In accordance with the law or established legal forms and requirements.

examples.en: legitimate reasoning; a legitimate standard or method
examples.zh: 合理的推理；合法的標準或方法

meanings.definition: Freedom provided by the cessation of activities.
The lumbar region; loin.
The distance measured along the longest dimension of an object.

examples.en: the length of a book
examples.zh: 一本書的長度

meanings.definition: To lengthen.
A lenitive; an emollient.
Lax; not strict; tolerant of dissent or deviation

examples.en: The standard is fairly lenient, so use your discretion.
examples.zh: 標準相當寬鬆，因此請酌情考慮。

meanings.definition: An object, usually made of glass, that focuses or defocuses the light that passes through it.
To film, shoot.
To make less; to diminish; to reduce.
A section of learning or teaching into which a wider learning content is divided.

examples.en: In our school a typical working week consists of around twenty lessons and ten hours of related laboratory work.
examples.zh: 在我們學校，典型的工作週包括大約二十節課和十個小時的相關實驗室工作。

meanings.definition: To give a lesson to; to teach.
Any weapon that causes death.
Deadly; mortal; fatal.
A symbol in an alphabet.

examples.en: There are twenty-six letters in the English alphabet.
examples.zh: 英文字母表中有二十六個字母。

meanings.definition: To print, inscribe, or paint letters on something.
A tool for finding whether a surface is level, or for creating a horizontal or vertical line of reference.

examples.en: Hand me the level so I can tell if this is correctly installed.
examples.zh: 把水平儀交給我，這樣我就可以判斷它是否安裝正確。

meanings.definition: To adjust so as to make as flat or perpendicular to the ground as possible.

examples.en: You can level the table by turning the pads that screw into the feet.
examples.zh: 您可以轉動旋入支腳的墊子來平整桌子。

meanings.definition: The same height at all places; parallel to a flat ground.

examples.en: This table isn't quite level; see how this marble rolls off it?
examples.zh: 這張桌子不太水平；看看這塊大理石是如何滾下來的嗎？

meanings.definition: A force compounded by means of a lever rotating around a pivot; see torque.

examples.en: A crowbar uses leverage to pry nails out of wood.
examples.zh: 撬棍利用槓桿作用從木頭上撬出釘子。

meanings.definition: To use; to exploit; to manipulate in order to take full advantage (of something).

examples.en: They plan to leverage off the publicity to get a good distribution agreement.
examples.zh: 他們計劃利用宣傳來達成良好的分銷協議。

meanings.definition: The act of levying.
To impose (a tax or fine) to collect monies due, or to confiscate property.

examples.en: to levy a tax
examples.zh: 課稅

meanings.definition: An obligation, debt or responsibility owed to someone.
Bound or obliged in law or equity; responsible; answerable.

examples.en: 1748. David Hume. Enquiries concerning the human understanding and concerning the principles of moral. London: Oxford University Press, 1973. § 34.
examples.zh: 1748.大衛休謨。關於人類理解和道德原則的詢問。倫敦：牛津大學出版社，1973 年。 § 34。

meanings.definition: One with liberal views, supporting individual liberty (see Wikipedia's article on Liberalism).

(now rare outside set phrases) Pertaining to those arts and sciences the study of which is considered to provide general knowledge, as opposed to vocational/occupational, technical or mechanical training.

examples.en: 1983, David Leslie Wagner, The Seven liberal arts in the Middle Ages
examples.zh: 1983，大衛‧萊斯利‧華格納，《中世紀的七門文科》

meanings.definition: To set free, to make or allow to be free, particularly

examples.en: We didn't need IDs. We just liberated these beers from the back of the shop.
examples.zh: 我們不需要身分證。我們剛剛從商店後面解放了這些啤酒。

meanings.definition: The condition of being free from control or restrictions.

examples.en: The army is here, your liberty is assured.
examples.zh: 軍隊來了，你的自由有保障。

meanings.definition: A legal document giving official permission to do something; a permit.

examples.en: In order to enter the building, I need to show my license.
examples.zh: 為了進入大樓，我需要出示我的執照。

meanings.definition: To give a formal (usually written) authorization.

examples.en: It was decided to license Wikipedia under the GFDL.
examples.zh: 決定根據 GFDL 授權維基百科。

meanings.definition: An act of lifting or raising.

examples.en: He gave me a lift to the bus station.
examples.zh: 他載我到汽車站。

meanings.definition: To raise or rise.

examples.en: The fog eventually lifted, leaving the streets clear.
examples.zh: 霧終於散去，街道變得清晰起來。

meanings.definition: Visible electromagnetic radiation. The human eye can typically detect radiation (light) in the wavelength range of about 400 to 750 nanometers. Nearby shorter and longer wavelength ranges, although not visible, are commonly called ultraviolet and infrared light.

examples.en: As you can see, this spacious dining-room gets a lot of light in the mornings.
examples.zh: 正如您所看到的，這間寬敞的餐廳在早晨光線充足。

meanings.definition: Something or somebody considered likely.
Probable; having a greater-than-even chance of occurring

examples.en: Rain is likely later this afternoon.
examples.zh: 今天下午晚些時候可能會下雨。

meanings.definition: A restriction; a bound beyond which one may not go.

examples.en: There are several existing limits to executive power.
examples.zh: 行政權力存在一些現有限制。

meanings.definition: Being a fixed limit game.
A path through two or more points (compare ‘segment’); a continuous mark, including as made by a pen; any path, curved or straight.

examples.en: The arrow descended in a curved line.
examples.zh: 箭矢呈曲線下降。

meanings.definition: To place (objects) into a line (usually used with "up"); to form into a line; to align.

examples.en: to line troops
examples.zh: 排列部隊

meanings.definition: Someone who fits a lining to something.

examples.en: 1973, A good liner has a pretty shrewd idea of the value of the painting he is treating and usually charges accordingly. — Kyril Bonfiglioli, Don't Point That Thing at Me (Penguin 2001, p. 41)
examples.zh: 1973年，一位優秀的畫師對他所處理的畫作的價值有相當精明的了解，並且通常會相應地收取費用。 — Kyril Bonfiglioli，《別用那個東西指著我》（Penguin 2001，第 41 頁）

meanings.definition: To stay or remain in a place or situation, especially as if unwilling to depart or not easily able to do so; to loiter.
Some text or a graphic in an electronic document that can be activated to display another document or trigger an action.

examples.en: Click the hyperlink to go to the next page.
examples.zh: 按一下超連結前往下一頁。

meanings.definition: To connect two or more things.

examples.en: My homepage links to my wife's.
examples.zh: 我的主頁連結到我妻子的主頁。

meanings.definition: A substance that is flowing, and keeping no shape, such as water; a substance of which the molecules, while not tending to separate from one another like those of a gas, readily change their relative position, and which therefore retains no definite shape, except that determined by the containing receptacle; an inelastic fluid.

examples.en: A liquid can freeze to become a solid or evaporate into a gas.
examples.zh: 液體可以凍結成固體或蒸發成氣體。

meanings.definition: Flowing freely like water; fluid; not solid and not gaseous; composed of particles that move freely among each other on the slightest pressure.

examples.en: liquid nitrogen
examples.zh: 液態氮

meanings.definition: To settle (a debt) by paying the outstanding amount.
A liquid, a fluid.
To drink liquor, usually to excess.
A strip of fabric, especially from the edge of a piece of cloth.
To create or recite a list.

examples.en: to list a door
examples.zh: 列出一扇門

meanings.definition: An instance of listening.

examples.en: Give the motor a listen and tell me if it sounds off.
examples.zh: 聽一下電機，告訴我是否有聲音。

meanings.definition: To pay attention to a sound or speech.

examples.en: Please listen carefully as I explain.  I like to listen to music.
examples.zh: 請仔細聽我解釋。  我喜歡聽音樂。

meanings.definition: The ability to read and write.
(epigraphy) A misprint (or occasionally a scribal error) that affects a letter.
Exactly as stated; read or understood without additional interpretation; according to the letter or verbal expression; real; not figurative or metaphorical.

examples.en: The literal translation is “hands full of bananas” but it means empty-handed.
examples.zh: 直譯是“滿手香蕉”，但它的意思是空手而歸。

meanings.definition: The body of all written works.

examples.en: However, even “literary” science fiction rarely qualifies as literature, because it treats characters as sets of traits rather than as fully realized human beings with unique life stories. —Adam Cadre, 2008
examples.zh: 然而，即使是「文學」科幻小說也很少有資格成為文學，因為它將人物視為一組特徵，而不是作為具有獨特生活故事的完全實現的人類。 ——亞當‧卡德爾，2008 年

meanings.definition: The conduct of a lawsuit.

examples.en: That attorney has been chastised for his litigation behavior.
examples.zh: 該律師因其訴訟行為而受到嚴厲批評。

meanings.definition: A platform mounted on two shafts, or a more elaborate construction, designed to be carried by two (or more) people to transport one (in luxury models sometimes more) third person(s) or (occasionally in the elaborate version) a cargo, such as a religious idol.

examples.en: sleep in the litter
examples.zh: 睡在垃圾堆裡

meanings.definition: To drop or throw trash without properly disposing of it (as discarding in public areas rather than trash receptacles).

examples.en: By tossing the bottle out the window, he was littering.
examples.zh: 他把瓶子丟出窗外，就是亂丟垃圾。

meanings.definition: A small amount.

examples.en: Can I try a little of that sauce?
examples.zh: 我可以嚐嚐那個醬汁嗎？

meanings.definition: Small in size.

examples.en: This is a little table.
examples.zh: 這是一張小桌子。

meanings.definition: Not much.

examples.en: This is a little known fact.  She spoke little and listened less.
examples.zh: 這是一個鮮為人知的事實。  她說話很少，聽得也更少。

meanings.definition: Not much; not a large amount.

examples.en: Little is known about his early life.
examples.zh: 人們對他的早年生活所知甚少。

meanings.definition: To be alive; to have life.

examples.en: He's not expected to live for more than a few months.
examples.zh: 預計他的壽命不會超過幾個月。

meanings.definition: A means of providing the necessities of life for oneself (for example, a job or income).

examples.en: an independent livelihood; to make / earn / gain a good livelihood
examples.zh: 獨立的生計；使/賺取/獲得良好的生計

meanings.definition: Term of address.
Full of life; energetic.
A burden; a weight to be carried.

examples.en: I struggled up the hill with the heavy load in my rucksack.
examples.zh: 我背著沉重的背包，艱難地爬上山。

meanings.definition: To put a load on or in (a means of conveyance or a place of storage).

examples.en: The dock workers refused to load the ship.
examples.zh: 碼頭工人拒絕裝船。

meanings.definition: An act or instance of lending, an act or instance of granting something for temporary use.

examples.en: Because of the loan that John made to me, I was able to pay my tuition for the upcoming semester.
examples.zh: 由於約翰向我提供的貸款，我能夠支付下學期的學費。

meanings.definition: To lend (something) to (someone).
An entryway or reception area; vestibule; passageway; corridor.

examples.en: I had to wait in the lobby for hours before seeing the doctor.
examples.zh: 我不得不在大廳等了幾個小時才去看醫生。

meanings.definition: To attempt to influence (a public official or decision-maker) in favor of a specific opinion or cause.

examples.en: For years, pro-life groups have continued to lobby hard for restrictions on abortion.
examples.zh: 多年來，反墮胎團體一直在努力遊說限制墮胎。

meanings.definition: A person who lives near a given place.

examples.en: It's easy to tell the locals from the tourists.
examples.zh: 很容易區分當地人和遊客。

meanings.definition: From or in a nearby location.

examples.en: We prefer local produce.
examples.zh: 我們更喜歡當地的產品。

meanings.definition: In the local area; within a city, state, country, etc.

examples.en: It's never been more important to buy local.
examples.zh: 購買本地產品從未如此重要。

meanings.definition: To place; to set in a particular spot or position.

examples.en: The council must locate the new hospital
examples.zh: 委員會必須找到新醫院

meanings.definition: Something used for fastening, which can only be opened with a key or combination.
To become fastened in place.

examples.en: If you put the brakes on too hard, the wheels will lock.
examples.zh: 如果煞車太用力，車輪就會鎖死。

meanings.definition: The power unit of a train that pulls the coaches or wagons.
Of or relating to locomotion
A building for recreational use such as a hunting lodge or a summer cabin.

examples.en: The tribe consists of about two hundred lodges, that is, of about a thousand individuals.
examples.zh: 該部落由大約兩百個小屋組成，即約一千人。

meanings.definition: To be firmly fixed in a specified position.

examples.en: I've got some spinach lodged between my teeth.
examples.zh: 我的牙縫裡塞了一些菠菜。

meanings.definition: (except in derivatives) air, the air; the sky, the heavens.

examples.en: an organ loft
examples.zh: 風琴閣樓

meanings.definition: To propel high into the air.
Lofty; proud; haughty
The trunk of a dead tree, cleared of branches.

examples.en: They walked across the stream on a fallen log.
examples.zh: 他們踩著一根倒下的木頭走過小溪。

meanings.definition: To cut trees into logs.
A method of human thought that involves thinking in a linear, step-by-step manner about how a problem can be solved. Logic is the basis of many principles including the scientific method.

examples.en: It's hard to work out his system of logic.
examples.zh: 很難弄清楚他的邏輯體系。

meanings.definition: To engage in excessive or inappropriate application of logic.
Logical
A logistic function or graph of a logistic curve.
A visual symbol or emblem that acts as a trademark or a means of identification of a company or organization.
Solitary; having no companion.

examples.en: a lone traveler or watcher
examples.zh: 孤獨的旅行者或觀察者

meanings.definition: Unhappy because of feeling isolated from contact with other people.
A long vowel.

examples.en: Every uptick made the longs cheer.
examples.zh: 每一次上漲都讓多頭歡呼雀躍。

meanings.definition: To take a long position in.
Having much distance from one terminating point on an object or an area to another terminating point (usually applies to horizontal dimensions; see Usage Notes below).

examples.en: It's a long way from the Earth to the Moon.
examples.zh: 從地球到月球有很長的距離。

meanings.definition: The release of an arrow.
To let loose, to free from restraints.
Not fixed in place tightly or firmly.

examples.en: This wheelbarrow has a loose wheel.
examples.zh: 這輛獨輪車的輪子鬆了。

meanings.definition: Begin shooting; release your arrows
A kind of scoop or ladle, chiefly used to remove the scum from brine-pans in saltworks.
The master of the servants of a household; the master of a feudal manor
Domineer or act like a lord.
To cause (something) to cease to be in one's possession or capability due to unfortunate or unknown circumstances, events or reasons.

examples.en: He lost his hearing in the explosion.
examples.zh: 他在爆炸中失去了聽力。

meanings.definition: The result of no longer possessing an object, a function, or a characteristic due to external causes or misplacement.

examples.en: In other areas, glacier loss creates serious risk of a dry period across the Third Pole, Wang said.
examples.zh: 王說，在其他地區，冰河消失造成第三極乾旱期的嚴重風險。

meanings.definition: A large quantity or number; a great deal.

examples.en: lots of people think so
examples.zh: 很多人都這麼認為

meanings.definition: To allot; to sort; to apportion.
A scheme for the distribution of prizes by lot or chance, especially a gaming scheme in which one or more tickets bearing particular numbers draw prizes, the other tickets are blanks.
A loud sound or part of a sound.
(of a sound) Of great intensity.

examples.en: Turn that music down; it's too loud.
examples.zh: 把音樂調小一點；太大聲了。

meanings.definition: A waiting room in an office, airport etc.
To relax; to spend time lazily; to stand, sit, or recline, in an indolent manner.
Strong affection.

examples.en: Hello love, how can I help you?
examples.zh: 你好，親愛的，有什麼可以幫你的嗎？

meanings.definition: (usually transitive, sometimes intransitive, stative) To have a strong affection for (someone or something).

examples.en: I love my spouse.   I love you!
examples.zh: 我愛我的配偶。   我愛你！

meanings.definition: An attractive, lovely person, especially a (professional) beauty.

examples.en: a calendar depicting young lovelies in bikinis
examples.zh: 描繪穿著比基尼的年輕美女的日曆

meanings.definition: Beautiful; charming; very pleasing in form, looks, tone, or manner.

examples.en: It's a lovely day and the sun is shining.
examples.zh: 這是美好的一天，陽光明媚。

meanings.definition: In a lovely fashion or manner; beautifully.
Something that is low; a low point.

examples.en: Economic growth has hit a new low.
examples.zh: 經濟增速再創新低。

meanings.definition: To depress; to lower.
Situated close to, or even below, the ground or another normal reference plane; not high or lofty.

examples.en: a low wall  a low shelf
examples.zh: 矮牆 矮架子

meanings.definition: Close to the ground.

examples.en: to speak low
examples.zh: 低聲說話

meanings.definition: Situated close to, or even below, the ground or another normal reference plane; not high or lofty.

examples.en: a low wall  a low shelf
examples.zh: 矮牆 矮架子

meanings.definition: Close to the ground.

examples.en: to speak low
examples.zh: 低聲說話

meanings.definition: To let descend by its own weight, as something suspended; to let down

examples.en: lower a bucket into a well
examples.zh: 將桶放入井中

meanings.definition: Having or demonstrating undivided and constant support for someone or something.
To make slippery or smooth (normally to minimize friction) by applying a lubricant.
Something that happens to someone by chance, a chance occurrence, especially a favourable one.

examples.en: Gilbert had some bad luck yesterday — he got pick-pocketed and lost fifty dollars.
examples.zh: 吉爾伯特昨天運氣不太好——他被扒手了，損失了五十美元。

meanings.definition: To succeed by chance.

examples.en: His plan lucked out.
examples.zh: 他的計劃很幸運。

meanings.definition: Producing a surplus; profitable.
The bags and other containers that hold a traveller's belongings.
Emitting light; glowing brightly.
Something that protrudes, sticks out, or sticks together; a cluster or blob; a mound or mass of no particular shape.

examples.en: Stir the gravy until there are no more lumps.
examples.zh: 攪拌肉汁，直到不再有結塊。

meanings.definition: To treat as a single unit; to group together in a casual or chaotic manner (as if forming an ill-defined lump of the items).

examples.en: People tend to lump turtles and tortoises together, when in fact they are different creatures.
examples.zh: 人們傾向於將烏龜和烏龜混為一談，但實際上它們是不同的生物。

meanings.definition: A light meal usually eaten around midday, notably when not as main meal of the day.

examples.en: After the funeral there was a lunch for those who didn't go to the cemetery.
examples.zh: 葬禮結束後，為那些沒有去墓地的人準備了午餐。

meanings.definition: To eat lunch.

examples.en: I like to lunch in Italian restaurants.
examples.zh: 我喜歡在義大利餐廳吃午餐。

meanings.definition: A biological organ of vertebrates that controls breathing and oxygenates the blood.

examples.en: He hasn't the lungs to play long rallies like he use to.
examples.zh: 他沒有像以前那樣有足夠的肺來進行長時間的拉力賽。

meanings.definition: Something that tempts or attracts, especially one with a promise of reward or pleasure
To attract by temptation etc.; to entice
Very wealthy and comfortable surroundings.
Very expensive
A device that directs and controls energy, often in the form of movement or electricity, to produce a certain effect.

examples.en: I called you earlier, but all I got was the machine.
examples.zh: 我早些時候給你打過電話，但我只拿到了機器。

meanings.definition: To make by machinery.
The machines constituting a production apparatus, in a plant etc., collectively.
To be or become mad.
Insane; crazy, mentally deranged.

examples.en: He's got this mad idea that he's irresistible to women.
examples.zh: 他有一個瘋狂的想法：他對女人來說是不可抗拒的。

meanings.definition: Intensifier; to a large degree; extremely; exceedingly; very; unbelievably.

examples.en: He seems mad keen on her.
examples.zh: 他似乎瘋狂地熱衷於她。

meanings.definition: A non-academic periodical publication, generally consisting of sheets of paper folded in half and stapled at the fold.
The application of rituals or actions, especially those based on occult knowledge, to subdue or manipulate natural or supernatural beings and forces in order to have some benefit from them
To produce, transform (something), (as if) by magic.
Having supernatural talents, properties or qualities attributed to magic.

examples.en: a magic moment
examples.zh: 一個神奇的時刻

meanings.definition: A piece of material that attracts some metals by magnetism.

examples.en: He always had a girl on his arm – he's a bit of a babe magnet.
examples.zh: 他總是把一個女孩摟在懷裡——他有點像磁鐵一樣。

meanings.definition: Grand, elegant or splendid in appearance.
To praise, glorify (someone or something, especially God).
A bag or wallet.

examples.en: Don't forget to pick up the mail on your way.
examples.zh: 別忘了在途中領取郵件。

meanings.definition: (ditransitive) To send (a letter, parcel, etc.) through the mail.

examples.en: Please mail me the spreadsheet by the end of the day.
examples.zh: 請在當天結束前將電子表格郵寄給我。

meanings.definition: Short for mainline.

examples.en: He mains the same character as me in that game.
examples.zh: 他在那場比賽中扮演的角色與我相同。

meanings.definition: Of chief or leading importance; prime, principal.

examples.en: main timbers  main branch of a river  main body of an army
examples.zh: 主要木材 河流主要支流 軍隊主體

meanings.definition: Exceedingly, extremely, greatly, mightily, very, very much.
To support (someone), to back up or assist (someone) in an action.
Actions performed to keep some machine or system functioning or in service.
A military rank between captain and lieutenant colonel.

examples.en: He used to be a major in the army.
examples.zh: 他曾經是一名陸軍少校。

meanings.definition: More than half (50%) of some group.

examples.en: The majority agreed that the new proposal was the best.
examples.zh: 大多數人同意新提案是最好的。

meanings.definition: Brand or kind; model.

examples.en: What make of car do you drive?
examples.zh: 您開什麼牌子的車？

meanings.definition: To create.

examples.en: He made as if to punch him, but they both laughed and shook hands.
examples.zh: 他做出好像要打他的樣子，但他們都笑了，握手了。

meanings.definition: Someone who makes; a person or thing that makes or produces something.
Faulty functioning
To function improperly

examples.en: She told him the machine was poorly built, but they both knew that she was the one who had malfunctioned.
examples.zh: 她告訴他這台機器製造得很差，但他們都知道她出了故障。

meanings.definition: The act of managing or controlling something.
To direct or be in charge of.

examples.en: He managed to climb the tower.
examples.zh: 他成功地登上了塔樓。

meanings.definition: Administration; the use of limited resources combined with forecasting, planning, leadership and execution skills to achieve predetermined specific goals.

examples.en: Excellent time management helped her succeed in all facets of her life.
examples.zh: 出色的時間管理幫助她在生活的各個方面都取得了成功。

meanings.definition: A person whose job is to manage something, such as a business, a restaurant, or a sports team.

examples.en: a file manager; a task manager; Program Manager
examples.zh: 文件管理器；任務管理器；專案經理

meanings.definition: An official or authoritative command; an order or injunction; a commission; a judicial precept.
To authorize
A sign or line that require the path of the disc to be above, below or to one side of it.
Obligatory; required or commanded by authority.

examples.en: Attendance at a school is usually mandatory for children.
examples.zh: 兒童通常必須上學。

meanings.definition: The planned movement of troops, vehicles etc.; a strategic repositioning; (later also) a large training field-exercise of fighting units.

examples.en: Joint NATO maneuvers are as much an exercise in diplomacy as in tactics and logistics.
examples.zh: 北約聯合演習既是外交演習，也是戰術和後勤演習。

meanings.definition: To move (something, or oneself) carefully, and often with difficulty, into a certain position.

examples.en: The patriarch maneuvered till his offspring occupied countless key posts
examples.zh: 族長縱橫捭閔，直到他的子孫佔據了無數要職

meanings.definition: A list or invoice of the passengers or goods being carried by a commercial vehicle or ship.
To show plainly; to make to appear distinctly, usually to the mind; to put beyond question or doubt; to display; to exhibit.

examples.en: His courage manifested itself through the look on his face.
examples.zh: 他的勇氣從臉上的表情就可見一斑。

meanings.definition: Evident to the senses, especially to the sight; apparent; distinctly perceived.
To move, arrange or operate something using the hands
Mode of action; way of performing or doing anything

examples.en: His natural manner makes him seem like the boss.
examples.zh: 他自然的舉止讓他看起來像個老闆。

meanings.definition: A handbook.

examples.en: The dishwasher isn't working; can you remember where we put the manual?
examples.zh: 洗碗機不工作；你還記得我們把手冊放在哪裡嗎？

meanings.definition: The action or process of making goods systematically or on a large scale.
To make things, usually on a large scale, with tools and either physical labor or machinery.

examples.en: to manufacture wool into blankets
examples.zh: 將羊毛製造成毯子

meanings.definition: A book, composition or any other document, written by hand (or manually typewritten), not mechanically reproduced.
Handwritten, or by extension manually typewritten, as opposed to being mechanically reproduced.
A visual representation of an area, whether real or imaginary.

examples.en: I don't want to play this map again!
examples.zh: 我不想再玩這張地圖了！

meanings.definition: To create a visual representation of a territory, etc. via cartography.
The edge of the paper, typically left blank when printing but sometimes used for annotations etc.
To add a margin to.
A soldier, normally a member of a marine corps, trained to serve on board or from a ship

examples.en: He was a marine in World War II.
examples.zh: 他是第二次世界大戰中的一名海軍陸戰隊員。

meanings.definition: Belonging to or characteristic of the sea; existing or found in the sea; formed or produced by the sea.
(heading) Boundary, land within a boundary.
To put a mark on (something); to make (something) recognizable by a mark; to label or write on (something).

examples.en: to mark a box or bale of merchandise
examples.zh: 標記一盒或一捆商品

meanings.definition: A gathering of people for the purchase and sale of merchandise at a set time, often periodic.

examples.en: The privilege to hold a weekly market was invaluable for any feudal era burgh.
examples.zh: 對於任何封建時代的城鎮來說，每週舉辦一次市集的特權都是無價的。

meanings.definition: To make (products or services) available for sale and promote them.

examples.en: We plan to market an ecology model by next quarter.
examples.zh: 我們計劃在下個季度之前推出生態模型。

meanings.definition: To make (products or services) available for sale and promote them.

examples.en: We plan to market an ecology model by next quarter.
examples.zh: 我們計劃在下個季度之前推出生態模型。

meanings.definition: Buying and selling in a market.
The state of being married.

examples.en: You should enter marriage for love.
examples.zh: 你應該為了愛情而結婚。

meanings.definition: A high-ranking officer in the household of a medieval prince or lord, who was originally in charge of the cavalry and later the military forces in general.
To arrange (troops, etc.) in line for inspection or a parade.
(physical) Matter, material.
To form or collect into a mass; to form into a collective body; to bring together into masses; to assemble.

examples.en: I mass 70 kilograms.
examples.zh: 我體重70公斤。

meanings.definition: Involving a mass of things; concerning a large quantity or number.

examples.en: There is evidence of mass extinctions in the distant past.
examples.zh: 有證據顯示在遙遠的過去曾發生過大規模滅絕。

meanings.definition: A homogeneous mass of rock, not layered and without an obvious crystal structure.

examples.en: karst massives in western Georgia
examples.zh: 喬治亞州西部的喀斯特地塊

meanings.definition: Very large in size or extent

examples.en: Compared to its counterparts from World War II, the Abrams main battle tank is truly massive.
examples.zh: 與二戰時期的同類坦克相比，艾布拉姆斯主戰坦克確實非常龐大。

meanings.definition: Someone who has control over something or someone.

examples.en: Mark Twain was a master of fiction.
examples.zh: 馬克·吐溫是小​​說大師。

meanings.definition: To be a master.

examples.en: It took her years to master the art of needlecraft.
examples.zh: 她花了好幾年的時間才掌握了針線的藝術。

meanings.definition: Masterful.

examples.en: a master performance
examples.zh: 大師級表演

meanings.definition: A competitive sporting event such as a boxing meet, a baseball game, or a cricket match.

examples.en: My local team are playing in a match against their arch-rivals today.
examples.zh: 我當地的球隊今天正在與他們的主要競爭對手進行一場比賽。

meanings.definition: To agree; to be equal; to correspond.

examples.en: Their interests didn't match, so it took a long time to agree what to do together.
examples.zh: 他們的興趣並不相符，所以花了很長時間才同意一起做什麼。

meanings.definition: A fellow, comrade, colleague, partner or someone with whom something is shared, e.g. shipmate, classmate.

examples.en: He's my best mate.
examples.zh: 他是我最好的夥伴。

meanings.definition: To match, fit together without space between.

examples.en: The pieces of the puzzle mate perfectly.
examples.zh: 拼圖的各個部分完美搭配。

meanings.definition: Matter which may be shaped or manipulated, particularly in making something.

examples.en: Asphalt, composed of oil and sand, is a widely used material for roads.
examples.zh: 瀝青由油和沙組成，是廣泛使用的道路材料。

meanings.definition: To form from matter; to materialize.
Having to do with matter; consisting of matter.

examples.en: This compound has a number of interesting material properties.
examples.zh: 這種化合物具有許多有趣的材料特性。

meanings.definition: An abstract representational system used in the study of numbers, shapes, structure, change and the relationships between these concepts.

examples.en: My mathematics is always improving.
examples.zh: 我的數學一直在進步。

meanings.definition: Substance, material.

examples.en: What's the matter?;   state matters
examples.zh: 怎麼了？ ;   國家事務

meanings.definition: To be important.

examples.en: Sorry for pouring ketchup on your clean white shirt! - Oh, don't worry, it does not matter.
examples.zh: 抱歉把番茄醬倒在你乾淨的白襯衫上！ -哦，別擔心，沒關係。

meanings.definition: Fully developed; grown up in terms of physical appearance, behaviour or thinking; ripe.

examples.en: She is quite mature for her age.
examples.zh: 就她的年齡而言，她已經相當成熟了。

meanings.definition: The highest limit
To the highest degree.

examples.en: Use the proper dose for the maximum effect.
examples.zh: 使用適當的劑量以獲得最大效果。

meanings.definition: The chief executive of the municipal government of a city, borough, &c., formerly usually appointed as a caretaker by European royal courts but now usually appointed or elected locally.
Food that is prepared and eaten, usually at a specific time, and usually in a comparatively large quantity (as opposed to a snack).

examples.en: Breakfast is the morning meal, lunch is the noon meal, and dinner, or supper, is the evening meal.
examples.zh: 早餐是早上的飯菜，午餐是中午的飯菜，晚餐或宵夜是晚上的飯菜。

meanings.definition: To lament.
(of words or symbols) The entity, perception, feeling or concept thereby represented or evoked.

examples.en: the meaning of life
examples.zh: 生命的意義

meanings.definition: To lament.
(now chiefly in the plural) A method or course of action used to achieve some result.
A prescribed quantity or extent.
To ascertain the quantity of a unit of material via calculated comparison with respect to a standard.

examples.en: We measured the temperature with a thermometer.   You should measure the angle with a spirit level.
examples.zh: 我們用溫度計測量了溫度。   您應該用水平儀測量角度。

meanings.definition: A manual worker; a labourer or artisan.

examples.en: This game has a mechanic where if you run toward a ledge you automatically jump off rather than just falling.
examples.zh: 這個遊戲有一個機制，如果你跑向壁架，你會自動跳下來，而不是直接掉下來。

meanings.definition: Mechanical; relating to the laws of motion in the art of constructing things
(within a machine or machinery) Any mechanical means for the conversion or control of motion, or the transmission or control of power.

examples.en: political machine
examples.zh: 政治機器

meanings.definition: A stamped metal disc used as a personal ornament, a charm, or a religious object.
To win a medal.

examples.en: He medalled twice at the Olympics.
examples.zh: 他在奧運會上兩次獲得獎牌。

meanings.definition: The middle layer of the wall of a blood vessel or lymph vessel which is composed of connective and muscular tissue.
A medical examination.

examples.en: You'll have to get a medical before you apply for that job.
examples.zh: 在申請該工作之前，您必須先進行體檢。

meanings.definition: Of or pertaining to the practice of medicine.

examples.en: Do you have any medical experience?
examples.zh: 您有醫療經驗嗎？

meanings.definition: A substance which specifically promotes healing when ingested or consumed in some way.

examples.en: She's studying medicine at university because she wants to be a doctor in the future.
examples.zh: 她在大學學習醫學，因為她將來想成為一名醫生。

meanings.definition: To treat with medicine.
(plural media or mediums) The chemistry of the surrounding environment, e.g. solid, liquid, gas, vacuum, or a specific substance such as a solvent.

examples.en: Acrylics, oils, charcoal, and gouache are all mediums I used in my painting.
examples.zh: 丙烯、油畫、木炭和水粉都是我在繪畫中使用的媒材。

meanings.definition: Arithmetically average.
To a medium extent
A sports competition, especially for track and field (a track meet) or swimming (a swim meet).

examples.en: OK, let's arrange a meet with Tyler and ask him.
examples.zh: 好的，我們安排一次與泰勒的會面並詢問他的情況。

meanings.definition: To make contact (with) while in proximity.

examples.en: This proposal meets my requirements. The company agrees to meet the cost of any repairs.
examples.zh: 這個建議符合我的要求。本公司同意承擔任何維修費用。

meanings.definition: (gerund) The act of persons or things that meet.

examples.en: Meeting him will be exciting. I enjoy meeting new people.
examples.zh: 見到他將會令人興奮。 我喜歡認識新朋友。

meanings.definition: Tune; sequence of notes that makes up a musical phrase
Molten material, the product of melting.
To change (or to be changed) from a solid state to a liquid state, usually by a gradual heat.

examples.en: I melted butter to make a cake.
examples.zh: 我融化了奶油來做蛋糕。

meanings.definition: One who officially belongs to a group.

examples.en: The I-beams were to become structural members of a pedestrian bridge.
examples.zh: 工字梁將成為一座人行天橋的結構構件。

meanings.definition: The state of being a member of a group or organization.

examples.en: He has memberships in clubs in three cities.
examples.zh: 他在三個城市的俱樂部擁有會員資格。

meanings.definition: A short note; a memorandum.
To record something; to make a note of something.

examples.en: I made sure to memo him about the client's complaints.
examples.zh: 我一定要把客戶的投訴告訴他。

meanings.definition: Memory; recollection.
Serving as a remembrance of someone or something; commemorative.

examples.en: a memorial building
examples.zh: 一座紀念建築

meanings.definition: The ability of the brain to record information or impressions with the facility of recalling them later at will.

examples.en: Memory is a facility common to all animals.
examples.zh: 記憶是所有動物共有的一種能力。

meanings.definition: A speaking or notice of anything, usually in a brief or cursory manner. Used especially in the phrase make mention of.
To make a short reference to something.
A wise and trusted counselor or teacher
To act as someone's mentor
The details of the food to be served at a banquet; a bill of fare.
A person who traffics in commodities for profit.
As a resident of a region, to buy goods from a non-resident and sell them to another non-resident.

examples.en: a merchanting service
examples.zh: 商業服務

meanings.definition: Relenting; forbearance to cause or allow harm to another.

examples.en: She took mercy on him and quit embarrassing him.
examples.zh: 她憐憫他，不再讓他難堪。

meanings.definition: To feel mercy
Expressing surprise or alarm.

examples.en: Mercy! Look at the state of you!
examples.zh: 憐憫！看看你的狀態！

meanings.definition: A body of standing water, such as a lake or a pond. More specifically, it can refer to a lake that is broad in relation to its depth. Also included in place names such as Windermere.
The joining together of multiple sources.

examples.en: The merge of the two documents failed.
examples.zh: 兩個文檔的合併失敗。

meanings.definition: To combine into a whole.

examples.en: Headquarters merged the operations of the three divisions.
examples.zh: 總部將三個部門的業務合併。

meanings.definition: One that merges.
A claim to commendation or a reward.

examples.en: For her good performance in the examination, her teacher gave her ten merits.
examples.zh: 由於她在考試中表現出色，老師給了她十個優點。

meanings.definition: To deserve, to earn.

examples.en: Her performance merited wild applause.
examples.zh: 她的表演贏得了熱烈的掌聲。

meanings.definition: A communication, or what is communicated; any concept or information conveyed.

examples.en: We've just received an urgent message from the President.
examples.zh: 我們剛剛收到總統的緊急訊息。

meanings.definition: To send a message to; to transmit a message to, e.g. as text via a cell phone.

examples.en: I messaged her about the concert.
examples.zh: 我給她發了關於音樂會的訊息。

meanings.definition: One who brings messages.
To send something by messenger.

examples.en: I'll messenger over the signed documents.
examples.zh: 我會把簽署的文件轉達給你。

meanings.definition: (heading) Chemical elements or alloys, and the mines where their ores come from.

examples.en: We have American Airlines tickets, but it's on British Airways metal.
examples.zh: 我們有美國航空的機票，但是是英國航空的金屬機票。

meanings.definition: To make a road using crushed rock, stones etc.
Characterized by strong drum-beats and distorted guitars.
(always meter) A device that measures things.

examples.en: a labouring coal-meter
examples.zh: 工作中的煤表

meanings.definition: To measure with a metering device.
A process by which a task is completed; a way of doing something (followed by the adposition of, to or for before the purpose of the process):

examples.en: If one method doesn't work, you should ask a friend to help you.
examples.zh: 如果一種方法不起作用，你應該請朋友來幫助你。

meanings.definition: To apply a method

examples.en: The company employs extensive use of 3D modelling combined with solidification simulation to ensure that critical castings are properly methoded.
examples.zh: 該公司廣泛使用 3D 建模與凝固模擬相結合，以確保關鍵鑄件採用正確的方法。

meanings.definition: A bishop empowered to oversee other bishops; an archbishop.
Pertaining to the see or province of a metropolitan.
An optical instrument used for observing small objects.
To examine with a microscope, to put under a microscope (literally or figuratively).
A centre, midpoint.

examples.en: The middle of a circle is the point which has the same distance to every point of circle.
examples.zh: 圓的中心是到圓各點距離相等的點。

meanings.definition: To take a middle view of.
Located in the middle; in between.

examples.en: the middle point
examples.zh: 中間點

meanings.definition: A place in the middle of something; may be used of a literal or metaphorical location.
Among, in the middle of; amid.
Power, strength, force or influence held by a person or group.

examples.en: He pushed with all his might, but still it would not move.
examples.zh: 他用盡全力一推，它還是紋絲不動。

meanings.definition: Mighty; powerful.
To relocate periodically from one region to another, usually according to the seasons.

examples.en: Twice a year the Minnesotans migrate from their state to the Gulf of Mexico.
examples.zh: 明尼蘇達人每年兩次從他們的州遷徙到墨西哥灣。

meanings.definition: A relatively low-gravity beer, often with a dark colour; mild ale
Gentle and not easily angered.

examples.en: a mild man
examples.zh: 一個溫和的人

meanings.definition: The international mile: a unit of length precisely equal to 1.609344 kilometers established by treaty among Anglophone nations in 1959, divided into 5,280 feet or 1,760 yards.

examples.en: The shot missed by a mile.
examples.zh: 射擊偏出一英里。

meanings.definition: Armed forces.

examples.en: He spent six years in the military.
examples.zh: 他在軍隊服役了六年。

meanings.definition: Characteristic of members of the armed forces.

examples.en: She was dishonorably discharged from all military duties.
examples.zh: 她被不光彩地解除了所有軍事職責。

meanings.definition: A white liquid produced by the mammary glands of female mammals to nourish their young. From certain animals, especially cows, it is also called dairy milk and is a common food for humans as a beverage or used to produce various dairy products such as butter, cheese, and yogurt.

examples.en: Table three ordered three milks. (Formally: The guests at table three ordered three glasses of milk.)
examples.zh: 三號桌點了三種牛奶。 （正式說法：三號桌的客人點了三杯牛奶。）

meanings.definition: A grinding apparatus for substances such as grains, seeds, etc.

examples.en: Pepper has a stronger flavor when it is ground straight from a mill.
examples.zh: 直接從磨坊中磨碎的胡椒具有更濃鬱的味道。

meanings.definition: To grind or otherwise process in a mill or other machine.

examples.en: to mill flour
examples.zh: 磨麵粉

meanings.definition: (long and short scales) The cardinal number 1,000,000: 106; a thousand thousand.

examples.en: I can think of millions of reasons not to go.
examples.zh: 我能想出數百萬個不去的理由。

meanings.definition: The ability for rational thought.

examples.en: Despite advancing age, his mind was still as sharp as ever.
examples.zh: 儘管年事已高，但他的頭腦依然敏銳。

meanings.definition: (originally and chiefly in negative or interrogative constructions) To dislike, to object to; to be bothered by.

examples.en: Do you mind if I smoke?
examples.zh: 你介意我抽煙嗎？

meanings.definition: My; belonging to me; that which belongs to me.
Any naturally occurring inorganic material that has a (more or less) definite chemical composition and characteristic physical properties.
Of, related to, or containing minerals
The lowest limit.

examples.en: We need a minimum of three staff members on duty at all time.
examples.zh: 我們始終需要至少三名工作人員值班。

meanings.definition: To the lowest degree.
A person who is trained to preach, to perform religious ceremonies, and to afford pastoral care at a Protestant church.

examples.en: The minister said a prayer on behalf of the entire congregation.
examples.zh: 牧師代表全體會眾做了禱告。

meanings.definition: To attend to (the needs of); to tend; to take care (of); to give aid; to give service.

Government department, at the administrative level normally headed by a minister (or equivalent rank, e.g. secretary of state), who holds it as portfolio, especially in a constitutional monarchy, but also as a polity

examples.en: She works for the ministry of finance.
examples.zh: 她在財政部工作。

meanings.definition: A person who is below the age of majority, consent, criminal responsibility or other adult responsibilities and accountabilities.

examples.en: It is illegal to sell weapons to minors under the age of eighteen.
examples.zh: 向十八歲以下未成年人出售武器是違法的。

meanings.definition: To choose or have an area of secondary concentration as a student in a college or university.

examples.en: I had so many credit hours of English, I decided to minor in it.
examples.zh: 我有很多英語學分，所以我決定輔修它。

meanings.definition: Of little significance or importance.

examples.en: The physical appearance of a candidate is a minor factor in recruitment.
examples.zh: 候選人的外表是招募中的次要因素。

meanings.definition: The state of being a minor; youth, the period of a person's life prior to reaching adulthood.

examples.en: Most people agreed, but a sizable minority were upset by the decision.
examples.zh: 大多數人都同意，但也有相當一部分人對這項決定感到不安。

meanings.definition: Of or relating to a minority.

examples.en: They hold a minority interest in the company.
examples.zh: 他們持有該公司的少數股權。

meanings.definition: A building or institution where money (originally, only coins) is produced under government licence.

examples.en: It must have cost a mint to produce!
examples.zh: 製作起來一定要花不少錢！

meanings.definition: To reproduce (coins), usually en masse, under licence.
(with condition) Like new.

examples.en: in mint condition.
examples.zh: 完好無損。

meanings.definition: The minus sign (−).
To subtract.
Being a negative quantity; pertaining to a deficit or reduction.

examples.en: a minus number
examples.zh: 一個負數

meanings.definition: Made less or reduced by (followed by an expression of number or quantity).

examples.en: seven minus two is five
examples.zh: 七減二等於五

meanings.definition: A unit of time equal to sixty seconds (one-sixtieth of an hour).

examples.en: You have twenty minutes to complete the test.
examples.zh: 您有二十分鐘的時間來完成測驗。

meanings.definition: Of an event, to write in a memo or the minutes of a meeting.

examples.en: I’ll minute this evening’s meeting.
examples.zh: 我將記錄今晚的會議。

meanings.definition: An event that appears inexplicable by the laws of nature and so is held to be supernatural in origin.

examples.en: An example of a miracle associated with Muhammad is the splitting of the moon.
examples.zh: 與穆罕默德有關的奇蹟的一個例子是月亮的分裂。

meanings.definition: To affect by a miracle; to work a miracle upon.
A smooth surface, usually made of glass with reflective material painted on the underside, that reflects light so as to give an image of what is in front of it.

examples.en: I had a look in the mirror to see if the blood had come off my face.
examples.zh: 我照了照鏡子，看看臉上的血有沒有掉下來。

meanings.definition: Of an event, activity, behaviour, etc, to be identical to, to be a copy of.
Consisting of a variety of ingredients or parts.
A miserable person; a wretch.
In a state of misery: very sad, ill, or poor.

examples.en: He's good at some sports, like tennis, but he's just miserable at football.
examples.zh: 他擅長某些運動，例如網球，但他不擅長足球。

meanings.definition: Great unhappiness; extreme pain of body or mind; wretchedness; distress; woe.

examples.en: Ever since his wife left him you can see the misery on his face.
examples.zh: 自從他的妻子離開他以來，你可以看到他臉上的痛苦。

meanings.definition: Bad luck

examples.en: It was my fortune, or misfortune, to be called to the office of Chief Executive without any previous political training. - Ulysses S. Grant
examples.zh: 我在沒有任何政治訓練的情況下就被任命為行政長官，這對我來說是幸運，也可以說是不幸。 ——尤利西斯·S·格蘭特

meanings.definition: To lead astray, in a false direction.

examples.en: The preacher elaborated Satan's ways to mislead us into sin
examples.zh: 傳道人詳細闡述了撒但誤導我們犯罪的方法

meanings.definition: A failure to hit.

examples.en: I think I’ll give the meeting a miss.
examples.zh: 我想我會錯過這次會議。

meanings.definition: To fail to hit.

examples.en: I missed the target.
examples.zh: 我錯過了目標。

meanings.definition: Any object used as a weapon by being thrown or fired through the air, such as stone, arrow or bullet.

examples.en: The Rhodians, who used leaden bullets, were able to project their missiles twice as far as the Persian slingers, who used large stones.
examples.zh: 使用鉛彈的羅德島人能夠將飛彈投射的距離是使用大石頭的波斯投石手的兩倍。

meanings.definition: A set of tasks that fulfills a purpose or duty; an assignment set by an employer, or by oneself.

examples.en: Many cities across the Americas grew from Spanish missions.
examples.zh: 美洲的許多城市都是從西班牙傳教區發展而來的。

meanings.definition: To send on a mission.
Water or other liquid finely suspended in air.

examples.en: It was difficult to see through the morning mist.
examples.zh: 透過晨霧很難看清東西。

meanings.definition: To form mist.

examples.en: It's misting this morning.
examples.zh: 今天早上有霧。

meanings.definition: An error; a blunder.
To understand wrongly, taking one thing or person for another.

examples.en: Don't mistake my kindness for weakness.
examples.zh: 別把我的善良當成軟弱。

meanings.definition: To stir together.

examples.en: Mix the eggs and milk with the flour until the consistency is smooth.
examples.zh: 將雞蛋和牛奶與麵粉混合，直到變得光滑。

meanings.definition: The act of mixing.

examples.en: A teaspoonful of the mixture to be taken three times daily after meals
examples.zh: 一茶匙混合物，每日三次，飯後服用

meanings.definition: A kinetic sculpture or decorative arrangement made of items hanging so that they can move independently from each other.

examples.en: there are many business opportunities in mobile
examples.zh: 行動領域有很多商機

meanings.definition: Capable of being moved, especially on wheels.

examples.en: a mobile home
examples.zh: 移動房屋

meanings.definition: One of several ancient Greek scales.

examples.en: What was the mode of entry?
examples.zh: 進入方式是什麼？

meanings.definition: A person who serves as a subject for artwork or fashion, usually in the medium of photography but also for painting or drawing.

examples.en: The beautiful model had her face on the cover of almost every fashion magazine imaginable.
examples.zh: 這位美麗的模特兒幾乎登上了所有可以想像到的時尚雜誌的封面。

meanings.definition: To display for others to see, especially in regard to wearing clothing while performing the role of a fashion model

examples.en: She modelled the shoes for her friends to see.
examples.zh: 她為她的朋友們製作了鞋子的模型。

meanings.definition: Worthy of being a model; exemplary.
One who holds an intermediate position between extremes, as in politics.

examples.en: The moderates are the natural advocates of ecumenism against the fanatics of their churches.
examples.zh: 溫和派是普世主義的天然倡導者，反對教會的狂熱分子。

meanings.definition: To reduce the excessiveness of (something)

examples.en: to moderate rage, action, desires, etc.
examples.zh: 控制憤怒、行動、慾望等。

meanings.definition: Not excessive; acting in moderation

examples.en: a moderate Calvinist
examples.zh: 溫和的加爾文主義者

meanings.definition: Someone who lives in modern times.
Pertaining to a current or recent time and style; not ancient.

examples.en: Our online interactive game is a modern approach to teaching about gum disease.  Although it was built in the 1600s, the building still has a very modern look.
examples.zh: 我們的線上互動遊戲是一種現代的牙齦疾病教學方法。  雖然建於1600年代，但這座建築仍然具有非常現代的外觀。

meanings.definition: Not bragging or boasting about oneself or one's achievements; unpretentious, humble.

examples.en: He earns a modest amount of money.
examples.zh: 他賺的錢不多。

meanings.definition: To change part of.
A self-contained component of a system, often interchangeable, which has a well-defined interface to the other components.

examples.en: Which modules are you studying next year?
examples.zh: 您明年將學習哪些模組？

meanings.definition: To moisten.
Slightly wet; characterised by the presence of moisture, not dry; damp.
A brief, unspecified amount of time.

examples.en: Wait a moment, while I lock the front door.
examples.zh: 等一下，我鎖上前門。

meanings.definition: Of a body in motion: the tendency of a body to maintain its inertial motion; the product of its mass and velocity.
Of, pertaining to, or consisting of money.
A legally or socially binding conceptual contract of entitlement to wealth, void of intrinsic value, payable for all debts and taxes, and regulated in supply.

examples.en: Before colonial times cowry shells imported from Mauritius were used as money in Western Africa.
examples.zh: 在殖民時代之前，從毛里求斯進口的貝殼在西非被用作貨幣。

meanings.definition: Someone who watches over something; a person in charge of something or someone.

examples.en: The camp monitors look after the children during the night, when the teachers are asleep.
examples.zh: 夜間，當老師們睡覺時，營地的監護人會照顧孩子。

meanings.definition: To watch over; to guard.

A situation, by legal privilege or other agreement, in which solely one party (company, cartel etc.) exclusively provides a particular product or service, dominating that market and generally exerting powerful control over it.

examples.en: A land monopoly renders its holder(s) nearly almighty in an agricultural society.
examples.zh: 土地壟斷使其所有者在農業社會中幾乎擁有全能。

meanings.definition: A publication that is published once a month.
Occurring every month.
Every month.
A structure built for commemorative or symbolic reasons, or as a memorial; a commemoration.

examples.en: There is a monument on the town green to the soldiers who died in World War I.
examples.zh: 鎮上的綠地上有一座紀念碑，紀念在第一次世界大戰中陣亡的士兵。

meanings.definition: To mark or memorialize with a monument.
A mental or emotional state, composure.

examples.en: I've been in a bad mood since I dumped my boyfriend.
examples.zh: 自從我甩了男友之後，我的心情一直很糟。

meanings.definition: (of a narrative) The ethical significance or practical lesson.

examples.en: a candidate with strong morals
examples.zh: 道德高尚的候選人

meanings.definition: To moralize.
Of or relating to principles of right and wrong in behaviour, especially for teaching right behaviour.

examples.en: moral judgments;  a moral poem
examples.zh: 道德判斷；  一首道德詩

meanings.definition: In addition to what has been said; furthermore; additionally.

A special form of secured loan where the purpose of the loan must be specified to the lender, to purchase assets that must be fixed (not movable) property, such as a house or piece of farm land. The assets are registered as the legal property of the borrower but the lender can seize them and dispose of them if they are not satisfied with the manner in which the repayment of the loan is conducted by the borrower. Once the loan is fully repaid, the lender loses this right of seizure and the assets are then deemed to be unencumbered.

examples.en: We're renting a property in the city centre because we can't afford to get a mortgage yet.
examples.zh: 我們在市中心租了一間房子，因為我們還付不起抵押貸款。

meanings.definition: To borrow against a property, to obtain a loan for another purpose by giving away the right of seizure to the lender over a fixed property such as a house or piece of land; to pledge a property in order to get a loan.

examples.en: We mortgaged our house in order to start a company.
examples.zh: 我們抵押了房子來創辦一家公司。

meanings.definition: The greatest amount.

examples.en: The most I can offer for the house is $150,000.
examples.zh: 我能出的最高價格是 15 萬美元。

meanings.definition: Forms the superlative of many adjectives.

examples.en: Correctness is most important.
examples.zh: 正確性最重要。

meanings.definition: The greater part of a group, especially a group of people.

examples.en: Most want the best for their children.
examples.zh: 大多數人都希望給自己的孩子最好的。

meanings.definition: A state of progression from one place to another.

examples.en: The motion to amend is now open for discussion.
examples.zh: 修正案動議現已開放討論。

meanings.definition: To gesture indicating a desired movement.

examples.en: He motioned for me to come closer.
examples.zh: 他示意我靠近一些。

meanings.definition: To provide someone with an incentive to do something; to encourage.

examples.en: The weekly staff meeting was meant to motivate employees.
examples.zh: 每週一次的員工會議旨在激勵員工。

meanings.definition: An idea or communication that makes one want to act, especially from spiritual sources; a divine prompting.

examples.en: No-one could understand why she had hidden the shovel; her motives were obscure at best.
examples.zh: 沒有人能理解她為什麼要把鏟子藏起來。她的動機充其量是模糊的。

meanings.definition: To prompt or incite by a motive or motives; to move.
Causing motion; having power to move, or tending to move

examples.en: a motive argument
examples.zh: 動機論證

meanings.definition: A machine or device that converts other energy forms into mechanical energy, or imparts motion.

examples.en: Nice motor!
examples.zh: 好馬達！

meanings.definition: To make a journey by motor vehicle; to drive.

examples.en: On Saturday we motored down to Plymouth.
examples.zh: 星期六我們開車去普利茅斯。

meanings.definition: Relating to the ability to move

examples.en: She has excellent motor skills.
examples.zh: 她擁有出色的運動技能。

meanings.definition: A hill or mountain.

examples.en: the mount of Jupiter
examples.zh: 木星山

meanings.definition: An elevation of land of considerable dimensions rising more or less abruptly, forming a conspicuous figure in the landscape, usually having a small extent of surface at its summit.

examples.en: We spent the weekend hiking in the mountains.
examples.zh: 我們週末在山裡徒步旅行。

meanings.definition: Sorrow, grief.
To express sadness or sorrow for; to grieve over (especially a death).
The opening of a creature through which food is ingested.

examples.en: "Open your mouth and say 'aah'," directed the doctor.
examples.zh: 「張開嘴，說『啊’，」醫生指示道。

meanings.definition: To speak; to utter.

examples.en: He mouthed his opinions on the subject at the meeting.
examples.zh: 他在會中就這個問題發表了自己的看法。

meanings.definition: The act of moving; a movement.

examples.en: A slight move of the tiller, and the boat will go off course.
examples.zh: 舵柄輕輕一動，船就會偏離航線。

meanings.definition: To change place or posture; to go, in any manner, from one place or position to another.

examples.en: A ship moves rapidly.
examples.zh: 一艘船快速移動。

meanings.definition: Physical motion between points in space.

examples.en: I saw a movement in that grass on the hill.
examples.zh: 我看到山上的草叢裡有動靜。

meanings.definition: A recorded sequence of images displayed on a screen at a rate sufficiently fast to create the appearance of motion.

examples.en: Let's go to the movies.
examples.zh: 我們去看電影吧。

meanings.definition: A mixture of water and soil or fine grained sediment.

examples.en: The campaign issues got lost in all the mud from both parties.
examples.zh: 競選議題在雙方的混亂中迷失了。

meanings.definition: To make muddy or dirty; to apply mud to (something).
A whole number that can be divided by another number with no remainder.

examples.en: 14, 21 and 70 are multiples of 7

meanings.definition: More than one (followed by plural).

examples.en: My Swiss Army knife has multiple blades.
examples.zh: 我的瑞士軍刀有多個刀片。

meanings.definition: An act or instance of multiplying.
To increase the amount, degree or number of (something).

examples.en: when you multiply 3 by 7, you get 21;  he multiplied several numbers
examples.zh: 3乘以7，得到21；  他把幾個數字相乘

meanings.definition: A financial instrument issued by a municipality.
Of or pertaining to a municipality (a city or a corporation having the right of administering local government).
A contractile form of tissue which animals use to effect movement.

examples.en: Muscle consists largely of actin and myosin filaments.
examples.zh: 肌肉主要由肌動蛋白和肌球蛋白絲組成。

meanings.definition: To use force to make progress, especially physical force.

examples.en: He muscled his way through the crowd.
examples.zh: 他用力擠過人群。

meanings.definition: A building or institution dedicated to the acquisition, conservation, study, exhibition, and educational interpretation of objects having scientific, historical, cultural or artistic value.
A series of sounds organized in time, employing melody, harmony, tempo etc. usually to convey a mood.

examples.en: I keep listening to this music because it's a masterpiece.
examples.zh: 我一直在聽這首音樂，因為它是一部傑作。

meanings.definition: To seduce or entice with music.
A mutual fund.
Having the same relationship, each to each other.

examples.en: They were mutual enemies.
examples.zh: 他們是共同的敵人。

meanings.definition: Something secret or unexplainable; an unknown.

examples.en: The truth behind the events remains a mystery.
examples.zh: 事件背後的真相仍是個謎。

meanings.definition: A traditional story which embodies a belief regarding some fact or phenomenon of experience, and in which often the forces of nature and of the soul are personified; a sacred narrative regarding a god, a hero, the origin of the world or of a people, etc.

examples.en: Myth was the product of man's emotion and imagination, acted upon by his surroundings. (E. Clodd, Myths & Dreams (1885), 7, cited after OED)
examples.zh: 神話是人類情感和想像力的產物，受到周遭環境的影響。 (E. Clodd, Myths & Dreams (1885), 7, 引自《牛津英語字典》)

meanings.definition: Bare, not covered by clothing.

examples.en: She was as naked as the day she was born.
examples.zh: 她就像出生那天一樣赤身裸體。

meanings.definition: Any nounal word or phrase which indicates a particular person, place, class, or thing.

examples.en: I've never liked the name my parents gave me so I changed it at the age of twenty.
examples.zh: 我一直不喜歡父母給我的名字，所以我在二十歲時改變了它。

meanings.definition: Specifically; that is to say.

examples.en: Some of the students — namely Paul, Alice and Jake — seem to have trouble with geometry.
examples.zh: 有些學生——即保羅、愛麗絲和傑克——似乎在幾何方面有困難。

meanings.definition: A serviette; a (usually rectangular) piece of cloth or paper used at the table for wiping the mouth and hands for cleanliness while eating.
The systematic recitation of an event or series of events.

examples.en: The plot is full of holes, but the narrative is extremely compelling.
examples.zh: 劇情漏洞百出，但敘事卻極為引人入勝。

meanings.definition: Telling a story.

examples.en: the narrative thrust of a film
examples.zh: 電影的敘事主旨

meanings.definition: (chiefly in the plural) A narrow passage, especially a contracted part of a stream, lake, or sea; a strait connecting two bodies of water.

examples.en: the narrows of New York harbor
examples.zh: 紐約港的狹窄地帶

meanings.definition: Having a small width; not wide; having opposite edges or sides that are close, especially by comparison to length or depth.

examples.en: a narrow hallway
examples.zh: 狹窄的走廊

meanings.definition: A historically constituted, stable community of people, formed on the basis of a common language, territory, economic life, ethnicity and/or psychological make-up manifested in a common culture.

examples.en: The Kurdish people constitute a nation in the Middle East
examples.zh: 庫德人在中東構成一個民族

meanings.definition: An aboriginal inhabitant of a region colonized by English-speaking people; in particular:
Aboriginal inhabitant of a region colonized by English-speaking people; in particular:
A native inhabitant of a place, country etc.

examples.en: He's a natural on the saxophone.
examples.zh: 他是薩克斯風的天生演奏者。

meanings.definition: That exists and evolved within the confines of an ecosystem.

examples.en: The species will be under threat if its natural habitat is destroyed.
examples.zh: 如果其自然棲息地遭到破壞，該物種將受到威脅。

meanings.definition: Naturally; in a natural manner.
The natural world; that which consists of all things unaffected by or predating human technology, production, and design. (Compare ecosystem.)

examples.en: Nature never lies (i.e. tells untruths).
examples.zh: 大自然從不說謊（即說謊）。

meanings.definition: To endow with natural qualities.
Of or relating to a navy.

examples.en: naval architect
examples.zh: 海軍建築師

meanings.definition: To plan, control and record the position and course of a vehicle, ship, aircraft, etc., on a journey; to follow a planned course.

examples.en: He navigated the bomber to the Ruhr.
examples.zh: 他駕駛轟炸機飛往魯爾區。

meanings.definition: The left side of a horse or of a team of horses pulling a carriage etc.
To come closer to; to approach.

examples.en: The ship nears the land.
examples.zh: 船靠近陸地。

meanings.definition: Physically close.

examples.en: I can't see near objects very clearly without my glasses.
examples.zh: 不戴眼鏡我就看不清楚近處的物體。

meanings.definition: At or towards a position close in space or time.

examples.en: He was near unconscious when I found him.
examples.zh: 當我發現他時，他幾乎失去知覺。

meanings.definition: Physically close to, in close proximity to.

examples.en: There are habitable planets orbiting many of the stars near our Sun.
examples.zh: 有宜居行星圍繞著太陽附近的許多恆星運行。

meanings.definition: Adjacent, near, close by

examples.en: He stopped at a nearby store for some groceries.
examples.zh: 他在附近的一家商店停下來買了一些雜貨。

meanings.definition: Next to, close to

examples.en: I'm glad my friends live nearby where I can visit them.
examples.zh: 我很高興我的朋友住在附近，我可以拜訪他們。

meanings.definition: An artificial intelligence researcher who believes that solutions should be elegant, clear and provably correct. Compare scruffy.
Clean, tidy; free from dirt or impurities.

examples.en: My room is neat because I tidied it this morning.  She has very neat hair.
examples.zh: 我的房間很整潔，因為我今天早上整理過。  她的頭髮非常整齊。

meanings.definition: (usually with the definite article) A place to do the "necessary" business of urination and defecation: an outhouse or lavatory.
Required, essential, whether logically inescapable or needed in order to achieve a desired result or avoid some penalty.

examples.en: Although I wished to think that all was false, it was yet necessary that I, who thus thought, must in some sense exist.
examples.zh: 雖然我希望認為一切都是假的，但我這樣認為的人在某種意義上必須存在。

meanings.definition: The quality or state of being necessary, unavoidable, or absolutely requisite.

examples.en: I bought a new table out of necessity. My old one was ruined.
examples.zh: 出於需要，我買了一張新桌子。我的舊的已經毀了。

meanings.definition: The part of the body connecting the head and the trunk found in humans and some animals.

examples.en: Giraffes have long necks.
examples.zh: 長頸鹿的脖子很長。
