meanings.definition: To hang by the neck; strangle; kill, eliminate

examples.en: Go neck yourself.
examples.zh: 自己去脖子吧。

meanings.definition: A requirement for something; something needed.

examples.en: I've always tried to have few needs beyond food, clothing and shelter.
examples.zh: 除了食物、衣服和住所之外，我一直試著滿足很少的需求。

meanings.definition: A fine, sharp implement usually for piercing such as sewing, or knitting, acupuncture, tattooing, body piercing, medical injections, etc.

examples.en: The seamstress threaded the needle to sew on a button.
examples.zh: 女裁縫將針穿過，縫上羈扣。

meanings.definition: To pierce with a needle, especially for sewing or acupuncture.

examples.en: Billy needled his sister incessantly about her pimples.
examples.zh: 比利不斷地用針刺妹妹的青春痘。

meanings.definition: Refusal or withholding of assents; prohibition, veto
To refuse; to veto.
Not positive nor neutral.

examples.en: Customers didn’t like it: feedback was mostly negative.
examples.zh: 客戶不喜歡它：回饋大多是負面的。

meanings.definition: (signalling) An elaborate synonym for no.
The act of neglecting.
To fail to care for or attend to something.

examples.en: to neglect duty or business;  to neglect to pay debts
examples.zh: 玩忽職守或疏忽職守；  忽視償還債務

meanings.definition: To confer with others in order to come to terms or reach an agreement.

examples.en: The client and server computers must first negotiate a network protocol to be used.
examples.zh: 客戶端和伺服器電腦必須先協商要使用的網路協定。

meanings.definition: A person living on adjacent or nearby land; a person situated adjacently or nearby; anything (of the same type of thing as the subject) in an adjacent or nearby position.

examples.en: My neighbour has two noisy cats.
examples.zh: 我的鄰居有兩隻吵鬧的貓。

meanings.definition: To be adjacent to

examples.en: Though France neighbours Germany, its culture is significantly different.
examples.zh: 儘管法國與德國相鄰，但其文化卻截然不同。

meanings.definition: Similarly not.

examples.en: Just as you would not correct it, neither would I.
examples.zh: 正如你不會糾正它一樣，我也不會。

meanings.definition: Not either one of two.

examples.en: I've tried on both shirts, but neither fits properly.
examples.zh: 兩件襯衫我都試過，但都不合身。

meanings.definition: Not either (used with nor).

examples.en: Neither now, nor ever will he forsake his mother.
examples.zh: 無論是現在，還是永遠，他都不會拋棄他的母親。

meanings.definition: A bundle of neurons with their connective tissue sheaths, blood vessels and lymphatics.

examples.en: The nerves can be seen through the skin.
examples.zh: 透過皮膚可以看到神經。

meanings.definition: To give courage.

examples.en: May their example nerve us to face the enemy.
examples.zh: 願他們的榜樣激勵我們面對敵人。

meanings.definition: Of sinews and tendons.
A structure built by a bird as a place to incubate eggs and rear young.

examples.en: That nightclub is a nest of strange people!
examples.zh: 那家夜總會是一群奇怪的人！

meanings.definition: (of animals) To build or settle into a nest.

examples.en: We loved the new house and were nesting there in two days!
examples.zh: 我們喜歡這個新房子，兩天後就在那裡安家了！

meanings.definition: A fabric or structure of fibrous elements attached to each other at regular intervals.

examples.en: A network of roads crisscrossed the country.
examples.zh: 公路網縱橫全國。

meanings.definition: To interact socially for the purpose of getting connections or personal advancement.

examples.en: Many people find it worthwhile to network for jobs and information.
examples.zh: 許多人發現透過網路尋找工作和資訊是值得的。

meanings.definition: A nonaligned state, or a member of such a state.
Not taking sides in a conflict such as war; nonaligned.

examples.en: I am neutral regarding the issue of gay marriage.
examples.zh: 我對同性婚姻議題持中立態度。

meanings.definition: In spite of what preceded; yet.
Things that are new.

examples.en: Out with the old, in with the new.
examples.zh: 淘汰舊的，引進新的。

meanings.definition: To make new; to recreate; to renew.
Recently made, or created.

examples.en: This is a new scratch on my car!   The band just released a new album.
examples.zh: 這是我車上的新刮痕！   樂團剛發行了一張新專輯。

meanings.definition: Newly (especially in composition).

examples.en: new-born, new-formed, new-found, new-mown
examples.zh: 新出生的，新形成的，新發現的，新割的

meanings.definition: Very recently; in the immediate past.

examples.en: She smelled the newly budding flowers.
examples.zh: 她聞到了剛剛含苞待放的花香。

meanings.definition: New information of interest.

examples.en: Is there any news about the storm?
examples.zh: 有關於暴風雨的消息嗎？

meanings.definition: To report; to make known.
A publication, usually published daily or weekly and usually printed on cheap, low-quality paper, containing news and other articles.
To cover with newspaper.

examples.en: She newspapered one end of the room before painting the bookcase.
examples.zh: 在粉刷書架之前，她在房間的一端塗了報紙。

meanings.definition: The one that follows after this one.

examples.en: Next, please, don't hold up the queue!
examples.zh: 接下來，請不要排隊！

meanings.definition: Nearest in place or position, having nothing similar intervening; adjoining.

examples.en: the next chapter; the next man I see; the next week; the Sunday next before Easter
examples.zh: 下一章；我見到的下一個人；下週；復活節前的下一個星期日

meanings.definition: In a time, place, rank or sequence closest or following.

examples.en: It's the next best thing to ice cream.
examples.zh: 這是僅次於冰淇淋的最好的東西。

meanings.definition: On the side of; nearest or adjacent to; next to.
Niceness.
Pleasant, satisfactory.

examples.en: What is a nice person like you doing in a place like this?
examples.zh: 像你這樣的好人在這種地方做什麼？

meanings.definition: Nicely.

examples.en: Children, play nice.
examples.zh: 孩子們，好好玩耍吧。

meanings.definition: Used to signify a job well done.

examples.en: Nice! I couldn't have done better.
examples.zh: 好的！我不可能做得更好。

meanings.definition: The period between sunset and sunrise, when a location faces far away from the sun, thus when the sky is dark.

examples.en: How do you sleep at night when you attack your kids like that!?
examples.zh: 當你這樣攻擊你的孩子時，你晚上怎麼睡！ ？

meanings.definition: To spend a night (in a place), to overnight.
Ellipsis of good night

examples.en: Night, y'all! Thanks for a great evening!
examples.zh: 晚安，你們！感謝您度過了一個美好的夜晚！

meanings.definition: Nothing; zero.
An aristocrat; one of aristocratic blood.

examples.en: This country house was occupied by nobles in the 16th century.
examples.zh: 這棟鄉間別墅在 16 世紀由貴族居住。

meanings.definition: Having honorable qualities; having moral eminence and freedom from anything petty, mean or dubious in conduct and character.

examples.en: He is a noble man who would never put his family in jeopardy.
examples.zh: 他是個高尚的人，永遠不會讓自己的家人陷入危險。

meanings.definition: Someone who is not important or well-known.

examples.en: Whether you're a nobody or a player, everyone in Tinseltown wants to be King of Hollywood! - Conquering Hollywood
examples.zh: 無論您是無名小卒還是玩家，好萊塢的每個人都想成為好萊塢之王！ - 征服好萊塢

meanings.definition: Not any person; the logical negation of somebody.

examples.en: I asked several people, but nobody knew how.
examples.zh: 我問了好幾個人，但沒人知道怎麼做。

meanings.definition: Various sounds, usually unwanted or unpleasant.

examples.en: He knew that it was trash day, when the garbage collectors made all the noise.
examples.zh: 他知道今天是垃圾日，垃圾收集者會發出各種噪音。

meanings.definition: To make a noise; to sound.
(grammar) A noun or word group that functions as part of a noun phrase.

examples.en: This sentence contains two nominals.
examples.zh: 這句話包含兩個名詞。

meanings.definition: Of, resembling, relating to, or consisting of a name or names.

examples.en: a nominal difference
examples.zh: 名義上的差異

meanings.definition: To name someone as a candidate for a particular role or position, including that of an office.

examples.en: 1658: the City of Norwich [...] was enlarged, builded and nominated by the Saxons. — Sir Thomas Browne, Urne-Burial (Penguin 2005, p. 12)
examples.zh: 1658 年：諾威奇市 [...] 被擴大、建造並由撒克遜人提名。 ——湯瑪斯‧布朗爵士，《甕葬》（Penguin 2005，第 12 頁）

meanings.definition: Nominotypical

examples.en: the nominate subspecies
examples.zh: 指名亞種

meanings.definition: A person without religious affiliation.
To no extent, in no way.

examples.en: I felt none the worse for my recent illness.
examples.zh: 我對最近的病並沒有感覺更糟。

meanings.definition: Not any of a given number or group.

examples.en: None of this meat tastes right.
examples.zh: 這些肉味道都不對勁。

meanings.definition: Nevertheless.
And not (introducing a negative statement, without necessarily following one).

examples.en: Nor did I stop to think, but ran.
examples.zh: 我也沒有停下來思考，而是跑了。

meanings.definition: That which is normal or typical.

examples.en: Unemployment is the norm in this part of the country.
examples.zh: 失業是該國這一地區的常態。

meanings.definition: A line or vector that is perpendicular to another line, surface, or plane.

examples.en: Heavy workload is the new normal.
examples.zh: 繁重的工作量已成為新常態。

meanings.definition: According to norms or rules or to a regular pattern.

examples.en: Organize the data into third normal form.
examples.zh: 將資料組織成第三範式。

meanings.definition: One of the four major compass points, specifically 0°, directed toward the North Pole, and conventionally upwards on a map, abbreviated as N.

examples.en: Minnesota is in the north of the USA.
examples.zh: 明尼蘇達州位於美國北部。

meanings.definition: To turn or move toward the north.
Of or pertaining to the north; northern.

examples.en: He lived in north Germany.
examples.zh: 他住在德國北部。

meanings.definition: Toward the north; northward; northerly.

examples.en: Switzerland is north of Italy.
examples.zh: 瑞士位於義大利北部。

meanings.definition: A protuberance on the face housing the nostrils, which are used to breathe or smell.

examples.en: She has a cold in the nose.
examples.zh: 她鼻子感冒了。

meanings.definition: To move cautiously by advancing its front end.

examples.en: The ship nosed through the minefield.
examples.zh: 這艘船駛過雷區。

meanings.definition: A person or thing of distinction.
Worthy of note; remarkable; memorable; noted or distinguished.
(heading) A symbol or annotation.

examples.en: a poet of note
examples.zh: 著名詩人

meanings.definition: To notice with care; to observe; to remark; to heed.

examples.en: If you look to the left, you can note the old cathedral.
examples.zh: 如果你向左看，你會注意到古老的大教堂。

meanings.definition: A book in which notes or memoranda are written.
Something trifling, or of no consequence or importance.

examples.en: You're nothing to me now!
examples.zh: 現在你對我來說什麼都不是了！

meanings.definition: Not at all; in no way.
Not any thing; no thing.
The act of observing; perception.

examples.en: He took no notice of the changes, and went on as though nothing had happened.
examples.zh: 他沒有註意到這些變化，繼續若無其事地繼續著。

meanings.definition: To remark upon; to mention.

examples.en: Did you notice the flowers in her yard?
examples.zh: 你注意到她院子裡的花了嗎？

meanings.definition: Capable of being seen or noticed.
To give (someone) notice (of some event).

examples.en: Once a decision has been reached and notified to the parties it becomes binding.
examples.zh: 一旦做出決定並通知各方，該決定就具有約束力。

meanings.definition: Mental apprehension of whatever may be known, thought, or imagined; idea, concept.

examples.en: Yankee notions
examples.zh: 洋基觀念

meanings.definition: Widely known, especially for something negative; infamous.
An instance of the word "notwithstanding", often characteristic of legalese.
Nevertheless, all the same.

examples.en: He, notwithstanding, persisted in his inquiries.
examples.zh: 儘管如此，他還是堅持詢問。

meanings.definition: In spite of, despite.
Although.
Newly made, formed or evolved; having no precedent; of recent origin; new.
The state of being new or novel; newness.
The present time.

examples.en: Now is the right time.
examples.zh: 現在正是時候。

meanings.definition: Present; current.

examples.en: I think this band's sound is very now.
examples.zh: 我覺得這個樂團的聲音很現在。

meanings.definition: At the present time.

examples.en: Now I am six.
examples.zh: 現在我六歲了。

meanings.definition: Since, because, in light of the fact; often with that.

examples.en: Now that we're all here, let's start the meeting.
examples.zh: 現在我們都到齊了，讓我們開始會議吧。

meanings.definition: Indicates a signal to begin.

examples.en: Now! Fire all we've got while the enemy is in reach!
examples.zh: 現在！當敵人觸手可及時，全力開火！

meanings.definition: No particular place, noplace.

examples.en: They went on a cruise to nowhere.
examples.zh: 他們乘船航行，不知去向。

meanings.definition: Unimportant; unworthy of notice.
In no place.

examples.en: Nowhere did the rules say anything about popcorn.
examples.zh: 規則中沒有任何地方提到有關爆米花的內容。

meanings.definition: Nuclear power
Pertaining to the nucleus of an atom.

examples.en: a nuclear reactor
examples.zh: 核反應器

meanings.definition: A minor annoyance or inconvenience.

examples.en: a public nuisance
examples.zh: 公害

meanings.definition: A non-existent or empty value or set of values.

examples.en: Since no date of birth was entered for the patient, his age is null.
examples.zh: 由於沒有輸入患者的出生日期，因此他的年齡為空。

meanings.definition: To nullify; to annul.
Having no validity; "null and void"
An abstract entity used to describe quantity.

examples.en: Zero, one, −1, 2.5, and pi are all numbers.
examples.zh: 零、一、-1、2.5 和 pi 都是數字。

meanings.definition: To label (items) with numbers; to assign numbers to (items).

examples.en: Number the baskets so that we can find them easily.
examples.zh: 將籃子編號，以便我們可以輕鬆找到它們。

meanings.definition: Indefinitely large numerically, many.

examples.en: There are numerous definitions of the word 'man'.
examples.zh: 「人」這個字有多種定義。

meanings.definition: A wet nurse.

examples.en: They hired a nurse to care for their young boy.
examples.zh: 他們雇了一名護士來照顧他們的小男孩。

meanings.definition: To breastfeed: to feed (a baby) at the breast; to suckle.

examples.en: She believes that nursing her baby will make him strong and healthy.
examples.zh: 她相信餵養她的孩子會讓他變得強壯和健康。

meanings.definition: A place where nursing or the raising of children is carried on.

examples.en: Commerce is the nursery of seamen.
examples.zh: 商業是海員的搖籃。

meanings.definition: The act of nourishing or nursing; tender care
To nourish or nurse.
The organic process by which an organism assimilates food and uses it for growth and maintenance.
A deciduous tree with distinctive deeply lobed leaves, acorns, and notably strong wood, typically of England and northeastern North America, included in genus Quercus.
To expose to oak in order for the oak to impart its flavors.
Having a rich brown colour, like that of oak wood.

examples.en: an oak table, oak beam, etc
examples.zh: 橡木桌、橡木橫樑等

meanings.definition: To do as ordered by (a person, institution etc), to act according to the bidding of.
A thing that has physical existence.

examples.en: Mary Jane had been the object of Peter's affection for years.
examples.zh: 瑪麗珍多年來一直是彼得喜愛的對象。

meanings.definition: To disagree with or oppose something or someone; (especially in a Court of Law) to raise an objection.

examples.en: I object to the proposal to build a new airport terminal.
examples.zh: 我反對興建新機場航站樓的提議。

meanings.definition: A material object that physically exists.
Of or relating to a material object, actual existence or reality.
The act of binding oneself by a social, legal, or moral tie to someone.

examples.en: X shall be entitled to subcontract its obligation to provide the Support Services. <>
examples.zh: X 有權分包其提供支援服務的義務。 <>

meanings.definition: To constrain someone by force or by social, moral or legal means.

examples.en: I am obliged to report to the police station every week.
examples.zh: 我有義務每週到警察局報到。

meanings.definition: To render obscure; to darken; to make dim; to keep in the dark; to hide; to make less visible, intelligible, legible, glorious, beautiful, or illustrious.
Dark, faint or indistinct.

examples.en: an obscure passage or inscription;    The speaker made obscure references to little-known literary works.
examples.zh: 晦澀的段落或銘文；    演講者含糊地提到了鮮為人知的文學作品。

meanings.definition: The practice of complying with a law, custom, command or rule.
The act of observing, and the fact of being observed (see observance)
To notice or view, especially carefully or with attention to detail.

examples.en: From this vantage point we can observe the behavior of the animals in their natural habitat.
examples.zh: 從這個有利位置我們可以觀察動物在自然棲息地的行為。

meanings.definition: One who makes observations, monitors or takes notice

examples.en: Most impartial observers agreed that Sampras had not served well.
examples.zh: 大多數公正的觀察家都認為桑普拉斯的表現不佳。

meanings.definition: A compulsive or irrational preoccupation.
(of words, equipment, etc.) No longer in use; gone into disuse; disused or neglected (often by preference for something newer, which replaces the subject).

examples.en: It is speculated that, within a few years, the Internet's speedy delivery of news worldwide will make newspapers obsolete.
examples.zh: 據推測，幾年之內，互聯網在全球範圍內快速傳播新聞將使報紙變得過時。

meanings.definition: Something that impedes, stands in the way of, or holds up progress

examples.en: A big obstacle to understanding the manual was that it had been poorly translated from the Japanese.
examples.zh: 理解該手冊的一個很大障礙是它的日文翻譯很差。

meanings.definition: The act of obstructing, or state of being obstructed.
To get hold of; to gain possession of, to procure; to acquire, in any way.
Easily discovered, seen, or understood; self-explanatory.
A favorable opportunity; a convenient or timely chance.

examples.en: At this point, she seized the occasion to make her own observation.
examples.zh: 這時，她抓住了這個機會，表達了自己的看法。

meanings.definition: To cause; to produce; to induce

examples.en: it is seen that the mental changes are occasioned by a change of polarity
examples.zh: 可以看出，精神變化是由極性的變化引起的

meanings.definition: A person who does something only occasionally.
Occurring or appearing irregularly from time to time, but not often.

examples.en: He took an occasional glass of wine.
examples.zh: 他偶爾會喝一杯酒。

meanings.definition: An activity or task with which one occupies oneself; usually specifically the productive activity, service, trade, or craft for which one is regularly paid; a job.
(of time) To take or use.
To happen or take place.

examples.en: The liftoff will occur in exactly twelve seconds.
examples.zh: 升空將在十二秒內完成。

meanings.definition: An actual instance when a situation occurs; an event or happening.
One of the large bodies of water separating the continents.

examples.en: The island is surrounded by ocean
examples.zh: 該島被海洋包圍

meanings.definition: (diminutive) An odd number.

examples.en: So let's see. There are two evens here and three odds.
examples.zh: 那麼讓我們來看看。這裡有兩個偶數和三個奇數。

meanings.definition: Differing from what is usual, ordinary or expected.

examples.en: She slept in, which was very odd.
examples.zh: 她睡懶覺了，這很奇怪。

meanings.definition: (diminutive) An odd number.

examples.en: So let's see. There are two evens here and three odds.
examples.zh: 那麼讓我們來看看。這裡有兩個偶數和三個奇數。

meanings.definition: The act of offending:
To hurt the feelings of; to displease; to make angry; to insult.

examples.en: Your accusations offend me deeply.
examples.zh: 你的指控深深地冒犯了我。

meanings.definition: A proposal that has been made.

examples.en: I decline your offer to contract.
examples.zh: 我拒絕你的合約提議。

meanings.definition: A ceremonial duty or service, particularly:

examples.en: When the office of Secretary of State is vacant, its duties fall upon an official within the department.
examples.zh: 當國務卿職位空缺時，其職責由部門內的一名官員承擔。

meanings.definition: To provide (someone) with an office.
One who has a position of authority in a hierarchical organization, especially in military, police or government organizations.
To supply with officers.
An office holder invested with powers and authorities.

examples.en: David Barnes was the official charged with the running of the sports club.
examples.zh: 大衛·巴恩斯是負責體育俱樂部運作的官員。

meanings.definition: Of or pertaining to an office or public trust.

examples.en: official duties
examples.zh: 公務

meanings.definition: Anything that acts as counterbalance; a compensating equivalent.

examples.en: Today's victory was an offset to yesterday's defeat.
examples.zh: 今天的勝利彌補了昨天的失敗。

meanings.definition: To compensate for, by applying a change in the opposite direction.

examples.en: I'll offset the time difference locally.
examples.zh: 我會在當地抵消時差。

meanings.definition: A person's daughter(s) and/or son(s); a person's children.

examples.en: Artists often treasure their works as their immortal offspring.
examples.zh: 藝術家常常將他們的作品視為不朽的後代。

meanings.definition: Liquid fat.

examples.en: I prefer to paint in oil
examples.zh: 我比較喜歡用油畫來作畫

meanings.definition: To leave out or exclude.
To be ongoing (occurring, happening); to last, proceed or continue.
Something that is going on; a happening.
Continuing, permanent, lasting.
To bring (a system, etc.) online; to promote to an active or running state.
Of a system: connected (generally electrically) to a larger network.

examples.en: I prefer to read online newspapers.
examples.zh: 我更喜歡閱讀線上報紙。

meanings.definition: While online; while in a running or active state, or connected to the Internet.

examples.en: He works online.
examples.zh: 他在網路上工作。

meanings.definition: To keep going; to progress or persevere.
Moving forward.

examples.en: There was an onward rush as the gates opened.
examples.zh: 當大門打開時，人們湧入。

meanings.definition: In a forward direction.
An area of darkness; a place or region with no light.
To make, render (more) opaque.
Neither reflecting nor emitting light.
Not closed

examples.en: an open hand; an open flower
examples.zh: 張開的手；一朵開放的花

meanings.definition: To make something accessible or allow for passage by moving from a shut position.

examples.en: Turn the doorknob to open the door.
examples.zh: 轉動門把打開門。

meanings.definition: An act or instance of making or becoming open.

examples.en: He remembered fondly the Christmas morning opening of presents.
examples.zh: 他深情地記得聖誕節早上拆禮物的情景。

meanings.definition: Pertaining to the start or beginning of a series of events.

examples.en: The opening theme of Beethoven's Fifth Symphony is, perhaps, the most recognizable in all of European art music.
examples.zh: 貝多芬第五號交響曲的開場主題也許是所有歐洲藝術音樂中最知名的。

meanings.definition: A theatrical work, combining drama, music, song and sometimes dance.
To perform a work or labour; to exert power or strength, physical or mechanical; to act.

examples.en: to operate a machine
examples.zh: 操作機器

meanings.definition: The method by which a device performs its function.

examples.en: The Katrina relief operation was considered botched.
examples.zh: 卡崔娜颶風救援行動被認為是拙劣的。

meanings.definition: One who operates.

examples.en: In the sentence "What did Bill say he wants to buy?", "what" is an operator, binding a phonetically empty variable.
examples.zh: 在「Bill 說他想買什麼？」這句話中，「what」是一個運算符，綁定了一個語音空變數。

meanings.definition: A subjective belief, judgment or perspective that a person has formed about a topic, issue, person or thing.

examples.en: Every man is a fool in some man's opinion.
examples.zh: 每個人在某些人看來都是傻瓜。

meanings.definition: To have or express as an opinion.
One who opposes another; one who works or takes a position against someone or something; one who attempts to stop the progress of someone or something.

examples.en: During the crackdown, many opponents of the regime were arrested.
examples.zh: 在鎮壓期間，許多反對政權的人被逮捕。

meanings.definition: Opposing; adverse; antagonistic.
A chance for advancement, progress or profit.

examples.en: The world is full of opportunities and it's up to me to see them and pursue them.
examples.zh: 世界充滿了機會，就看我去發現它們並去追求它們​​。

meanings.definition: To attempt to stop the progression of; to resist or antagonize by physical means, or by arguments, etc.; to contend against.

examples.en: There is still time to oppose this plan.
examples.zh: 現在還有時間反對這個計劃。

meanings.definition: Something opposite or contrary to something else.

examples.en: "Up" is the opposite of "down".
examples.zh: 「上」與「下」相反。

meanings.definition: Located directly across from something else, or from each other.

examples.en: She saw him walking on the opposite side of the road.
examples.zh: 她看到他走在馬路對面。

meanings.definition: In an opposite position.

examples.en: I was on my seat and she stood opposite.
examples.zh: 我坐在座位上，她站在對面。

meanings.definition: Facing, or across from.

examples.en: He lives opposite the pub.
examples.zh: 他住在酒吧對面。

meanings.definition: The action of opposing or of being in conflict.
To keep down by unjust force.

examples.en: The rural poor were oppressed by the land-owners.
examples.zh: 農村的窮人受到地主的壓迫。

meanings.definition: To choose; select.

examples.en: He opted not to go.
examples.zh: 他選擇不去。

meanings.definition: Of, or relating to sight; visual.

examples.en: Strabismus is an optical defect.
examples.zh: 斜視是一種光學缺陷。

meanings.definition: The best of its kind
The best, most favourable or desirable, especially under some restriction.

examples.en: Finding the optimal balance between features and price is a common problem.
examples.zh: 在功能和價格之間尋找最佳平衡是一個常見問題。

meanings.definition: A tendency to expect the best, or at least, a favourable outcome

examples.en: I love her youth, her beauty and above all her optimism that everything will turn out fine.
examples.zh: 我愛她的青春、她的美麗，最重要的是她對一切都會好起來的樂觀態度。

meanings.definition: One of a set of choices that can be made.
To purchase an option on something.

examples.en: The new novel was optioned by the film studio, but they'll probably never decide to make a movie from it.
examples.zh: 電影製片廠選擇了這部新小說，但他們可能永遠不會決定將其拍成電影。

meanings.definition: Something that is not compulsory, especially part of an academic course.
Not compulsory; left to personal choice; elective.

examples.en: On that beach clothing is entirely optional.
examples.zh: 在海灘上，衣服是完全可選的。

meanings.definition: A spoken test or examination, particularly in a language class.

examples.en: We've got our Spanish oral tomorrow.
examples.zh: 明天我們有西班牙語口語。

meanings.definition: Relating to the mouth.

examples.en: oral hygiene
examples.zh: 口腔衛生

meanings.definition: An evergreen tree of the genus Citrus such as Citrus sinensis.
To color orange.
Having the colour of the fruit of an orange tree; yellowred; reddish-yellow.
A circular or elliptical path of one object around another object, particularly in astronomy and space travel.

examples.en: The Moon's orbit around the Earth takes nearly one month to complete.
examples.zh: 月球繞地球運行一周需要近一個月的時間。

meanings.definition: To circle or revolve around another object.

examples.en: The Earth orbits the Sun.
examples.zh: 地球繞太陽運行。

meanings.definition: A garden or an area of land for the cultivation of fruit or nut trees.
A large group of musicians who play together on various instruments, usually including some from strings, woodwind, brass and/or percussion; the instruments played by such a group.
Arrangement, disposition, or sequence.

examples.en: The house is in order; the machinery is out of order.
examples.zh: 房子秩序井然；機器出了故障。

meanings.definition: To set in some sort of order.

examples.en: He ordered me to leave.
examples.zh: 他命令我離開。

meanings.definition: A person with authority; authority, ordinance.
(of a judge) Having regular jurisdiction; now only used in certain phrases.

examples.en: On an ordinary day I wake up at nine o'clock, work for six hours, and then go to the gym.
examples.zh: 平常的一天我九點起床，工作六個小時，然後去健身房。

meanings.definition: Rock or other material that contains valuable or utilitarian materials; primarily a rock containing metals or gems for which it is typically mined and processed.
A larger part of an organism, composed of tissues that perform similar functions.

examples.en: bodily organs
examples.zh: 身體器官

meanings.definition: To supply with an organ or organs; to fit with organs.
An organic compound.
Pertaining to or derived from living organisms.

examples.en: The writing of the script was an organic process.
examples.zh: 劇本的寫作是一個有機的過程。

meanings.definition: The quality of being organized.

examples.en: This painting shows little organization at first glance, but little by little the structure becomes clear.
examples.zh: 這幅畫乍看之下沒有什麼組織，但漸漸地結構就清晰了。

meanings.definition: To arrange in working order.

examples.en: an organized being
examples.zh: 有組織的存在

meanings.definition: Usually preceded by the: a region or a part of the world to the east of a certain place; countries of Asia, the East (especially East Asia).
The part of the horizon where the sun first appears in the morning; the east.
To build or place (something) so as to face eastward.

examples.en: The workers oriented all the signs to face the road.
examples.zh: 工人們將所有標誌牌都面對道路。

meanings.definition: Rising, like the morning sun.
The beginning of something.
An object or other creation (e.g. narrative work) from which all later copies and variations are derived

examples.en: This manuscript is the original.
examples.zh: 此手稿為原稿。

meanings.definition: Relating to the origin or beginning; preceding all others

examples.en: the original state of mankind;  the original laws of a country;  the original inventor of a process
examples.zh: 人類的原始狀態；  一個國家的原有法律；  流程的原始發明人

meanings.definition: An element of decoration; that which embellishes or adorns.
To decorate.

examples.en: We will ornament the windows with trim to make the room seem brighter.
examples.zh: 我們會用飾條裝飾窗戶，讓房間看起來更明亮。

meanings.definition: A person, especially a minor, both or (rarely) one of whose parents have died.
To deprive of parents (used almost exclusively in the passive)

examples.en: What do you do when you come across two orphaned polar bear cubs?
examples.zh: 當你遇到兩隻北極熊孤兒時你會怎麼做？

meanings.definition: Deprived of parents (also orphaned).

examples.en: She is an orphan child.
examples.zh: 她是個孤兒。

meanings.definition: An other, another (person, etc), more often rendered as another.

examples.en: I'm afraid little Robbie does not always play well with others.
examples.zh: 恐怕小羅比總是不能和別人相處得很好。

meanings.definition: To regard, label or treat as an "other", as not part of the same group; to view as different and alien.
See other (determiner) below

examples.en: I get paid every other week.
examples.zh: 我每隔一周收到一次薪水。

meanings.definition: Apart from; in the phrase "other than".

examples.en: Other than that, I'm fine.
examples.zh: 除此之外，我很好。

meanings.definition: Other than supposed; different.

examples.en: He said he didn’t do it, but the evidence was otherwise.
examples.zh: 他說他沒有這麼做，但證據卻並非如此。

meanings.definition: (manner) Differently, in another way.

examples.en: Could I do otherwise than smile?.
examples.zh: 除了微笑我還能做些什麼嗎？

meanings.definition: Property; possession
An avoirdupois ounce, weighing 1/16 of an avoirdupois pound, or 28.3495 grams.

examples.en: He didn't feel even an ounce of regret for his actions.
examples.zh: 對於自己的行為，他沒有半點後悔。

meanings.definition: An eruption; the sudden appearance of a rash, disease, etc.

examples.en: Any epidemic outbreak causes understandable panic.
examples.zh: 任何流行病的爆發都會引起可以理解的恐慌。

meanings.definition: To burst out.
That which is produced or occurs as a result of an event or process.

examples.en: A quality automobile is the outcome of the work of skilled engineers and thousands of workers.
examples.zh: 高品質的汽車是熟練工程師和數千名工人工作的成果。

meanings.definition: (in some African communities) To publicly display a child after it has been named
Situated in, designed to be used in, or carried on in the open air.
An outer part.
Outside; external.
A set of clothing (with accessories).

examples.en: She wore a fashionable outfit with matching purse and shoes.
examples.zh: 她穿著一套時尚的服裝，搭配相配的皮夾和鞋子。

meanings.definition: To provide with, usually for a specific purpose.

examples.en: The expedition was outfitted with proper clothing, food, and other necessities.
examples.zh: 探險隊配備了適當的衣服、食物和其他必需品。

meanings.definition: To go out, to set forth.
The act of leaving or going out; exit, departure.
Extraverted: talkative, friendly, and social, especially with respect to meeting new people easily and comfortably.

examples.en: Tom is very outgoing and enjoys meeting people; his brother, on the other hand, is painfully shy.
examples.zh: 湯姆非常外向，喜歡認識朋友；另一方面，他的兄弟卻非常害羞。

meanings.definition: To eject; to expel.

examples.en: A Brazilian company outed the new mobile phone design.
examples.zh: 一家巴西公司推出了新的手機設計。

meanings.definition: A pleasure trip or excursion.
A vent or similar passage to allow the escape of something.
A line marking the boundary of an object figure.

examples.en: the outline of a speech
examples.zh: 演講大綱

meanings.definition: To draw an outline of.

examples.en: Wikipedia items featuring books usually outline them after giving their background.
examples.zh: 維基百科上的書籍項目通常會在給出背景後概述它們。

meanings.definition: A place from which something can be viewed.

examples.en: Perched on the edge of the cliff was a hidden outlook.
examples.zh: 懸崖邊有一個隱藏的景觀。

meanings.definition: To face or look in an outward direction.
Production; quantity produced, created, or completed.

examples.en: The factory increased its output this year.
examples.zh: 今年工廠增加了產量。

meanings.definition: To produce, create, or complete.

examples.en: We output 1400 units last year.
examples.zh: 去年我們的產量為 1400 台。

meanings.definition: An excessively violent or vicious attack; an atrocity.
To cause or commit an outrage upon; to treat with violence or abuse.
The beginning or initial stage of something.

examples.en: He agreed and understood from the outset, so don't bother explaining again.
examples.zh: 他一開始就答應了，也明白了，所以不用再解釋了。

meanings.definition: The part of something that faces out; the outer surface.

examples.en: He's repainting the outside of his house.
examples.zh: 他正在重新粉刷房子的外面。

meanings.definition: Of or pertaining to the outer surface, limit or boundary.

examples.en: The outside surface looks good.
examples.zh: 外表面看起來不錯。

meanings.definition: To or in the outdoors or outside; to or in an area that is beyond the scope, limits or borders of a given place.

examples.en: I am going outside.
examples.zh: 我要出去。

meanings.definition: On the outside of, not inside (something, such as a building).

examples.en: tourists from outside the country
examples.zh: 來自國外的遊客

meanings.definition: To resist effectually; withstand; sustain without yielding.

examples.en: outstanding contracts
examples.zh: 未完成的合約

meanings.definition: Prominent or noticeable; standing out from others.
Outer; located towards the outside

examples.en: By all outward indications, he's a normal happy child, but if you talk to him, you will soon realize he has some psychological problems.
examples.zh: 從所有外表來看，他是一個正常快樂的孩子，但如果你和他交談，你很快就會意識到他有一些心理問題。

meanings.definition: Towards the outside; away from the centre.

examples.en: We are outward bound.
examples.zh: 我們是外向的。

meanings.definition: An elongated round shape resembling an egg or ellipse.
Having the shape of an oval.

examples.en: oval conceptions
examples.zh: 橢圓形構想

meanings.definition: A chamber used for baking or heating.

A garment worn over other clothing to protect it; a coverall or boiler suit. A garment, for manual labor or for casual wear, often made of a single piece of fabric, with long legs and a bib upper, supported from the shoulders with straps, and having several large pockets and loops for carrying tools.
All-encompassing, all around.
Generally; with everything considered.

examples.en: Overall, there is not enough evidence to form a clear conclusion.
examples.zh: 整體而言，沒有足夠的證據來得出明確的結論。

meanings.definition: The burden or recurring theme in a song.
To surmount (a physical or abstract obstacle); to prevail over, to get the better of.

examples.en: to overcome enemies in battle
examples.zh: 在戰鬥中戰勝敵人

meanings.definition: Late; especially, past a deadline or too late to fulfill a need.

examples.en: his library books were three days overdue;  my car is overdue for an oil change;  she finally left on a long overdue vacation
examples.zh: 他圖書館的書逾期三天了；  我的車逾期更換機油；  她終於去度了一個姍姍來遲的假期

meanings.definition: The expense of a business not directly assigned to goods or services provided.

examples.en: Network overhead is the header data that is required to route and transport data over network, whereas fork overhead is the additional time and memory cost of creating and managing new processes within operating system.
examples.zh: 網路開銷是透過網路路由和傳輸數據所需的標頭數據，而分叉開銷則是在作業系統內創建和管理新進程的額外時間和記憶體成本。

meanings.definition: Located above, especially over the head

examples.en: Place your luggage in the overhead lockers.
examples.zh: 將行李放入頭頂的置物櫃。

meanings.definition: Above one's head; in the sky.

examples.en: birds flying overhead
examples.zh: 鳥兒飛過頭頂

meanings.definition: Something that overlaps or is overlapped
To extend over and partly cover something.

examples.en: The mouse-mat overlapped the edge of the desk.
examples.zh: 滑鼠墊與桌子的邊緣重疊。

meanings.definition: A vista or point that gives a beautiful view.
To offer a view (of something) from a higher position.

examples.en: Our hotel room overlooks the lake.
examples.zh: 我們的飯店房間可以俯瞰湖景。

meanings.definition: Items delivered or completed overnight.

examples.en: Have you looked at the overnights yet?
examples.zh: 你看過過夜了嗎？

meanings.definition: To stay overnight; to spend the night.

examples.en: We can overnight you the documents for signature.
examples.zh: 我們可以在一夜之間為您提供文件以供簽名。

meanings.definition: Occurring between dusk and dawn.

examples.en: The overnight ferry docked at 10AM.
examples.zh: 過夜渡輪於上午 10 點停靠。

meanings.definition: During or throughout the night, especially during the evening or night just past.

examples.en: Let it run overnight and we'll check on it in the morning.
examples.zh: 讓它運行一整夜，我們會在早上檢查它。

meanings.definition: Abroad.

examples.en: Overseas branches of the company are doing well.
examples.zh: 本公司海外分公司經營狀況良好。

meanings.definition: Abroad.

examples.en: He emigrated and now lives overseas.
examples.zh: 他移居國外，現在居住在海外。

meanings.definition: To pass a more slowly moving object or entity.

examples.en: The car was so slow we were overtaken by a bus.
examples.zh: 汽車開得很慢，我們被一輛公共汽車超越了。

meanings.definition: Working time outside of one's regular hours.

examples.en: Workers are usually paid extra for working overtime.
examples.zh: 工人通常會因加班而獲得額外報酬。

meanings.definition: To measure something incorrectly, as taking more time than it actually did.
Exceeding regular working hours.
A brief summary, as of a book or a presentation.
To engage in an overview; to provide a brief summary.
The state or condition of being overwhelmed.
To engulf, surge over and submerge.

examples.en: The dinghy was overwhelmed by the great wave.
examples.zh: 小艇被巨浪淹沒了。

meanings.definition: To be under an obligation to give something back to someone or to perform some action for someone.
Any of various birds of prey of the order Strigiformes that are primarily nocturnal and have forward-looking, binocular vision, limited eye movement, and good hearing.
To smuggle contraband goods.
Belonging to; possessed; proper to. Often marks a possessive determiner as reflexive, referring back to the subject of the clause or sentence.

examples.en: When we move into the new house, the kids will each have their own bedroom.
examples.zh: 當我們搬進新房子時，孩子們將每個人都有自己的臥室。

meanings.definition: One who owns something.

examples.en: The police recovered the stolen car and returned it to its owner.
examples.zh: 警方追回了被盜汽車並將其歸還給車主。

meanings.definition: The state of having complete legal control of something; possession; proprietorship.

examples.en: The successful candidate will take ownership of all internal design projects.
examples.zh: 成功的候選人將擁有所有內部設計項目的所有權。

meanings.definition: The chemical element (symbol O) with an atomic number of 8 and relative atomic mass of 15.9994. It is a colorless and odorless gas.
Step.
To walk back and forth in a small distance.
Describing a bowler who bowls fast balls.
Calm, peaceful.
A bundle made up and prepared to be carried; especially, a bundle to be carried on the back, but also a load for an animal, a bale.

examples.en: The horses carried the packs across the plain.
examples.zh: 馬匹馱著行李穿越平原。

meanings.definition: Something which is packed, a parcel, a box, an envelope.

examples.en: Did you test the software package to ensure completeness?
examples.zh: 您是否測試了軟體包以確保完整性？

meanings.definition: To pack or bundle something.
A small pack or package; a little bundle or parcel

examples.en: Don't throw the crisp packet on the floor!
examples.zh: 不要將薯片包丟在地板上！

meanings.definition: To make up into a packet or bundle.
A flattened mass of anything soft, to sit or lie on.
To stuff.

examples.en: May 21, 2008, Austin American-Statesman
examples.zh: 2008年5月21日，奧斯汀美國政治家

meanings.definition: One of the many pieces of paper bound together within a book or similar document.

examples.en: the page of history
examples.zh: 歷史的一頁

meanings.definition: To mark or number the pages of, as a book or manuscript.

examples.en: The patient paged through magazines while he waited for the doctor.
examples.zh: 病人一邊等待醫生，一邊翻閱雜誌。

meanings.definition: An ache or bodily suffering, or an instance of this; an unpleasant sensation, resulting from a derangement of functions, disease, or injury by violence; hurt.

examples.en: I had to stop running when I started getting pains in my feet.
examples.zh: 當我的腳開始疼痛時，我不得不停止跑步。

meanings.definition: To hurt; to put to bodily uneasiness or anguish; to afflict with uneasy sensations of any degree of intensity; to torment; to torture.

examples.en: The wound pained him.
examples.zh: 傷口讓他很痛。

meanings.definition: A substance that is applied as a liquid or paste, and dries into a solid coating that protects or adds color/colour to an object or surface to which it has been applied.

examples.en: The Nimrods are strong on the outside, but not very good in the paint.
examples.zh: 尼姆羅德隊的外線很強，但在內線卻不太好。

meanings.definition: To apply paint to.

examples.en: to paint a portrait or a landscape
examples.zh: 畫肖像或風景

meanings.definition: Two similar or identical things taken together; often followed by of.

examples.en: I couldn't decide which of the pair of designer shirts I preferred, so I bought the pair.
examples.zh: 我無法決定我更喜歡哪一件名牌襯衫，所以我買了這件。

meanings.definition: To group into one or more sets of two.

examples.en: The wedding guests were paired boy/girl and groom's party/bride's party.
examples.zh: 婚禮嘉賓是配對的男孩/女孩和新郎一方/新娘一方。

meanings.definition: Official residence of a head of state or other dignitary, especially in a monarchical or imperial governmental system.
To decorate or ornate.
Paleness; pallor.
To turn pale; to lose colour.
Light in color.

examples.en: I have pale yellow wallpaper.
examples.zh: 我有淡黃色的壁紙。

meanings.definition: Any of various evergreen trees from the family Palmae or Arecaceae, which are mainly found in the tropics.
A small booklet of printed informational matter, often unbound, having only a paper cover.
A wide, flat receptacle used around the house, especially for cooking.
To wash in a pan (of earth, sand etc. when searching for gold).
A (usually) rectangular section of a surface, or of a covering or of a wall, fence etc.

examples.en: Behind the picture was a panel on the wall.
examples.zh: 照片後面是牆上的一塊面板。

meanings.definition: To fit with panels.
Overpowering fright, often affecting groups of people or animals.
To feel overwhelming fear.
Pertaining to the god Pan.
An unbroken view of an entire surrounding area.
A sheet material used for writing on or printing on (or as a non-waterproof container), usually made by draining cellulose fibres from a suspension in water.

examples.en: a paper of pins, tacks, opium, etc.
examples.zh: 由別針、圖釘、鴉片等製成的紙

meanings.definition: To apply paper to.

examples.en: to paper the hallway walls
examples.zh: 給走廊的牆壁貼紙

meanings.definition: Made of paper.

examples.en: paper bag; paper plane
examples.zh: 紙袋；紙飛機

meanings.definition: An organized procession consisting of a series of consecutive displays, performances, exhibits, etc. displayed by moving down a street past a crowd of spectators.

examples.en: The floats and horses in the parade were impressive, but the marching bands were really amazing.
examples.zh: 遊行中的花車和馬匹令人印象深刻，但遊行樂隊確實令人驚嘆。

meanings.definition: To march in or as if in a procession.

examples.en: They paraded around the field, simply to show their discipline.
examples.zh: 他們在球場上遊行，只是為了展現他們的紀律。

meanings.definition: A pattern, a way of doing something, especially a pattern of thought, a system of beliefs, a conceptual framework.

examples.en: Thomas Kuhn's landmark “The Structure of Scientific Revolutions” got people talking about paradigm shifts, to the point the word itself now suggests an incomplete or biased perspective.
examples.zh: 托馬斯·庫恩的里程碑式的《科學革命的結構》讓人們開始談論範式轉變，以至於這個詞本身現在暗示著一種不完整或有偏見的觀點。

meanings.definition: A passage in text that is about a different subject from the preceding text, marked by commencing on a new line, the first line sometimes being indented.
To sort text into paragraphs.
One of a set of parallel lines.

examples.en: The 31st parallel passes through the center of my town.
examples.zh: 北緯 31 度線穿過我鎮中心。

meanings.definition: To construct or place something parallel to something else.
Equally distant from one another at all points.

examples.en: The horizontal lines on my notebook paper are parallel.
examples.zh: 我的筆記本紙上的水平線是平行的。

meanings.definition: With a parallel relationship.

examples.en: The road runs parallel to the canal.
examples.zh: 這條路與運河平行。

meanings.definition: A value kept constant during an experiment, equation, calculation or similar, but varied over other versions of the experiment, equation, calculation, etc.

examples.en: Roughly, a tuple of arguments could be thought of as a vector, whereas a tuple of parameters could be thought of as a covector (i.e., linear functional). When a function is called, a parameter tuple becomes "bound" to an argument tuple, allowing the function instance itself to be computed to yield a return value. This would be roughly analogous to applying a covector to a vector (by taking their dot product (or, rather, matrix-product of row vector and column vector)) to obtain a scalar.
examples.zh: 粗略地說，參數元組可以被視為向量，而參數元組可以被視為協向量（即線性函數）。當呼叫函數時，參數元組「綁定」到參數元組，從而允許計算函數實例本身以產生返回值。這大致類似於將協向量應用於向量（透過取它們的點積（或更確切地說，行向量和列向量的矩陣積））來獲得標量。

meanings.definition: A package wrapped for shipment.

examples.en: I saw a brown paper parcel on my doorstep.
examples.zh: 我看到我家門口有一個牛皮紙包裹。

meanings.definition: To wrap something up into the form of a package.
Part or half; in part; partially.
Forgiveness for an offence.
To forgive (a person).
Often used when someone does not understand what another person says.
One of the two persons from whom one is immediately biologically descended; a mother or father.
To act as parent, to raise or rear.
An area of land set aside for environment preservation or recreation.

examples.en: A country's tank park or artillery park.
examples.zh: 一個國家的坦克公園或砲兵公園。

meanings.definition: To bring (something such as a vehicle) to a halt or store in a specified place.

examples.en: I parked the drive heads of my hard disk before travelling with my laptop.
examples.zh: 在攜帶筆記型電腦旅行之前，我停放了硬碟的驅動器磁頭。

meanings.definition: A formal council summoned (especially by a monarch) to discuss important issues.

examples.en: Following the general election, Jane Doe took her oath of office as a member of the nation's fifth parliament.
examples.zh: 大選後，簡·多伊宣誓就任美國第五屆國會議員。

meanings.definition: A portion; a component.

examples.en: to do one’s part
examples.zh: 盡自己的一份力量

meanings.definition: To leave the company of.

examples.en: to part the curtains
examples.zh: 拉開窗簾

meanings.definition: Fractional; partial.

examples.en: Fred was part owner of the car.
examples.zh: 弗雷德是這輛車的部分車主。

meanings.definition: Partly; partially; fractionally.

examples.en: Part finished
examples.zh: 部分完成

meanings.definition: A partial derivative: a derivative with respect to one independent variable of a function in multiple variables while holding the other variables constant.
To take the partial regression coefficient.
Existing as a part or portion; incomplete

examples.en: So far, I have only pieced together a partial account of the incident.
examples.zh: 到目前為止，我只拼湊出該事件的部分內容。

meanings.definition: To join in, to take part, to involve oneself (in something).
Acting in common; participating.
A very small piece of matter, a fragment; especially, the smallest possible part of something.
A small individual part of something larger; a detail, a point.
Pertaining only to a part of something; partial.

examples.en: I couldn't find the particular model you asked for, but I hope this one will do.
examples.zh: 我找不到您要求的特定型號，但我希望這個能找到。

meanings.definition: Someone who is associated with another in a common activity or interest.
To join as a partner.
The state of being associated with a partner.
A person or group of people constituting a particular side in a contract or legal action.

examples.en: The contract requires that the party of the first part pay the fee.
examples.zh: 合約要求甲方支付費用。

meanings.definition: To celebrate at a party, to have fun, to enjoy oneself.

examples.en: We partied until the early hours.
examples.zh: 我們一直狂歡到凌晨。

meanings.definition: To change place.
A paragraph or section of text or music with particular meaning.

examples.en: She struggled to play the difficult passages.
examples.zh: 她努力演奏困難的樂段。

meanings.definition: To pass something, such as a pathogen or stem cell, through a host or medium

examples.en: After 24 hours, the culture was passaged to an agar plate.
examples.zh: 24小時後，將培養物傳代至瓊脂平板。

meanings.definition: Describing a bird that has left the nest, is living on its own, but is less than a year old. (commonly used in falconry)

examples.en: Passage red-tailed hawks are preferred by falconers because these younger birds have not yet developed the adult behaviors which would make them more difficult to train.
examples.zh: 紅尾鷹是獵鷹者的首選，因為這些年幼的鳥類尚未發展出成年行為，這使得它們更難訓練。

meanings.definition: One who rides or travels in a vehicle, but who does not operate it and is not a member of the crew.

examples.en: Somebody in a team who does not do their fair share of the work.
examples.zh: 團隊中某個人沒有盡到他們應得的工作份額。

meanings.definition: To ride as a passenger in a vehicle.
Any great, strong, powerful emotion, especially romantic love or extreme hate.

examples.en: We share a passion for books.
examples.zh: 我們對書籍有著共同的熱情。

meanings.definition: To suffer pain or sorrow; to experience a passion; to be extremely agitated.
(grammar) The passive voice of verbs.
Being subjected to an action without producing a reaction.

examples.en: He remained passive during the protest.
examples.zh: 他在抗議期間保持被動。

meanings.definition: An official document normally used for international journeys, which proves the identity and nationality of the person for whom it was issued.

examples.en: You will have to bring your passport to prove who you are.
examples.zh: 您必須攜帶護照來證明您的身分。

meanings.definition: To change place.
The period of time that has already happened, in contrast to the present and the future.

examples.en: a book about a time machine that can transport people back into the past
examples.zh: 一本關於時間機器的書，可以將人們帶回過去

meanings.definition: Having already happened; in the past; finished.

examples.en: past glories
examples.zh: 過去的輝煌

meanings.definition: In a direction that passes.

examples.en: I watched him walk past
examples.zh: 我看著他走過

meanings.definition: Beyond in place, quantity or time.

examples.en: count past twenty
examples.zh: 數到二十

meanings.definition: A soft moist mixture, in particular:
To stick with paste; to cause to adhere by or as if by paste.
Something which amuses, and serves to make time pass agreeably.

examples.en: Chatting is a pleasant pastime.
examples.zh: 聊天是一種愉快的消遣。

meanings.definition: To sport; to amuse oneself
A piece of cloth, or other suitable material, sewed or otherwise fixed upon a garment to repair or strengthen it, especially upon an old garment to cover a hole.

examples.en: His sleeves had patches on the elbows where different fabric had been sewn on to replace material that had worn away.
examples.zh: 他的袖子肘部有補丁，縫上了不同的布料以替換磨損的材料。

meanings.definition: To mend by sewing on a piece or pieces of cloth, leather, or the like

examples.en: My coat needs patching.
examples.zh: 我的外套需要補補。

meanings.definition: A declaration issued by a government agency declaring someone the inventor of a new invention and having the privilege of stopping others from making, using or selling the claimed invention; a letter patent.
To successfully register an invention with a government agency; to secure a letter patent.
A trail for the use of, or worn by, pedestrians.

examples.en: the path of a meteor, of a caravan, or of a storm
examples.zh: 流星、商隊或風暴的路徑

meanings.definition: To make a path in, or on (something), or for (someone).
The quality of being patient.
A person or animal who receives treatment from a doctor or other medically educated person.

examples.en: The subject of a passive verb is usually a patient.
examples.zh: 被動動詞的主詞通常是病人。

meanings.definition: (of a person) Willing to wait if necessary; not losing one's temper while waiting.

examples.en: Be patient: your friends will arrive in a few hours.
examples.zh: 請耐心等待：您的朋友將在幾個小時內到達。

meanings.definition: One who protects or supports; a defender or advocate.

examples.en: This car park is for patrons only.
examples.zh: 此停車場僅供顧客使用。

meanings.definition: To be a patron of; to patronize; to favour.
Model, example.
To apply a pattern.
A button whose functions are pausing and resuming something, such as a DVD player, a video game or a computer.
To take a temporary rest, take a break for a short period after an effort.

examples.en: When telling the scary story, he paused for effect.
examples.zh: 當講這個可怕的故事時，他為了表達效果而停頓了一下。

meanings.definition: To cover something with paving slabs.
An ornate tent.
To furnish with a pavilion.
The soft foot of a mammal or other animal, generally a quadruped, that has claws or nails; comparable to a human hand or foot.

examples.en: Get your grubby paws off my things!
examples.zh: 把你骯髒的爪子從我的東西上拿開！

meanings.definition: (of an animal) To go through something (such as a garbage can) with paws.
Money given in return for work; salary or wages.

examples.en: Many employers have rules designed to keep employees from comparing their pays.
examples.zh: 許多雇主都有旨在阻止員工比較薪資的規則。

meanings.definition: To give money or other compensation to in exchange for goods or services.

examples.en: he paid her off the books and in kind where possible
examples.zh: 他付清了她的帳本費用，並盡可能以實體形式付清她的費用

meanings.definition: Operable or accessible on deposit of coins.

examples.en: pay toilet
examples.zh: 付費廁所

meanings.definition: The act of paying.
A state of tranquility, quiet, and harmony; absence of violence. For instance, a state free from civil disturbance.

examples.en: Our lounge strives to maintain an environment of peace for the comfort of our customers.
examples.zh: 我們的休息室致力於維持一個安靜的環境，讓顧客感到舒適。

meanings.definition: To make peace; to put at peace; to be at peace.
Shut up!, silence!; be quiet, be silent.
A point; the sharp end or top of anything that terminates in a point; as, the peak, or front, of a cap.

examples.en: The stock market reached a peak in September 1929.
examples.zh: 1929 年 9 月，股市達到高峰。

meanings.definition: To reach a highest degree or maximum.

examples.en: Historians argue about when the Roman Empire began to peak and ultimately decay.
examples.zh: 歷史學家爭論羅馬帝國何時開始達到頂峰並最終衰落。

meanings.definition: Maximal, maximally quintessential or representative; constituting the culmination of
That which is peculiar; a sole or exclusive property; a prerogative; a characteristic.
Out of the ordinary; odd; strange; unusual.

examples.en: It would be rather peculiar to see a kangaroo hopping down a city street.
examples.zh: 看到一隻袋鼠在城市街道上跳躍是相當奇怪的。

meanings.definition: A lever operated by one's foot that is used to control or power a machine or mechanism, such as a bicycle or piano

examples.en: A piano usually has two or three pedals.
examples.zh: 鋼琴通常有兩個或三個踏板。

meanings.definition: To operate a pedal attached to a wheel in a continuous circular motion.

examples.en: to pedal one's loom
examples.zh: 踩織機

meanings.definition: Of or relating to the foot.
A walker; one who walks or goes on foot, especially as opposed to one who uses a vehicle.
Of or intended for those who are walking.

examples.en: pedestrian crossing
examples.zh: 行人穿越道

meanings.definition: (usually uncountable) The skin or outer layer of a fruit, vegetable, etc.
To remove the skin or outer covering of.

examples.en: I sat by my sister's bed, peeling oranges for her.
examples.zh: 我坐在姊姊的床邊，為她剝橘子。

meanings.definition: A look; a glance.
To look with difficulty, or as if searching for something.
A legal sentence.

examples.en: The penalty for his crime was to do hard labor.
examples.zh: 對他的罪行的懲罰是做苦役。

meanings.definition: A paintbrush.
To write (something) using a pencil.

examples.en: I penciled (BrE: pencilled) a brief reminder in my notebook.
examples.zh: 我在筆記本上寫下了（BrE：鉛筆）一個簡短的提醒。

meanings.definition: To hang down.
Awaiting a conclusion or a confirmation.
While waiting for something; until.

examples.en: Pending the outcome of the investigation, the police officer is suspended from duty.
examples.zh: 在調查結果出來之前，該名警官已被停職。

meanings.definition: To enter into; to make way into the interior of; to pierce.

examples.en: Light penetrates darkness.
examples.zh: 光穿透黑暗。

meanings.definition: An annuity paid regularly as benefit due to a retired employee, serviceman etc. in consideration of past services, originally and chiefly by a government but also by various private pension schemes.

examples.en: Many old people depend on their pension to pay the bills.
examples.zh: 許多老年人依靠養老金來支付帳單。

meanings.definition: To grant a pension to.
Used as plural of person; a body of human beings considered generally or collectively; a group of two or more persons.

examples.en: There were so many people at the restaurant last night.
examples.zh: 昨晚餐廳裡有很多人。

meanings.definition: To stock with people or inhabitants; to fill as with people; to populate.
For each.

examples.en: $2.50 per dozen
examples.zh: 每打 2.50 美元

meanings.definition: To become aware of, through the physical senses or by thinking; to see; to understand.
A percentage, a proportion (especially per hundred).

examples.en: only a small percent attain the top ranks
examples.zh: 只有一小部分人達到最高排名

meanings.definition: For every hundred (used with preceding numeral to form a noun phrase expressing a proportion).
The organisation, identification and interpretation of sensory information.
(grammar) The perfect tense, or a form in that tense.
Fitting its definition precisely.

examples.en: a perfect circle
examples.zh: 一個完美的圓

meanings.definition: To do something; to execute.

examples.en: It took him only twenty minutes to perform the task.
examples.zh: 他只花了二十分鐘就完成了這項任務。

meanings.definition: The act of performing; carrying into execution or action; execution; achievement; accomplishment; representation by action.

examples.en: the performance of an undertaking or a duty
examples.zh: 履行承諾或職責

meanings.definition: A length of time.

examples.en: There was a period of confusion following the announcement.
examples.zh: 公告發布後，出現了一段混亂時期。

meanings.definition: To come to a period; to conclude.
Designating anything from a given historical era.

examples.en: a period TV commercial
examples.zh: 一段時期的電視廣告

meanings.definition: That's final; that's the end of the matter (analogous to a period ending a sentence); end of story

examples.en: I know you don't want to go to the dentist but your teeth need to be checked, period!
examples.zh: 我知道你不想去看牙醫，但你的牙齒需要檢查，就這樣！

meanings.definition: Relative to a period or periods.
A peripheral device.
On the periphery or boundary.
To decay and disappear; to waste away to nothing.
A chemical hair treatment imparting or removing curliness, whose effects typically last for a period of weeks; a perm.
To perm (the hair).
Without end, eternal.

examples.en: Nothing in this world is truly permanent.
examples.zh: 這世界上沒有什麼是真正永恆的。

meanings.definition: Authorisation; consent (especially formal consent from someone in authority)

examples.en: Sire, do I have your permission to execute this traitor?
examples.zh: 陛下，您允許我處決這個叛徒嗎？

meanings.definition: To grant or obtain authorization for.
An artifact or document rendering something allowed or legal.

examples.en: A construction permit can be obtained from the town offices.
examples.zh: 施工許可證可以從鎮辦公室獲得。

meanings.definition: To allow (something) to happen, to give permission for.

examples.en: The Building Department permitted that project last week.
examples.zh: 建築部上週批准了該項目。

meanings.definition: Lasting forever, or for an indefinitely long time

examples.en: By means of artificial hybridization, practised for a series of years, he has succeeded in producing a race of carnations which are perpetual bloomers.
examples.zh: 透過多年的人工雜交，他成功地培育了一個永遠開花的康乃馨品種。

meanings.definition: To go on stubbornly or resolutely.
An individual; usually a human being.

examples.en: Each person is unique, both mentally and physically.
examples.zh: 每個人都是獨一無二的，無論是精神上或身體上。

meanings.definition: To represent as a person; to personify; to impersonate.
An advertisement by which individuals attempt to meet others with similar interests.
Pertaining to human beings as distinct from things.

examples.en: Her song was her personal look at the values of friendship.
examples.zh: 她的歌曲表達了她對友誼價值觀的個人看法。

meanings.definition: Employees; office staff.
A view, vista or outlook.
Of, in or relating to perspective.

examples.en: a perspective drawing
examples.zh: 透視圖

meanings.definition: To successfully convince (someone) to agree to, accept, or do something, usually through reasoning and verbal influence.

examples.en: That salesman was able to persuade me into buying this bottle of lotion.
examples.zh: 那個推銷員說服我買了這瓶乳液。

meanings.definition: To belong to or be a part of; be an adjunct, attribute, or accessory of
Important with regard to (a subject or matter); relevant
A general belief that bad things will happen.
A plague, pestilence, epidemic
A formal, written request made to an official person or organized body, often containing many signatures.
To make a request to, commonly in written form.

examples.en: The villagers petitioned the council to demolish the dangerous building.
examples.zh: 村民向議會請願拆除危屋。

meanings.definition: Petroleum, a fluid consisting of a mixture of refined petroleum hydrocarbons, primarily consisting of octane, commonly used as a motor fuel.
(countable) A place where prescription drugs are dispensed; a dispensary.
A thing or being, event or process, perceptible through senses; or a fact or occurrence thereof.

examples.en: An electromagnetic phenomenon.
examples.zh: 一種電磁現象。

meanings.definition: The love of wisdom.

examples.en: Philosophy is often divided into five major branches: logic, metaphysics, epistemology, ethics and aesthetics.
examples.zh: 哲學通常分為五個主要分支：邏輯學、形上學、知識論、倫理學和美學。

meanings.definition: To philosophize.
A device for transmitting conversations and other sounds in real time across distances, now often a small portable unit also capable of running software etc.
To call (someone) using a telephone.
A copy made using a photocopier.
To make a copy using a photocopier.
A picture created by projecting an image onto a photosensitive surface such as a chemically treated plate or film, CCD receptor, etc.
To take a photograph of.

examples.en: She photographs well. The camera loves her.
examples.zh: 她拍照很好。相機很喜歡她。

meanings.definition: A short written or spoken expression.
To express (an action, thought or idea) by means of particular words.

examples.en: I wasn't sure how to phrase my condolences without sounding patronising.
examples.zh: 我不知道如何表達我的哀悼但又不顯得居高臨下。

meanings.definition: Physical examination.

examples.en: How long has it been since your last physical?
examples.zh: 距離您上次體檢有多長時間了？

meanings.definition: Of medicine.
A practitioner of physic, i.e. a specialist in internal medicine, especially as opposed to a surgeon; a practitioner who treats with medication rather than with surgery.
A medicine or drug, especially a cathartic or purgative.
To cure or heal.
A tool used for digging; a pickaxe.

examples.en: so many picks to an inch
examples.zh: 一英寸有這麼多選擇

meanings.definition: To grasp and pull with the fingers or fingernails.

examples.en: Don't pick at that scab.
examples.zh: 別摳那個痂。

meanings.definition: An informal social gathering, usually in a natural outdoor setting, to which the participants bring their own food and drink.

examples.en: We went out for a picnic in the forest.
examples.zh: 我們出去在森林裡野餐。

meanings.definition: To take part in a picnic.
A representation of anything (as a person, a landscape, a building) upon canvas, paper, or other surface, by drawing, painting, printing, photography, etc.

examples.en: There was a picture hanging above the fireplace.
examples.zh: 壁爐上方掛著一幅畫。

meanings.definition: To represent in or with a picture.
A part of a larger whole, usually in such a form that it is able to be separated from other parts.

examples.en: I’d like another piece of pie.
examples.zh: 我想要另一塊餡餅。

meanings.definition: (usually with together) To assemble (something real or figurative).

examples.en: These clues allowed us to piece together the solution to the mystery.
examples.zh: 這些線索讓我們能夠拼湊出謎團的答案。

meanings.definition: A raised platform built from the shore out over water, supported on piles; used to secure, or provide access to shipping; a jetty.
A mass of things heaped together; a heap.

examples.en: When we were looking for a new housemate, we put the nice woman on the "maybe" pile, and the annoying guy on the "no" pile
examples.zh: 當我們尋找新室友時，我們把好女人放在「可能」那一類，而煩人的傢伙則放在「不」那一類

meanings.definition: (often used with the preposition "up") To lay or throw into a pile or heap; to heap up; to collect into a mass; to accumulate

examples.en: They were piling up wood on the wheelbarrow.
examples.zh: 他們正在獨輪車上堆木頭。

meanings.definition: A small, usually round or cylindrical object designed for easy swallowing, usually containing some sort of medication.

examples.en: Jane went on the pill when she left for college.
examples.zh: 簡上大學時繼續服用避孕藥。

meanings.definition: Of a woven fabric surface, to form small matted balls of fiber.

examples.en: Pilling is a skill rarely used by modern pharmacists.
examples.zh: 起丸是現代藥劑師很少使用的技能。

meanings.definition: A large post, often used as supporting architecture.

examples.en: a pillar of smoke
examples.zh: 一根煙柱

meanings.definition: To provide with pillars or added strength as if from pillars.
A person who steers a ship, a helmsman.

examples.en: We would like to run a pilot in your facility before rolling out the program citywide.
examples.zh: 在全市範圍內推廣該計劃之前，我們希望在您的工廠進行試點。

meanings.definition: To control (an aircraft or watercraft).
Made or used as a test or demonstration of capability.

examples.en: The pilot plant showed the need for major process changes.
examples.zh: 試點工廠表明需要對製程進行重大改變。

meanings.definition: A needle without an eye (usually) made of drawn-out steel wire with one end sharpened and the other flattened or rounded into a head, used for fastening.

examples.en: Pull the pin out of the grenade before throwing it at the enemy.
examples.zh: 在將手榴彈扔向敵人之前，將其從手榴彈中拔出。

meanings.definition: (often followed by a preposition such as "to" or "on") To fasten or attach (something) with a pin.

examples.en: to pin a window to the Taskbar
examples.zh: 將視窗固定到工作列

meanings.definition: Any coniferous tree of the genus Pinus.

examples.en: The northern slopes were covered mainly in pine.
examples.zh: 北坡主要覆蓋著松樹。

meanings.definition: One who goes before, as into the wilderness, preparing the way for others to follow.

examples.en: Certain politicians can be considered as pioneers of reform.
examples.zh: 某些政治家可以被視為改革的先驅。

meanings.definition: To be the first to do or achieve (something), preparing the way for others to follow.

examples.en: The young doctor pioneered a new life-saving surgical technique.
examples.zh: 這位年輕的醫生開創了一種新的救生手術技術。

meanings.definition: Meanings relating to a wind instrument.
To play (music) on a pipe instrument, such as a bagpipe or a flute.

examples.en: to pipe flowers on to a cupcake
examples.zh: 將花朵用管道裝飾到紙杯蛋糕上

meanings.definition: A hole in the ground.

examples.en: The meadow around the town is full of old pits.
examples.zh: 小鎮周圍的草地上佈滿了古老的坑洞。

meanings.definition: To make pits in; to mark with little hollows.

examples.en: Exposure to acid rain pitted the metal.
examples.zh: 暴露在酸雨中會使金屬出現凹坑。

meanings.definition: A sticky, gummy substance secreted by trees; sap.

examples.en: It is hard to get this pitch off my hand.
examples.zh: 很難把這個球從我手中拿走。

meanings.definition: To cover or smear with pitch.
(physical) An area; somewhere within an area.

examples.en: I'm in a strange place at the moment.
examples.zh: 我現在正處於一個奇怪的地方。

meanings.definition: The act of placing or putting in place; the act of locating or positioning; the state of being placed.

examples.en: It seems to me that the placement of that poster could be better.
examples.zh: 在我看來，那張海報的位置可能會更好。

meanings.definition: Flat, level.

examples.en: Throughout high school she worried that she had a rather plain face.
examples.zh: 整個高中時期，她都擔心自己的臉相當平淡。

meanings.definition: Simply.

examples.en: I plain forgot.
examples.zh: 我完全忘記了。

meanings.definition: A drawing showing technical details of a building, machine, etc., with unwanted details omitted, and often using symbols rather than detailed drawing to represent doors, valves, etc.

examples.en: The plans for many important buildings were once publicly available.
examples.zh: 許多重要建築的計劃都曾經公開過。

meanings.definition: To design (a building, machine, etc.).

examples.en: The architect planned the building for the client.
examples.zh: 建築師為客戶規劃了這棟建築。

meanings.definition: A level or flat surface.
Of a surface: flat or level.
Each of the seven major bodies which move relative to the fixed stars in the night sky—the Moon, Mercury, Venus, the Sun, Mars, Jupiter and Saturn.
To design (a building, machine, etc.).

examples.en: The architect planned the building for the client.
examples.zh: 建築師為客戶規劃了這棟建築。

meanings.definition: Action of the verb to plan.

examples.en: My neighbours were going to build an extension but they didn't get planning.
examples.zh: 我的鄰居打算擴建，但他們沒有進行規劃。

meanings.definition: An organism that is not an animal, especially an organism capable of photosynthesis. Typically a small or herbaceous organism of this kind, rather than a tree.

examples.en: The garden had a couple of trees, and a cluster of colourful plants around the border.
examples.zh: 花園裡有幾棵樹，邊界周圍還有一簇色彩繽紛的植物。

meanings.definition: To place (a seed or plant) in soil or other substrate in order that it may live and grow.

examples.en: That gun's not mine! It was planted there by the real murderer!
examples.zh: 那把槍不是我的！是真兇栽在那裡的！

meanings.definition: A large farm; estate or area of land designated for agricultural growth. Often includes housing for the owner and workers.
A synthetic, solid, hydrocarbon-based polymer, whether thermoplastic or thermosetting.
Capable of being moulded; malleable, flexible, pliant.
A slightly curved but almost flat dish from which food is served or eaten.

examples.en: I filled my plate from the bountiful table.
examples.zh: 我從豐盛的桌子上裝滿了我的盤子。

meanings.definition: To cover the surface material of an object with a thin coat of another material, usually a metal.

examples.en: This ring is plated with a thin layer of gold.
examples.zh: 這枚戒指鍍有一層薄薄的金。

meanings.definition: A raised stage from which speeches are made and on which musical and other performances are made.

examples.en: This new talk show will give a platform to everyday men and women.
examples.zh: 這個新的脫口秀將為日常生活中的男人和女人提供一個平台。

meanings.definition: To furnish with or shape into a platform
The chemical element with atomic number 78 and symbol Pt; a dense, malleable, ductile, highly unreactive, silverish-white transition metal of great value.
To reach platinum level in a game

examples.en: I platinumed in Clash of Clans yesterday.
examples.zh: 我昨天在《部落衝突》中獲得了白金。

meanings.definition: Of a whitish grey colour, like that of the metal.
Activity for amusement only, especially among the young.

examples.en: Children learn through play.
examples.zh: 孩子們透過玩耍來學習。

meanings.definition: To act in a manner such that one has fun; to engage in activities expressly for the purpose of recreation or entertainment.

examples.en: They played long and hard.
examples.zh: 他們打得又長又辛苦。

meanings.definition: One that plays

examples.en: He thought he could become a player, at least at the state level.
examples.zh: 他認為他可以成為一名球員，至少在州一級。

meanings.definition: An appeal, petition, urgent prayer or entreaty.

examples.en: a plea for mercy
examples.zh: 求饒

meanings.definition: To plead; to argue.
A wit; a humorist; a buffoon.
Giving pleasure; pleasing in manner.

examples.en: It wasn't so hot outside, but pleasant enough to have lunch in the garden.
examples.zh: 外面不太熱，但在花園裡吃午餐還是很舒服的。

meanings.definition: To make happy or satisfy; to give pleasure to.

examples.en: Her presentation pleased the executives.
examples.zh: 她的演講使高層很高興。

meanings.definition: A state of being pleased or contented; gratification.

examples.en: He remembered with pleasure his home and family.
examples.zh: 他高興地回憶起他的家和家人。

meanings.definition: To give or afford pleasure to.

examples.en: Johnny pleasured Jackie orally last night.
examples.zh: 約翰尼昨晚口頭上取悅了傑基。

meanings.definition: Pleased to meet you, "It's my pleasure"
A solemn promise to do something.
To make a solemn promise (to do something).
Existing in large number or ample amount.

examples.en: She accumulated a plentiful collection of books.
examples.zh: 她累積了大量的藏書。

meanings.definition: A more-than-adequate amount.

examples.en: We are lucky to live in a land of peace and plenty.
examples.zh: 我們很幸運生活在一片和平富足的土地上。

meanings.definition: Plentiful
More than sufficiently.

examples.en: She was plenty mad at him.
examples.zh: 她對他很生氣。

meanings.definition: More than enough.

examples.en: I think six eggs should be plenty for this recipe.
examples.zh: 我認為六個雞蛋對於這個食譜來說應該足夠了。

meanings.definition: (authorship) The course of a story, comprising a series of incidents which are gradually unfolded, sometimes by unexpected means.

examples.en: The assassination of Lincoln was part of a larger plot.
examples.zh: 暗殺林肯是一個更大陰謀的一部分。

meanings.definition: To conceive (a crime, etc).

examples.en: They had plotted a robbery.
examples.zh: 他們策劃了一次搶劫。

meanings.definition: The notional area of land able to be farmed in a year by a team of 8 oxen pulling a carruca plow, usually reckoned at 120 acres.
To use a plough on to prepare for planting.

examples.en: I've still got to plough that field.
examples.zh: 我還得耕那塊地。

meanings.definition: A pronged connecting device which fits into a mating socket, especially an electrical one.

examples.en: I pushed the plug back into the electrical socket and the lamp began to glow again.
examples.zh: 我將插頭重新插入電源插座，燈又開始發光。

meanings.definition: To stop with a plug; to make tight by stopping a hole.

examples.en: He attempted to plug the leaks with some caulk.
examples.zh: 他試著用一些填縫劑堵住漏洞。

meanings.definition: The pipes, together with the joints, tanks, stopcocks, taps and other fixtures of a water, gas or sewage system in a house or other building.

examples.en: My plumbing was playing up, so I had to see the doctor.
examples.zh: 我的水管出了問題，所以我必須去看醫生。

meanings.definition: The act of plunging or submerging

examples.en: A plunge into the sea
examples.zh: 一頭栽進海裡

meanings.definition: To thrust into liquid, or into any penetrable substance; to immerse.

examples.en: to plunge the body into water
examples.zh: 將身體浸入水中

meanings.definition: A positive quantity.

examples.en: He is a real plus to the team.
examples.zh: 他對球隊來說是一個真正的優勢。

meanings.definition: To add; to subject to addition.
Being positive rather than negative or zero.

examples.en: −2 * −2 = +4 ("minus 2 times minus 2 equals plus four")

meanings.definition: And; sum of the previous one and the following one.

examples.en: A water molecule is made up of two hydrogen atoms plus one of oxygen.
examples.zh: 水分子由兩個氫原子加一個氧原子組成。

meanings.definition: A bag stitched to an item of clothing, used for carrying small items.

examples.en: I paid for it out of my own pocket.
examples.zh: 我自己掏腰包付了錢。

meanings.definition: To put (something) into a pocket.

examples.en: Record executives pocketed most of the young singer's earnings.
examples.zh: 這位年輕歌手的大部分收入都被唱片公司高層收入囊中。

meanings.definition: Of a size suitable for putting into a pocket.

examples.en: a pocket dictionary
examples.zh: 袖珍字典

meanings.definition: A literary piece written in verse.
A person who writes poems.
A discrete division of something.

examples.en: point de Venise; Brussels point
examples.zh: 威尼斯角；布魯塞爾角

meanings.definition: A substance that is harmful or lethal to a living organism.

examples.en: We used a poison to kill the weeds.
examples.zh: 我們用毒藥來殺死雜草。

meanings.definition: To use poison to kill or paralyse (somebody).

examples.en: The assassin poisoned the king.
examples.zh: 刺客毒害了國王。

meanings.definition: Originally, a stick; now specifically, a long and slender piece of metal or (especially) wood, used for various construction or support purposes.
To propel by pushing with poles, to push with a pole.

examples.en: Huck Finn poled that raft southward down the Mississippi because going northward against the current was too much work.
examples.zh: 哈克·芬恩（Huck Finn）撐著木筏沿著密西西比河向南航行，因為逆流向北航行的工作量太大。

meanings.definition: A civil force granted the legal authority for law enforcement and maintaining public order.

examples.en: Call the police!
examples.zh: 打電話叫警察！

meanings.definition: To enforce the law and keep order among (a group).

examples.en: Extra security was hired to police the crowd at the big game.
examples.zh: 大型比賽期間聘請了額外的保安人員來監督人群。

meanings.definition: A principle of behaviour, conduct etc. thought to be desirable or necessary, especially as formally expressed by a government or other authoritative body.

examples.en: The Communist Party has a policy of returning power to the workers.
examples.zh: 共產黨有一項將權力還給工人的政策。

meanings.definition: To regulate by laws; to reduce to order.
A substance used to polish.

examples.en: A good silver polish will remove tarnish easily.
examples.zh: 好的銀拋光劑可以輕鬆去除鏽跡。

meanings.definition: To shine; to make a surface very smooth or shiny by rubbing, cleaning, or grinding.

examples.en: He polished up the chrome until it gleamed.
examples.zh: 他把鍍鉻物擦亮，直到它閃閃發光。

meanings.definition: To polish; to refine; to render polite.
Well-mannered, civilized.

examples.en: It's not polite to use a mobile phone in a restaurant.
examples.zh: 在餐廳使用手機是不禮貌的。

meanings.definition: A political agent or officer.
Concerning or relating to politics, the art and process of governing.

examples.en: Political principles are rarely absolute, as political logic holds an imperfect result by compromise is better than a theoretically perfect abstention from the political process in the opposition.
examples.zh: 政治原則很少是絕對的，因為政治邏輯認為妥協的不完美結果比理論上完美的反對派政治過程棄權好。

meanings.definition: To engage in political activity; politick.
A methodology and activities associated with running a government, an organization, or a movement.

examples.en: He made a career out of politics.
examples.zh: 他以政治為職業。

meanings.definition: A survey of people, usually statistically analyzed to gauge wider public opinion.

examples.en: The student council had a poll to see what people want served in the cafeteria.
examples.zh: 學生會進行了一項民意調查，了解人們希望食堂提供什麼服務。

meanings.definition: To take, record the votes of (an electorate).

examples.en: He polled a hundred votes more than his opponent.
examples.zh: 他比他的對手多了一百票。

meanings.definition: (of kinds of livestock which typically have horns) Bred without horns, and thus hornless.

examples.en: Poll Hereford
examples.zh: 赫里福德民意調查

meanings.definition: To make something harmful, especially by the addition of some unwanted product.
