examples.en: The factory polluted the river when it cleaned its tanks.
examples.zh: 該工廠在清理水箱時污染了河流。

meanings.definition: Polluted; defiled.
The desecration of something holy or sacred; defilement, profanation.
An inland body of standing water, either natural or man-made, that is smaller than a lake.

examples.en: I haven't been back home across the pond in twenty years.
examples.zh: 我已經二十年沒有回過池塘對面的家了。

meanings.definition: To block the flow of water so that it can escape only through evaporation or seepage; to dam.
A small and rather deep collection of (usually) fresh water, as one supplied by a spring, or occurring in the course of a stream; a reservoir for water.

examples.en: the pools of Solomon
examples.zh: 索羅門池

meanings.definition: (of a liquid) To form a pool.
(with "the") Those who have little or no possessions or money, taken as a group.

examples.en: The poor are always with us.
examples.zh: 窮人永遠與我們同在。

meanings.definition: With no or few possessions or money, particularly in relation to contemporaries who do have them.

examples.en: We were so poor that we couldn't afford shoes.
examples.zh: 我們太窮了，買不起鞋子。

meanings.definition: A person who is popular, especially at a school.
Common among the general public; generally accepted.
A place on the coast at which ships can shelter, or dock to load and unload cargo or passengers.

examples.en: Each eight has four ports and four starboards.
examples.zh: 每八艘船都有四個左舷和四個右舷。

meanings.definition: To turn or put to the left or larboard side of a ship; said of the helm.

examples.en: Port your helm!
examples.zh: 掌舵！

meanings.definition: Of or relating to port, the left-hand side of a vessel when facing the bow.

examples.en: on the port side
examples.zh: 在左舷

meanings.definition: A portable building used for temporary purposes, particularly:
Able to be carried or easily moved.

examples.en: a portable pension plan
examples.zh: 便攜式退休金計劃

meanings.definition: A case for carrying papers, drawings, photographs, maps and other flat documents.

examples.en: I would like to introduce you to our portfolio of services.
examples.zh: 我想向您介紹我們的服務組合。

meanings.definition: An allocated amount.
To divide into amounts, as for allocation to specific purposes.
A painting or other picture of a person, especially the head and shoulders.

examples.en: The author painted a good portrait of urban life in New York in his latest book.
examples.zh: 作者在他的最新著作中很好地描繪了紐約的城市生活。

meanings.definition: To portray; to draw.
Representing the actual features of an individual; not ideal.

examples.en: a portrait bust; a portrait statue
examples.zh: 半身像；肖像雕像

meanings.definition: A place or location.

examples.en: Chief of Staff is the second-highest position in the army.
examples.zh: 參謀長是軍隊中第二高的職位。

meanings.definition: To put into place.
A thing capable of being affirmed; something real or actual.
Not negative or neutral.

examples.en: I’m absolutely positive you've spelt that wrong.
examples.zh: 我絕對肯定你拼字錯誤。

meanings.definition: To have; to have ownership of.

examples.en: He does not even possess a working telephone.
examples.zh: 他甚至沒有可用的電話。

meanings.definition: A possible one.

examples.en: Jones is a possible for the new opening in sales.
examples.zh: 瓊斯有可能參與新開幕的銷售。

meanings.definition: (usually not comparable) Able but not certain to happen; neither inevitable nor impossible.

examples.en: It's not just possible, it's probable.
examples.zh: 這不僅是可能，而且很有可能。

meanings.definition: A long dowel or plank protruding from the ground; a fencepost; a lightpost.

examples.en: ram a post into the ground
examples.zh: 將一根柱子撞入地面

meanings.definition: To hang (a notice) in a conspicuous manner for general review.

examples.en: Post no bills.
examples.zh: 不發布任何帳單。

meanings.definition: The charge for posting an item.
A rectangular piece of thick paper or thin cardboard intended to be written on and mailed without an envelope. In the case of a picture postcard one side carries a picture or photograph.
To send a postcard to.

examples.en: to postcard one's friends
examples.zh: 寄明信片

meanings.definition: A picture of a celebrity, an event etc., intended to be attached to a wall.

examples.en: He has posters of his favorite band, sports teams and holiday resorts up.
examples.zh: 他貼著他最喜歡的樂隊、運動隊和度假勝地的海報。

meanings.definition: To decorate with posters.

examples.en: to poster the walls of a bedroom
examples.zh: 在臥室的牆上張貼海報

meanings.definition: To delay or put off an event, appointment etc.
Currently unrealized ability (with the most common adposition being to)

examples.en: Even from a young age it was clear that she had the potential to become a great musician.
examples.zh: 即使從很小的時候起，她就很明顯地有成為偉大音樂家的潛力。

meanings.definition: Existing in possibility, not in actuality.
A heraldic fur formed by a regular tessellation of blue and white T shapes.
Possessing strength.

examples.en: a potent argument
examples.zh: 有力的論據

meanings.definition: Domestic fowl (e.g. chickens, ducks, turkeys, and geese) raised for food (either meat or eggs).
The act of pouring.

examples.en: The bartender's inexpert pour left me with a pint of beer that was half foam.
examples.zh: 調酒師不熟練的倒酒給我留下了一品脫半泡沫的啤酒。

meanings.definition: To cause (liquid, or liquid-like substance) to flow in a stream, either out of a container or into it.

examples.en: pour water from a jug
examples.zh: 從壺裡倒水

meanings.definition: The quality or state of being poor; lack of money

examples.en: poverty of soil
examples.zh: 土壤貧瘠

meanings.definition: Ability to do or undergo something.

examples.en: the mechanical powers
examples.zh: 機械功率

meanings.definition: To provide power for (a mechanical or electronic device).

examples.en: This CD player is powered by batteries.
examples.zh: 此 CD 播放機由電池供電。

meanings.definition: Impressive.
A part of an exam or series of exams in which the candidate has to demonstrate their practical ability
Based on practice or action rather than theory or hypothesis

examples.en: Jack didn't get an engineering degree, but has practical knowledge of metalworking.
examples.zh: 傑克沒有獲得工程學位，但擁有金屬加工的實用知識。

meanings.definition: Repetition of an activity to improve a skill.

examples.en: He will need lots of practice with the lines before he performs them.
examples.zh: 在表演之前，他需要大量練習台詞。

meanings.definition: To repeat (an activity) as a way of improving one's skill in that activity.

examples.en: You should practise playing piano every day.
examples.zh: 你應該每天練習彈鋼琴。

meanings.definition: Commendation; favourable representation in words
To give praise to; to commend, glorify, or worship.

examples.en: Be sure to praise Bobby for his excellent work at school this week.
examples.zh: 一定要讚揚鮑比本週在學校的出色表現。

meanings.definition: To direct words and/or thoughts to God or any higher being, for the sake of adoration, thanks, petition for help, etc.

examples.en: Muslims pray in the direction of Mecca.
examples.zh: 穆斯林朝麥加的方向祈禱。

meanings.definition: Please; used to make a polite request.

examples.en: pray silence for…
examples.zh: 為……祈禱沉默

meanings.definition: A religious discourse.
To give a sermon.

examples.en: A learned local Muslim used to preach in the small mosque every Friday.
examples.zh: 當地一位博學的穆斯林每週五都會在小清真寺傳教。

meanings.definition: Previous caution or care; caution previously employed to prevent misfortune or to secure good

examples.en: his life was saved by precaution
examples.zh: 預防措施挽救了他的生命

meanings.definition: To warn or caution beforehand.
Brief editorial preface (usually to an article or essay)
To go before, go in front of.

examples.en: Cultural genocide precedes physical genocide.
examples.zh: 文化滅絕先於物質滅絕。

meanings.definition: An act in the past which may be used as an example to help decide the outcome of similar instances in the future.
To provide precedents for.
Happening or taking place earlier in time; previous or preceding.
Someone (or something) who is loved; a darling.
Of high value or worth.

examples.en: The crown had many precious gemstones.   This building work needs site access, and tell the city council that I don't care about a few lorry tyre ruts across their precious grass verge.
examples.zh: 王冠上鑲嵌著許多珍貴的寶石。   這項建築工程需要場地訪問權，並告訴市議會，我不在乎他們珍貴的草地邊緣上的一些卡車輪胎車轍。

meanings.definition: Very; an intensifier.

examples.en: There is precious little we can do.
examples.zh: 我們無能為力。

meanings.definition: (NNES or European Union documents) To make or render precise; to specify.
Exact, accurate
The state of being precise or exact; exactness.
Used for exact or precise measurement.
A prediction.
To make a prediction: to forecast, foretell, or estimate a future event on the basis of knowledge and reasoning; to prophesy a future event on the basis of mystical knowledge or power.
A subdominant.
Common or widespread; prevalent.
The beginning or introductory portion that comes before the main text of a document or book.

examples.en: The book included a brief preface by a leading expert in the field.
examples.zh: 該書包括該領域一位領先專家的簡短序言。

meanings.definition: To introduce or make a comment before (the main point).

examples.en: Let me preface this by saying that I don't know him that well.
examples.zh: 首先我要說的是我不太了解他。

meanings.definition: To be in the habit of choosing something rather than something else; to favor; to like better.

examples.en: I prefer tea to coffee.
examples.zh: 與咖啡相比，我更喜歡茶。

meanings.definition: The selection of one thing or person over others (with the main adposition being "for" in relation to the thing or person, but possibly also "of")

examples.en: He has a preference for crisp wines.
examples.zh: 他偏愛清爽的葡萄酒。

meanings.definition: To give preferential treatment to; to give a preference to.
Something placed before another
To determine beforehand; to set in advance.
A pregnant person.
Carrying developing offspring within the body.

examples.en: I went to the doctor and, guess what, I'm pregnant!
examples.zh: 我去看醫生，你猜怎麼著，我懷孕了！

meanings.definition: To have a negative impact on (someone's position, chances etc.).
Having prejudices.
A preparation for a main matter; an introduction.
In preparation for the main matter; initial, introductory, preparatory.

examples.en: These are just the preliminary results.
examples.zh: 這些只是初步結果。

meanings.definition: An introductory or preliminary performance or event.
To introduce something, as a prelude.
An infant born prematurely
Occurring before a state of readiness or maturity has arrived

examples.en: a premature birth
examples.zh: 早產

meanings.definition: (Westminster system) The head of government in parliament and leader of the cabinet.
To perform, display or exhibit for the first time.

examples.en: The composer invited all his friends when they premiered the movie he orchestrated, we got to see it before anyone but the crew.
examples.zh: 作曲家在首映他精心編排的電影時邀請了他所有的朋友，我們在除了工作人員之外的任何人之前看到了它。

meanings.definition: Foremost; first or highest in quality or degree.
Land, and all the built structures on it, especially when considered as a single place.
A prize or award.
Superior in quality; higher in price or value.
The act of preparing or getting ready.
Preparation
To make ready for a specific future purpose; to set up; to assemble or equip.

examples.en: We prepared the spacecraft for takeoff.
examples.zh: 我們準備太空船起飛。

meanings.definition: The act of prescribing a rule, law, etc..

examples.en: "Jurisdiction to prescribe" is a state's authority to make its laws applicable to certain persons or activities. -- Richard G. Alexander, Iran and Libya Sanctions Act of 1996: Congress exceeds its jurisdiction to prescribe law. Washington and Lee Law Review, 1997.
examples.zh: 「規定管轄權」是國家使其法律適用於某些個人或活動的權力。 ——理查德‧亞歷山大 (Richard G. Alexander)，《1996 年伊朗和利比亞制裁法》：國會超越其製定法律的管轄權。華盛頓和李法律評論，1997 年。

meanings.definition: (of a drug, etc.) only available with a physician or nurse practitioner's written prescription

examples.en: Many powerful pain killers are prescription drugs in the U.S.
examples.zh: 在美國，許多強效止痛藥都是處方藥。

meanings.definition: The fact or condition of being present, or of being within sight or call, or at hand.

examples.en: Any painter can benefit from the presence of a live model from which to draw.
examples.zh: 任何畫家都可以從現場模型的存在中受益，並從中進行繪畫。

meanings.definition: To make or become present.
The current moment or period of time.
Relating to now, for the time being; current.

examples.en: The barbaric practice continues to the present day.
examples.zh: 這種野蠻的做法一直延續到今天。

meanings.definition: The act of presenting, or something presented
A sweet spread made of any of a variety of fruits.
To protect; to keep from harm or injury.

examples.en: Every people have the right to preserve its identity and culture.
examples.zh: 每個民族都有權利保護其身分和文化。

meanings.definition: An act in the past which may be used as an example to help decide the outcome of similar instances in the future.
Occupying the first rank or chief place; having the highest authority; presiding.
A device used to apply pressure to an item.

examples.en: a flower press
examples.zh: 花卉壓榨機

meanings.definition: A pressing; a force applied to a surface.

examples.en: Apply pressure to the wound to stop the bleeding.
examples.zh: 對傷口施加壓力以止血。

meanings.definition: To encourage or heavily exert force or influence.

examples.en: Do not let anyone pressure you into buying something you do not want.
examples.zh: 不要讓任何人強迫您購買您不想要的東西。

meanings.definition: The quality of how good the reputation of something or someone is, how favourably something or someone is regarded.

examples.en: Oxford has a university of very high prestige.
examples.zh: 牛津擁有一所享有很高聲譽的大學。

meanings.definition: (of a linguistic form) Regarded as relatively prestigious; often, considered the standard language or language variety, or a part of such a variety.
With infinitive object: to be so presumptuous as (to do something) without proper authority or permission.

examples.en: I wouldn't presume to tell him how to do his job.
examples.zh: 我不會冒昧地告訴他如何做他的工作。

meanings.definition: To claim, to allege, especially when falsely or as a form of deliberate deception.
Not really what it is represented as being; imaginary, feigned.

examples.en: As children we used to go on "spying" missions around the neighbour's house, but it was all pretend.
examples.zh: 小時候，我們經常在鄰居家周圍執行「間諜」任務，但這都是假的。

meanings.definition: A false, contrived, or assumed purpose or reason; a pretense.

examples.en: The reporter called the company on the pretext of trying to resolve a consumer complaint.
examples.zh: 記者以試圖解決消費者投訴為由致電該公司。

meanings.definition: To employ a pretext, which involves using a false or contrived purpose for soliciting the gain of something else.

examples.en: The spy obtained his phone records using possibly-illegal pretexting methods.
examples.zh: 間諜使用可能非法的藉口方法獲取了他的電話記錄。

meanings.definition: A pretty person; a term of address to a pretty person.

examples.en: We'll stop at the knife store and look at the sharp pretties.
examples.zh: 我們會在刀店停下來看看那些鋒利的漂亮東西。

meanings.definition: To make pretty; to beautify
Pleasant to the sight or other senses; attractive, especially of women or children, but less strikingly than something beautiful.
Somewhat, fairly, quite; sometimes also (by meiosis) very.
To be superior in strength, dominance, influence or frequency; to have or gain the advantage over others; to have the upper hand; to outnumber others.

examples.en: Red colour prevails in the Canadian flag.
examples.zh: 加拿大國旗以紅色為主。

meanings.definition: To stop (an outcome); to keep from (doing something).

examples.en: I brush my teeth regularly to prevent them from turning yellow.
examples.zh: 我會定期刷牙以防止牙齒變黃。

meanings.definition: An existing criminal record (short for "previous convictions")

examples.en: It turned out the shoplifter had a lot of previous.
examples.zh: 原來，這個扒手有很多前科。

meanings.definition: Prior; occurring before something else, either in time or order.

examples.en: He is no better than the previous Prime Minister.
examples.zh: 他並不比前首相好多少。

meanings.definition: The cost required to gain possession of something.

examples.en: I paid a high price for my folly.
examples.zh: 我為我的愚蠢付出了高昂的代價。

meanings.definition: To determine the monetary value of (an item); to put a price on.

examples.en: to price eggs
examples.zh: 給雞蛋定價

meanings.definition: The quality or state of being proud; an unreasonable overestimation of one's own superiority in terms of talents, looks, wealth, importance etc., which manifests itself in lofty airs, distance, reserve and often contempt of others.

examples.en: He had pride of ownership in his department.
examples.zh: 他對自己部門的所有權感到自豪。

meanings.definition: To take or experience pride in something; to be proud of it.

examples.en: I pride myself on being a good judge of character.
examples.zh: 我為自己能很好地判斷性格而感到自豪。

meanings.definition: A primary election; a preliminary election to select a political candidate of a political party.

examples.en: most common primaries
examples.zh: 最常見的初選

meanings.definition: To challenge (an incumbent sitting politician) for their political party's endorsement to run for re-election, through running a challenger campaign in a primary election, especially one that is more ideologically extreme.
First or earliest in a group or series.

examples.en: Children attend primary school, and teenagers attend secondary school.
examples.zh: 兒童上小學，青少年上中學。

meanings.definition: The first hour of daylight; the first canonical hour.

examples.en: 3 is a prime.
examples.zh: 3 是質數。

meanings.definition: First in importance, degree, or rank.

examples.en: Our prime concern here is to keep the community safe.
examples.zh: 我們最關心的是確保社區安全。

meanings.definition: The money originally invested or loaned, on which basis interest and returns are calculated.

examples.en: A portion of your mortgage payment goes to reduce the principal, and the rest covers interest.
examples.zh: 您的抵押貸款付款的一部分用於減少本金，其餘部分用於支付利息。

meanings.definition: Primary; most important; first level in importance.

examples.en: Smith is the principal architect of this design.
examples.zh: 史密斯是該設計的主要建築師。

meanings.definition: A fundamental assumption or guiding belief.

examples.en: We need some sort of principles to reason from.
examples.zh: 我們需要某種原則來推理。

meanings.definition: To equip with principles; to establish, or fix, in certain principles; to impress with any tenet or rule of conduct.
Books and other material created by printing presses, considered collectively or as a medium.

examples.en: TV and the internet haven't killed print.
examples.zh: 電視和網路並沒有消滅印刷品。

meanings.definition: To produce one or more copies of a text or image on a surface, especially by machine; often used with out or off: print out, print off.

examples.en: Print the draft double-spaced so we can mark changes between the lines.
examples.zh: 以雙倍行距列印草稿，以便我們可以標記行之間的變更。

meanings.definition: Of, relating to, or writing for printed publications.

examples.en: a print edition of a book
examples.zh: 一本書的印刷版

meanings.definition: A high-ranking member of a monastery, usually lower in rank than an abbot.
Advance; previous; coming before.

examples.en: I had no prior knowledge you were coming.
examples.zh: 我事先並不知道你會來。

meanings.definition: Previously.

examples.en: The doctor had known three months prior.
examples.zh: 醫生三個月前就知道了。

meanings.definition: An item's relative importance.

examples.en: He set his e-mail message's priority to high.
examples.zh: 他將電子郵件的優先順序設定為高。

meanings.definition: A soldier of the lowest rank in the army.

examples.en: If you want to learn ballet, consider taking privates.
examples.zh: 如果你想學習芭蕾舞，可以考慮參加私人芭蕾舞課程。

meanings.definition: Belonging to, concerning, or accessible only to an individual person or a specific group.

examples.en: Her address is private; you can't have it.
examples.zh: 她的地址是私人的；你不能擁有它。

meanings.definition: (ecclesiastical law) An exemption from certain laws granted by the Pope.

examples.en: All first-year professors here must teach four courses a term, yet you're only teaching one! What entitled you to such a privilege?
examples.zh: 這裡所有的一年級教授每學期必須教授四門課程，而你只教一門！是什麼讓您享有這樣的特權？

meanings.definition: To grant some particular right or exemption to; to invest with a peculiar right or immunity; to authorize

examples.en: to privilege representatives from arrest
examples.zh: 使代表免受逮捕

meanings.definition: That which is taken from another; something captured; a thing seized by force, stratagem, or superior power.
Something that is likely.
Likely or most likely to be true.

examples.en: It's probable that it will rain tomorrow.
examples.zh: 明天很可能會下雨。

meanings.definition: A period of time when a person occupies a position only conditionally and may easily be removed for poor performance

examples.en: You'll be on probation for first six months. After that, if you work out, they'll hire you permanently.
examples.zh: 前六個月你將處於試用期。之後，如果你運動了，他們就會永久僱用你。

meanings.definition: Any of various medical instruments used to explore wounds, organs, etc.

examples.en: They launched a probe into the cause of the accident.
examples.zh: 他們對事故原因展開調查。

meanings.definition: To explore, investigate, or question

examples.en: If you probe further, you may discover different reasons.
examples.zh: 如果你進一步探究，你可能會發現不同的原因。

meanings.definition: A difficulty that has to be resolved or dealt with.

examples.en: She's leaving because she faced numerous problems to do with racism.
examples.zh: 她離開是因為她面臨許多與種族主義有關的問題。

meanings.definition: (of a person or an animal) Difficult to train or guide; unruly.
A particular method for performing a task.

examples.en: Ensure that you follow procedure when accessing customers' personal information.
examples.zh: 確保您在存取客戶的個人資訊時遵循程序。

meanings.definition: To move, pass, or go forward or onward; to advance; to carry on

examples.en: To proceed on a journey.
examples.zh: 繼續旅行。

meanings.definition: A series of events which produce a result (the product).

examples.en: This product of last month's quality standards committee is quite good, even though the process was flawed.
examples.zh: 上個月品質標準委員會的這個產品相當不錯，儘管工藝有缺陷。

meanings.definition: To perform a particular process on a thing.

examples.en: We have processed the data using our proven techniques, and have come to the following conclusions.
examples.zh: 我們使用經過驗證的技術處理數據，並得出以下結論。

meanings.definition: To announce or declare.
To acquire or obtain.
To yield, make or manufacture; to generate.

examples.en: to produce a side of a triangle
examples.zh: 生成三角形的一邊

meanings.definition: A commodity offered for sale.

examples.en: That store offers a variety of products.  We've got to sell a lot of product by the end of the month.
examples.zh: 那家商店提供各種產品。  我們必須在月底前賣出很多產品。

meanings.definition: To produce.
Capable of producing something, especially in abundance; fertile.

examples.en: a productive set
examples.zh: 一個富有成效的集合

meanings.definition: A declaration of belief, faith or one's opinion, whether genuine or pretended.

examples.en: Despite his continued professions of innocence, the court eventually sentenced him to five years.
examples.zh: 儘管他繼續聲稱自己無罪，但法院最終判處他五年徒刑。

meanings.definition: The most senior rank for an academic at a university or similar institution, informally also known as "full professor." Abbreviated Prof.
Ability, skill, competence.

examples.en: a test of proficiency in English
examples.zh: 英語能力測試

meanings.definition: The outermost shape, view, or edge of an object.

examples.en: His fingers traced the profile of the handle.
examples.zh: 他的手指描繪著手柄的輪廓。

meanings.definition: To create a summary or collection of information about (a person, etc.).
Total income or cash flow minus expenditures. The money or other benefit a non-governmental organization or individual receives in exchange for products and services sold at an advertised price.

examples.en: Reading such an enlightening book on the subject was of much profit to his studies.
examples.zh: 閱讀這樣一本關於該主題的啟發性書籍對他的學習大有裨益。

meanings.definition: To benefit (somebody), be of use to (somebody).
The deep; the sea; the ocean.
To cause to sink deeply; to cause to dive or penetrate far down.
Descending far below the surface; opening or reaching to great depth; deep.

examples.en: a profound investigation
examples.zh: 深刻的調查

meanings.definition: A set of structured activities.

examples.en: Our program for today’s exercise class includes swimming and jogging.
examples.zh: 我們今天的運動課程包括游泳和慢跑。

meanings.definition: To enter a program or other instructions into (a computer or other electronic device) to instruct it to do a particular task.

examples.en: He programmed the DVR to record his favorite show.
examples.zh: 他對 DVR 進行了編程來錄製他最喜歡的節目。

meanings.definition: Movement or advancement through a series of events, or points in time; development through time.

examples.en: Testing for the new antidote is currently in progress.
examples.zh: 新解毒劑的測試目前正在進行中。

meanings.definition: To forbid, disallow, or proscribe officially; to make illegal or illicit.

examples.en: The restaurant prohibits smoking on the patio.
examples.zh: 餐廳禁止在露台上吸煙。

meanings.definition: A planned endeavor, usually with a specific goal and accomplished in several steps or stages.

examples.en: Projects like Pruitt-Igoe were considered irreparably dangerous and demolished.
examples.zh: 像 Pruitt-Igoe 這樣的項目被認為具有不可挽回的危險而被拆除。

meanings.definition: To extend beyond a surface.

examples.en: The CEO is projecting the completion of the acquisition by April 2007.
examples.zh: 執行長預計收購將於 2007 年 4 月完成。

meanings.definition: Fertile; producing offspring or fruit in abundance — applied to plants producing fruit, animals producing young, etc.
To extend in space or length.

examples.en: The government shouldn't prolong deciding on this issue any further.
examples.zh: 政府不應再拖延對此問題的決定。

meanings.definition: Standing out, or projecting; jutting; protuberant
An oath or affirmation; a vow

examples.en: if I make a promise, I always stick to it;  he broke his promise
examples.zh: 如果我做出了承諾，我就一定會遵守；  他違背了他的諾言

meanings.definition: To commit to (some action or outcome), or to assure (a person) of such commitment; to make an oath or vow.

examples.en: He promised to never return to this town again.
examples.zh: 他發誓再也不會回到這個小鎮。

meanings.definition: To raise (someone) to a more important, responsible, or remunerative job or rank.

examples.en: He promoted his clerk to office manager.
examples.zh: 他把他的職員提升為辦公室經理。

meanings.definition: A reminder or cue.

examples.en: I filled in my name where the prompt appeared on the computer screen but my account wasn't recognized.
examples.zh: 我在電腦螢幕上出現提示時填寫了我的名字，但我的帳戶未被識別。

meanings.definition: To lead (someone) toward what they should say or do.

examples.en: I prompted him to get a new job.
examples.zh: 我促使他去找新工作。

meanings.definition: Quick; acting without delay.

examples.en: He was very prompt at getting a new job.
examples.zh: 他很快就找到了一份新工作。

meanings.definition: Lying face downward.

examples.en: prone position
examples.zh: 伏地

meanings.definition: To declare formally, officially or ceremoniously.

examples.en: I hereby pronounce you man and wife.
examples.zh: 我在此宣布你們結為夫妻。

meanings.definition: An effort, process, or operation designed to establish or discover a fact or truth; an act of testing; a test; a trial.
To proofread.
Used in proving or testing.

examples.en: a proof load; a proof charge
examples.zh: 驗證負載；證明費用

meanings.definition: Something set apart for a special use.
(heading) Suitable.
Properly; thoroughly; completely.
Something that is owned.

examples.en: Important types of property include real property (land), personal property (other physical possessions), and intellectual property (rights over artistic creations, inventions, etc.).
examples.zh: 重要的財產類型包括不動產（土地）、動產（其他實體財產）和智慧財產權（藝術創作、發明等的權利）。

meanings.definition: To invest with properties, or qualities.
A quantity of something that is part of the whole amount or number.

examples.en: the proportion of the parts of a building, or of the body
examples.zh: 建築物或身體各部位的比例

meanings.definition: To divide into proper shares; to apportion.
Something which is proposed, or offered for consideration or acceptance
An objective or aim.
To suggest a plan, course of action, etc.

examples.en: I propose going to see a film.
examples.zh: 我建議去看電影。

meanings.definition: The act of offering (an idea) for consideration.

examples.en: “'Wiktionary is a good dictionary' is a proposition” is a proposition.
examples.zh: 「『維基字典是好字典』是一個命題」是一個命題。

meanings.definition: To make a suggestion of sexual intercourse to (someone with whom one is not sexually involved).
An owner.

examples.en: From 10 September 1621 till 12 June 1632, Sir William Alexander, styled Earl of Stirling and Viscount of Canada, was proprietor of the Scottish colony Nova Scotia.
examples.zh: 從1621年9月10日到1632年6月12日，威廉·亞歷山大爵士（斯特林伯爵和加拿大子爵）是蘇格蘭殖民地新斯科細亞省的所有者。

meanings.definition: The region which the eye overlooks at one time; view; scene; outlook.
To search, as for gold.
The condition of being prosperous, of having good fortune
To keep safe; to defend; to guard; to prevent harm coming to.

examples.en: Condoms are designed to protect against sexually-transmitted diseases.
examples.zh: 避孕套旨在預防性傳染病。

meanings.definition: Any of numerous large, complex naturally-produced molecules composed of one or more long chains of amino acids, in which the amino acid groups are held together by peptide bonds.
A formal objection, especially one by a group.

examples.en: They lodged a protest with the authorities.
examples.zh: 他們向當局提出抗議。

meanings.definition: To make a strong objection.

examples.en: How dare you, I protest!
examples.zh: 你怎麼敢，我抗議！

meanings.definition: The minutes, or official record, of a negotiation or transaction; especially a document drawn up officially which forms the legal basis for subsequent agreements based on it.
To make a protocol of.
Feeling honoured (by something); feeling happy or satisfied about an event or fact; gratified.

examples.en: I am proud of Sivu’s schoolwork.
examples.zh: 我為 Sivu 的功課感到自豪。

meanings.definition: To proofread.
The process of dough proofing.
To make a living; earn money for necessities.

examples.en: It is difficult to provide for my family working on minimum wage.
examples.zh: 我靠最低工資工作很難養家。

meanings.definition: A region of the earth or of a continent; a district or country.
An item of goods or supplies, especially food, obtained for future use.

examples.en: We increased our provision for bad debts on credit sales going into the recession.
examples.zh: 我們在經濟衰退期間增加了信貸銷售壞帳準備金。

meanings.definition: To supply with provisions.

examples.en: to provision an army
examples.zh: 提供軍隊

meanings.definition: To cause someone to become annoyed or angry.

examples.en: Don't provoke the dog; it may try to bite you.
examples.zh: 不要激怒狗；它可能會試圖咬你。

meanings.definition: Closeness; the state of being near as in space, time, or relationship.

examples.en: The proximity of the heat source allowed it to be detected by the sensor.
examples.zh: 熱源的接近使得感測器能夠檢測到它。

meanings.definition: An agent or substitute authorized to act for another person.
To serve as a proxy for.
Used as a proxy or acting as a proxy.

examples.en: a proxy indicator
examples.zh: 代理指標

meanings.definition: Sagacious in adapting means to ends; circumspect in action, or in determining any line of conduct; careful, discreet, sensible; — opposed to rash; directed by prudence or wise forethought; evincing prudence

examples.en: His prudent career moves reliably brought him to the top
examples.zh: 他謹慎的職業生涯可靠地將他帶到了頂峰

meanings.definition: The study of the human mind.
The people in general, regardless of membership of any particular group.

examples.en: Members of the public may not proceed beyond this point.
examples.zh: 公眾不得超越這一點。

meanings.definition: Able to be seen or known by everyone; open to general view, happening without concealment.
The act of publishing printed or other matter.
Advertising or other activity designed to rouse public interest in something.
To issue (something, such as printed work) for distribution and/or sale.

examples.en: Most of the sketches Faulkner published in 1925 appeared in the Sunday magazine section of the New Orleans Times-Picayune.
examples.zh: 福克納 1925 年發表的大部分草圖都出現在《新奧爾良時報-皮卡尤恩》的星期日雜誌版塊上。

meanings.definition: An act of pulling (applying force)

examples.en: He gave the hair a sharp pull and it came out.
examples.zh: 他猛地一拉頭髮，頭髮就掉了出來。

meanings.definition: To apply a force to (an object) so that it comes toward the person or thing applying the force.

examples.en: When I give the signal, pull the rope.
examples.zh: 當我發出信號時，拉動繩子。

meanings.definition: Command used by a target shooter to request that the target be released/launched.
A normally regular beat felt when arteries are depressed, caused by the pumping action of the heart.
A device for moving or compressing a liquid or gas.

examples.en: This pump can deliver 100 gallons of water per minute.
examples.zh: 此泵浦每分鐘可輸送 100 加侖的水。

meanings.definition: To use a pump to move (liquid or gas).

examples.en: I've pumped over 1000 gallons of water in the last ten minutes.
examples.zh: 在過去 10 分鐘內我已抽出超過 1000 加侖的水。

meanings.definition: A hit or strike with one's fist.
To strike with one's fist.

examples.en: If she punches me, I'm gonna break her nose.
examples.zh: 如果她打我，我就打斷她的鼻子。

meanings.definition: Prompt; on time.
The acquisition of title to, or property in, anything for a price; buying for money or its equivalent.

examples.en: They offer a free hamburger with the purchase of a drink.
examples.zh: 購買飲料即可免費獲得一個漢堡。

meanings.definition: To buy, obtain by payment of a price in money or its equivalent.

examples.en: to purchase land, to purchase a house
examples.zh: 購買土地、購買房屋

meanings.definition: One who, or that which, is pure.
To hit (the ball) completely cleanly and accurately

examples.en: Tiger Woods pured his first drive straight down the middle of the fairway.
examples.zh: 泰格伍茲第一次開球直接擊中球道中部。

meanings.definition: Free of flaws or imperfections; unsullied.

examples.en: That idea is pure madness!
examples.zh: 這個想法純粹是瘋狂的！

meanings.definition: To a great extent or degree; extremely; exceedingly.

examples.en: You’re pure busy.
examples.zh: 你很忙。

meanings.definition: To cleanse, or rid of impurities.
An objective to be reached; a target; an aim; a goal.

examples.en: The purpose of turning off the lights overnight is to save energy.
examples.zh: 晚上關燈的目的是為了節省能源。

meanings.definition: To follow urgently, originally with intent to capture or harm; to chase.

examples.en: Her rival pursued a quite different course.
examples.zh: 她的競爭對手採取了完全不同的做法。

meanings.definition: A short, directed application of force; an act of pushing.

examples.en: Give the door a hard push if it sticks.
examples.zh: 如果門卡住了，用力推一下。

meanings.definition: To apply a force to (an object) such that it moves away from the person or thing applying the force.

examples.en: In his anger he pushed me against the wall and threatened me.
examples.zh: 他一怒之下把我推到牆上並威脅我。

meanings.definition: A right to sell something at a predetermined price.

examples.en: He bought a January '08 put for Procter and Gamble at 80 to hedge his bet.
examples.zh: 他以 80 美元的價格買入了寶潔公司 08 年 1 月的看跌期權來對沖他的賭注。

meanings.definition: To place something somewhere.

examples.en: She put her books on the table.
examples.zh: 她把書放在桌上。

meanings.definition: Anything that is difficult to understand or make sense of.

examples.en: Where he went after he left the house is a puzzle.
examples.zh: 他離開家後去了哪裡是一個謎。

meanings.definition: To perplex (someone).

examples.en: We puzzled over the curious-shaped lock, but were unable to discover how the key should be inserted.
examples.zh: 我們對形狀奇特的鎖感到困惑，但不知道鑰匙應該如何插入。

meanings.definition: Of a person: cunning, crafty.
An instance of throwing and catching each prop at least twice.
To describe or characterize something by listing its qualities.

examples.en: to qualify seven balls you need at least fourteen catches
examples.zh: 要獲得七個球的資格，您需要至少十四次接球

meanings.definition: Level of excellence.

examples.en: Quality of life is usually determined by health, education, and income.
examples.zh: 生活品質通常由健康、教育和收入決定。

meanings.definition: Being of good worth, well made, fit for purpose.

examples.en: A quality system ensures products meet customer requirements.
examples.zh: 品質系統確保產品滿足客戶的要求。

meanings.definition: A fundamental, generic term used when referring to the measurement (count, amount) of a scalar, vector, number of items or to some other way of denominating the value of a collection or group of items.

examples.en: You have to choose between quantity and quality.
examples.zh: 你必須在數量和品質之間做出選擇。

meanings.definition: A verbal dispute or heated argument.

examples.en: We got into a silly quarrel about what food to order.
examples.zh: 我們為了點什麼食物發生了一場愚蠢的爭吵。

meanings.definition: To disagree.

examples.en: to quarrel with one's lot
examples.zh: 與自己的命運發生爭執

meanings.definition: A fourth part of something.
To divide into quarters; to divide by four.

examples.en: Quarter the horses in the third stable.
examples.zh: 將第三個馬厩中的馬匹分成四份。

meanings.definition: Pertaining to an aspect of a quarter.

examples.en: a quarter hour; a quarter century; a quarter note; a quarter pound
examples.zh: 一刻鐘；四分之一個世紀；四分音符；四分之一磅

meanings.definition: A periodical publication that appears four times per year.
Occurring once every quarter year (three months).

examples.en: quarterly rent payments
examples.zh: 按季度支付租金

meanings.definition: Once every quarter year (three months).
A female monarch. Example: Queen Victoria.
To make a queen.
A question, an inquiry (US), an enquiry (UK).

examples.en: The teacher answered the student’s query concerning biosynthesis.
examples.zh: 老師解答了學生關於生物合成的疑問。

meanings.definition: To ask a question.
A journey or effort in pursuit of a goal (often lengthy, ambitious, or fervent); a mission.

examples.en: to rove in quest of game, of a lost child, of property, etc.
examples.zh: 四處遊蕩尋找遊戲、迷失的孩子、財產等。

meanings.definition: To seek or pursue a goal; to undertake a mission or job.
A sentence, phrase or word which asks for information, reply or response; an interrogative.

examples.en: What is your question?
examples.zh: 你的問題是什麼？

meanings.definition: To ask questions about; to interrogate; to enquire for information.
An animal's tail.
To put oneself or itself at the end of a waiting line.
Raw or sensitive flesh, especially that underneath finger and toe nails.
To amalgamate surfaces prior to gilding or silvering by dipping them into a solution of mercury in nitric acid.
Moving with speed, rapidity or swiftness, or capable of doing so; rapid; fast.

examples.en: He's a quick runner.
examples.zh: 他跑得很快。

meanings.definition: Quickly, in a quick manner.

examples.en: Come here, quick!
examples.zh: 過來，快點！

meanings.definition: The absence of sound; quietness.

examples.en: There was a strange quiet in the normally very lively plaza.
examples.zh: 原本熱鬧非凡的廣場，出現了一絲詭異的安靜。

meanings.definition: To become quiet, silent, still, tranquil, calm.

examples.en: When you quiet, we can start talking.
examples.zh: 當你安靜下來時，我們就可以開始說話了。

meanings.definition: With little or no sound; free of disturbing noise.

examples.en: I can't hear the music; it is too quiet.
examples.zh: 我聽不到音樂；太安靜了。

meanings.definition: Be quiet.

examples.en: Quiet! The children are sleeping.
examples.zh: 安靜的！孩子們正在睡覺。

meanings.definition: To pay (a debt, fine etc.).

examples.en: After having to work overtime without being paid, I quit my job.
examples.zh: 由於加班沒有工資，我辭掉了工作。

meanings.definition: (usually followed by of) Released from obligation, penalty, etc; free, clear, or rid.
(heading) To the greatest extent or degree; completely, entirely.
Indicates agreement; "exactly so".
A proportional part or share; the share or proportion assigned to each in a division.

A fragment of a human expression that is repeated by somebody else. Most often a quotation is taken from literature or speech, but also scenes from a movie, elements of a painting, a passage of music, etc., may be quoted.

examples.en: "Where they burn books, they will also burn people" is a famous quotation from Heinrich Heine.
examples.zh: 「他們燒書的地方，他們也會燒人」是海因里希·海涅的一句名言。

meanings.definition: A quotation; a statement attributed to a person.

examples.en: After going over the hefty quotes, the board decided it was cheaper to have the project executed by its own staff.
examples.zh: 在仔細考慮了高額報價後，董事會決定由自己的員工執行專案會更便宜。

meanings.definition: To repeat the exact words of (a person).

examples.en: The writer quoted the president.
examples.zh: 作者引用了總統的話。

meanings.definition: A contest between people, animals, vehicles, etc. where the goal is to be the first to reach some objective. Example: Several horses run in a horse race, and the first one to reach the finishing post wins

examples.en: The race around the park was won by Johnny, who ran faster than the others.
examples.zh: 約翰尼贏得了圍繞公園的比賽，他跑得比其他人都快。

meanings.definition: To take part in a race (in the sense of a contest).

examples.en: The drivers were racing around the track.
examples.zh: 車手們在賽道上競速。

meanings.definition: A skill possessed by all characters of a certain race.
Of or relating to a race (or a people).
A series of one or more shelves, stacked one above the other

examples.en: I bought a rack of lamb at the butcher's yesterday.
examples.zh: 我昨天在肉店買了一塊羊排。

meanings.definition: To place in or hang on a rack.

examples.en: Post-and-lintel construction racks easily.
examples.zh: 柱門楣施工架輕鬆安裝。

meanings.definition: A point source from which radiation is emitted.
Radiating light and/or heat.

examples.en: the radiant sun
examples.zh: 燦爛的太陽

meanings.definition: (historical: 19th-century Britain) A member of the most progressive wing of the Liberal Party; someone favouring social reform (but generally stopping short of socialism).
Favoring fundamental change, or change at the root cause of a matter.

examples.en: His beliefs are radical.
examples.zh: 他的信念是激進的。

meanings.definition: The technology that allows for the transmission of sound or other signals by modulation of electromagnetic waves.
To use two-way radio to transmit (a message) (to another radio or other radio operator).

examples.en: I think the boat is sinking; we'd better radio for help. / I radioed him already. / Radio the coordinates this time. / OK. I radioed them the coordinates.
examples.zh: 我認為船正在下沉；我們最好透過無線電尋求幫助。 /我已經用無線電聯絡他了。 / 這次用無線電發送座標。 / 好的。我用無線電通知了他們座標。

meanings.definition: The long bone in the forearm, on the side of the thumb.
A drawing, often held as a fundraiser, in which tickets or chances are sold to win a prize.

examples.en: He entered a raffle to win a lifetime supply of toothpaste, but he did not win.
examples.zh: 他參加了一次抽獎活動，以贏得終生可用的牙膏，但他沒有中獎。

meanings.definition: To award something by means of a raffle or random drawing, often used with off.

examples.en: They raffled off four gift baskets.
examples.zh: 他們抽獎了四個禮物籃。

meanings.definition: Violent uncontrolled anger.

examples.en: Miniskirts were all the rage back then.
examples.zh: 那時迷你裙風靡一時。

meanings.definition: To act or speak in heightened anger.
A horizontal bar extending between supports and used for support or as a barrier; a railing.

examples.en: We travelled to the seaside by rail.
examples.zh: 我們搭火車去海邊。

meanings.definition: To travel by railway.
A transport system using rails used to move passengers or goods.
Condensed water falling from a cloud.

examples.en: The rains came late that year.
examples.zh: 那年的雨來得很晚。

meanings.definition: To have rain fall from the sky.

examples.en: It will rain today.
examples.zh: 今天會下雨。

meanings.definition: An increase in wages or salary; a rise (UK).

examples.en: The boss gave me a raise.
examples.zh: 老闆給我加薪了。

meanings.definition: (physical) To cause to rise; to lift or elevate.

examples.en: to raise your hand if you want to say something; to raise your walking stick to defend yourself
examples.zh: 如果你想說些什麼，請舉手；舉起你的手杖來保護自己

meanings.definition: A public gathering or mass meeting that is not mainly a protest and is organized to inspire enthusiasm for a cause.

examples.en: a campaign rally
examples.zh: 競選集會

meanings.definition: To collect, and reduce to order, as troops dispersed or thrown into confusion; to gather again; to reunite.
A line or series of mountains, buildings, etc.

examples.en: We sell a wide range of cars.
examples.zh: 我們銷售各種汽車。

meanings.definition: To travel over (an area, etc); to roam, wander.

examples.en: to range the fields
examples.zh: 確定字段範圍

meanings.definition: Strong of its kind or in character; unmitigated; virulent; thorough; utter (used of negative things).

examples.en: rank nonsense
examples.zh: 胡說八道

meanings.definition: Quickly, eagerly, impetuously.
(often in the plural) a rough section of a river or stream which is difficult to navigate due to the swift and turbulent motion of the water.
Very swift or quick.

examples.en: a rapid stream
examples.zh: 急流

meanings.definition: (archaic or colloquial) Rapidly.
A scarce or uncommon item.
Very uncommon; scarce.

examples.en: Black pearls are very rare and therefore very valuable.
examples.zh: 黑珍珠非常稀有，因此非常有價值。

meanings.definition: Acting too quickly without considering the risks and consequences; not careful; hasty.

examples.en: rash words spoken in the heat of debate
examples.zh: 在激烈的辯論中說出輕率的話

meanings.definition: The worth of something; value.

examples.en: At the height of his powers, he was producing pictures at the rate of four a year.
examples.zh: 在他的巔峰時期，他以每年四張的速度製作影片。

meanings.definition: To assign or be assigned a particular rank or level.

examples.en: She is rated fourth in the country.
examples.zh: 她在全國排名第四。

meanings.definition: To give formal consent to; make officially valid, sign off on.
A number representing a comparison between two named things.
(social media) To respond to a post or message on social media in a greater number than the number of likes the post receives.

examples.en: The schoolchildren ganged up to ratio the school catering over its shamefully undemocratic inclusion of vegetables in meals.
examples.zh: 學生們聯合起來，對學校的膳食與在膳食中添加蔬菜的可恥的不民主行為進行了比較。

meanings.definition: A rational number: a number that can be expressed as the quotient of two integers.

examples.en: The quotient of two rationals is again a rational.
examples.zh: 兩個有理數的商數又是一個有理數。

meanings.definition: Capable of reasoning.

examples.en: Man is a rational creature.
examples.zh: 人是一種理性的生物。

meanings.definition: (sugar refining, sugar trade) An unprocessed sugar; a batch of such.
(of food) Not cooked.

examples.en: raw cane sugar
examples.zh: 原蔗糖

meanings.definition: Without a condom.

examples.en: We did it raw.
examples.zh: 我們是生吃的。

meanings.definition: The act of stretching or extending; extension.

examples.en: The fruit is beyond my reach.
examples.zh: 果實是我搆不到的。

meanings.definition: To extend, stretch, or thrust out (for example a limb or object held in the hand).

examples.en: He reached for a weapon that was on the table.
examples.zh: 他伸手去拿桌上的一件武器。

meanings.definition: An emoji used to express a reaction to a post on social media.

examples.en: Sad reacts only
examples.zh: 只有悲傷的反應

meanings.definition: To act or perform a second time; to do over again; to reenact.

examples.zh: 每個身體都會對身體產生反應，迫使脫離自然狀態。

meanings.definition: A reading or an act of reading, especially an actor's part of a play.

examples.en: His thrillers are always a gripping read.
examples.zh: 他的驚悚小說讀起來總是扣人心弦。

synonyms: read aloud, read out, read out loud, speak

examples.en: Have you read this book?
examples.zh: 你讀過這本書嗎？

meanings.definition: Ready money; cash
To prepare; to make ready for action.
Prepared for immediate action or use.

examples.en: The porridge is ready to serve.
examples.zh: 粥就可以上桌了。

meanings.definition: A commodity; see realty.
True, genuine, not merely nominal or apparent.

examples.en: This is real leather.
examples.zh: 這是真皮。

meanings.definition: Really, very.

examples.en: When I told him the truth, he got real mad.
examples.zh: 當我告訴他真相時，他真的很生氣。

meanings.definition: Expressed or represented as being accurate, practicable, or not idealistic.

examples.en: A realistic appraisal of the situation.
examples.zh: 對形勢的現實評估。

meanings.definition: To make real; to convert from the imaginary or fictitious into reality; to bring into real existence

examples.en: The objectives of the project were never fully realized.
examples.zh: 該項目的目標從未完全實現。

meanings.definition: An abstract sphere of influence, real or imagined.
To bring up to maturity, as offspring; to educate; to instruct; to foster.

examples.en: The family has been rearing cattle for 200 years.
examples.zh: 該家族飼養牛已有 200 年歷史。

meanings.definition: A cause:

examples.en: Mankind should develop reason above all other virtues.
examples.zh: 人類應該發展理性高於所有其他美德。

meanings.definition: To deduce or come to a conclusion by being rational

examples.en: I reasoned the matter with my friend.
examples.zh: 我和朋友就這件事進行了推理。

meanings.definition: Having the faculty of reason; rational, reasoning.

examples.en: a reasonable demand, amount, or price
examples.zh: 合理的需求、數量或價格

meanings.definition: To assure anew; to restore confidence to; to free from fear or self-doubt.

examples.en: The boy's mother reassured him that there was no monster hiding under the bed.
examples.zh: 男孩的母親向他保證床底下沒有藏著怪物。

meanings.definition: A deduction from an amount that is paid; an abatement.
To deduct or return an amount from a bill or payment
A person who resists an established authority, often violently
The action or fact of calling someone or something back.
To withdraw, retract (one's words etc.); to revoke (an order).

examples.en: He was recalled to service after his retirement.
examples.zh: 退休後，他被召回服役。

meanings.definition: To move back; to retreat; to withdraw.

examples.en: to recede conquered territory
examples.zh: 收回被征服的領土

meanings.definition: The act of receiving, or the fact of having been received.

examples.en: A balance payable on receipt of the goods.
examples.zh: 收到貨物時應付的餘額。

meanings.definition: To give or write a receipt (for something).

examples.en: to receipt delivered goods
examples.zh: 收到已交付的貨物

meanings.definition: An operation in which data is received.
To take, as something that is offered, given, committed, sent, paid, etc.; to accept; to be given something.

examples.en: She received many presents for her birthday.
examples.zh: 她生日時收到了很多禮物。

meanings.definition: Having happened a short while ago.

examples.en: I met three recent graduates at the conference.
examples.zh: 我在會議上遇到了三位應屆畢業生。

meanings.definition: The act of receiving.

examples.en: The new system provides exceptional quality of the reception signal.
examples.zh: 新系統提供卓越的接收訊號品質。

meanings.definition: The act or an instance of receding or withdrawing.
A formula for preparing or using a medicine; a prescription; also, a medicine prepared from such instructions.

examples.en: His new approach is definitely a recipe for success.
examples.zh: 他的新方法絕對是成功的秘訣。

meanings.definition: One who receives.

examples.en: My e-mail never reached the intended recipient.
examples.zh: 我的電子郵件從未到達預定收件者。

meanings.definition: Receiving
The number obtained by dividing 1 by another given number; the result of exchanging the numerator and the denominator of a fraction.

examples.en: 0.5 is the reciprocal of 2.
examples.zh: 0.5是2的倒數。

meanings.definition: Of a feeling, action or such: mutual, uniformly felt or done by each party towards the other or others; two-way.

examples.en: reciprocal love; reciprocal duties
examples.zh: 彼此的愛；互惠義務

meanings.definition: To count; to enumerate; to number; also, to compute; to calculate.

examples.en: I reckon he won't try that again.
examples.zh: 我想他不會再嘗試了。

meanings.definition: The calling back of a hawk.
To return land to a suitable condition for use.
To match (something or someone which one currently perceives) to a memory of some previous encounter with the same person or thing.

examples.en: I recognised his face immediately.
examples.zh: 我立刻認出了他的臉。

meanings.definition: To bestow commendation on; to represent favourably; to suggest, endorse or encourage as an appropriate choice.

examples.en: The board recommends Philips, given his ample experience in similar positions.
examples.zh: 鑑於飛利浦在類似職位上的豐富經驗，董事會推薦飛利浦。

meanings.definition: To restore a friendly relationship; to bring back to harmony.

examples.en: to reconcile people who have quarrelled
examples.zh: 使爭吵的人和解

meanings.definition: A disk, usually made of a polymer, used to record sound for playback on a phonograph.
Narration, account, description, rendering
To tell; narrate; to relate in detail

examples.en: The old man recounted the tale of how he caught the big fish.
examples.zh: 老人講述了他如何捕到大魚的故事。

meanings.definition: Recovery.
To get back, to regain (a physical thing; in astronomy and navigation, sight of a thing or a signal).

examples.en: After days of inquiries, he finally recovered his lost wallet.
examples.zh: 經過幾天的詢問，他終於找回了遺失的錢包。

meanings.definition: Any activity, such as play, that amuses, diverts or stimulates.
A supply of anything wasted or exhausted; a reinforcement.

examples.en: These new recruits were hired after passing the interviews
examples.zh: 這些新進員工通過面試後被錄用

meanings.definition: To enroll or enlist new members or potential employees on behalf of an employer, organization, sports team, the military, etc.

examples.en: We need to recruit more admin staff to deal with the massive surge in popularity of our products
examples.zh: 我們需要招募更多的管理人員來應對我們產品受歡迎程度的大幅上升

meanings.definition: To heal (an organ or part of the body).

examples.en: to rectify the crisis
examples.zh: 糾正危機

meanings.definition: An act of recycling.
To break down and reuse component materials.

examples.en: Both paper and plastic can be recycled.
examples.zh: 紙張和塑膠都可以回收。

meanings.definition: To recover ownership of something by buying it back.
To bring down the size, quantity, quality, value or intensity of something; to diminish, to lower.

examples.en: to reduce weight, speed, heat, expenses, price, personnel etc.
examples.zh: 減少重量、速度、熱量、費用、價格、人員等。

meanings.definition: Superfluous; exceeding what is necessary.

examples.en: Four employees were made redundant.
examples.zh: 四名員工被裁員。

meanings.definition: To direct the attention of.
An umpire or judge; an official who makes sure the rules are followed during a game.

examples.en: The referee kicked Jim out of the game for fighting.
examples.zh: 裁判因吉姆打架而將其踢出了比賽。

meanings.definition: To act as a referee.

examples.en: He has to referee three hockey games this weekend.
examples.zh: 本週末他必須執法三場曲棍球比賽。

meanings.definition: A relationship or relation (to something).
To provide a list of references for (a text).

examples.en: You must thoroughly reference your paper before submitting it.
examples.zh: 在提交之前，您必須徹底參考您的論文。

meanings.definition: To purify; reduce to a fine, unmixed, or pure state; to free from impurities.

examples.en: to refine gold
examples.zh: 提煉黃金

meanings.definition: To bend back (light, etc.) from a surface.

examples.en: A mirror reflects the light that shines on it.
examples.zh: 鏡子反射照在其上的光。

meanings.definition: The change of something that is defective, broken, inefficient or otherwise negative, in order to correct or improve it

examples.en: A major reform is needed to improve the efficiency in the factory.
examples.zh: 需要進行重大改革來提高工廠的效率。

meanings.definition: To put into a new and improved form or condition; to restore to a former good state, or bring from bad to good; to change from worse to better

examples.en: 1909, H. G. Wells, The History of Mr. Polly Chapter 9
examples.zh: 1909 年，H. G. Wells，《波利先生的歷史》第 9 章

meanings.definition: To hold back, to restrain (someone or something).
The periodic energizing required to maintain the contents of computer memory, the display luminance of a computer screen, etc.
To renew or revitalize.

examples.en: Sleep refreshes the body and the mind.
examples.zh: 睡眠可以使身體和精神煥發活力。

meanings.definition: To cool down, make cool.

examples.en: Please refrigerate your uncooked meats at or below 40° Fahrenheit.
examples.zh: 請將未煮熟的肉類冷藏在華氏 40 度或以下。

meanings.definition: An amount of money returned.

examples.en: If the camera is faulty, you can return it to the store where you bought it for a full refund.
examples.zh: 如果相機發生故障，您可以將其退回購買相機的商店以獲得全額退款。

meanings.definition: To return (money) to (someone); to reimburse.

examples.en: If you find this computer for sale anywhere at a lower price, we'll refund you the difference.
examples.zh: 如果您發現這台計算機在任何地方以更低的價格出售，我們將退還差價。

meanings.definition: To rebuild or replenish with all new material; to restore to original (or better) working order and appearance.
Collectively, items or material that have been discarded; rubbish, garbage.
Discarded, rejected.
A steady look, a gaze.

examples.en: He is held in great regard in Whitehall.
examples.zh: 他在白廳受到高度尊重。

meanings.definition: Having no regard; heedless; careless.
Without attention to warnings or indications of bad consequences.

examples.en: Tatiana knew the meat had passed its expiry date, but she ate it regardless.
examples.zh: 塔蒂亞娜知道肉已經過了保質期，但她還是吃了。

meanings.definition: Paying no attention to; regardless of.
Mode of rule or management.

examples.en: a prison regime
examples.zh: 監獄制度

meanings.definition: Any considerable and connected part of a space or surface; specifically, a tract of land or sea of considerable but indefinite extent; a country; a district; in a broad sense, a place without special reference to location or extent but viewed as an entity for geographical, social or cultural reasons.

examples.en: the equatorial regions
examples.zh: 赤道地區

meanings.definition: A machine that tabulates the amount of sales transactions, makes a permanent and cumulative record of them, and has a drawer in which cash can be kept.
To enter in a register.

examples.en: This is a trial version, and will expire in 30 days. Please register!
examples.zh: 這是試用版，並將在 30 天後過期。請註冊！

meanings.definition: Emotional pain on account of something done or experienced in the past, with a wish that it had been different; a looking back with dissatisfaction or with longing.
To feel sorry about (a thing that has or has not happened), afterthink: to wish that a thing had not happened, that something else had happened instead.

examples.en: He regretted his words.
examples.zh: 他對自己的話感到後悔。

meanings.definition: A member of the British Army (as opposed to a member of the Territorial Army or Reserve).

examples.en: Bartenders usually know their regulars by name.
examples.zh: 調酒師通常都知道常客的名字。

meanings.definition: Bound by religious rule; belonging to a monastic or religious order (often as opposed to secular).

examples.en: regular clergy, in distinction from the secular clergy
examples.zh: 普通神職人員，與世俗神職人員不同

meanings.definition: Regularly, on a regular basis.
To dictate policy.

examples.en: to regulate a watch, i.e. adjust its rate of running so that it will keep approximately standard time
examples.zh: 調節手錶，即調整其運行速度，使其保持約標準時間

meanings.definition: To repeat, as what has been already said; to tell over again; to recite.

examples.en: There's no need to rehearse the same old argument; we've heard it before, and we all agree.
examples.zh: 沒有必要重複同樣的舊論點；我們以前聽說過，而且都同意。

meanings.definition: The exercise of sovereign power.

examples.en: England prospered under Elizabeth I's reign.
examples.zh: 英國在伊莉莎白一世的統治下繁榮昌盛。

meanings.definition: To exercise sovereign power, to rule as a monarch.

examples.en: He reigned in an autocratic manner.
examples.zh: 他以專制方式統治。

meanings.definition: To compensate with payment; especially, to repay money spent on one's behalf.

examples.en: The company will reimburse you for your expenses for the business trip.
examples.zh: 本公司將報銷您出差的費用。

meanings.definition: Something that is rejected.
To refuse to accept.

examples.en: She even rejected my improved offer.
examples.zh: 她甚至拒絕了我改進的提議。

meanings.definition: To be very happy, be delighted, exult; to feel joy.
To tell in a descriptive way.

examples.en: Please relate the circumstances of your journey here today.
examples.zh: 請講述一下您今天來這裡的情況。

meanings.definition: The manner in which two things may be associated.

examples.en: The relation between diet and health is complex.
examples.zh: 飲食與健康之間的關係很複雜。

meanings.definition: Someone in the same family; someone connected by blood, marriage, or adoption.

examples.en: Why do my relatives always talk about sex?
examples.zh: 為什麼我的親戚總是談論性？

meanings.definition: Connected to or depending on something else; comparative.

examples.en: The relative URL /images/pic.jpg, when evaluated in the context of http://example.com/docs/pic.html, corresponds to the absolute URL http://example.com/images/pic.jpg.
examples.zh: 在 http://example.com/docs/pic.html 上下文中計算時，相對 URL /images/pic.jpg 對應於絕對 URL http://example.com/images/pic.jpg。

meanings.definition: To calm down.

examples.en: to relax a rope or cord
examples.zh: 放鬆繩索或繩索

meanings.definition: A new set of hounds.
To release a new set of hounds.

examples.en: Can you relay this message to John?
examples.zh: 你能將此訊息轉達給約翰嗎？

meanings.definition: The event of setting (someone or something) free (e.g. hostages, slaves, prisoners, caged animals, hooked or stuck mechanisms).

examples.en: The video store advertised that it had all the latest releases.
examples.zh: 這家音像商店宣稱擁有所有最新版本。

meanings.definition: To let go (of); to cease to hold or contain.

examples.en: He released his grasp on the lever.
examples.zh: 他鬆開了控制桿。

meanings.definition: Directly related, connected, or pertinent to a topic.

examples.en: His mother provided some relevant background information concerning his medical condition.
examples.zh: 他的母親提供了一些有關他的健康狀況的相關背景資訊。

meanings.definition: Something or someone reliable or dependable

examples.en: the old reliables
examples.zh: 老可靠

meanings.definition: Suitable or fit to be relied on; worthy of dependence, reliance or trust; dependable, trustworthy
The act of relying (on or in someone or something); trust.

examples.en: Your reliance on his expertise may be misplaced.
examples.zh: 您對他的專業知識的依賴可能是錯誤的。

meanings.definition: The removal of stress or discomfort.

examples.en: I sighed with relief when I found out that my daughter hadn't got lost, but was waiting for me at home.
examples.zh: 當我發現女兒沒有迷路，而是在家裡等我時，我鬆了一口氣。

meanings.definition: To ease (a person, person's thoughts etc.) from mental distress; to stop (someone) feeling anxious or worried, to alleviate the distress of.

examples.en: I was greatly relieved by the jury's verdict.
examples.zh: 陪審團的裁決讓我鬆了一口氣。

meanings.definition: Belief in a spiritual or metaphysical reality (often including at least one deity), accompanied by practices or rituals pertaining to the belief.

examples.en: My brother tends to value religion, but my sister not as much.
examples.zh: 我哥哥傾向於重視宗教，但我妹妹則不那麼重視。

meanings.definition: Engage in religious practice.
To give up, abandon or retire from something. To trade away.

examples.en: to relinquish a title
examples.zh: 放棄頭銜

meanings.definition: A pleasant taste
To taste or eat with pleasure, to like the flavor of

examples.en: He relishes their time together.
examples.zh: 他很享受他們在一起的時光。

meanings.definition: Opposing; offering resistance (to).

examples.en: She was reluctant to lend him the money
examples.zh: 她不願意借錢給他

meanings.definition: (with on or upon, formerly also with in) to trust; to have confidence in; to depend.
(chiefly in the plural) That which is left; relic; remainder.

To stay behind while others withdraw; to be left after others have been removed or destroyed; to be left after a number or quantity has been subtracted or cut off; to be left as not included or comprised.

examples.en: There was no food in the house, so I had to remain hungry.
examples.zh: 家裡沒有食物，我只能挨餓。

meanings.definition: A part or parts remaining after some has/have been removed.

examples.en: My son ate part of his cake and I ate the remainder.
examples.zh: 我兒子吃了一部分蛋糕，我吃了剩下的。

meanings.definition: To mark or declare items left unsold as subject to reduction in price.

examples.en: The bookstore remaindered the unsold copies of that book at the end of summer at a reduced price.
examples.zh: 夏末，書店以折扣價保留了該書未售出的書籍。

meanings.definition: Remaining.
An act of pointing out or noticing; notice or observation.

examples.en: a biting remark
examples.zh: 尖刻的話

meanings.definition: To make a remark or remarks; to comment.

examples.en: He remarked that it was getting late.
examples.zh: 他說已經很晚了。

meanings.definition: Something that corrects or counteracts.
To provide or serve as a remedy for.
To recall from one's memory; to have an image in one's memory.

examples.en: Please remember this formula!
examples.zh: 請記住這個公式！

meanings.definition: To cause one to experience a memory (of someone or something); to bring to the notice or consideration (of a person).
Terms of reference; set of responsibilities; scope.
To transmit or send (e.g. money in payment); to supply.

examples.en: to remit the performance of an obligation
examples.zh: 免除履行義務

meanings.definition: The small portion remaining of a larger thing or group.
Remaining; still left.
An element of broadcast programming originating away from the station's or show's control room.
To connect to a computer from a remote location.
At a distance; disconnected.

examples.en: A remote operator may control the vehicle with a wireless handset.
examples.zh: 遠端操作員可以使用無線手持設備控制車輛。

meanings.definition: The process of moving, or the fact of being removed.
The act of removing something.
To move something from one place to another, especially to take away.

examples.en: He removed the marbles from the bag.
examples.zh: 他從袋子裡取出彈珠。

meanings.definition: Stucco or plaster applied to walls (mostly to outside masonry walls).

examples.en: A low-resolution render might look blocky.
examples.zh: 低解析度渲染可能看起來是塊狀的。

meanings.definition: (ditransitive) To cause to become.

examples.en: The shot rendered her immobile.
examples.zh: 這一槍使她動彈不得。

meanings.definition: To make (something) new again; to restore to freshness or original condition.

examples.en: I'd like to renew these three books.  Did you know that you can renew online?
examples.zh: 我想續訂這三本書。  您知道可以在線上續訂嗎？

meanings.definition: The act of renewing.
An act of renouncing.
To give up, resign, surrender, atsake.

examples.en: to renounce a title to land or to a throne
examples.zh: 放棄土地或王位的所有權

meanings.definition: To renew; to revamp something to make it look new again.
Fame; celebrity; wide recognition.
To make famous.
A payment made by a tenant at intervals in order to occupy a property.

examples.en: A New York city taxicab license earns more than $10,000 a year in rent.
examples.zh: 紐約市計程車執照每年可賺取超過 10,000 美元的租金。

meanings.definition: To occupy premises in exchange for rent.

examples.en: I rented a house from my friend's parents for a year.
examples.zh: 我從朋友的父母那裡租了房子一年。

meanings.definition: The act of repairing something.

examples.en: I took the car to the workshop for repair.
examples.zh: 我把車開到修理廠修理。

meanings.definition: To restore to good working order, fix, or improve damaged condition; to mend; to remedy.

examples.en: to repair a house, a road, a shoe, or a ship
examples.zh: 修理房屋、道路、鞋子或船

meanings.definition: To pay back.

examples.en: I finally repaid my student loans, just before sending my kids to college.
examples.zh: 在送孩子上大學之前，我終於償還了學生貸款。

meanings.definition: An act or instance of repealing.
To cancel, invalidate, annul.

examples.en: to repeal a law
examples.zh: 廢除法律

meanings.definition: An iteration; a repetition.

examples.en: We gave up after the third repeat because it got boring.
examples.zh: 第三次重複後我們放棄了，因為它變得無聊。

meanings.definition: To do or say again (and again).

examples.en: The scientists repeated the experiment in order to confirm the result.
examples.zh: 科學家重複了實驗以證實結果。

meanings.definition: To turn (someone) away from a privilege, right, job, etc.
To restore to a former place, position, condition, etc.; to put back

examples.en: When you've finished using the telephone, please replace the handset.
examples.zh: 使用完電話後，請放回聽筒。

meanings.definition: A written or spoken response; part of a conversation.
To give a written or spoken response, especially to a question, request, accusation or criticism; to answer.

examples.en: Joanne replied to Pete's insult with a slap to his face.
examples.zh: 喬安妮用一巴掌回應了皮特的侮辱。

meanings.definition: A piece of information describing, or an account of certain events given or presented to someone, with the most common adpositions being by (referring to creator of the report) and on (referring to the subject).

examples.en: A report by the telecommunications ministry on the phone network revealed a severe capacity problem.
examples.zh: 電信部關於電話網路的一份報告揭示了嚴重的容量問題。

meanings.definition: To relate details of (an event or incident); to recount, describe (something).

examples.en: For insurance reasons, I had to report the theft to the local police station.
examples.zh: 出於保險原因，我不得不向當地警察局報案。

meanings.definition: To present again or anew; to present by means of something standing in the place of; to exhibit the counterpart or image of; to typify.

examples.en: The sculptor represented a horse in bronze.
examples.zh: 雕塑家用青銅描繪了一匹馬。

meanings.definition: A record pressed again; a repressing.
To press again.

examples.en: to repress a vinyl record
examples.zh: 抑制黑膠唱片

meanings.definition: A severe, formal or official reproof; reprehension, rebuke, private or public.
To reprove in a formal or official way.
The act of reproducing new individuals biologically.

examples.en: Unauthorized reproduction of this article is prohibited.
examples.zh: 本文未經授權禁止轉載。

meanings.definition: A state where sovereignty rests with the people or their representatives, rather than with a monarch or emperor; a country with no monarchy.

examples.en: The United States is a republic; the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland is a constitutional monarchy.
examples.zh: 美國是一個共和國；大不列顛及北愛爾蘭聯合王國是君主立憲制國家。

meanings.definition: What somebody is known for.
Act of requesting (with the adposition at in the presence of possessives, and on in their absence).

examples.en: The server returned a 404 error to the HTTP request.
examples.zh: 伺服器向 HTTP 請求傳回 404 錯誤。

meanings.definition: To ask for (something).

examples.en: I have requested that the furniture be moved back to its original position.
examples.zh: 我已要求將家具移回原來的位置。

meanings.definition: Suitableness.

examples.en: This phone has the ability to have its software upgraded wirelessly.
examples.zh: 這款手機能夠無線升級軟體。

meanings.definition: To receive, especially with a consent, with favour, or with approval.

examples.en: The Boy Scouts were going to accept him as a member.
examples.zh: 童子軍將接受他為會員。

meanings.definition: Accepted.
A way or means of approaching or entering; an entrance; a passage.

examples.en: an access of territory
examples.zh: 領土准入

meanings.definition: To render fit, suitable, or correspondent; to adapt.

examples.en: to accommodate ourselves to circumstances
examples.zh: 適應環境

meanings.definition: Suitable; fit; adapted; as, means accommodate to end.
A registry of pecuniary transactions; a written or printed statement of business dealings or debts and credits, and also of other things subjected to a reckoning or review.

examples.en: to keep one's account at the bank.
examples.zh: 在銀行保留帳戶。

meanings.definition: Something that accrues; advantage accruing
To increase, to rise

examples.en: The monthly financial statements show all the actual but only some of the accrued expenses.
examples.zh: 每月財務報表顯示所有實際費用，但僅顯示部分應計費用。

meanings.definition: To succeed in something, now especially in academic performance.

examples.en: Show all the spoils by valiant kings achieved.
examples.zh: 顯示勇敢的國王所獲得的所有戰利品。

meanings.definition: To get.

examples.en: He acquired a title.
examples.zh: 他獲得了一個頭銜。

meanings.definition: Direction.
To prepare oneself.

examples.en: He addressed some portions of his remarks to his supporters, some to his opponents.
examples.zh: 他的言論有些是針對他的支持者的，有些是針對他的反對者的。

meanings.definition: To modify.

examples.en: Morimoto's recipes are adjusted to suit the American palate.
examples.zh: 森本的食譜經過調整以適應美國人的口味。

meanings.definition: To cause to ingest (a drug), either by openly offering or through deceit.

examples.en: We administered the medicine to our dog by mixing it in his food.
examples.zh: 我們透過將藥物混合在狗的食物中來給他服用。

meanings.definition: A forward move; improvement or progression.

examples.en: an advance in health or knowledge
examples.zh: 健康或知識的進步

meanings.definition: To promote or advantage.
Completed before necessary or a milestone event.

examples.en: He made an advance payment on the prior shipment to show good faith.
examples.zh: 他在先前的出貨中預付了貨款以示誠意。

meanings.definition: To give (especially public) notice of (something); to announce publicly.

examples.en: For personal needs, advertise on the internet or in a local newspaper.
examples.zh: 出於個人需求，請在網路或當地報紙上做廣告。

meanings.definition: To give advice to; to offer an opinion to, as worthy or expedient to be followed.

examples.en: The dentist advised me to brush three times a day.
examples.zh: 牙醫建議我每天刷牙3次。

meanings.definition: To incur, stand, or bear without serious detriment, as an act which might under other circumstances be injurious;—with an auxiliary, as can, could, might, etc.; to be able or rich enough.

examples.en: I think we can afford the extra hour it will take.  We can only afford to buy a small car at the moment.
examples.zh: 我想我們可以承受額外的時間。  我們目前只能買一輛小型汽車。

meanings.definition: A temporally organized plan for matters to be attended to.
An understanding between entities to follow a specific course of conduct.

examples.en: to enter an agreement;  the UK and US negotiators nearing agreement;  he nodded his agreement.
examples.zh: 簽訂協議；  英國和美國談判代表接近達成協議；  他點頭表示同意。

meanings.definition: To set aside for a purpose.

examples.en: The bulk of K–12 education funds are allocated to school districts that in turn pay for the cost of operating schools.
examples.zh: K-12 教育資金的大部分分配給學區，學區又支付學校的營運成本。

meanings.definition: To grant, give, admit, accord, afford, or yield; to let one have.

examples.en: to allow a servant his liberty;  to allow a free passage;  to allow one day for rest
examples.zh: 給予僕人自由；  允許自由通行；  留一天休息時間

meanings.definition: To change the form or structure of.
(usually in the plural) An act of righting a wrong; compensation.
To make better; improve.
Decomposition into components in order to study (a complex thing, concept, theory etc.).
To give public notice, especially for the first time; to make known

An annual publication; a book, periodical, journal, report, comic book, yearbook, etc., which is published serially once a year, which may or may not be in addition to regular weekly or monthly publication.

examples.en: I read the magazine, but I usually don't purchase the annuals.
examples.zh: 我讀雜誌，但通常不買年刊。

meanings.definition: Happening once every year.

examples.en: an annual general meeting;  an annual publication
examples.zh: 年度股東大會；  年度出版物

meanings.definition: (often followed by “for”) To make an apology or excuse; to acknowledge some fault or offense, with expression of regret for it, by way of amends

examples.en: My correspondent apologized for not answering my letter.
examples.zh: 我的通訊員為沒有回覆我的信而道歉。

meanings.definition: One who applies for something; one who makes a request; a petitioner.

examples.en: Our job advertisement attracted seven applicants.
examples.zh: 我們的招募廣告吸引了七位應徵者。

meanings.definition: To set, fix or determine (a time or place for something such as a meeting, or the meeting itself) by authority or agreement.
The act or process of developing an opinion of value.
To sanction officially; to ratify; to confirm; to set as satisfactory.

examples.en: Although we may disagree with it, we must nevertheless approve the sentence handed down by the court-martial.
examples.zh: 儘管我們可能不同意，但我們必須批准軍事法庭的判決。

meanings.definition: To reach; to get to a certain place.

examples.en: He arrived home for two days.
examples.zh: 他回家已經兩天了。

meanings.definition: A piece of nonfictional writing such as a story, report, opinion piece, or entry in a newspaper, magazine, journal, dictionary, encyclopedia, etc.

examples.en: a sales article
examples.zh: 銷售文章

meanings.definition: To bind by articles of apprenticeship.

examples.en: to article an apprentice to a mechanic
examples.zh: 給機械師當學徒

meanings.definition: Any specific feature, part, or element of something.

examples.en: Japan's aging population is an important aspect of its economy.
examples.zh: 日本人口老化是其經濟的一個重要面向。

meanings.definition: (of a planet) To have a particular aspect or type of aspect.
To put together.

examples.en: He assembled the model ship.
examples.zh: 他組裝了模型船。

meanings.definition: To determine, estimate or judge the value of; to evaluate

examples.en: He assessed the situation.
examples.zh: 他評估了形勢。

meanings.definition: Something or someone of any value; any portion of one's property or effects so considered.

examples.en: These shares are a valuable asset.
examples.zh: 這些股票是寶貴的資產。

meanings.definition: An assignee.
To designate or set apart something for some purpose.

examples.en: to assign a day for trial
examples.zh: 指定一天進行試用

meanings.definition: A helpful action or an act of giving.

examples.en: The foundation gave a much needed assist to the shelter.
examples.zh: 基金會為避難所提供了急需的幫助。

meanings.definition: To help.

examples.en: This book will assist you in getting your life in order.
examples.zh: 這本書將幫助你讓你的生活井然有序。

meanings.definition: To authenticate by means of belief; to surmise; to suppose to be true, especially without proof

examples.en: We assume that, as her parents were dentists, she knows quite a bit about dentistry.
examples.zh: 我們假設，由於她的父母是牙醫，她對牙科了解很多。

meanings.definition: To make sure and secure.

examples.en: He assured of his commitment to her happiness.
examples.zh: 他向她保證他會致力於讓她幸福。

meanings.definition: To fasten, to join to (literally and figuratively).

examples.en: An officer is attached to a certain regiment, company, or ship.
examples.zh: 軍官隸屬於某個團、連或艦艇。

meanings.definition: To listen to (something or someone); to pay attention to; regard; heed.

examples.en: Secretaries attend to correspondence.
examples.zh: 秘書負責處理信件。

meanings.definition: A lawyer; one who advises or represents others in legal matters as a profession.
To work as a legal attorney.
A public event where goods or property are sold to the highest bidder.
To sell at an auction.
A group of people within hearing; specifically, a large gathering of people listening to or watching a performance, speech, etc.

examples.en: We joined the audience just as the lights went down.
examples.zh: 燈光剛暗下來，我們就加入了觀眾席。

meanings.definition: A judicial examination.

examples.en: National Assembly audit
examples.zh: 國會審計

meanings.definition: To examine and adjust (e.g. an account).

examples.en: to audit the accounts of a treasure, or of parties who have a suit depending in court
examples.zh: 審計寶藏的帳目，或在法庭上提起訴訟的當事人的帳目

meanings.definition: To grant (someone) the permission or power necessary to do (something).

examples.en: The General Assembly authorized the Council to take up the matter.
examples.zh: 大會授權理事會處理此事。

meanings.definition: Such as one may avail oneself of; capable of being used for the accomplishment of a purpose.

examples.en: We have an available candidate.
examples.zh: 我們有一個可用的候選人。

meanings.definition: The arithmetic mean.

examples.en: The average of 10, 20 and 24 is (10 + 20 + 24)/3 = 18.

meanings.definition: To compute the average of, especially the arithmetic mean.

examples.en: If you average 10, 20 and 24, you get 18.
examples.zh: 如果您平均 10、20 和 24，您將得到 18。

meanings.definition: Constituting or relating to the average.

examples.en: The average age of the participants was 18.5.
examples.zh: 參與者的平均年齡為18.5歲。

meanings.definition: A judgment, sentence, or final decision. Specifically: The decision of arbitrators in a case submitted.
