To give by sentence or judicial determination; to assign or apportion, after careful regard to the nature of the case; to adjudge

examples.en: the arbitrators awarded damages to the complainant
examples.zh: 仲裁人判給申訴人賠償損失

meanings.definition: (usually uncountable) Luggage; traveling equipment

examples.en: Please put your baggage in the trunk.
examples.zh: 請將您的行李放入行李箱。

meanings.definition: A state in which opposing forces harmonise; equilibrium.

examples.en: Blair thought he could provide a useful balance to Bush's policies.
examples.zh: 布萊爾認為他可以為布希的政策提供有益的平衡。

meanings.definition: To bring (items) to an equipoise, as the scales of a balance by adjusting the weights.

examples.en: I balanced my mug of coffee on my knee.
examples.zh: 我把咖啡杯放在膝蓋上。

meanings.definition: One who becomes unable to pay his or her debts; an insolvent person.
To force into bankruptcy.
In a condition of bankruptcy; unable to pay one's debts.

examples.en: a bankrupt merchant
examples.zh: 破產商人

meanings.definition: An agreement between parties concerning the sale of property; or a contract by which one party binds himself to transfer the right to some property for a consideration, and the other party binds himself to receive the property and pay the consideration.

examples.en: At that price, it's not just a bargain, it's a steal.
examples.zh: 以這個價格來說，這不僅僅是討價還價，而且是偷竊。

meanings.definition: To make a bargain; to make a deal or contract for the exchange of property or services; to negotiate

examples.en: They had to bargain for a few minutes to get a decent price for the rug.
examples.zh: 他們不得不討價還價幾分鐘才能為地毯拿到一個合適的價格。

meanings.definition: A structure that bars passage.
To block or obstruct with a barrier.
An advantage; help or aid from something.

examples.en: It was for her benefit.   His benefit was free beer.
examples.zh: 這是為了她的利益。   他的好處是免費啤酒。

meanings.definition: To be or to provide a benefit to.
A liquid to consume; a drink, such as tea, coffee, liquor, beer, milk, juice, or soft drinks, usually excluding water.
To issue a command; to tell.

examples.en: He bade me come in.
examples.zh: 他請我進來。

meanings.definition: To dig, chop, etc., with a bill.
Amounts billed.
A relatively long, wide and thin piece of any material, usually wood or similar, often for use in construction or furniture-making.

examples.en: Each player starts the game with four counters on the board.
examples.zh: 每個玩家開始遊戲時棋盤上有四個計數器。

meanings.definition: To step or climb onto or otherwise enter a ship, aircraft, train or other conveyance.

examples.en: It is time to board the aircraft.
examples.zh: 已經到了登機時間。

meanings.definition: Something extra that is good; an added benefit.

examples.en: The employee of the week receives a bonus for his excellent work.
examples.zh: 本週的最佳員工因其出色的工作而獲得獎金。

meanings.definition: To pay a bonus, premium
To reserve (something) for future use.

examples.en: I can book tickets for the concert next week.
examples.zh: 我可以訂下週音樂會的票。

meanings.definition: The act or process of writing something down in a book or books, e.g. in accounting.
A push from behind, as to one who is endeavoring to climb.

examples.en: The controversy gave a boost to the author's sales.
examples.zh: 這場爭議促進了作者的銷售。

meanings.definition: To lift or push from behind (one who is endeavoring to climb); to push up.

examples.en: This campaign will boost your chances of winning the election.
examples.zh: 這次競選活動將增加您贏得選舉的機會。

meanings.definition: The woody part of a tree arising from the trunk and usually dividing.

examples.en: the branch of an antler, a chandelier, or a railway
examples.zh: 鹿角、枝形吊燈或鐵路的分支

meanings.definition: To arise from the trunk or a larger branch of a tree.
A conflagration; a flame.

examples.en: To burn something to brands and ashes.
examples.zh: 將某物燒成灰燼。

meanings.definition: To burn the flesh with a hot iron, either as a marker (for criminals, slaves etc.) or to cauterise a wound.

examples.en: When they caught him, he was branded and then locked up.
examples.zh: 當他們抓住他時，他被打上烙印，然後被關起來。

meanings.definition: A writ summoning one to answer to any action.

examples.en: We got a news brief.
examples.zh: 我們收到了一份新聞簡報。

meanings.definition: To summarize a recent development to some person with decision-making power.

examples.en: The U.S. president was briefed on the military coup and its implications on African stability.
examples.zh: 美國總統聽取了有關軍事政變及其對非洲穩定影響的簡報。

meanings.definition: Of short duration; happening quickly.

examples.en: Her reign was brief but spectacular.
examples.zh: 她的統治短暫但輝煌。

meanings.definition: Briefly.
A booklet of printed informational matter, like a pamphlet, often for promotional purposes.
The amount of money or resources earmarked for a particular institution, activity or time-frame.
To construct or draw up a budget.

examples.en: Budgeting is even harder in times of recession
examples.zh: 在經濟衰退時期制定預算更加困難

meanings.definition: Of or relating to a budget.

examples.en: We flew on a budget airline.
examples.zh: 我們搭乘廉價航空公司的班機。

meanings.definition: A specific commercial enterprise or establishment.

examples.en: I was left my father's business.
examples.zh: 我離開了父親的生意。

meanings.definition: Of, to, pertaining to or utilized for purposes of conducting trade, commerce, governance, advocacy or other professional purposes.

examples.en: Please do not use this phone for personal calls; it is a business phone.
examples.zh: 請不要使用此電話撥打私人電話；這是一部商務電話。

meanings.definition: A storage closet either separate from, or built into, a wall.
To determine the value of something or the solution to something by a mathematical process.

examples.en: Calculate the square root of 3 to 10 decimal places.
examples.zh: 計算小數點後 3 到 10 位的平方根。

meanings.definition: A series of operations undertaken to achieve a set goal.

examples.en: The company is targeting children in their latest advertising campaign.
examples.zh: 該公司在最新的廣告活動中瞄準了兒童。

meanings.definition: To take part in a campaign.

examples.en: She campaigned for better social security.
examples.zh: 她致力於改善社會保障。

meanings.definition: A cancellation (US); (nonstandard in some kinds of English).
To cross out something with lines etc.

examples.en: He cancelled his order on their website.
examples.zh: 他在他們的網站上取消了訂單。

meanings.definition: A person who is running in an election.
To stand as a candidate for an office, especially a religious one.
The ability to hold, receive or absorb

examples.en: It was hauling a capacity load.
examples.zh: 它正在牽引容量負載。

meanings.definition: Filling the allotted space.

examples.en: There will be a capacity crowd at Busch stadium for the sixth game.
examples.zh: 第六場比賽布希體育場將擠滿觀眾。

meanings.definition: Already-produced durable goods available for use as a factor of production, such as steam shovels (equipment) and office buildings (structures).

examples.en: He does not have enough capital to start a business.
examples.zh: 他沒有足夠的資金來創業。

meanings.definition: Of prime importance.

examples.en: London and Paris are capital cities.
examples.zh: 倫敦和巴黎是首都。

meanings.definition: One's calling in life; a person's occupation; one's profession.

examples.en: Washington's career as a soldier
examples.zh: 華盛頓的軍人生涯

meanings.definition: To move rapidly straight ahead, especially in an uncontrolled way.

examples.en: The car careered down the road, missed the curve, and went through a hedge.
examples.zh: 汽車沿著道路猛衝，錯過了彎道，並穿過了樹籬。

meanings.definition: Freight carried by a ship, aircraft, or motor vehicle.
To dismiss (someone, especially military personnel) from service
A systematic list of names, books, pictures etc.
To put into a catalogue.
A group, often named or numbered, to which items are assigned based on similarity or defined criteria.

examples.en: I wouldn't put this book in the same category as the author's first novel.
examples.zh: 我不會將這本書與作者的第一部小說歸為同一類。

meanings.definition: Precept or warning against evil or danger of any kind; exhortation to wariness; advice; injunction; prudence in regard to danger; provident care

examples.en: The guideline expressed caution against excessive radiographic imaging.
examples.zh: 該指南對過度放射線成像表示謹慎。

meanings.definition: To warn; to alert, advise that caution is warranted.
A document containing a certified statement.

examples.en: The film is certificate 15.
examples.zh: 這部電影的證書是15號。

meanings.definition: To supply with a certificate, especially following certification
A person presiding over a meeting.
To serve as chairman.
The process of becoming different.

examples.en: The product is undergoing a change in order to improve it.
examples.zh: 為了改進該產品，正在進行更改。

meanings.definition: To become something different.

examples.en: The tadpole changed into a frog.   Stock prices are constantly changing.
examples.zh: 小蝌蚪變成了青蛙。   股票價格不斷變動。

meanings.definition: The amount of money levied for a service.

examples.en: There will be a charge of five dollars.
examples.zh: 將收取五美元的費用。

meanings.definition: To assign a duty or responsibility to

examples.en: Let's charge this to marketing.
examples.zh: 讓我們將其歸咎於行銷。

meanings.definition: A document issued by some authority, creating a public or private institution, and defining its purposes and privileges.

examples.en: 2005 November 30, Stephen Foley "The market where 'caveat emptor' has become a charter for fraud" The Independent, London
examples.zh: 2005 年 11 月 30 日，Stephen Foley，「『買者自負』已成為詐欺憲章的市場」《獨立報》，倫敦

meanings.definition: To grant or establish a charter.
Leased or hired.
A situation in which the king is directly threatened by an opposing piece.

examples.en: I don't know if she will be there, but it's worth a check.
examples.zh: 我不知道她是否會在那裡，但值得檢查一下。

meanings.definition: A leader or head of a group of people, organisation, etc.

examples.en: All firefighters report to the fire chief.
examples.zh: 所有消防員均向消防隊長報告。

meanings.definition: To smoke cannabis.
Primary; principal.

examples.en: Negligence was the chief cause of the disaster.
examples.zh: 疏忽是造成這場災難的主要原因。

meanings.definition: Short for circular letter.
To distribute circulars to or at.
Of or relating to a circle.

examples.en: Your dictionary defines "brave" as "courageous", and "courageous" as "brave". That's a circular definition.
examples.zh: 你的字典將“勇敢”定義為“勇敢”，將“勇敢”定義為“勇敢”。這是一個循環定義。

meanings.definition: A demand of ownership made for something.

examples.en: a claim of ownership
examples.zh: 所有權主張

meanings.definition: To demand ownership of.

examples.en: A fire claimed two homes.
examples.zh: 一場大火燒毀了兩棟房子。

meanings.definition: A customer, a buyer or receiver of goods or services.
To work together with others to achieve a common goal.

examples.en: Let's collaborate on this project, and get it finished faster.
examples.zh: 讓我們合作完成這個項目，並更快地完成它。

meanings.definition: A fellow member of a profession, staff, academic faculty or other organization; an associate.
To unite or associate with another or with others.

examples.en: Young Fortinbras,/ Holding a weak supposal of our worth/...Colleagued with the dream of his advantage,/...hath not failed to pester us with message/ Importing the surrender of those lands/Lost by his father. - Hamlet (Act I, Scene 2)
examples.zh: 年輕的福丁布拉斯，/對我們的價值抱有微弱的假設/...與他的優勢夢想同行，/...不遺餘力地用信息糾纏我們/進口那些土地的投降/被他父親失去。 - 哈姆雷特（第一幕第二場）

meanings.definition: To gather together; amass.

examples.en: Suzanne collected all the papers she had laid out.
examples.zh: 蘇珊娜收集了她擺好的所有文件。

meanings.definition: To be paid for by the recipient, as a telephone call or a shipment.

examples.en: It was to be a collect delivery, but no-one was available to pay.
examples.zh: 本來是到付的，但沒有人可以付款。

meanings.definition: With payment due from the recipient.

examples.en: I had to call collect.
examples.zh: 我不得不撥打對方付費電話。

meanings.definition: A solid upright structure designed usually to support a larger structure above it, such as a roof or horizontal beam, but sometimes for decoration.

examples.en: It was too hard to read the text across the whole page, so I split it into two columns.
examples.zh: 閱讀整頁的文字太困難了，所以我把它分成兩欄。

meanings.definition: A combine harvester

examples.en: We can't finish harvesting because our combine is stuck in the mud.
examples.zh: 我們無法完成收割，因為我們的聯合收割機陷在泥巴裡了。

meanings.definition: To bring (two or more things or activities) together; to unite.

examples.en: Combine the milk and the hot water in a large bowl.   I'm combining business and pleasure on this trip.
examples.zh: 將牛奶和熱水混合在一個大碗中。   我這趟旅行將商務和休閒結合在一起。

meanings.definition: Contentment, ease.

examples.en: Sleep in comfort with our new mattress.
examples.zh: 使用我們的新床墊讓您享受舒適的睡眠。

meanings.definition: To relieve the distress or suffering of; to provide comfort to.

examples.en: Rob comforted Aaron because he was lost and very sad.
examples.zh: 羅布安慰亞倫，因為他很失落，非常難過。

meanings.definition: An order to do something.

examples.en: I was given a command to cease shooting.
examples.zh: 我接到命令停止射擊。

meanings.definition: To order, give orders; to compel or direct with authority.

examples.en: The king commanded his servant to bring him dinner.
examples.zh: 國王吩咐僕人送晚餐給他。

meanings.definition: To exchange substantially; to abate but not abolish completely, a penalty, obligation, or payment in return for a great, single thing or an aggregate; to cash in; to lessen

examples.en: to commute tithes into rentcharges for a sum; to commute market rents for a premium, to commute daily fares for a season ticket
examples.zh: 將什一稅轉換為一筆租金；以溢價換算市場租金，以季票換算每日票價

meanings.definition: Comparison.
To assess the similarities and differences between two or more things ["to compare X with Y"]. Having made the comparison of X with Y, one might have found it similar to Y or different from Y.

examples.en: Compare the tiger's coloration with that of the zebra.
examples.zh: 比較老虎和斑馬的顏色。

meanings.definition: The act or principle of compensating.
To be in battle or in a rivalry with another for the same thing, position, or reward; to contend
To express feelings of pain, dissatisfaction, or resentment.

examples.en: Joe was always complaining about the noise made by his neighbours.
examples.zh: 喬總是抱怨鄰居發出的噪音。

meanings.definition: A completed survey.
To finish; to make done; to reach the end.

examples.en: He completed the assignment on time.
examples.zh: 他按時完成了作業。

meanings.definition: With all parts included; with nothing missing; full.

examples.en: After she found the rook, the chess set was complete.
examples.zh: 當她找到車後，這盤棋就完成了。

meanings.definition: A network of interconnected systems.

examples.en: Jim has a real complex about working for a woman boss.
examples.zh: 吉姆對為女老闆工作感到非常複雜。

meanings.definition: To form a complex with another substance
Made up of multiple parts; composite; not simple.
To yield assent; to accord; to acquiesce, agree, consent; to adapt oneself, to conform.
A smaller, self-contained part of a larger entity. Often refers to a manufactured object that is part of a larger device.

examples.en: A CPU is a component of a computer.
examples.zh: CPU 是電腦的一個元件。

meanings.definition: Making up a larger whole; as a component word.
To make something by merging parts.

examples.en: The editor composed a historical journal from many individual letters.
examples.zh: 編輯根據許多個人信件撰寫了一本歷史日記。

meanings.definition: The settlement of differences by arbitration or by consent reached by mutual concessions.

examples.en: a compromise of character or right
examples.zh: 品格或權利的妥協

meanings.definition: To bind by mutual agreement.

examples.en: He tried to compromise the security in the computer by guessing the password.
examples.zh: 他試圖透過猜測密碼來破壞電腦的安全性。

meanings.definition: Computational power
To reckon or calculate.

examples.en: Can anyone here compute the square root of 10201?
examples.zh: 這裡有人能算出 10201 的平方根嗎？

meanings.definition: To hide something from view or from public knowledge, to try to keep something secret.

examples.en: He tried to conceal the truth about his health.
examples.zh: 他試圖隱瞞有關他健康狀況的真相。

meanings.definition: That which affects one’s welfare or happiness. A matter of interest to someone. The adposition before the matter of interest is usually over, about or for.

examples.en: Judy's eyes filled with concern as she listened to the news report.
examples.zh: 朱迪聽著新聞報道，眼神充滿了擔憂。

meanings.definition: To relate or belong to; to have reference to or connection with; to affect the interest of; to be of importance to.

examples.en: A good prince concerns himself in the happiness of his subjects.
examples.zh: 好的君主關心臣民的幸福。

meanings.definition: To end; to come to an end.

examples.en: The story concluded with a moral.
examples.zh: 這個故事以一個寓意結束。

meanings.definition: A logical clause or phrase that a conditional statement uses. The phrase can either be true or false.

examples.en: Environmental protection is a condition for sustainability.   What other planets might have the right conditions for life?   The union had a dispute over sick time and other conditions of employment.
examples.zh: 環境保護是永續發展的條件。   還有哪些行星可能具備適合生命存在的條件？   工會在病假和其他就業條件方面有爭議。

meanings.definition: To subject to the process of acclimation.

examples.en: I became conditioned to the absence of seasons in San Diego.
examples.zh: 我已經習慣了聖地牙哥沒有季節的情況。

meanings.definition: The act or method of controlling or directing

examples.en: Good conduct will be rewarded and likewise poor conduct will be punished.
examples.zh: 良好的行為將得到獎勵，同樣，不良的行為將受到懲罰。

meanings.definition: To lead, or guide; to escort.

examples.en: The commander conducted thousands of troops.
examples.zh: 統帥率領千軍萬馬。

meanings.definition: To grant as a possession; to bestow.

examples.en: The college has conferred an honorary degree upon the visiting Prime Minister.
examples.zh: 該學院已向來訪的總理授予榮譽學位。

meanings.definition: Self-assurance.
To strengthen; to make firm or resolute.
A clash or disagreement, often violent, between two or more opposing groups or individuals.

examples.en: The conflict between the government and the rebels began three years ago.
examples.zh: 政府與叛軍之間的衝突始於三年前。

meanings.definition: To be at odds (with); to disagree or be incompatible

examples.en: It appears that our schedules conflict.
examples.zh: 看來我們的行程有衝突。

meanings.definition: (of persons, often followed by to) To act in accordance with expectations; to behave in the manner of others, especially as a result of social pressure.
To express one’s sympathetic pleasure or joy to the person(s) it is felt for.

examples.en: Remind me to congratulate Dave and Lisa on their wedding.
examples.zh: 提醒我祝賀戴夫和麗莎的婚禮。

meanings.definition: (of an object) To join (to another object): to attach, or to be intended to attach or capable of attaching, to another object.

examples.en: I think this piece connects to that piece over there.
examples.zh: 我認為這件作品與那邊的那件作品有連結。

meanings.definition: Voluntary agreement or permission.
To express willingness, to give permission.

examples.en: After reflecting a little bit, I've consented.
examples.zh: 我稍微想了一下，就答應了。

meanings.definition: That which follows something on which it depends; that which is produced by a cause.

examples.en: I'm warning you. If you don't get me the report on time, there will be consequences.
examples.zh: 我警告你。如果你沒有準時給我報告，就會產生後果。

meanings.definition: To threaten or punish (a child, etc.) with specific consequences for misbehaviour.
Wilderness where human development is prohibited.
To save for later use, sometimes by the use of a preservative.

examples.en: to conserve fruits with sugar
examples.zh: 用糖保存水果

meanings.definition: To think about seriously.

examples.en: Consider that we’ve had three major events and the year has hardly begun.
examples.zh: 考慮一下我們已經發生了三件重大事件，而這一年才剛開始。

meanings.definition: To be.

examples.en: The greeting package consists of some brochures, a pen, and a notepad.
examples.zh: 問候語包裡有一些小冊子、一支筆和一本記事本。

meanings.definition: That which is permanent or invariable.
Unchanged through time or space; permanent.

examples.en: constant time   constant space
examples.zh: 恆定時間   恆定空間

meanings.definition: Something constructed from parts.

examples.en: Loops and conditional statements are constructs in computer programming.
examples.zh: 循環和條件語句是電腦程式設計中的構造。

meanings.definition: To build or form (something) by assembling parts.

examples.en: We constructed the radio from spares.
examples.zh: 我們用備用零件組裝了收音機。

meanings.definition: The act of consulting or deliberating; consultation
To seek the opinion or advice of another; to take counsel; to deliberate together; to confer.
To use up.

examples.en: The power plant consumes 30 tons of coal per hour.
examples.zh: 該發電廠每小時消耗30噸煤。

meanings.definition: The act of touching physically; being in close association.

examples.en: I haven't been in contact with her for years.
examples.zh: 我已經很多年沒有和她聯絡了。

meanings.definition: To touch; to come into physical contact with.

examples.en: The side of the car contacted the pedestrian.
examples.zh: 汽車的側面接觸到了行人。

meanings.definition: To hold inside.

examples.en: I'm so excited, I can hardly contain myself!
examples.zh: 我太興奮了，簡直無法自抑！

meanings.definition: Someone or something living at the same time, or of roughly the same age as another.

examples.en: Cervantes was a contemporary of Shakespeare.
examples.zh: 塞萬提斯是莎士比亞的同時代人。

meanings.definition: From the same time period, coexistent in time.
(except in phrases) Satisfaction, contentment; pleasure.
To give contentment or satisfaction; to satisfy; to make happy.

examples.en: You can't have any more - you'll have to content yourself with what you already have.
examples.zh: 你不能再擁有更多了——你必須滿足於你已經擁有的。

meanings.definition: Satisfied
Alright, agreed
Controversy; debate.

examples.en: no contest
examples.zh: 沒有比賽

meanings.definition: To contend.

examples.en: I will contest for the open seat on the board.
examples.zh: 我將爭奪董事會的空缺席位。

meanings.definition: The surroundings, circumstances, environment, background or settings that determine, specify, or clarify the meaning of an event or other occurrence.

examples.en: In what context did your attack on him happen? - We had a pretty tense relationship at the time, and when he insulted me I snapped.
examples.zh: 你是在什麼情況下對他進行攻擊的？ - 當時我們的關係非常緊張，當他侮辱我時，我勃然大怒。

meanings.definition: To knit or bind together; to unite closely.
Knit or woven together; close; firm.
An agreement between two or more parties, to perform a specific job or work order, often temporary or of fixed duration and usually governed by a written agreement.

examples.en: Marriage is a contract.
examples.zh: 婚姻是一份契約。

meanings.definition: Contracted; affianced; betrothed.
A difference in lightness, brightness and/or hue between two colours that makes them more or less distinguishable.

examples.en: Israel is a country of many contrasts.
examples.zh: 以色列是一個充滿反差的國家。

meanings.definition: To set in opposition in order to show the difference or differences between.

examples.en: Foreground and background strongly contrast.
examples.zh: 前景和背景形成強烈對比。

meanings.definition: To give something that is or becomes part of a larger whole.

examples.en: to contribute articles to a journal
examples.zh: 向期刊投稿

meanings.definition: Influence or authority over something.

examples.en: The government has complete control over the situation.
examples.zh: 政府完全控制局勢。

meanings.definition: To exercise influence over; to suggest or dictate the behavior of.

examples.en: With a simple remote, he could control the toy truck.
examples.zh: 透過一個簡單的遙控器，他就可以控制玩具卡車。

meanings.definition: Serving to reduce a difficulty, or accessible with minimum difficulty; expedient.

examples.en: Fast food might be convenient, but it's also very unhealthy.
examples.zh: 快餐可能很方便，但也很不健康。

meanings.definition: A person who has converted to a religion.

examples.en: They were all converts to Islam.
examples.zh: 他們都皈依了伊斯蘭教。

meanings.definition: To transform or change (something) into another form, substance, state, or product.

examples.en: A kettle converts water into steam.
examples.zh: 水壺將水轉化為蒸氣。

meanings.definition: To move (something) from one place to another.

examples.en: Air conveys sound. Water is conveyed through the pipe.
examples.zh: 空氣傳遞聲音。水透過管道輸送。

meanings.definition: To make someone believe, or feel sure about something, especially by using logic, argument or evidence.

examples.en: I wouldn't have or do something, unless I'm convinced that it's good.
examples.zh: 我不會做某事，除非我確信它是好的。

meanings.definition: To work or act together, especially for a common purpose or benefit.
A number representing the position of a point along a line, arc, or similar one-dimensional figure.

examples.en: Give me your coordinates and we'll come and rescue you.
examples.zh: 告訴我你的座標，我們會來救你。

meanings.definition: To synchronize (activities).

examples.en: I was playing tennis for the first time, and it was difficult to coordinate.
examples.zh: 我第一次打網球，協調起來很困難。

meanings.definition: Of the same rank; equal.

examples.en: two coordinate terms
examples.zh: 兩個座標項

meanings.definition: A bond issued by a corporation.

examples.en: McDonald's corporate issued a new policy today.
examples.zh: 麥當勞公司今天發布了一項新政策。

meanings.definition: To incorporate.
Of or relating to a corporation.

examples.en: The one on Seventh Street is a corporate franchise.
examples.zh: 第七街的那家是一家企業特許經營店。

meanings.definition: Free from error; true; accurate.
(constructed with to) To be equivalent or similar in character, quantity, quality, origin, structure, function etc.

examples.en: I've been corresponding with my German pen pal for three years.
examples.zh: 我和我的德國筆友已經通信三年了。

meanings.definition: To incur a charge of; to require payment of a (specified) price.

examples.en: It will cost you a lot of money to take a trip around the world.
examples.zh: 環遊世界一趟要花很多錢。

meanings.definition: A committee that leads or governs (e.g. city council, student council).
The exchange of opinions and advice especially in legal issues; consultation.
To give advice, especially professional advice, to (somebody).

examples.en: Psychiatrists, psychologists, social workers and other mental health professionals counsel clients.
examples.zh: 精神科醫生、心理學家、社會工作者和其他心理健康專業人員為客戶提供諮詢。

meanings.definition: One who counts

examples.en: He's only 16 months, but is already a good counter – he can count to 100.
examples.zh: 他只有 16 個月大，但已經是一個很好的算數者了——他可以數到 100。

meanings.definition: A section of a ticket, showing the holder to be entitled to some specified accommodation or service, as to a passage over a designated line of travel, a particular seat in a theater, a discount, etc.
To use coupons to a such extent that makes the user actively looking for coupons in magazines, online and whatever they can be found.

examples.en: Jenna coupons and she goes through three magazines a day to find the coupons.
examples.zh: 珍娜有優惠券，她每天都要瀏覽三本雜誌才能找到優惠券。

meanings.definition: A person who looks after and guides tourists
To deliver by courier.

examples.en: We'll have the contract couriered to you.
examples.zh: 我們會將合約快遞給您。

meanings.definition: An amount by which something or someone is covered.

examples.en: Before laying sod on that clay, the ground needs two inches of coverage with topsoil.
examples.zh: 在黏土上鋪草皮之前，地面需要覆蓋兩英吋的表土。

meanings.definition: To bring into existence; (sometimes in particular:)

examples.en: You can create the color orange by mixing yellow and red.
examples.zh: 您可以透過混合黃色和紅色來創建橙色。

meanings.definition: Created, resulting from creation.
Reliance on the truth of something said or done; faith; trust.

examples.en: He arrived five minutes late, but to his credit he did work an extra ten minutes at the end of his shift.
examples.zh: 他遲到了五分鐘，但值得讚揚的是，他在輪班結束時確實多工作了十分鐘。

meanings.definition: To believe; to put credence in.

examples.en: Someone said there were over 100,000 people there, but I can't credit that.
examples.zh: 有人說那裡有超過10萬人，但我不敢相信。

meanings.definition: A crucial or decisive point or situation; a turning point.
A single criterion.
A person who appraises the works of others.
To criticise.
Essential or decisive for determining the outcome or future of something; extremely important.

examples.en: A secure supply of crude oil is crucial for any modern nation, let alone a superpower.
examples.zh: 原油的安全供應對於任何現代國家都至關重要，更不用說超級大國了。

meanings.definition: A sea or lake voyage, especially one taken for pleasure.
To sail about, especially for pleasure.

examples.en: Germany cruised to a World Cup victory over the short-handed Australians.
examples.zh: 德國隊在世界盃上輕鬆戰勝了人手不足的澳洲隊。

meanings.definition: Money or other items used to facilitate transactions.

examples.en: Wampum was used as a currency by Amerindians.
examples.zh: Wampum 被美洲印第安人用作貨幣。

meanings.definition: The generally unidirectional movement of a gas or fluid.
Existing or occurring at the moment

examples.en: current events;  current leaders;  current negotiations
examples.zh: 時事;  現任領導；  目前的談判

meanings.definition: The set of courses, coursework, and their content, offered at a school or university.
Frequent repetition of the same behavior; way of behavior common to many; ordinary manner; habitual practice; method of doing, living or behaving.

examples.en: The Ancient Egyptian culture had many distinctive and interesting beliefs and customs.
examples.zh: 古埃及文化有許多獨特而有趣的信仰和習俗。

meanings.definition: To make familiar; to accustom.
Created under particular specifications, specially to fit one's needs: specialized, unique, custom-made

examples.en: My feet are very large, so I need custom shoes.
examples.zh: 我的腳很大，所以我需要定製鞋子。

meanings.definition: An interval of space or time in which one set of events or phenomena is completed.

examples.en: the cycle of the seasons, or of the year
examples.zh: 季節或一年的循環

meanings.definition: To ride a bicycle or other cycle.

examples.en: Avoid cycling the device unnecessarily.
examples.zh: 避免不必要地循環使用設備。

meanings.definition: Injury or harm; the condition or measure of something not being intact.

examples.en: The storm did a lot of damage to the area.
examples.zh: 暴風雨對該地區造成了極大的破壞。

meanings.definition: To impair the soundness, goodness, or value of; to harm or cause destruction.

examples.en: Be careful not to damage any of the fragile items while unpacking them.
examples.zh: 打開包裝時小心不要損壞任何易碎物品。

meanings.definition: (general) A collection of (usually) organized information in a regular structure, usually but not necessarily in a machine-readable format accessible by a computer.

examples.en: I have a database of all my contacts in my personal organizer.
examples.zh: 我的個人記事本中有一個包含所有聯絡人的資料庫。

meanings.definition: To enter data into a database.
A time limit in the form of a date on or before which something must be completed.

examples.en: I must make this deadline or my boss will kill me!
examples.zh: 我必須在最後期限前完成，否則我的老闆會殺了我！

meanings.definition: To render an item non-mission-capable; to ground an aircraft, etc.
A division, a portion, a share.

examples.en: We gave three deals of grain in tribute to the king.
examples.zh: 我們向國王進貢了三筆糧食。

meanings.definition: An argument, or discussion, usually in an ordered or formal setting, often with more than two people, generally ending with a vote or other decision.

examples.en: After a four-hour debate, the committee voted to table the motion.
examples.zh: 經過四個小時的辯論，委員會投票決定擱置該動議。

meanings.definition: To participate in a debate; to dispute, argue, especially in a public arena.

examples.en: "Debate me, coward!" snarled the completely normal intellectual.
examples.zh: 「跟我辯論吧，膽小鬼！」完全正常的知識分子咆哮道。

meanings.definition: An action, state of mind, or object one has an obligation to perform for another, adopt toward another, or give to another.

examples.en: I am in your debt.
examples.zh: 我欠你的情。

meanings.definition: A group, set, or series of ten , particularly:

examples.en: a decade of soldiers
examples.zh: 十年軍人

meanings.definition: To resolve (a contest, problem, dispute, etc.); to choose, determine, or settle

examples.en: Her last-minute goal decided the game.
examples.zh: 她最後一刻的進球決定了比賽的勝負。

meanings.definition: Downward movement, fall.
To move downwards, to fall, to drop.

examples.en: The dollar has declined rapidly since 2001.
examples.zh: 自2001年以來，美元迅速貶值。

meanings.definition: An amount by which a quantity is decreased.

examples.en: One research team has recorded Baishui’s decrease at about 27 meters per year over the last 10 years.
examples.zh: 一個研究小組記錄，在過去 10 年裡，白水的水位每年下降約 27 公尺。

meanings.definition: Of a quantity, to become smaller.
To take one thing from another; remove from; make smaller by some amount.

examples.en: I will deduct the cost of the can of peas from the money I owe you.
examples.zh: 我將從欠你的錢中扣除豌豆罐頭的費用。

meanings.definition: The condition of failing to meet an obligation.

examples.en: He failed to make payments on time and is now in default.
examples.zh: 他未能按時付款，現已違約。

meanings.definition: To fail to meet an obligation.

examples.en: If you do not make your payments, you will default on your loan.
examples.zh: 如果您不付款，您將拖欠貸款。

meanings.definition: A fault or malfunction.

examples.en: a defect in the ear or eye; a defect in timber or iron; a defect of memory or judgment
examples.zh: 耳朵或眼睛有缺陷；木材或鐵的缺陷；記憶或判斷力缺陷

meanings.definition: To abandon or turn against; to cease or change one's loyalty, especially from a military organisation or political party.
(grammar) A word or phrase that designates a specified or identified person or entity.
Having distinct limits.

examples.en: definite dimensions; a definite measure; a definite period or interval
examples.zh: 確定的尺寸；明確的措施；一定的時期或間隔

meanings.definition: A stage of proficiency or qualification in a course of study, now especially an award bestowed by a university or, in some countries, a college, as a certification of academic achievement. (In the United States, can include secondary schools.)

examples.en: She has two bachelor's degrees and is studying towards a master's degree.
examples.zh: 她擁有兩個學士學位，正在攻讀碩士學位。

meanings.definition: A period of time before an event occurs; the act of delaying; procrastination; lingering inactivity.

examples.en: the delay before the echo of a sound
examples.zh: 聲音迴聲之前的延遲

meanings.definition: To put off until a later time; to defer.

examples.en: The mail is delayed by a heavy fall of snow.
examples.zh: 郵件因大雪而延誤。

meanings.definition: A person authorized to act as representative for another; a deputy
To authorize someone to be a delegate
A key that may be pressed to delete something (such as text or files) from a computer.
To remove, get rid of or erase, especially written or printed material, or data on a computer or other device.
To set free from restraint or danger.

examples.en: deliver a captive from the prison
examples.zh: 將一名俘虜從監獄中解救出來

meanings.definition: The desire to purchase goods and services.

examples.en: Prices usually go up when demand exceeds supply.
examples.zh: 當需求超過供應時，價格通常會上漲。

meanings.definition: To request forcefully.

examples.en: I demand to see the manager.
examples.zh: 我要求見經理。

meanings.definition: To show how to use (something).

examples.en: Can you demonstrate the new tools for us?
examples.zh: 您能為我們示範一下新工具嗎？

meanings.definition: The negation in logic.

examples.en: The denial of "There might be X" is the null, "False, there is no X."
examples.zh: 否認「可能有X」是無效的，「假，不存在X」。

meanings.definition: Division; separation, as of compound substances.
To leave.

examples.en: His latest statements seemed to depart from party policy somewhat.
examples.zh: 他的最新言論似乎與黨的政策有些背離。

meanings.definition: (followed by on or upon, formerly also by of) To be contingent or conditioned; to have something as a necessary condition; to hinge on.

examples.en: We would like to go skiing, but it depends on the amount of snow.
examples.zh: 我們想去滑雪，但這取決於雪量。

meanings.definition: Sediment or rock that is not native to its present location or is different from the surrounding material. Sometimes refers to ore or gems.

examples.en: a deposit of seaweed on the shore
examples.zh: 海岸上的海藻沉積物

meanings.definition: To lay down; to place; to put.

examples.en: A crocodile deposits her eggs in the sand.
examples.zh: 鱷魚將卵產在沙子裡。

meanings.definition: A storage facility, in particular, a warehouse.
To obtain or receive (something) from something else.
To represent in words.

examples.en: The feeling is difficult to describe, but not unpleasant.
examples.zh: 這種感覺很難描述，但並不令人不快。

meanings.definition: To be entitled to, as a result of past actions; to be worthy to have.

examples.en: After playing so well, the team really deserved their win.
examples.zh: 在打得如此出色之後，球隊確實配得上這場勝利。

meanings.definition: A specification of an object or process, referring to requirements to be satisfied and thus conditions to be met for them to solve a problem.

examples.en: To be hateful of the truth by design.
examples.zh: 故意憎恨真相。

meanings.definition: To plan and carry out (a picture, work of art, construction etc.).

examples.en: The king designed to mount an expedition to the New World.
examples.zh: 國王計劃對新大陸進行一次探險。

meanings.definition: Someone or something wished for.

examples.en: It is my desire to speak with you.
examples.zh: 我希望與你交談。

meanings.definition: To want; to wish for earnestly.

examples.en: I desire to speak with you.
examples.zh: 我想和你談談。

meanings.definition: A table, frame, or case, in past centuries usually with a sloping top but now usually with a flat top, for the use of writers and readers. It often has a drawer or repository underneath.

examples.en: city desk
examples.zh: 城市服務台

meanings.definition: To shut up, as in a desk; to treasure.
Disdain, contemptuous feelings, hatred.
To vex; to annoy; to offend contemptuously.
In spite of, notwithstanding, regardless of.
The act of destining or appointing.
Something small enough to escape casual notice.

examples.en: Note this fine detail in the lower left corner.
examples.zh: 請注意左下角的這個細節。

meanings.definition: To explain in detail.

examples.en: I'll detail the exact procedure to you later.
examples.zh: 稍後我會向您詳細介紹具體流程。

meanings.definition: To discover or find by careful search, examination, or probing
Detected.
To set the boundaries or limits of.

examples.en: The news of his father's illness determined him to depart immediately.
examples.zh: 他父親生病的消息決定他立刻離開。

meanings.definition: To change with a specific direction, progress.

examples.en: Let's see how things develop and then make our decision.
examples.zh: 讓我們看看事情如何發展，然後再做決定。

meanings.definition: Any piece of equipment made for a particular purpose, especially a mechanical or electrical one.

examples.en: There are a number of household devices in a kitchen such as a dishwasher, a garbage disposal, or an electric can opener.
examples.zh: 廚房裡有許多家用設備，例如洗碗機、垃圾處理器或電動開罐器。

meanings.definition: To give one's time, focus one's efforts, commit oneself, etc. entirely for, on, or to a certain matter

examples.en: I devoted this afternoon to repainting my study, and nothing will get in my way.
examples.zh: 今天下午我專門重新粉刷我的書房，沒有什麼會妨礙我的。

meanings.definition: Devoted; addicted; devout
A plan, drawing, sketch or outline to show how something works, or show the relationships between the parts of a whole.

examples.en: Electrical diagrams show device interconnections.
examples.zh: 電氣圖顯示設備互連。

meanings.definition: To represent or indicate something using a diagram.
A graduated, circular scale over which a needle moves to show a measurement (such as speed).
To control or select something with a dial, or (figuratively) as if with a dial.

examples.en: President Trump has recently dialled down the rhetoric.
examples.zh: 川普總統最近緩和了相關言論。

meanings.definition: An order or command.

examples.en: I must obey the dictates of my conscience.
examples.zh: 我必須服從我良心的指揮。

meanings.definition: To order, command, control.

examples.en: She is dictating a letter to a stenographer.
examples.zh: 她正在向速記員口述一封信。

meanings.definition: Not to have the same traits or characteristics; to be unalike or distinct.

examples.en: These shoes only differ from those ones in having slightly longer laces.
examples.zh: 這些鞋與那些鞋的唯一區別在於鞋帶稍長。

meanings.definition: A digital option.

examples.en: He moved to digital for the first time, using a Sony camera.
examples.zh: 他第一次轉向數位相機，使用Sony相機。

meanings.definition: Having to do with digits (fingers or toes); performed with a finger.

examples.en: digital computer;  digital clock
examples.zh: 數位計算機；  數位時鐘

meanings.definition: A circumstance in which a choice must be made between two or more alternatives that seem equally undesirable.
Performing with industrious concentration; hard-working and focused.

examples.en: Scientists are very diligent in their work.
examples.zh: 科學家們非常勤奮地工作。

meanings.definition: To make smaller.
To manage, control, steer.

examples.en: to direct the affairs of a nation or the movements of an army
examples.zh: 指揮國家事務或軍事行動

meanings.definition: Proceeding without deviation or interruption.

examples.en: the most direct route between two buildings
examples.zh: 兩棟建築物之間最直接的路線

meanings.definition: Directly.
A list of names, addresses etc, of specific classes of people or organizations, often in alphabetical order or in some classification.
Containing directions; instructing; directorial.
To render unable; to take away an ability of, as by crippling.

examples.en: Falling off the horse disabled him.
examples.zh: 他從馬上摔下來，殘了。

meanings.definition: Lacking ability; unable.
To fail to agree; to have a different opinion or belief.

examples.en: Bob says cats are friendlier than dogs, but I disagree.
examples.zh: 鮑伯說貓比狗更友好，但我不同意。

meanings.definition: To sadden or displease (someone) by underperforming, or by not delivering something promised or hoped for.

examples.en: His lack of respect disappointed her.
examples.zh: 他的不尊重令她失望。

meanings.definition: Disappointed
An unexpected natural or man-made catastrophe of substantial extent causing significant physical damage or destruction, loss of life or sometimes permanent change to the natural environment.

examples.en: People would suffer disasters when society's morality degenerates.
examples.zh: 當社會道德敗壞時，人們就會遭受災難。

meanings.definition: Anything discarded.
To throw away, to reject.
Pus or exudate (other than blood) from a wound or orifice, usually due to infection or pathology.
To accomplish or complete, as an obligation.

examples.en: to discharge a prisoner
examples.zh: 釋放囚犯

meanings.definition: A controlled behaviour; self-control.
To train someone by instruction and practice.
A disclosure.
To open up, unfasten.
A reduction in price.

examples.en: This store offers discounts on all its wares. That store specializes in discount wares, too.
examples.zh: 這家商店的所有商品均提供折扣。那家商店也專門販售折扣商品。

meanings.definition: To deduct from an account, debt, charge, and the like.

examples.en: Merchants sometimes discount five or six per cent for prompt payment of bills.
examples.zh: 商家有時會為及時支付帳單提供百分之五或百分之六的折扣。

meanings.definition: (of a store) Specializing in selling goods at reduced prices.

examples.en: If you're looking for cheap clothes, there's a discount clothier around the corner.
examples.zh: 如果您正在尋找便宜的衣服，拐角處就有一家折扣服裝店。

meanings.definition: To find or learn something for the first time.

examples.en: Turning the corner, I discovered a lovely little shop. I discovered that they sold widgets.
examples.zh: 轉過街角，我發現了一家可愛的小商店。我發現他們賣小部件。

meanings.definition: Respectful of privacy or secrecy; exercising caution in order to avoid causing embarrassment; quiet; diplomatic.

examples.en: John just doesn't understand that laughing at Mary all day is not very discreet.
examples.zh: 約翰就是不明白，整天嘲笑瑪麗是不太謹慎的行為。

meanings.definition: An inconsistency between facts or sentiments.

examples.en: They found a discrepancy between the first set of test results and the second, and they're still trying to figure out why.
examples.zh: 他們發現第一組測試結果和第二組測試結果之間存在差異，並且仍在試圖找出原因。

meanings.definition: The quality of being discreet or circumspect.

examples.en: Bob showed great discretion despite his knowledge of the affair.
examples.zh: 儘管鮑伯對這件事瞭如指掌，但他還是表現出了極大的謹慎。

meanings.definition: To make distinctions.

examples.en: The law prohibits discriminating against people based on their skin color.
examples.zh: 法律禁止基於膚色歧視他人。

meanings.definition: Having the difference marked; distinguished by certain tokens.
To converse or debate concerning a particular topic.

examples.en: I don't wish to discuss this further. Let's talk about something else.
examples.zh: 我不想進一步討論這個問題。我們來談談別的事情吧。

meanings.definition: An abnormal condition of a human, animal or plant that causes discomfort or dysfunction; distinct from injury insofar as the latter is usually instantaneously acquired.

examples.en: The tomato plants had some kind of disease that left their leaves splotchy and fruit withered.
examples.zh: 番茄植株患有某種疾病，導致葉子出現斑點，果實枯萎。

meanings.definition: To cause unease; to annoy, irritate.
Material (such as clothing, makeup, a wig) used to alter one’s visual appearance in order to hide one's identity or assume another.

examples.en: A cape and moustache completed his disguise.
examples.zh: 斗篷和小鬍子完成了他的偽裝。

meanings.definition: To change the appearance of (a person or thing) so as to hide, or to assume an identity.

examples.en: Spies often disguise themselves.
examples.zh: 間諜經常偽裝自己。

meanings.definition: Not honest.
To sterilize by the use of cleaning agent.
To discharge; to end the employment or service of.

examples.en: The company dismissed me after less than a year.
examples.zh: 不到一年，公司就解雇了我。

meanings.definition: A message sent quickly, as a shipment, a prompt settlement of a business, or an important official message sent by a diplomat, or military officer.

examples.en: We must act with dispatch in this matter.
examples.zh: 在這件事上我們必須迅速採取行動。

meanings.definition: To send (a shipment) with promptness.
To put out of place; to disarrange.

examples.en: Electronic calculators soon displaced the older mechanical kind.
examples.zh: 電子計算器很快就取代了老式的機械計算器。

meanings.definition: A show or spectacle.

examples.en: The trapeze artist put on an amazing acrobatic display.
examples.zh: 這位空中飛人表演了令人驚嘆的雜技表演。

meanings.definition: To show conspicuously; to exhibit; to demonstrate; to manifest.
The disposal or management of something.
(used with "of") To eliminate or to get rid of something.

examples.en: I dispose of my trash in the garbage can.
examples.zh: 我把垃圾丟進垃圾桶。

meanings.definition: An argument or disagreement, a failure to agree.
To contend in argument; to argue against something maintained, upheld, or claimed, by another

examples.en: Some residents disputed the proposal, saying it was based more on emotion than fact.
examples.zh: 一些居民對此提議提出異議，稱這更多是基於情感而非事實。

meanings.definition: The act or state of deliberately not paying attention or caring about; misregard.

examples.en: The government's disregard for the needs of disabled people is outrageous.
examples.zh: 政府無視殘疾人的需求是令人憤慨的。

meanings.definition: To ignore; pay no attention to.
To throw into confusion or disorder.

examples.en: Hecklers disrupted the man's speech.
examples.zh: 質問者打斷了男子的演講。

meanings.definition: Torn off or torn asunder; severed; disrupted.
Feeling or displaying disappointment or a lack of contentment.

examples.en: We offer refunds to dissatisfied customers.
examples.zh: 我們為不滿意的客戶提供退款。

meanings.definition: To fail to satisfy; to displease.
A form of film punctuation in which there is a gradual transition from one scene to the next
To terminate a union of multiple members actively, as by disbanding.

examples.en: The ruling party or coalition sometimes dissolves parliament early when the polls are favorable, hoping to reconvene with a larger majority.
examples.zh: 執政黨或聯盟有時會在民調有利時提前解散議會，希望以更多多數重新召開會議。

meanings.definition: The amount of space between two points, usually geographical points, usually (but not necessarily) measured along a straight line.

examples.en: From Moscow, the distance is relatively short to Saint Petersburg, relatively long to Novosibirsk, but even greater to Vladivostok.
examples.zh: 從莫斯科到聖彼得堡的距離相對較短，到新西伯利亞的距離相對較長，但到符拉迪沃斯托克的距離則更長。

meanings.definition: To move away (from) someone or something.

examples.en: He distanced himself from the comments made by some of his colleagues.
examples.zh: 他與一些同事的評論保持了距離。

meanings.definition: Capable of being perceived very clearly.

examples.en: Her voice was distinct despite the heavy traffic.
examples.zh: 儘管交通繁忙，她的聲音還是清晰的。

meanings.definition: To recognize someone or something as different from others based on its characteristics.

examples.en: The soldier distinguished himself in combat and received a medal.
examples.zh: 該士兵在戰鬥中表現出色並獲得了一枚獎章。

meanings.definition: To bring something out of shape, to misshape.

examples.en: In their articles, journalists sometimes distort the truth.
examples.zh: 記者在文章中有時會扭曲事實。

meanings.definition: Distorted; misshapen.
To divert the attention of.

examples.en: The crowd was distracted by a helicopter hovering over the stadium when the only goal of the game was scored.
examples.zh: 當比賽唯一進球打進時，一架在體育場上空盤旋的直升機分散了觀眾的注意力。

meanings.definition: Separated; drawn asunder.
To divide into portions and dispense.

examples.en: He distributed the bread amongst his followers.
examples.zh: 他將麵包分發給他的追隨者。

meanings.definition: An administrative division of an area.

examples.en: the Soho district of London
examples.zh: 倫敦蘇豪區

meanings.definition: To divide into administrative or other districts.
Rigorous; stringent; harsh
Disturbance
To confuse a quiet, constant state or a calm, continuous flow, in particular: thoughts, actions or liquids.

examples.en: A school of fish disturbed the water.
examples.zh: 一群魚擾亂了水面。

meanings.definition: Consisting of many different elements; various.

examples.en: Idris Elba was a diverse hire for the franchise
examples.zh: 伊德里斯艾爾巴 (Idris Elba) 是該系列的多元化員工

meanings.definition: In different directions; diversely.
To turn aside from a course.

examples.en: The workers diverted the stream away from the road.
examples.zh: 工人們將水流從道路上引開。

meanings.definition: A thing that divides.

examples.en: Stay on your side of the divide, please.
examples.zh: 請站在分歧的一邊。

meanings.definition: To split or separate (something) into two or more parts.

examples.en: a wall divides two houses; a stream divides the towns
examples.zh: 一面牆將兩棟房子分開；一條小溪將城鎮分開

meanings.definition: A number or expression that is to be divided by another.

examples.en: In "42 ÷ 3" the dividend is the 42.

meanings.definition: The act or process of dividing anything.

examples.en: I've got ten divisions to do for my homework.
examples.zh: 我有十個部門的作業要做。

meanings.definition: An original or official paper used as the basis, proof, or support of anything else, including any writing, book, or other instrument conveying information pertinent to such proof or support.
To record in documents.

examples.en: He documented each step of the process as he did it, which was good when the investigation occurred.
examples.zh: 他記錄了整個過程的每一步，這在調查發生時是很好的。

meanings.definition: A house servant; a maid; a household worker.

examples.en: 2005: Bellingham-Whatcom County Commission Against Domestic Violence, Domestic Violence in Whatcom County (read on the Whatcom County website athttps//web.archive.org/web/20060618212243/http://www.co.whatcom.wa.us/boards/dv_whatcom042505.pdf on 20 May 2006) - The number of “verbal domestics” (where law enforcement determines that no assault has occurred and where no arrest is made), decreased significantly.
examples.zh: 2005 年：貝靈厄姆-霍特科姆縣反家庭暴力委員會，霍特科姆縣的家庭暴力（請參閱霍特科姆縣網站 https://web.archive.org/web/20060618212243/http://www.co.whatcom.wa.us/boards/dv_whatcom042505.pdf 「言語家庭暴力」的數量（其中法律執法部門確定沒有發生攻擊事件，也沒有逮捕任何人），顯著減少。

meanings.definition: Of or relating to the home.

examples.en: domestic violence;  domestic hot water
examples.zh: 家庭暴力；  生活熱水

meanings.definition: Ruling; governing; prevailing

examples.en: The dominant party controlled the government.
examples.zh: 執政黨控制政府。

meanings.definition: The late period of the Roman Empire, following the principate, during which the emperor's rule became more explicitly autocratic and remaining vestiges of the Roman Republic were removed from the formal workings of government; the reign of any particular emperor during this period.
To govern, rule or control by superior authority or power
To make a donation; to give away something of value to support or contribute towards a cause or for the benefit of another.

examples.en: He donated an etching from his own collection to the new art gallery.
examples.zh: 他將自己收藏的一幅蝕刻版畫捐贈給了新美術館。

meanings.definition: A room containing a number of beds (and often some other furniture and/or utilities) for sleeping, often applied to student and backpacker accommodation of this kind.
Twice the number, amount, size, etc.

examples.en: Saddam Hussein was rumored to have many doubles.
examples.zh: 據傳薩達姆·侯賽因有很多替身。

meanings.definition: To multiply by two.

examples.en: The company doubled their earnings per share over last quarter.
examples.zh: 該公司的每股收益比上季翻了一番。

meanings.definition: Made up of two matching or complementary elements.

examples.en: The closet has double doors.
examples.zh: 衣櫃有雙門。

meanings.definition: Twice over; twofold; doubly.

examples.en: There are only a few beds, so some of the children will have to sleep double for the night.
examples.zh: 床位很少，所以有些孩子晚上必須睡雙人床。

meanings.definition: Disbelief or uncertainty (about something); a particular instance of such disbelief or uncertainty.

examples.en: I have doubts about how to convert this code to JavaScript.
examples.zh: 我對如何將此程式碼轉換為 JavaScript 有疑問。

meanings.definition: To be undecided about; to lack confidence in; to disbelieve, to question.

examples.en: He doubted that was really what you meant.
examples.zh: 他懷疑這確實是你的意思。

meanings.definition: To reduce in size or number.

examples.en: Joe and Edna moved to a bungalow after deciding to downsize when the children had left home.
examples.zh: 孩子們離家後，喬和愛德娜決定縮小規模，搬進了一間平房。

meanings.definition: A set of twelve.

examples.en: Can I have a dozen eggs, please?
examples.zh: 請問我可以吃一打雞蛋嗎？

meanings.definition: A current of air, usually coming into a room or vehicle.

examples.en: She took a deep draft from the bottle of water.
examples.zh: 她從瓶子裡深深地喝了一口水。

meanings.definition: To write a first version, make a preliminary sketch.

examples.en: He was drafted during the Vietnam War.
examples.zh: 他在越戰期間應徵入伍。

meanings.definition: Referring to drinks on tap, in contrast to bottled.

examples.en: I'd rather have a fresh, cheap draft beer.
examples.zh: 我寧願喝新鮮、便宜的生啤酒。

meanings.definition: A conduit allowing liquid to flow out of an otherwise contained volume; a plughole (UK)

examples.en: The drain in the kitchen sink is clogged.
examples.zh: 廚房水槽的排水管堵塞了。

meanings.definition: To lose liquid.

examples.en: The clogged sink drained slowly.
examples.zh: 堵塞的水槽排水緩慢。

meanings.definition: A powerful, fast-acting purgative medicine.
Having a strong or far-reaching effect; extreme, severe.

examples.en: If our third attempt fails, we may need drastic ideas.
examples.zh: 如果我們的第三次嘗試失敗，我們可能需要激進的想法。

meanings.definition: A disadvantage; something that detracts or takes away.

examples.en: Poor fuel economy is a common drawback among larger vehicles.
examples.zh: 燃油經濟性差是大型車輛的常見缺點。

meanings.definition: An open-topped box that can be slid in and out of the cabinet that contains it, used for storing clothing or other articles.
A tool used to remove material so as to create a hole, typically by plunging a rotating cutting bit into a stationary workpiece.

examples.en: Wear safety glasses when operating an electric drill.
examples.zh: 操作電鑽時配戴防護眼鏡。

meanings.definition: To create (a hole) by removing material with a drill (tool).

examples.en: Drill a small hole to start the screw in the right direction.
examples.zh: 鑽一個小孔，以正確的方向旋入螺絲。

meanings.definition: Motivation to do or achieve something; ability coupled with ambition.

examples.en: Crassus had wealth and wit, but Pompey had drive and Caesar as much again.
examples.zh: 克拉蘇擁有財富和智慧，但龐培和凱撒同樣擁有動力。

meanings.definition: To provide an impetus for motion or other physical change, to move an object by means of the provision of force thereto.

examples.en: You drive nails into wood with a hammer.
examples.zh: 您用錘子將釘子釘入木頭。

meanings.definition: A small quantity of liquid, just large enough to hold its own round shape via surface tension, especially one that falls from a source of liquid.

examples.en: Put three drops of oil into the mixture.
examples.zh: 將三滴油放入混合物中。

meanings.definition: To fall in droplets (of a liquid).

examples.en: The equipment shows how much the glacier has moved and the amount it dropped in height over the summer.
examples.zh: 該設備顯示了夏季冰川移動的程度以及高度下降的程度。

meanings.definition: Of an item that is one of a pair, the other item in the pair.

examples.en: The octahedron is the dual of the cube.
examples.zh: 八面體是正方體的對偶。

meanings.definition: To convert from single to dual; specifically, to convert a single-carriageway road to a dual carriageway.
Exhibiting duality; characterized by having two (usually equivalent) components.

examples.en: a dual-headed computer
examples.zh: 雙頭計算機

meanings.definition: Deserved acknowledgment.

examples.en: Give him his due — he is a good actor.
examples.zh: 給予他應得的－他是個好演員。

meanings.definition: Owed or owing.

examples.en: He can wait for the amount due him.
examples.zh: 他可以等待應付的金額。

meanings.definition: (used with compass directions) Directly; exactly.

examples.en: The river runs due north for about a mile.
examples.zh: 這條河向正北方流動約一英里。

meanings.definition: To render dull; to remove or blunt an edge or something that was sharp.

examples.en: Years of misuse have dulled the tools.
examples.zh: 多年的誤用已經使這些工具變得遲鈍。

meanings.definition: Lacking the ability to cut easily; not sharp.

examples.en: All these knives are dull.
examples.zh: 所有這些刀都鈍了。

meanings.definition: One that resembles or corresponds to another; an identical copy.

examples.en: This is a duplicate, but a very good replica.
examples.zh: 這是一個複製品，但卻是一個非常好的複製品。

meanings.definition: To make a copy of.

examples.en: Can you duplicate this kind of key?
examples.zh: 你能複製這種鑰匙嗎？

meanings.definition: Being the same as another; identical.

examples.en: This is a duplicate entry.
examples.zh: 這是重複條目。

meanings.definition: A durable thing, one useful over more than one period, especially a year.
Able to resist wear, decay; lasting; enduring.
An amount of time or a particular time interval.

examples.en: Rationing will last at least for the duration.
examples.zh: 配給至少會持續一段時間。

meanings.definition: That which one is morally or legally obligated to do.

examples.en: We don't have a duty to keep you here.
examples.zh: 我們沒有義務把你留在這裡。

meanings.definition: A characteristic or manner of an interaction; a behavior.

examples.en: Watch the dynamic between the husband and wife when they disagree.
examples.zh: 當夫妻意見不一致時，觀察他們之間的動態。

meanings.definition: Changing; active; in motion.

examples.en: The environment is dynamic, changing with the years and the seasons.
examples.zh: 環境是動態的，隨著年份和季節的變化而變化。

meanings.definition: Sharp; sour; acid.

examples.en: I was eager to show my teacher how much I'd learned over the holidays.
examples.zh: 我渴望向老師展示我在假期裡學到了多少東西。

meanings.definition: Wages, money earned, income.
Ability, the means to do something, particularly:
To free (something) from pain, worry, agitation, etc.

examples.en: He eased his conscience by confessing.
examples.zh: 他坦白了自己的良心。

meanings.definition: A reflected sound that is heard again by its initial observer.
(of a sound or sound waves) To reflect off a surface and return.

examples.en: Sid echoed his father's point of view.
examples.zh: 席德贊同父親的觀點。

meanings.definition: Pertaining to an economy.
The boundary line of a surface.

examples.en: I have the edge on him.
examples.zh: 我比他有優勢。

meanings.definition: To move an object slowly and carefully in a particular direction.

examples.en: He edged the book across the table.
examples.zh: 他把書推過桌子。

meanings.definition: A change to the text of a document.
To change a text, or a document.

examples.en: He edits the Chronicle.
examples.zh: 他編輯編年史。

meanings.definition: A written work edited and published, as by a certain editor or in a certain manner.

examples.en: The first edition was soon sold.
examples.zh: 第一版很快就售出了。

meanings.definition: A person who edits or makes changes to documents.

examples.en: John Johnson wrote this term paper and the editor was Joan Johnson.
examples.zh: 約翰·約翰遜撰寫了這篇學期論文，編輯是瓊·約翰遜。

meanings.definition: To instruct or train

examples.en: Wang said such changes to the Baishui glacier provide the chance to educate visitors about global warming.
examples.zh: 王說，白水冰川的這種變化為遊客提供了了解全球暖化的機會。

meanings.definition: The result or outcome of a cause.

examples.en: The effect of the hurricane was a devastated landscape.
examples.zh: 颶風的影響是一片滿目瘡痍的景象。

meanings.definition: To make or bring about; to implement.

examples.en: The best way to effect change is to work with existing stakeholders.
examples.zh: 實現變革的最佳方式是與現有利害關係人合作。

meanings.definition: A cause; something that causes an effect
Making good, thorough, or careful use of resources; not consuming extra. Especially, making good use of time or energy

examples.en: An efficient process would automate all the routine work.
examples.zh: 高效率的流程將使所有日常工作自動化。

meanings.definition: The work involved in performing an activity; exertion.

examples.en: It took a lot of effort to find a decent-sized, fully-furnished apartment within walking distance of the office.   He made a conscious effort to not appear affected by the stories in the paper.
examples.zh: 費了九牛二虎之力，才在辦公室步行範圍內找到了一間面積合適、家具齊全的公寓。   他有意識地努力不讓自己顯得受到報紙上報道的影響。

meanings.definition: To make an effort.
To develop in detail or complexity

examples.en: Could you elaborate on the plot for your novel for me?
examples.zh: 你能為我詳細說明一下你的小說的情節嗎？

meanings.definition: Complex, detailed, or sophisticated.

examples.en: After reading a long, elaborate description, I was impressed but no wiser.
examples.zh: 讀完長篇大論的描述後，我印象深刻，但並沒有更明智。

meanings.definition: An elastic material used in clothing, particularly in waistbands and cuffs.

examples.en: running shorts use elastic to eliminate the need for a belt
examples.zh: 跑步短褲採用鬆緊帶，無需繫腰帶

meanings.definition: Capable of stretching; particularly, capable of stretching so as to return to an original shape or size when force is released.

examples.en: The rope is somewhat elastic, so expect it to give when you pull on it.
examples.zh: 繩子有一定的彈性，所以當你拉動它時，它會產生彈性。

meanings.definition: An elderly person
Old; having lived for relatively many years.
One chosen or set apart.
To choose or make a decision (to do something)
Who has been elected in a specified post, but has not yet entered office.

examples.en: He is the President elect.
examples.zh: 他是當選總統。

meanings.definition: : Of or pertaining to an electron or electrons.

examples.en: electronic music
examples.zh: 電子音樂

meanings.definition: Characterised by or exhibiting elegance.

examples.en: an elegant solution
examples.zh: 一個優雅的解決方案

meanings.definition: One of the simplest or essential parts or principles of which anything consists, or upon which the constitution or fundamental powers of anything are based.

examples.en: Letters are the elements of written language.
examples.zh: 字母是書寫語言的元素。

meanings.definition: To compound of elements.
To raise (something) to a higher position.

examples.en: The doctor told me elevating my legs would help reduce the swelling.
examples.zh: 醫生告訴我抬高雙腿可以幫助減輕腫脹。

meanings.definition: Elevated; raised aloft.
One who is eligible.
Allowed to and meeting the necessary conditions required to participate in or be chosen for something
To completely remove, get rid of, put an end to.

examples.en: a ruthless mobster who eliminated his enemies
examples.zh: 消滅了敵人的殘酷暴徒

meanings.definition: A special group or social class of people which have a superior intellectual, social or economic status as, the elite of society.
Of high birth or social position; aristocratic or patrician.
An order by the government prohibiting ships from leaving port.

examples.en: This copy of the federal budget is under embargo until 2 p.m.
examples.zh: 這份聯邦預算副本在下午 2 點前都處於禁運狀態。

meanings.definition: To impose an embargo on trading certain goods with another country.
To get on a boat or ship or (outside the USA) an aeroplane.

examples.en: All passengers please embark now.
examples.zh: 請所有旅客立即登機。

meanings.definition: To humiliate; to disrupt somebody's composure or comfort with acting publicly or freely; to disconcert; to abash

examples.en: The crowd's laughter and jeers embarrassed him.
examples.zh: 人群的笑聲和嘲笑讓他感到尷尬。

meanings.definition: The function or duty of an ambassador.

examples.en: The American embassy to France is located in Paris.
examples.zh: 美國駐法國大使館位於巴黎。

meanings.definition: An embedded reporter or journalist, such as a war reporter assigned to and travelling with a military unit, or a political reporter assigned to follow and report on the campaign of a candidate.
To lay as in a bed; to lay in surrounding matter; to bed.

examples.en: to embed something in clay, mortar, or sand
examples.zh: 將某物嵌入黏土、砂漿或沙中

meanings.definition: To represent in a physical or concrete form; to incarnate or personify.

examples.en: As the car salesman approached, wearing a plaid suit and slicked-back hair, he seemed to embody sleaze.
examples.zh: 當汽車推銷員走近時，他穿著格子西裝，頭髮向後梳，看起來很骯髒。

meanings.definition: An act of putting arms around someone and bringing the person close to the chest; a hug.
To clasp (someone or each other) in the arms with affection; to take in the arms; to hug.

examples.en: I wholeheartedly embrace the new legislation.
examples.zh: 我全心全意地接受新立法。

meanings.definition: To come into view.

examples.en: He emerged unscathed from the accident.
examples.zh: 他在事故中毫髮無傷。

meanings.definition: A situation which poses an immediate risk and which requires urgent attention.

examples.en: Cardiac arrest is an emergency and if you find someone in cardiac arrest you should call 999 immediately.
examples.zh: 心臟驟停是一種緊急情況，如果您發現有人心臟驟停，請立即撥打 999。

meanings.definition: To stress, give emphasis or extra weight to (something).

examples.en: His two-fingered gesture emphasized what he had told his boss to do with his job.
examples.zh: 他的兩個手指的手勢強調了他告訴老闆如何處理他的工作。

meanings.definition: (furniture) Following or imitating a style popular during the First French Empire (1804–1814).
A political unit, typically having an extensive territory or comprising a number of territories or nations (especially one comprising one or more kingdoms) and ruled by a single supreme authority.

examples.en: the Russian empire
examples.zh: 俄羅斯帝國

meanings.definition: The state of being an employee; employment.

examples.en: The school district has six thousand teachers in its employ.
examples.zh: 該學區有六千名教師。

meanings.definition: To hire (somebody for work or a job).

examples.en: Yesterday our local garage employed a new mechanic.
examples.zh: 昨天我們當地的車庫僱用了一位新機械師。

meanings.definition: To give permission, power, or the legal right to do something.

examples.en: John found that starting up his own business empowered him greatly in social situations.

meanings.definition: (usually plural) A container, especially a bottle, whose contents have been used up, leaving it empty.

examples.en: Put the empties out to be recycled.
examples.zh: 把空的東西拿出來回收。

meanings.definition: To make empty; to void; to remove the contents of.

examples.en: The cinema emptied quickly after the end of the film.
examples.zh: 電影結束後，電影院很快就空了。

meanings.definition: Devoid of content; containing nothing or nobody; vacant.

examples.en: an empty purse; an empty jug; an empty stomach
examples.zh: 空錢包；空罐子；空腹

meanings.definition: To make somebody able (to do, or to be, something); to give sufficient ability or power to do or to be; to give strength or ability to.

examples.en: April 16, 2018, Norimitsu Onishi and Selam Gebrekidan writing in The New York Times, ‘They Eat Money’: How Mandela’s Political Heirs Grow Rich Off Corruption
examples.zh: 2018 年 4 月 16 日，Norimitsu Onishi 和 Selam Gebrekidan 在《紐約時報》上發表文章「他們吃錢」：曼德拉的政治繼承人如何透過腐敗致富

meanings.definition: Purpose; determination
To make (a bill) into law
To surround with a wall, fence, etc.

examples.en: to enclose lands
examples.zh: 圈地

meanings.definition: A meeting, especially one that is unplanned or unexpected.

examples.en: Their encounter was a matter of chance.
examples.zh: 他們的相遇純屬偶然。

meanings.definition: To meet (someone) or find (something), especially unexpectedly.

examples.en: Three armies encountered at Waterloo.
examples.zh: 三支軍隊在滑鐵盧遭遇。

meanings.definition: To mentally support; to motivate, give courage, hope or spirit.

examples.en: I encouraged him during his race.
examples.zh: 我在比賽期間鼓勵他。

meanings.definition: A sincere attempt; a determined or assiduous effort towards a specific goal; assiduous or persistent activity.
To exert oneself.
A diminutive of the pale, usually appearing in pairs on either side of a pale.
To support, to back, to give one's approval to, especially officially or by signature.
To continue or carry on, despite obstacles or hardships; to persist.

examples.en: The singer's popularity endured for decades.
examples.zh: 這位歌手的受歡迎程度持續了數十年。

meanings.definition: The impetus behind all motion and all activity.
To keep up, impose or bring into effect something, not necessarily by force.

examples.en: The police are there to enforce the law.
examples.zh: 警察在那裡執行法律。

meanings.definition: (heading) To interact socially.
A large construction used in warfare, such as a battering ram, catapult etc.

examples.en: a graphics engine
examples.zh: 圖形引擎

meanings.definition: To equip with an engine; said especially of steam vessels.

examples.en: Vessels are often built by one firm and engined by another.
examples.zh: 船舶通常由一家公司建造並由另一家公司提供發動機。

meanings.definition: To lift, raise up.

examples.en: A debt enhances rapidly by compound interest.
examples.zh: 債務透過複利迅速增加。

meanings.definition: To receive pleasure or satisfaction from something

examples.en: Enjoy your holidays!   I enjoy dancing.
examples.zh: 祝你假期愉快！   我喜歡跳舞。

meanings.definition: To make larger.

examples.en: Knowledge enlarges the mind.
examples.zh: 知識擴大了心胸。

meanings.definition: To supply with light.

examples.en: The sun enlightens the Earth.
examples.zh: 太陽照亮了大地。

meanings.definition: Deviating from the norm; unusual, extraordinary.
To make an enquiry.

examples.en: He enquired about the availability of rental bicycles in the town.
examples.zh: 他詢問了鎮上是否有出租自行車的情況。

meanings.definition: To enter (a name, etc.) in a register, roll or list

examples.en: They were eager to enroll new recruits.
examples.zh: 他們急於招收新兵。

meanings.definition: To make a pledge to (someone); to promise, guarantee (someone of something); to assure.

examples.en: I use an alarm clock to ensure that I get up on time.
examples.zh: 我使用鬧鐘來確保我準時起床。

meanings.definition: To imply or require.

examples.en: This activity will entail careful attention to detail.
examples.zh: 這項活動需要特別注意細節。

meanings.definition: The "Enter" key on a computer keyboard.
To go or come into an enclosed or partially enclosed space.

examples.en: You should knock before you enter, unless you want to see me naked.
examples.zh: 進去之前應該先敲門，除非你想看我裸體。

meanings.definition: Entertainment; pleasure.
To amuse (someone); to engage the attention of agreeably.

examples.en: The motivational speaker not only instructed but also entertained the audience.
examples.zh: 勵志演講者不僅指導而且娛樂觀眾。

meanings.definition: With zealous fervor; excited, motivated.

examples.en: an enthusiastic lover of art
examples.zh: 一位熱心的藝術愛好者

meanings.definition: The whole of something; the entirety.
(sometimes postpositive) Whole; complete.

examples.en: We had the entire building to ourselves for the evening.
examples.zh: 整個晚上我們獨享整棟大樓。

meanings.definition: To give a title to.

examples.en: A passport entitles the bearer to travel to other countries.
examples.zh: 護照使持有人有權前往其他國家。

meanings.definition: That which has a distinct existence as an individual unit. Often used for organisations which have no physical form.

examples.en: The group successfully maintains its tribal entity.
examples.zh: 該團體成功地維持了其部落實體。

meanings.definition: The action of entering, or going in.

examples.en: Her entrance attracted no attention whatsoever.
examples.zh: 她的出現並沒有引起任何注意。

meanings.definition: To trust to the care of.

examples.en: Can I entrust you with a secret?
examples.zh: 我可以託付你一個秘密嗎？

meanings.definition: The act of entering.

examples.en: Children are allowed entry only if accompanied by an adult.
examples.zh: 兒童只有在成人陪同下才可以進入。

meanings.definition: To specify each member of a sequence individually in incrementing order.
A paper or cardboard wrapper used to enclose small, flat items, especially letters, for mailing.
Feeling or exhibiting envy; jealously desiring the excellence or good fortune of another; maliciously grudging

examples.en: an envious man, disposition, or attack; envious tongues
examples.zh: 嫉妒的人、性情或攻擊；嫉妒的舌頭

meanings.definition: The surroundings of, and influences on, a particular item of interest.

examples.en: That program uses the Microsoft Windows environment.
examples.zh: 該程式使用 Microsoft Windows 環境。

meanings.definition: To conceive or see something within one's mind; to imagine or envision.
A short stanza at the end of a poem, used either to address a person or to comment on the preceding body of the poem.
An incident, action, or time period standing out by itself, but more or less connected with a complete series of events.

examples.en: It was a most embarrassing episode in my life.
examples.zh: 這是我一生中最尷尬的一件事。

meanings.definition: A person or thing of equal status to others.

examples.en: This beer has no equal.
examples.zh: 這種啤酒無與倫比。

meanings.definition: To be equal to, to have the same value as; to correspond to.

examples.en: Two plus two equals four.
examples.zh: 二加二等於四。

meanings.definition: The same in all respects.

examples.en: All men are created equal.
examples.zh: 人人生而平等。

meanings.definition: The act or process of equating two or more things, or the state of those things being equal (that is, identical).

examples.en: We need to bring the balance of power into equation
examples.zh: 我們需要將力量平衡納入方程式

meanings.definition: To supply with something necessary in order to carry out a specific action or task; to provide with (e.g. weapons, provisions, munitions, rigging)
Anything that is virtually equal to something else, or has the same value, force, etc.
To make equivalent to; to equal.
Similar or identical in value, meaning or effect; virtually equal.

examples.en: To burn calories, a thirty-minute jog is equivalent to a couple of hamburgers.
examples.zh: 為了燃燒卡路里，三十分鐘的慢跑相當於兩個漢堡。

meanings.definition: The operation of deleting data.
To remove markings or information

examples.en: I erased that note because it was wrong.
examples.zh: 我刪除了那張紙條，因為它是錯的。

meanings.definition: Upright; vertical or reaching broadly upwards.

examples.en: The penis should be fully erect before commencing copulation.
examples.zh: 開始交配前陰莖應完全勃起。

meanings.definition: The result of having been worn away or eroded, as by a glacier on rock or the sea on a cliff face.

examples.en: the erosion of a person's trust
examples.zh: 一個人的信任受到侵蝕

meanings.definition: A journey undertaken to accomplish some task.
To send someone on an errand.

examples.en: All the servants were on holiday or erranded out of the house.
examples.zh: 所有僕人都在度假或外出辦事。

meanings.definition: The state, quality, or condition of being wrong.
To function improperly due to an error, especially accompanied by error message.

examples.en: Remove that line of code and the script should stop erroring there.
examples.zh: 刪除該行程式碼，腳本應該會停止出錯。

meanings.definition: To increase (something) in extent or intensity; to intensify or step up.

examples.en: The shooting escalated the existing hostility.
examples.zh: 槍擊事件加劇了現有的敵意情緒。

meanings.definition: The act of leaving a dangerous or unpleasant situation.

examples.en: The prisoners made their escape by digging a tunnel.
examples.zh: 囚犯們挖了一條隧道越獄。

meanings.definition: To get free; to free oneself.

examples.en: The factory was evacuated after toxic gases escaped from a pipe.
examples.zh: 有毒氣體從管道中逸出後，工廠被疏散。

meanings.definition: A group of people or vehicles, generally armed, who go with a person or people of importance to safeguard them on a journey or mission.
To attend to in order to guard and protect; to accompany as a safeguard (for the person escorted or for others); to give honorable or ceremonious attendance to

examples.en: 2018, TST-00493-18 (Re), Ontario Landlord and Tenant Board (Anna Solomon, member)
examples.zh: 2018，TST-00493-18（重新），安大略省房東和租客委員會（Anna Solomon，成員）

meanings.definition: (authorship) A written composition of moderate length, exploring a particular issue or subject.
A necessary ingredient.
Necessary.

examples.en: Don’t mind him being grumpy. That’s the essential Fred.
examples.zh: 別介意他脾氣暴躁。這就是弗雷德的本質。

meanings.definition: To make stable or firm; to confirm.
The collective property and liabilities of someone, especially a deceased person.
To give an estate to.
(jewelry) Previously owned; secondhand.

examples.en: an estate diamond; estate jewelry
examples.zh: 遺產鑽石；地產珠寶

meanings.definition: Favourable regard.

examples.en: We hold her in high esteem.
examples.zh: 我們非常尊敬她。

meanings.definition: To set a high value on; to regard with respect or reverence.

examples.en: Mary is an esteemed member of the community.
examples.zh: 瑪麗是社區中一位受人尊敬的成員。

meanings.definition: A rough calculation or assessment of the value, size, or cost of something.
To calculate roughly, often from imperfect data.
One who lives forever; an immortal.
Lasting forever; unending.

examples.en: some eternal villain
examples.zh: 某個永恆的惡棍

meanings.definition: The study of principles relating to right and wrong conduct.
An ethnic person, especially a foreigner or member of an immigrant community.
Of or relating to a group of people having common racial, ancestral, national, religious or cultural origins.

examples.en: There are many ethnic Indonesians in the Netherlands
examples.zh: 荷蘭有很多印尼裔

meanings.definition: To leave or withdraw from; to quit; to retire from

examples.en: The firefighters told us to evacuate the area as the flames approached.
examples.zh: 當火勢逼近時，消防隊員告訴我們撤離該地區。

meanings.definition: To get away from by cunning; to avoid by dexterity, subterfuge, address, or ingenuity; to elude; to cleverly escape from

examples.en: He evaded his opponent's blows.
examples.zh: 他躲開了對手的攻擊。

meanings.definition: To draw conclusions from examining; to assess.

examples.en: It will take several years to evaluate the material gathered in the survey.
examples.zh: 評估調查中收集的材料需要幾年時間。

meanings.definition: An occurrence; something that happens.

examples.en: I went to an event in San Francisco last week.
examples.zh: 上週我參加了舊金山的活動。

meanings.definition: To occur, take place.
In the end; at some later time, especially after a long time, a series of problems, struggles, delays or setbacks.

examples.en: Eventually, all prime numbers are odd.
examples.zh: 最終，所有質數都是奇數。

meanings.definition: Facts or observations presented in support of an assertion.

examples.en: There is no evidence that anyone was here earlier.
examples.zh: 沒有證據表明有人早些時候來過這裡。

meanings.definition: To provide evidence for, or suggest the truth of.

examples.en: She was furious, as evidenced by her slamming the door.
examples.zh: 她很生氣，關上門就證明了這一點。

meanings.definition: Obviously true by simple observation.

examples.en: It was evident she was angry, after she slammed the door.
examples.zh: 關上門後，看得出來她很生氣。

meanings.definition: Moral badness; wickedness; malevolence; the forces or behaviors that are the opposite or enemy of good.

examples.en: Evil lacks spirituality, hence its need for mind control.
examples.zh: 邪惡缺乏靈性，因此需要精神控制。

meanings.definition: Intending to harm; malevolent.

examples.en: an evil plot to brainwash and even kill innocent people
examples.zh: 洗腦甚至殘殺無辜的邪惡陰謀

meanings.definition: To call out; to draw out or bring forth.
