A straight, round stick, shaft, bar, cane, or staff.

examples.en: When I hooked a snake and not a fish, I got so scared I dropped my rod in the water.
examples.zh: 當我釣到一條蛇而不是一條魚時，我非常害怕，我把魚竿掉進水裡了。

meanings.definition: To reinforce concrete with metal rods.
A character or part played by a performer or actor.

examples.en: Her dream was to get a role in a Hollywood movie, no matter how small.
examples.zh: 她的夢想是在好萊塢電影中獲得一個角色，無論多小。

meanings.definition: The act or result of rolling, or state of being rolled.

examples.en: Look at the roll of the waves.
examples.zh: 看看波浪的翻滾。

meanings.definition: To cause to revolve by turning over and over; to move by turning on an axis; to impel forward by causing to turn over and over on a supporting surface.

examples.en: To roll a wheel, a ball, or a barrel.
examples.zh: 滾動輪子、球或桶子。

meanings.definition: The external covering at the top of a building.

examples.en: The roof was blown off by the tornado.
examples.zh: 屋頂被龍捲風吹掉了。

meanings.definition: Opportunity or scope (to do something).

examples.en: Go to your room!
examples.zh: 去你的房間吧！

meanings.definition: To reside, especially as a boarder or tenant.

examples.en: Doctor Watson roomed with Sherlock Holmes at Baker Street.
examples.zh: 華生醫生與福爾摩斯住在貝克街。

meanings.definition: The part of a plant, generally underground, that anchors and supports the plant body, absorbs and stores water and nutrients, and in some plants is able to perform vegetative reproduction.

examples.en: This tree's roots can go as deep as twenty metres underground.
examples.zh: 這棵樹的根可以深入地下二十公尺。

meanings.definition: To grow roots; to enter the earth, as roots; to take root and begin to grow.

examples.en: The cuttings are starting to root.
examples.zh: 插枝開始生根。

meanings.definition: Thick strings, yarn, monofilaments, metal wires, or strands of other cordage that are twisted together to form a stronger line.

examples.en: Nylon rope is usually stronger than similar rope made of plant fibers.
examples.zh: 尼龍繩通常比由植物纖維製成的類似繩索更堅固。

meanings.definition: To tie (something) with rope.

examples.en: The robber roped the victims.
examples.zh: 強盜用繩子捆住了受害者。

meanings.definition: A shrub of the genus Rosa, with red, pink, white or yellow flowers.
To make rose-coloured; to redden or flush.
Having a purplish-red or pink colour. See rosy.
The process of becoming rotten; putrefaction.
To suffer decomposition due to biological action, especially by fungi or bacteria.

examples.en: The apple left in the cupboard all that time had started to rot.
examples.zh: 一直放在櫃子裡的蘋果已經開始腐爛。

meanings.definition: To spin, turn, or revolve.

examples.en: He rotated in his chair to face me.
examples.zh: 他在椅子上旋轉面對我。

meanings.definition: Having the parts spreading out like a wheel; wheel-shaped.

examples.en: a rotate spicule or scale; a rotate corolla
examples.zh: 旋轉骨針或鱗片；旋轉花冠

meanings.definition: The unmowed part of a golf course.
To create in an approximate form.

examples.en: Rough in the shape first, then polish the details.
examples.zh: 先粗略形狀，再打磨細節。

meanings.definition: Not smooth; uneven.

examples.en: a rough estimate; a rough sketch of a building; a rough plan
examples.zh: 粗略估計；建築物的草圖；一個粗略的計劃

meanings.definition: In a rough manner; rudely; roughly.
So as to form a circle or trace a circular path, or approximation thereof.

examples.en: High above, vultures circled around.
examples.zh: 高處，禿鷹盤旋。

meanings.definition: Defining a circle or closed curve containing a thing.

examples.en: I planted a row of lilies around the statue.
examples.zh: 我在雕像周圍種了一排百合花。

meanings.definition: A circular or spherical object or part of an object.

examples.en: The guards have started their rounds; the prisoner should be caught soon.
examples.zh: 警衛們已經開始巡視；囚犯應該很快就會被抓獲。

meanings.definition: To shape something into a curve.

examples.en: The carpenter rounded the edges of the table.
examples.zh: 木匠把桌子的邊緣修圓了。

meanings.definition: (physical) Shape.

examples.en: The baker sold us a round dozen.
examples.zh: 麵包師傅賣給我們一打。

meanings.definition: A course or way which is traveled or passed.

examples.en: The route was used so much that it formed a rut.
examples.zh: 這條路線被頻繁使用，以至於形成了車轍。

meanings.definition: To direct or divert along a particular course.

examples.en: All incoming mail was routed through a single office.
examples.zh: 所有傳入的郵件都透過一個辦公室進行路由。

meanings.definition: A course of action to be followed regularly; a standard procedure.

examples.en: Connie was completely robotic and emotionless by age 12; her entire life had become one big routine.
examples.zh: 康妮 12 歲時就完全變成了機器人，沒有感情。她的整個生活變成了一件大事。

meanings.definition: According to established procedure.
A royal person; a member of a royal family.
Of or relating to a monarch or their family.

examples.en: royal mast;  royal sail
examples.zh: 皇家桅杆；  皇家航行

meanings.definition: An act of rubbing.

examples.en: Give that lamp a good rub and see if any genies come out
examples.zh: 好好擦一擦那盞燈，看看有沒有精靈出來

meanings.definition: To move (one object) while maintaining contact with another object over some area, with pressure and friction.

examples.en: I rubbed my hands together for warmth.
examples.zh: 我搓著手取暖。

meanings.definition: Pliable material derived from the sap of the rubber tree; a hydrocarbon polymer of isoprene.

examples.en: Jones toes the rubber and then fires to the plate.
examples.zh: 瓊斯用腳尖踩住橡膠，然後朝本壘板射擊。

meanings.definition: (of a draft/check) Not covered by funds on account.
Bad-mannered.

examples.en: Karen broke up with Fred because he was often rude to her.
examples.zh: 凱倫與弗雷德分手，因為他經常對她無禮。

meanings.definition: A partial covering for a floor.
To pull roughly or hastily; to plunder; to spoil; to tear.
Snug; cosy
(sometimes in the plural) The remains of a destroyed or dilapidated construction, such as a house or castle.

examples.en: The monastery has fallen into ruin.
examples.zh: 修道院已成為廢墟。

meanings.definition: To cause the fiscal ruin of.

examples.en: With all these purchases, you surely mean to ruin us!
examples.zh: 買這麼多東西，你一定是想毀了我們！

meanings.definition: A regulation, law, guideline.

examples.en: All participants must adhere to the rules.
examples.zh: 所有參與者都必須遵守規則。

meanings.definition: A (usually rigid), flat, rectangular measuring or drawing device with graduations in units of measurement; a straightedge with markings.
To beat with a ruler (as a traditional school punishment).
A statement or claim of questionable accuracy, from no known reliable source, usually spread by word of mouth.

examples.en: There's a rumor going round that he's going to get married.
examples.zh: 有傳言說他要結婚了。

meanings.definition: (usually used in the passive voice) To tell a rumor about; to gossip.

examples.en: John is rumored to be next in line for a promotion.
examples.zh: 據傳約翰將是下一個晉升的人選。

meanings.definition: To run.
Act or instance of running, of moving rapidly using the feet.

examples.en: I just got back from my morning run.
examples.zh: 我剛剛晨跑回來。

meanings.definition: In a liquid state; melted or molten.

examples.en: Put some run butter on the vegetables.
examples.zh: 在蔬菜上塗一些黃油。

meanings.definition: A person from the countryside; a rustic.
Relating to the countryside or to agriculture.
Any of several stiff plants of the genus Juncus, or the family Juncaceae, having hollow or pithy stems and small flowers, and often growing in marshes or near water.
The deteriorated state of iron or steel as a result of moisture and oxidation.

examples.en: The rust on my bicycle chain made cycling to work very dangerous.
examples.zh: 我的自行車鏈條生鏽了，騎自行車上班非常危險。

meanings.definition: A bag; especially a large bag of strong, coarse material for storage and handling of various commodities, such as potatoes, coal, coffee; or, a bag with handles used at a supermarket, a grocery sack; or, a small bag for small items, a satchel.

examples.en: The American sack of salt is 215 pounds; the sack of wheat, two bushels. — McElrath.
examples.zh: 美國一袋鹽重 215 磅；一袋小麥，兩蒲式耳。 ——麥克埃爾拉斯。

meanings.definition: To put in a sack or sacks.

examples.en: Help me sack the groceries.
examples.zh: 幫我把雜貨裝袋。

meanings.definition: Characterized by solemn religious ceremony or religious use, especially, in a positive sense; consecrated, made holy.

examples.en: a sacred day
examples.zh: 神聖的一天

meanings.definition: The offering of anything to a god; a consecratory rite.

examples.en: the sacrifice of one's spare time in order to volunteer
examples.zh: 為了做志工而犧牲自己的業餘時間

meanings.definition: To offer (something) as a gift to a deity.

examples.en: Venison has many advantages over meat from factory farms, although it still requires a hunter to sacrifice the life of a deer.
examples.zh: 鹿肉比工廠化農場的肉有很多優點，儘管它仍然需要獵人犧牲鹿的生命。

meanings.definition: To make melancholy; to sadden or grieve (someone).
(heading) Emotionally negative.

examples.en: I can't believe you use drugs; you're so sad!
examples.zh: 我不敢相信你竟然吸毒；你太傷心了！

meanings.definition: A box, usually made of metal, in which valuables can be locked for safekeeping.
To make something safe.
Not in danger; out of harm's reach.

examples.en: You’ll be safe here.
examples.zh: 你在這裡會很安全。

meanings.definition: The condition or feeling of being safe; security; certainty.

examples.en: If you push it to the limit, safety is not guaranteed.
examples.zh: 如果把它推到極限，安全就得不到保障。

meanings.definition: To secure (a mechanical component, as in aviation) to keep it from becoming detached even under vibration.
A piece of fabric attached to a boat and arranged such that it causes the wind to drive the boat along. The sail may be attached to the boat via a combination of mast, spars and ropes.

examples.en: Take in sail: a storm is coming.
examples.zh: 收起帆：暴風雨即將來臨。

meanings.definition: A person in the business of navigating ships or other vessels

examples.en: He's a talented sailor and has spent many years at sea.
examples.zh: 他是一位才華橫溢的水手，在海上度過了多年。

meanings.definition: A person whom a church or another religious group has officially recognised as especially holy or godly; one eminent for piety and virtue.

examples.en: Kateri Tekakwitha was proclaimed a saint.
examples.zh: 卡泰里·特卡維薩 (Kateri Tekakwitha) 被宣佈為聖人。

meanings.definition: Cause, interest or account

examples.en: For the sake of argument
examples.zh: 為了論證

meanings.definition: A fixed amount of money paid to a worker, usually calculated on a monthly or annual basis, not hourly, as wages. Implies a degree of professionalism and/or autonomy.
To pay on the basis of a period of a week or longer, especially to convert from another form of compensation.
Saline.
A hall.
A person whose job is to sell things, either in a shop/store or elsewhere.
A part or snippet of something taken or presented for inspection, or shown as evidence of the quality of the whole; a specimen.

examples.en: a blood sample
examples.zh: 血液樣本

meanings.definition: To take or to test a sample or samples of.
An approval, by an authority, generally one that makes something valid.

examples.en: The whalers had been operating in the contested waters off the island with sanction from the Japanese government.
examples.zh: 這些捕鯨船一直在日本政府的批准下在該島附近有爭議的水域進行作業。

meanings.definition: To ratify; to make valid.
A moon or other smaller body orbiting a larger one.

examples.en: A spent upper stage is a derelict satellite.
examples.zh: 用過的上級是一顆廢棄的衛星。

meanings.definition: To transmit by satellite.
To do enough for; to meet the needs of; to fulfill the wishes or requirements of.

examples.en: I'm not satisfied with the quality of the food here.
examples.zh: 我對這裡的食物品質不滿意。

meanings.definition: A liquid (often thickened) condiment or accompaniment to food.

examples.en: apple sauce; mint sauce
examples.zh: 蘋果醬；薄荷醬

meanings.definition: To add sauce to; to season.
In various sports, a block that prevents an opponent from scoring.

examples.en: The goaltender made a great save.
examples.zh: 守門員做出了一次精彩的撲救。

meanings.definition: To prevent harm or difficulty.
Except; with the exception of.
Unless; except
To prevent harm or difficulty.
A reduction in cost or expenditure.

examples.en: The shift of the supplier gave us a saving of 10 percent.
examples.zh: 供應商的轉變為我們節省了 10%。

meanings.definition: That saves someone from damnation; redemptive.

examples.en: a saving cook
examples.zh: 一個節約的廚師

meanings.definition: With the exception of; except; save.
A ladder; a series of steps; a means of ascending.

examples.en: Please rate your experience on a scale from 1 to 10.
examples.zh: 請按照 1 到 10 的等級對您的體驗進行評分。

meanings.definition: To change the size of something whilst maintaining proportion; especially to change a process in order to produce much larger amounts of the final product.

examples.en: We should scale that up by a factor of 10.
examples.zh: 我們應該將其擴大 10 倍。

meanings.definition: Close investigation.

examples.en: The operators vacated the room during the scan.
examples.zh: 掃描期間操作員離開房間。

meanings.definition: To examine sequentially, carefully, or critically; to scrutinize; to behold closely.

examples.en: She scanned the passage carefully but could not find what she was looking for.
examples.zh: 她仔細地瀏覽這段文字，卻找不到她要找的東西。

meanings.definition: An incident or event that disgraces or damages the reputation of the persons or organization involved.

examples.en: Their affair was reported as a scandal by most tabloids.
examples.zh: 他們的戀情被大多數小報報道為醜聞。

meanings.definition: To treat opprobriously; to defame; to slander.
Uncommon, rare; difficult to find; insufficient to meet a demand.
Scarcely, only just.
A minor fright.

examples.en: Johnny had a bad scare last night.
examples.zh: 約翰尼昨晚嚇壞了。

meanings.definition: The act of scattering or dispersing.
To (cause to) separate and go in different directions; to disperse.

examples.en: The crowd scattered in terror.
examples.zh: 眾人驚恐四散。

meanings.definition: The location of an event that attracts attention.

examples.en: the scene of the crime
examples.zh: 犯罪現場

meanings.definition: To exhibit as a scene; to make a scene of; to display.
A slip of paper; a short note.

examples.en: schedule of tribes
examples.zh: 部落時間表

meanings.definition: To create a time-schedule.

examples.en: I'll schedule you for three-o'clock then.
examples.zh: 那我就幫你安排下午三點。

meanings.definition: A systematic plan of future action.
To plot, or contrive a plan.
A student; one who studies at school or college, typically having a scholarship.
A grant-in-aid to a student.
To attend an institution on a scholarship.
A particular discipline or branch of learning, especially one dealing with measurable or systematic principles rather than intuition or natural ability.

examples.en: Of course in my opinion Social Studies is more of a science than an art.
examples.zh: 當然，在我看來，社會研究與其說是一門藝術，不如說是一門科學。

meanings.definition: To cause to become versed in science; to make skilled; to instruct.
Of, or having to do with science.

One whose activities make use of the scientific method to answer questions regarding the measurable universe. A scientist may be involved in original research, or make use of the results of the research of others.
One blade on a pair of scissors.
To cut using, or as if using, scissors.

examples.en: The erroneous testimony was scissored from the record.
examples.zh: 錯誤的證詞已從記錄中刪除。

meanings.definition: The breadth, depth or reach of a subject; a domain.

examples.en: the scope of an adverb
examples.zh: 副詞的範圍

meanings.definition: To perform a cursory investigation of; scope out.

examples.en: The surgeon will scope the football player's knee to repair damage to a ligament.
examples.zh: 外科醫生將檢查足球員的膝蓋以修復韌帶損傷。

meanings.definition: The total number of goals, points, runs, etc. earned by a participant in a game.

examples.en: The player with the highest score is the winner.
examples.zh: 得分最高的玩家就是勝利者。

meanings.definition: To cut a notch or a groove in a surface.

examples.en: The baker scored the cake so that the servers would know where to slice it.
examples.zh: 麵包師傅在蛋糕上打了記號，以便服務生知道在哪裡切蛋糕。

meanings.definition: Acknowledgement of success
Contempt or disdain.
To feel or display contempt or disdain for something or somebody; to despise.

examples.en: He scorned her romantic advances.
examples.zh: 他蔑視她的浪漫追求。

meanings.definition: A person sent out to gain and bring in tidings; especially, one employed in war to gain information about the enemy and ground.
To explore a wide terrain, as if on a search; to reconnoiter.
A rush or hurry, especially making use of the limbs against a surface.

examples.en: a last-minute scramble to the finish line
examples.zh: 最後一刻衝向終點線

meanings.definition: To move hurriedly to a location, especially by using all limbs against a surface.

examples.en: I scrambled some eggs with spinach and cheese.
examples.zh: 我用菠菜和起司炒了一些雞蛋。

meanings.definition: Shouted when something desirable is thrown into a group of people who individually want that item, causing them to rush for it.
A (small) piece; a fragment; a detached, incomplete portion.

examples.en: I found a scrap of cloth to patch the hole.
examples.zh: 我找了一塊布來修補這個洞。

meanings.definition: To discard.
A disruption, mark or shallow cut on a surface made by scratching.

examples.en: Her skin was covered with tiny scratches.
examples.zh: 她的皮膚上佈滿了細小的抓痕。

meanings.definition: To rub a surface with a sharp object, especially by a living creature to remove itching with nails, claws, etc.

examples.en: Could you please scratch my back?
examples.zh: 你能幫我搔搔背嗎？

meanings.definition: For or consisting of preliminary or tentative, incomplete, etc. work.

examples.en: This is scratch paper, so go ahead and scribble whatever you want on it.
examples.zh: 這是草稿紙，所以請繼續在上面寫下您想要的任何內容。

meanings.definition: A physical divider intended to block an area from view, or provide shelter from something dangerous.

examples.en: a fire screen
examples.zh: 防火螢幕

meanings.definition: To filter by passing through a screen.

examples.en: Mary screened the beans to remove the clumps of gravel.
examples.zh: 瑪莉篩選豆子，除去碎石塊。

meanings.definition: A device that has a helical function.

examples.en: the skeleton screw (Caprella);  the sand screw
examples.zh: 骨架螺絲（Caprella）；  砂螺絲

meanings.definition: To connect or assemble pieces using a screw.
A writing; a written document.
To make or write a script.
A roll of paper or parchment; a writing formed into a roll.

examples.en: I ordered a glass of lemonade and a coffee scroll.
examples.zh: 我點了一杯檸檬水和一份咖啡捲。

meanings.definition: To change one's view of data on a computer's display, typically using a scroll bar or a scroll wheel to move in gradual increments.

examples.en: She scrolled the offending image out of view.
examples.zh: 她把那張令人不快的圖片滾到了視線之外。

meanings.definition: One who labors hard and lives meanly; a mean fellow.

examples.en: What a scrub! Instead of washing the dishes she put the used food on her face!
examples.zh: 多麼磨砂啊！她沒有洗碗，而是把用過的食物放在臉上！

meanings.definition: Mean; dirty; contemptible; scrubby.
To examine something with great care or detail, as to look for hidden or obscure flaws.

examples.en: to scrutinize the conduct or motives of individuals
examples.zh: 檢視個人的行為或動機

meanings.definition: A pinniped (Pinnipedia), particularly an earless seal (true seal) or eared seal.

examples.en: The seals in the harbor looked better than they smelled.
examples.zh: 港口裡的海豹看起來比聞起來好。

meanings.definition: To hunt seals.

examples.en: They're organizing a protest against sealing.
examples.zh: 他們正在組織一場反對封鎖的抗議活動。

meanings.definition: An attempt to find something.

examples.en: With only five minutes until we were meant to leave, the search for the keys started in earnest.
examples.zh: 距離我們離開還有五分鐘，尋找鑰匙的工作就正式開始了。

meanings.definition: To look in (a place) for something.

examples.en: I searched the garden for the keys and found them in the vegetable patch.
examples.zh: 我在花園裡尋找鑰匙，在菜園裡找到了它們。

meanings.definition: Each of the four divisions of a year: spring, summer, autumn (fall) and winter

examples.en: mating season
examples.zh: 交配季節

meanings.definition: To make fit for any use by time or habit; to habituate; to accustom; to inure.

examples.en: to season oneself to a climate
examples.zh: 使自己適應氣候

meanings.definition: Something to be sat upon.
To put an object into a place where it will rest; to fix; to set firm.

examples.en: Be sure to seat the gasket properly before attaching the cover.
examples.zh: 在安裝蓋子之前，請務必正確固定墊圈。

meanings.definition: Something that is number two in a series.

examples.en: They were discounted because they contained blemishes, nicks or were otherwise factory seconds.
examples.zh: 它們之所以被打折，是因為它們有瑕疵、划痕或工廠次品。

meanings.definition: To agree as a second person to (a proposal), usually to reach a necessary quorum of two. (See under #Etymology 3 for translations.)

examples.en: I second the motion.
examples.zh: 我附議該動議。

meanings.definition: Number-two; following after the first one with nothing between them. The ordinal number corresponding to the cardinal number two.

examples.en: He lives on Second Street.
examples.zh: 他住在第二街。

meanings.definition: (with superlative) After the first; at the second rank.

examples.en: Saturn is the second largest planet.
examples.zh: 土星是第二大行星。

meanings.definition: Any flight feather attached to the ulna (forearm) of a bird.

examples.en: the secondary, or undersheriff, of the city of London
examples.zh: 倫敦市的二級警長或副警長

meanings.definition: Next in order to the first or primary; of second place in origin, rank, etc.

examples.en: the work of secondary hands
examples.zh: 輔助手的工作

meanings.definition: A piece of knowledge that is hidden and intended to be kept hidden.

examples.en: "Can you keep a secret?" "Yes." "So can I."
examples.zh: “你能保守秘密嗎？” “是的。” “我也可以。”

meanings.definition: To make or keep secret.

examples.en: He was so scared for his safety he secreted arms around the house.
examples.zh: 他非常擔心自己的安全，於是用雙臂圍住了房子。

meanings.definition: Being or kept hidden.

examples.en: We went down a secret passage.
examples.zh: 我們沿著一條秘密通道往下走。

meanings.definition: Someone entrusted with a secret; a confidant.

examples.en: Ban Ki-Moon was a secretary general of the United Nations.
examples.zh: 潘基文曾任聯合國秘書長。

meanings.definition: To serve as a secretary of.
A cutting; a part cut out from the rest of something.
To cut, divide or separate into pieces.
Section

examples.en: public sector;  private sector
examples.zh: 公部門；  私部門

meanings.definition: To make safe; to relieve from apprehensions of, or exposure to, danger; to guard; to protect.

examples.en: to secure a creditor against loss; to secure a debt by a mortgage
examples.zh: 確保債權人免受損失；透過抵押貸款擔保債務

meanings.definition: Free from attack or danger; protected.

examples.en: secure of a welcome
examples.zh: 確保受到歡迎

meanings.definition: The condition of not being threatened, especially physically, psychologically, emotionally, or financially.
The operation of navigating through a stream.
To try to find; to look for; to search for.

examples.en: I seek wisdom.
examples.zh: 我尋求智慧。

meanings.definition: To appear; to look outwardly; to be perceived as.

examples.en: He seems to be ill.   Her eyes seem blue.   It seems like it is going to rain later.   How did she seem to you?
examples.zh: 他好像病了。   她的眼睛看起來是藍色的。   看來稍後要下雨了。   你覺得她怎麼樣？

meanings.definition: A length of some object.

examples.en: a segment of rope
examples.zh: 一段繩子

meanings.definition: To divide into segments or sections.

examples.en: Segment the essay by topic.
examples.zh: 依主題對文章進行分段。

meanings.definition: To deliberately take hold of; to grab or capture.

examples.en: to seize a ship after libeling
examples.zh: 誹謗後扣押一艘船

meanings.definition: Rare; infrequent.
Infrequently, rarely.

examples.en: They seldom come here now.
examples.zh: 他們現在很少來這裡了。

meanings.definition: To choose one or more elements of a set, especially a set of options.

examples.en: He looked over the menu, and selected the roast beef.
examples.zh: 他看了看菜單，選擇了烤牛肉。

meanings.definition: Privileged, specially selected.

examples.en: Only a select few were allowed into the premiere.
examples.zh: 只有少數人被允許參加首映。

meanings.definition: A process by which heritable traits conferring survival and reproductive advantage to individuals, or related individuals, tend to be passed on to succeeding generations and become more frequent in a population, whereas other less favourable traits tend to become eliminated; the differential survival and reproduction of phenotypes.
A class held for advanced studies in which students meet regularly to discuss original research, under the guidance of a professor.
In some bicameral legislative systems, the upper house or chamber.
The rising motion of water as a wave passes; a surge; the upward angular displacement of a vessel, opposed to pitch, the correlative downward movement.
To make something (such as an object or message) go from one place to another.

examples.en: Every day at two o'clock, he sends his secretary out to buy him a coffee.
examples.zh: 每天兩點，他都會派秘書出去買咖啡給他。

meanings.definition: An old person.

examples.en: He was four years her senior.
examples.zh: 他比她大四歲。

meanings.definition: Older; superior

examples.en: senior citizen
examples.zh: 老年人

meanings.definition: A physical feeling or perception from something that comes into contact with the body; something sensed.
Any of the manners by which living beings perceive the physical world: for humans sight, smell, hearing, touch, taste.

examples.en: a sense of security
examples.zh: 安全感

meanings.definition: To use biological senses: to either see, hear, smell, taste, or feel.

examples.en: She immediately sensed her disdain.
examples.zh: 她立刻就感受到了她的不屑。

meanings.definition: A person with a paranormal sensitivity to something that most cannot perceive.
Having the faculty of sensation; pertaining to the senses.

examples.en: Max is very sensitive; he cried today because of the bad news.
examples.zh: 麥克斯非常敏感；他今天因為這個壞消息而哭了。

meanings.definition: The decision or judgement of a jury or court; a verdict.

examples.en: The court returned a sentence of guilt in the first charge, but innocence in the second.
examples.zh: 法院對第一項指控作出有罪判決，但對第二項指控作出無罪判決。

meanings.definition: To declare a sentence on a convicted person; to doom; to condemn to punishment.

examples.en: The judge sentenced the embezzler to ten years in prison, along with a hefty fine.
examples.zh: 法官判處貪污犯十年監禁，並處巨額罰款。

meanings.definition: A general thought, feeling, or sense.

examples.en: The sentiment emerged that we were acting too soon.
examples.zh: 人們開始認為我們行動得太早了。

meanings.definition: (usually in the plural) Anything that is sold by itself, especially an article of clothing.
To divide (a thing) into separate parts.

examples.en: Separate the articles from the headings.
examples.zh: 將文章與標題分開。

meanings.definition: Apart from (the rest); not connected to or attached to (anything else).

examples.en: This chair can be disassembled into five separate pieces.
examples.zh: 這張椅子可以拆成五個獨立的部分。

meanings.definition: A set of things next to each other in a set order; a series

examples.en: Complete the listed tasks in sequence.
examples.zh: 按順序完成列出的任務。

meanings.definition: To arrange in an order
A number of things that follow on one after the other or are connected one after the other.

examples.en: A series of seemingly inconsequential events led cumulatively to the fall of the company.
examples.zh: 一系列看似無關緊要的事件累積起來導致了公司的垮台。

meanings.definition: Connected one after the other in a circuit.

examples.en: You have to connect the lights in series for them to work properly.
examples.zh: 您必須將燈串聯起來才能正常工作。

meanings.definition: Without humor or expression of happiness; grave in manner or disposition

examples.en: It was a surprise to see the captain, who had always seemed so serious, laugh so heartily.
examples.zh: 看到一向嚴肅的船長笑得這麼開心，真是讓人驚訝。

meanings.definition: In a serious manner; seriously.
One who is hired to perform regular household or other duties, and receives compensation. As opposed to a slave.

examples.en: There are three servants in the household, the butler and two maids.
examples.zh: 家裡一共有三個僕人，管家和兩個女僕。

meanings.definition: To subject.
An act of putting the ball or shuttlecock in play in various games.

examples.en: Whose serve is it?
examples.zh: 是誰的發球？

meanings.definition: (personal) To provide a service (or, by extension, a product, especially food or drink).

examples.en: I've received a summons for jury duty. It says I serve one day or one trial.
examples.zh: 我收到了擔任陪審員的傳票。上面寫著我服刑一天或試用一次。

meanings.definition: An act of being of assistance to someone.

examples.en: I say I did him a service by ending our relationship – now he can freely pursue his career.
examples.zh: 我說我結束了我們的關係，幫了他一個忙──現在他可以自由地追求他的事業了。

meanings.definition: To serve.

examples.en: They service the customer base.
examples.zh: 他們為客戶群提供服務。

meanings.definition: An informal gathering of musicians to play music, especially improvised jazz or a similar genre.
To hold or participate in a jam session with other musicians.
To put (something) down, to rest.

examples.en: Set the tray there.
examples.zh: 將托盤放在那裡。

meanings.definition: To put (something) down, to rest.

examples.en: Set the tray there.
examples.zh: 將托盤放在那裡。

meanings.definition: The time, place and circumstance in which something (such as a story or picture) is set; context; scenario.

examples.en: the setting of the sun
examples.zh: 太陽下山

meanings.definition: That disappears below the horizon

examples.en: the setting sun
examples.zh: 夕陽

meanings.definition: To conclude or resolve (something):

examples.en: to settle her estate
examples.zh: 解決她的遺產

meanings.definition: The act of settling.
An area of land in private ownership (as opposed to common land).
By itself; severally.
Very bad or intense.

examples.en: a severe taskmaster
examples.zh: 嚴厲的監工

meanings.definition: To use a needle to pass thread repeatedly through (pieces of fabric) in order to join them together.

examples.en: Balls were first made of grass or leaves held together by strings, and later of pieces of animal skin sewn together and stuffed with feathers or hay.
examples.zh: 球最初是用草或樹葉用繩子綁在一起製成的，後來又用動物皮片縫合在一起並填充羽毛或乾草製成。

meanings.definition: Darkness where light, particularly sunlight, is blocked.

examples.en: The old oak tree gave shade in the heat of the day.
examples.zh: 這棵老橡樹在炎熱的天氣裡提供了陰涼。

meanings.definition: A dark image projected onto a surface where light (or other radiation) is blocked by the shade of an object.

examples.en: My shadow lengthened as the sun began to set.
examples.zh: 當太陽開始落山時，我的影子拉長了。

meanings.definition: To shade, cloud or darken.

examples.en: The artist chose to shadow this corner of the painting.
examples.zh: 藝術家選擇對畫作的這個角落進行陰影處理。

meanings.definition: Unofficial, informal, unauthorized, but acting as though it were.

examples.en: The human resources department has a shadow information technology group without headquarters knowledge.
examples.zh: 人力資源部有一個總部不知情的影子資訊科技小組。

meanings.definition: The act of shaking or being shaken; tremulous or back-and-forth motion.

examples.en: She replied in the negative, with a shake of her head.
examples.zh: 她搖搖頭，給出否定的回答。

meanings.definition: To cause (something) to move rapidly in opposite directions alternatingly.

examples.en: He shook the can of soda for thirty seconds before delivering it to me, so that, when I popped it open, soda went everywhere.
examples.zh: 他把汽水罐搖晃了三十秒，然後把它遞給我，這樣，當我打開它時，汽水到處都是。

meanings.definition: A shallow portion of an otherwise deep body of water.

examples.en: The ship ran aground in an unexpected shallow.
examples.zh: 船在一個意想不到的淺灘擱淺了。

meanings.definition: To make or become less deep.
Having little depth; significantly less deep than wide.

examples.en: Saute the onions in a shallow pan.
examples.zh: 在淺鍋中炒洋蔥。

meanings.definition: Uncomfortable or painful feeling due to recognition or consciousness of one's own impropriety or dishonor or something being exposed that should have been kept private.

examples.en: The teenager couldn’t bear the shame of introducing his parents.
examples.zh: 少年無法忍受介紹自己父母的恥辱。

meanings.definition: A cry of admonition for the subject of a speech, either to denounce the speaker or to agree with the speaker's denunciation of some person or matter; often used reduplicated, especially in political debates.

examples.en: Shame, you poor thing, you must be cold!
examples.zh: 真可惜，你這個可憐的東西，你一定很冷吧！

meanings.definition: The status or condition of something

examples.en: The used bookshop wouldn't offer much due to the poor shape of the book.
examples.zh: 由於書的形狀很差，二手書店不會提供太多東西。

meanings.definition: To create or make.

examples.en: Earth was shapen by God for God's folk.
examples.zh: 地球是上帝為上帝的子民所創造的。

meanings.definition: A portion of something, especially a portion given or allotted to someone.

examples.en: Upload media from the browser or directly to the file share.
examples.zh: 從瀏覽器上傳媒體或直接上傳到檔案共用。

meanings.definition: To give part of what one has to somebody else to use or consume.

examples.en: to share a shelter with another;  They share a language.
examples.zh: 與他人共用一個庇護所；  他們共享一種語言。

meanings.definition: One who owns shares of stock in a corporation.

examples.en: Shareholders are the real owners of a publicly traded business, but management runs it.
examples.zh: 股東是上市企業的真正所有者，但管理層負責經營該企業。

meanings.definition: The symbol ♯, placed after the name of a note in the key signature or before a note on the staff to indicate that the note is to be played a semitone higher.

examples.en: The pitch pipe sounded out a perfect F♯ (F sharp).
examples.zh: 音管發出完美的F♯（升F）音。

meanings.definition: To raise the pitch of a note half a step making a natural note a sharp.

examples.en: That new musician must be tone deaf: he sharped half the notes of the song!
examples.zh: 那位新音樂家一定是五音不全：他把這首歌的一半音符調高了！

meanings.definition: Terminating in a point or edge, especially one that can cut easily; not obtuse or rounded.

examples.en: A face with sharp features
examples.zh: 一張五官稜角分明的臉

meanings.definition: To a point or edge; piercingly; eagerly; sharply.

examples.en: I'll see you at twelve o'clock sharp.
examples.zh: 我會在十二點整見。

meanings.definition: A fragment of anything shattered.

examples.en: to break a glass into shatters
examples.zh: 將玻璃打碎

meanings.definition: To violently break something into pieces.

examples.en: The miners used dynamite to shatter rocks.
examples.zh: 礦工們使用炸藥來炸碎岩石。

meanings.definition: To make bald or shorter by using a tool such as a razor or pair of electric clippers to cut the hair close to the skin.

examples.en: The labourer with the bending scythe is seen / Shaving the surface of the waving green.
examples.zh: 可以看到拿著彎鐮刀的勞動者/正在修剪波浪狀果嶺的表面。

meanings.definition: A cutting tool similar to scissors, but often larger.
To cut, originally with a sword or other bladed weapon, now usually with shears, or as if using shears.
To part, separate or divide.

examples.en: A metal comb shed her golden hair.
examples.zh: 一把金屬梳子梳理著她金色的頭髮。

meanings.definition: A thin bed cloth used as a covering for a mattress or as a layer over the sleeper.

examples.en: Use the sheets in the hall closet to make the bed.
examples.zh: 使用大廳壁櫥裡的床單鋪床。

meanings.definition: To cover or wrap with cloth, or paper, or other similar material.

examples.en: Remember to sheet the floor before you start painting.
examples.zh: 記得在開始粉刷前先鋪好地板。

meanings.definition: A flat, rigid structure, fixed at right angles to a wall or forming a part of a cabinet, desk etc., and used to support, store or display objects.

examples.en: a shelf of videos
examples.zh: 一個視訊架

meanings.definition: A hard external covering of an animal.

examples.en: The restaurant served caramelized onion shells.
examples.zh: 餐廳供應焦糖洋蔥殼。

meanings.definition: To remove the outer covering or shell of something.

examples.en: Nuts shell in falling.
examples.zh: 堅果殼掉落。

meanings.definition: A refuge, haven or other cover or protection from something.
To provide cover from damage or harassment; to shield; to protect.

examples.en: During the rainstorm, we sheltered under a tree.
examples.zh: 暴雨期間，我們躲在一棵樹下。

meanings.definition: A modifier key whose main function is shifting between two or more functions of any of certain other keys (usually by pressing Shift and the other button simultaneously).
(sometimes figurative) To move from one place to another; to redistribute.

examples.en: We'll have to shift these boxes to the downtown office.
examples.zh: 我們得把這些箱子搬到市中心的辦公室。

meanings.definition: Brightness from a source of light.

examples.en: She's certainly taken a shine to you.
examples.zh: 她肯定對你很有興趣。

meanings.definition: To emit light.

examples.en: My nephew tried other sports before deciding on football, which he shone at right away, quickly becoming the star of his school team.
examples.zh: 我的侄子在決定選擇足球之前嘗試過其他運動，他很快就在足球中表現出色，很快就成為學校球隊的明星。

meanings.definition: A water-borne vessel generally larger than a boat.
A load of goods that is transported by any method (not just by ship)
The transportation of goods.

examples.en: The shipping' is included in the quoted price.
examples.zh: 運費已包含在報價中。

meanings.definition: An article of clothing that is worn on the upper part of the body, and often has sleeves, either long or short, that cover the arms.

examples.en: It can take a while to learn how to iron a shirt properly.
examples.zh: 學習如何正確熨燙襯衫可能需要一段時間。

meanings.definition: The act of shivering.

examples.en: A shiver went up my spine.
examples.zh: 我的脊背一陣顫抖。

meanings.definition: To tremble or shake, especially when cold or frightened.

examples.en: They stood outside for hours, shivering in the frosty air.
examples.zh: 他們在外面站了幾個小時，在寒冷的空氣中瑟瑟發抖。

meanings.definition: A sudden, heavy impact.

examples.en: The train hit the buffers with a great shock.
examples.zh: 火車猛地撞到了緩衝器。

meanings.definition: To cause to be emotionally shocked, to cause (someone) to feel surprised and upset.

examples.en: The disaster shocked the world.
examples.zh: 這場災難震驚了全世界。

meanings.definition: A protective covering for the foot, with a bottom part composed of thick leather or plastic sole and often a thicker heel, and a softer upper part made of leather or synthetic material. Shoes generally do not extend above the ankle, as opposed to boots, which do.

examples.en: Get your shoes on now, or you'll be late for school.
examples.zh: 現在穿上鞋子，不然你上學就要遲到了。

meanings.definition: To put shoes on one's feet.

examples.en: The billiard cue stick was shod in silver.
examples.zh: 撞球桿的鞋底是銀色的。

meanings.definition: The emerging stem and embryonic leaves of a new plant.
To launch a projectile.

examples.en: The land shoots into a promontory.
examples.zh: 陸地伸向海角。

meanings.definition: An establishment that sells goods or services to the public; originally only a physical location, but now a virtual establishment as well.

examples.en: This is where I do my weekly shop.
examples.zh: 這是我每週購物的地方。

meanings.definition: To visit stores or shops to browse or explore merchandise, especially with the intention of buying such merchandise.

examples.en: He’s shopping for clothes.
examples.zh: 他正在買衣服。

meanings.definition: Used to attract the services of a shop assistant
Land adjoining a non-flowing body of water, such as an ocean, lake or pond.

examples.en: lake shore;  bay shore;  gulf shore;  island shore;  mainland shore;  river shore;  estuary shore;  pond shore;  sandy shore;  rocky shore
examples.zh: 湖岸；  海灣海岸；  海灣沿岸；  島岸；  大陸海岸；  河岸；  河口岸；  池塘岸邊；  沙灘；  岩石海岸

meanings.definition: To set on shore.
A short circuit.

examples.en: 38 short suits fit me right off the rack.
examples.zh: 38 件短褲套裝現成的就很適合我。

meanings.definition: To cause a short circuit in (something).

examples.en: This is the third time I’ve caught them shorting us.
examples.zh: 這是我第三次發現他們做空我們。

meanings.definition: Having a small distance from one end or edge to another, either horizontally or vertically.

examples.en: Our meeting was a short six minutes today. Every day for the past month it’s been at least twenty minutes long.
examples.zh: 今天我們的會議只有短短的六分鐘。過去一個月的每一天都至少有二十分鐘長。

meanings.definition: Abruptly, curtly, briefly.

examples.en: He cut me short repeatedly in the meeting.
examples.zh: 他在會議上多次打斷我的話。

meanings.definition: Deficient in.

examples.en: He's short common sense.
examples.zh: 他缺乏常識。

meanings.definition: A lack or deficiency; an insufficient amount.
In a short or brief time or manner; quickly.

examples.en: Ideas are generally expressed more shortly in verse than in prose
examples.zh: 詩歌中表達的思想通常比散文短

meanings.definition: To launch a projectile.

examples.en: The land shoots into a promontory.
examples.zh: 陸地伸向海角。

meanings.definition: The result of launching a projectile or bullet.

examples.en: The shot was wide off the mark.
examples.zh: 射門偏離目標。

meanings.definition: Worn out or broken.

examples.en: The rear axle will have to be replaced. It’s shot.
examples.zh: 後軸必須更換。已拍攝。

meanings.definition: The part of an animal's body between the base of the neck and forearm socket.
To push (a person or thing) using one's shoulder.

examples.en: shoulder the blame
examples.zh: 承擔責任

meanings.definition: A loud burst of voice or voices; a violent and sudden outcry, especially that of a multitude expressing joy, triumph, exultation, anger, or animated courage.

examples.en: Next up the new single from Beyoncé, but first a shout to Barry Bloggins and his wife Belinda...
examples.zh: 接下來是碧昂絲的新單曲，但首先要向巴里·布洛金斯和他的妻子貝琳達喊一聲…

meanings.definition: To utter a sudden and loud cry, as in joy, triumph, exultation or anger, or to attract attention, to animate others, etc.

examples.en: They shouted his name to get his attention.
examples.zh: 他們大聲喊他的名字以引起他的注意。

meanings.definition: A play, dance, or other entertainment.

examples.en: art show;  dog show
examples.zh: 藝術展；  狗展

meanings.definition: To display, to have somebody see (something).

examples.en: All he had to show for four years of attendance at college was a framed piece of paper.
examples.zh: 他所需要證明的只是一張鑲在鏡框裡的紙片，證明他在大學就讀了四年。

meanings.definition: A brief fall of precipitation (spell of rain, or a similar fall of snow, sleet, or cascade).

examples.en: Today there will be frequent showers and some sunny spells.
examples.zh: 今天將會有頻繁的陣雨和一些晴朗的天氣。

meanings.definition: (followed by with) To spray with (a specified liquid).
Showing clever resourcefulness in practical matters.

examples.en: a shrewd guess
examples.zh: 精明的猜測

meanings.definition: Shrinkage; contraction; recoil.

examples.en: My shrink said that he was an enabler, bad for me.
examples.zh: 我的心理醫生說他是個推動者，對我不利。

meanings.definition: To cause to become smaller.

examples.en: The dryer shrank my sweater.
examples.zh: 烘乾機使我的毛衣縮水了。

meanings.definition: A lifting of the shoulders to signal indifference or a casual lack of knowledge.

examples.en: He dismissed my comment with a shrug.
examples.zh: 他聳聳肩駁斥了我的評論。

meanings.definition: To raise (the shoulders) to express uncertainty, lack of concern, (formerly) dread, etc.

examples.en: I asked him for an answer and he just shrugged.
examples.zh: 我問他答案，他只是聳聳肩。

meanings.definition: Closed, shut.

examples.en: a close alley; close quarters
examples.zh: 一條小巷；近距離

meanings.definition: The act or time of shutting; close.

examples.en: the shut of a door
examples.zh: 一扇門關上

meanings.definition: To close, to stop from being open.

examples.en: Please shut the door.
examples.zh: 請關上門。

meanings.definition: The part of a loom that carries the woof back and forth between the warp threads.
To go back and forth between two places.
An act of throwing.

examples.en: coconut shy
examples.zh: 椰子害羞

meanings.definition: To avoid due to timidness or caution.

examples.en: I shy away from investment opportunities I don't understand.
examples.zh: 我迴避我不了解的投資機會。

meanings.definition: Easily frightened; timid.

examples.en: He is very shy with strangers.
examples.zh: 他對陌生人非常害羞。

meanings.definition: Sick people in general as a group.

examples.en: We have to cure the sick.
examples.zh: 我們必須治愈病人。

meanings.definition: To vomit.

examples.en: I woke up at 4 am and sicked on the floor.
examples.zh: 我凌晨 4 點醒來，暈倒在地板上。

meanings.definition: Having an urge to vomit.

examples.en: She was sick all day with the flu.
examples.zh: 她因流感而病了一整天。

meanings.definition: A bounding straight edge of a two-dimensional shape.

examples.en: A square has four sides.
examples.zh: 正方形有四個邊。

meanings.definition: To ally oneself, be in an alliance, usually with "with" or rarely "in with"

examples.en: Which will you side with, good or evil?
examples.zh: 你會站在正義這一邊還是邪惡一邊？

meanings.definition: Being on the left or right, or toward the left or right; lateral.

examples.en: a side issue; a side view or remark
examples.zh: 一個附帶問題；側視圖或評論

meanings.definition: (in the singular) The ability to see.

examples.en: He is losing his sight and now can barely read.
examples.zh: 他的視力正在喪失，現在幾乎無法閱讀。

meanings.definition: To register visually.

examples.en: to sight land from a ship
examples.zh: 從船上觀察陸地

meanings.definition: To go sightseeing; to visit places of interest in a city, town or geographical area.
The activity of going out looking at things; tourism.

examples.en: Sightseeing is a frequent reason to visit San Francisco.
examples.zh: 觀光是造訪舊金山的常見原因。

meanings.definition: (sometimes also used uncountably) A visible indication.

examples.en: I gave them a thumbs-up sign.
examples.zh: 我向他們豎起了大拇指。

meanings.definition: A sequence of states representing an encoded message in a communication channel.

examples.en: My mobile phone can't get a signal in the railway station.
examples.zh: 我的手機在火車站收不到訊號。

meanings.definition: To indicate; to convey or communicate by a signal.

examples.en: Seeing the flames, he ran to the control room and signalled headquarters.
examples.zh: 看到火焰，他跑到控制室並向總部發出訊號。

meanings.definition: Standing above others in rank, importance, or achievement.

examples.en: a signal exploit; a signal service; a signal act of benevolence
examples.zh: 信號利用；信號服務；仁慈的標誌性行為

meanings.definition: A person's name, written by that person, used as identification or to signify approval of accompanying material, such as a legal contract.

examples.en: Your signature must not exceed 600 pixels.
examples.zh: 您的簽名不得超過 600 像素。

meanings.definition: Distinctive, characteristic, indicative of identity.

examples.en: Rabbit in mustard sauce is my signature dish.
examples.zh: 芥末兔肉是我的招牌菜。

meanings.definition: The extent to which something matters; importance

examples.en: As a juror your opinion is of great significance for the outcome of the trial.
examples.zh: 身為陪審員，您的意見對於審判結果具有重要意義。

meanings.definition: That which has significance; a sign; a token; a symbol.
Signifying something; carrying meaning.

examples.en: a significant word or sound; a significant look
examples.zh: 重要的單字或聲音；一個重要的外觀

meanings.definition: The absence of any sound.

examples.en: When the motor stopped, the silence was almost deafening.
examples.zh: 當馬達停下來時，寂靜得幾乎震耳欲聾。

meanings.definition: To make (someone or something) silent.

examples.en: Can you silence the crowd, so we can start the show?
examples.zh: 你能讓人群安靜下來，這樣我們就可以開始表演了嗎？

meanings.definition: Be silent.

examples.en: Silence! Enough of your insolence!
examples.zh: 沉默！夠了你的無禮！

meanings.definition: That which is silent; a time of silence.
Free from sound or noise; absolutely still; perfectly quiet.

examples.en: The wind is silent.
examples.zh: 風無聲無息。

meanings.definition: A fine fiber excreted by the silkworm or other arthropod (such as a spider).

examples.en: The thread made of silk was barely visible.
examples.zh: 絲線幾乎看不見。

meanings.definition: To remove the silk from (corn).
A silly person.
Laughable or amusing through foolishness or a foolish appearance.
Sillily: in a silly manner.
A lustrous, white, metallic element, atomic number 47, atomic weight 107.87, symbol Ag.
To acquire a silvery colour.

examples.en: to silver a pin;  to silver a glass mirror plate with an amalgam of tin and mercury
examples.zh: 將別針鍍銀；  用錫和汞的混合物將玻璃鏡板鍍銀

meanings.definition: Made from silver.

examples.en: a silver-voiced young girl
examples.zh: 銀嗓少女

meanings.definition: That which is similar to, or resembles, something else, as in quality, form, etc.
Having traits or characteristics in common; alike, comparable.
A herbal preparation made from one plant, as opposed to something made from more than one plant.
To gather simples, i.e. medicinal herbs.
Uncomplicated; taken by itself, with nothing added.
To make simpler, either by reducing in complexity, reducing to component parts, or making easier to understand.
To model, replicate, duplicate the behavior, appearance or properties of.

examples.en: We will use a smoke machine to simulate the fog you will actually encounter.
examples.zh: 我們將使用煙霧機來模擬您實際遇到的霧。

meanings.definition: Feigned; pretended.
Happening at the same moment.
From a specified time in the past.

examples.en: A short/long time since
examples.zh: 很短/很長一段時間以來

meanings.definition: From: referring to a period of time ending in the present and defining it by the point in time at which it started, or the period in which its starting point occurred.
From the time that.

examples.en: I have loved you since I first met you.
examples.zh: 自從第一次見到你我就喜歡你了。

meanings.definition: Genuine; meaning what one says or does; heartfelt.

examples.en: I believe he is sincere in his offer to help.
examples.zh: 我相信他是真誠地願意提供幫助。

meanings.definition: A gathering at which people sing songs.
To produce musical or harmonious sounds with one’s voice.

examples.en: "I really want to sing in the school choir," said Vera.
examples.zh: 「我真的很想在學校合唱團唱歌，」維拉說。

meanings.definition: A 45 RPM vinyl record with one song on side A and one on side B.

examples.en: The Offspring released four singles from their most recent album.
examples.zh: The Offspring 發行了他們最新專輯中的四首單曲。

meanings.definition: To identify or select one member of a group from the others; generally used with out, either to single out or to single (something) out.

examples.en: Eddie singled out his favorite marble from the bag.
examples.zh: 艾迪從包包裡挑出了他最喜歡的大理石。

meanings.definition: Not accompanied by anything else; one in number.

examples.en: Can you give me a single reason not to leave right now?
examples.zh: 你能給我一個現在不離開的理由嗎？

meanings.definition: A basin used for holding water for washing.

examples.en: Jones has a two-seamer with heavy sink.
examples.zh: 瓊斯有一台帶有重型水槽的兩縫機。

meanings.definition: (heading, physical) To move or be moved into something.

examples.en: I have sunk thousands of pounds into this project.
examples.zh: 我已經在這個項目上投入了數千英鎊。

meanings.definition: Sorrow, grief.
The combination of circumstances at a given moment; a state of affairs.

examples.en: The United States is in an awkward situation with debt default looming.
examples.zh: 美國處境尷尬，債務違約迫在眉睫。

meanings.definition: (obsolete outside dialectal) An assize.

examples.en: The size of the building seemed to have increased since I was last there.
examples.zh: 自從我上次來這裡以來，這棟建築的規模似乎有所擴大。

meanings.definition: To adjust the size of; to make a certain size.
A rapidly executed freehand drawing that is not intended as a finished work, often consisting of a multitude of overlapping lines.

examples.en: to keep sketch
examples.zh: 保留草圖

meanings.definition: To make a brief, basic drawing.

examples.en: I usually sketch with a pen rather than a pencil.
examples.zh: 我通常用鋼筆而不是鉛筆素描。

meanings.definition: Sketchy, shady, questionable.
Capacity to do something well; technique, ability. Skills are usually acquired or learned, as opposed to abilities, which are often thought of as innate.
Great, excellent.
Having or showing skill; skillful.
The outer protective layer of the body of any animal, including of a human.

examples.en: He is so disgusting he makes my skin crawl.
examples.zh: 他太噁心了，讓我起雞皮疙瘩。

meanings.definition: To injure the skin of.

examples.en: Can I skin the application to put the picture of my cat on it?
examples.zh: 我可以為應用程式設定皮膚以將我的貓的照片放在上面嗎？

meanings.definition: A leaping, jumping or skipping movement.
To move by hopping on alternate feet.

examples.en: She will skip from one end of the sidewalk to the other.
examples.zh: 她會從人行道的一端跳到另一端。

meanings.definition: An article of clothing, usually worn by women and girls, that hangs from the waist and covers the lower part of the body.
To be on or form the border of.

examples.en: The plain was skirted by rows of trees.
examples.zh: 平原的周圍是一排排的樹木。

meanings.definition: The atmosphere above a given point, especially as visible from the ground during the day.

examples.en: That year, a meteor fell from the sky.
examples.zh: 那一年，一顆流星從天而降。

meanings.definition: To hit, kick or throw (a ball) extremely high.
The part of anything that hangs loose, having no strain upon it.

examples.en: take in the slack
examples.zh: 採取鬆弛措施

meanings.definition: (normally said of a rope) Lax; not tense; not firmly extended.

examples.en: a slack rope
examples.zh: 一條鬆弛的繩子

meanings.definition: Slackly.

examples.en: slack dried hops
examples.zh: 鬆弛的乾啤酒花

meanings.definition: A sudden impact or blow.

examples.en: I don't mean this as a slam, but you can be really impatient sometimes.
examples.zh: 我並不是說這是一個大滿貫，但有時你可能會非常不耐煩。

meanings.definition: To shut with sudden force so as to produce a shock and noise.

examples.en: Don't slam the door!
examples.zh: 別關上門！

meanings.definition: A blow, especially one given with the open hand, or with something broad and flat.
To give a slap to.

examples.en: She slapped him in response to the insult.
examples.zh: 她打了他一巴掌以回應這種侮辱。

meanings.definition: Exactly, precisely

examples.en: He tossed the file down slap in the middle of the table.
examples.zh: 他把文件丟到桌子中間。

meanings.definition: A slashing action or motion, particularly:

examples.en: Slash generated during logging may constitute a fire hazard.
examples.zh: 伐木過程中產生的切痕可能會引發火災。

meanings.definition: To cut or attempt to cut, particularly:

examples.en: The province's traditional slash-and-burn agriculture was only sustainable with a much smaller population.
examples.zh: 該省傳統的刀耕火種農業只有在人口少得多的情況下才能可持續。

meanings.definition: Used to note the sound or action of a slash.
Used to connect two or more identities in a list.
A fine-grained homogeneous sedimentary rock composed of clay or volcanic ash which has been metamorphosed so that it cleaves easily into thin layers.

examples.en: Put it on my slate – I’ll pay you next week.
examples.zh: 把它記在我的記事本上——我下週付錢給你。

meanings.definition: Having the bluish-grey/gray colour of slate.
To rest in a state of reduced consciousness.

examples.en: You should sleep 8 hours a day.
examples.zh: 你每天應該睡8小時。

meanings.definition: The part of a garment that covers the arm.
To fit a sleeve to
Thin; slim.

examples.en: A rod is a long slender pole used for angling.
examples.zh: 魚竿是一種用於釣魚的細長桿。

meanings.definition: That which is thin and broad.

examples.en: Jim was munching on a slice of toast.
examples.zh: 吉姆正在嚼著一片吐司。

meanings.definition: To cut into slices.

examples.en: Slice the cheese thinly.
examples.zh: 將起司切成薄片。

meanings.definition: Having the properties of a slice knot.
An item of play equipment that children can climb up and then slide down again.

examples.en: The long, red slide was great fun for the kids.
examples.zh: 紅色的長滑梯對孩子們來說非常有趣。

meanings.definition: To (cause to) move in continuous contact with a surface

examples.en: He slid the boat across the grass.
examples.zh: 他把船滑過草地。

meanings.definition: The act of slighting; a deliberate act of neglect or discourtesy.
To treat as unimportant or not worthy of attention; to make light of.
Small

examples.en: a slight but graceful woman
examples.zh: 嬌小但優雅的女人

meanings.definition: A type of cigarette substantially longer and thinner than normal cigarettes.

examples.en: I only smoke slims.
examples.zh: 我只抽瘦身煙。

meanings.definition: To lose weight in order to achieve slimness.
Slender, thin.

examples.en: I'm afraid your chances are quite slim.
examples.zh: 恐怕你的機會很小。

meanings.definition: A thin, slippery mix of clay and water.
A catch phrase associated with the product or service being advertised.
An area of ground that tends evenly upward or downward.

examples.en: I had to climb a small slope to get to the site.
examples.zh: 我必須爬一個小斜坡才能到達現場。

meanings.definition: To tend steadily upward or downward.

examples.en: The road slopes sharply down at that point.
examples.zh: 道路在那一點急劇傾斜。

meanings.definition: Sloping.
Slopingly
A broad, flat, wooden bar, a slat, especially as used to secure a door, window, etc.
To bar, bolt or lock a door or window.

examples.en: to slot a door
examples.zh: 給門開槽

meanings.definition: Someone who is slow; a sluggard.
To make (something) run, move, etc. less quickly; to reduce the speed of.
Taking a long time to move or go a short distance, or to perform an action; not quick in motion; proceeding at a low speed.

examples.en: a slow train;  a slow computer
examples.zh: 慢速火車；  一台運轉緩慢的計算機

meanings.definition: Slowly.

examples.en: That clock is running slow.
examples.zh: 那個時鐘走得很慢。

meanings.definition: A dilapidated neighborhood where many people live in a state of poverty.
To visit a neighborhood of a status below one's own.
Any part of something that is smaller or slimmer than the rest, now usually with anatomical reference to the back.
To make little or less.
Not large or big; insignificant; few in number.

examples.en: A small group.
examples.zh: 一個小團體。

meanings.definition: In a small fashion.
To hurt or sting.

examples.en: After being hit with a pitch, the batter exclaimed "Ouch, my arm smarts!"
examples.zh: 擊球手被球擊中後驚呼“哎呀，我的手臂受傷了！”

meanings.definition: The sound of a violent impact; a violent striking together.

examples.en: I could hear the screech of the brakes, then the horrible smash of cars colliding.
examples.zh: 我聽到煞車的刺耳聲，然後是汽車相撞的可怕撞擊聲。

meanings.definition: To break (something brittle) violently.

examples.en: The demolition team smashed the buildings to rubble.
examples.zh: 拆除隊將建築物夷為廢墟。

meanings.definition: A sensation, pleasant or unpleasant, detected by inhaling air (or, the case of water-breathing animals, water) carrying airborne molecules of a substance.

examples.en: I love the smell of fresh bread.
examples.zh: 我喜歡新鮮麵包的味道。

meanings.definition: To sense a smell or smells.

examples.en: I can smell fresh bread.
examples.zh: 我能聞到新鮮麵包的味道。

meanings.definition: A facial expression comprised by flexing the muscles of both ends of one's mouth, often showing the front teeth, without vocalisation, and in humans is a common involuntary or voluntary expression of happiness, pleasure, amusement or anxiety.

examples.en: He always puts a smile on my face.
examples.zh: 他總是把微笑掛在我的臉上。

meanings.definition: To have (a smile) on one's face.

examples.en: I don't know what he's smiling about.
examples.zh: 我不知道他在笑什麼。

meanings.definition: The visible vapor/vapour, gases, and fine particles given off by burning or smoldering material.

examples.en: Can I bum a smoke off you?;  I need to go buy some smokes.
examples.zh: 我可以給你抽煙嗎？  我得去買點煙。

meanings.definition: Of the colour known as smoke.
Something that is smooth, or that goes smoothly and easily.
To make smooth or even.

examples.en: to smooth a person's temper
examples.zh: 緩和一個人的脾氣

meanings.definition: Having a texture that lacks friction. Not rough.

examples.en: We hope for a smooth transition to the new system.
examples.zh: 我們希望能夠順利過渡到新系統。

meanings.definition: Smoothly.
A light meal.
To eat a light meal.
A quick breaking or cracking sound or the action of producing such a sound.

examples.en: We took a few snaps of the old church before moving on.
examples.zh: 在繼續前進之前，我們拍了幾張老教堂的照片。

meanings.definition: To fracture or break apart suddenly.

examples.en: He snapped his stick in anger.
examples.zh: 他憤怒地折斷了手杖。

meanings.definition: Done, made, performed, etc., quickly and unexpectedly, or without deliberation.

examples.en: a snap judgment or decision    a snap political convention
examples.zh: 倉促的判斷或決定   倉促的政治會議

meanings.definition: The cry used in a game of snap when winning a hand.

examples.en: Snap! We've both got pink buckets and spades.
examples.zh: 折斷！我們都有粉紅色的水桶和黑桃。

meanings.definition: A quick grab or catch.

examples.en: The leftfielder makes a nice snatch to end the inning.
examples.zh: 左外野手漂亮地搶奪結束了這一局。

meanings.definition: To grasp and remove quickly.

examples.en: He snatched up the phone.
examples.zh: 他一把奪過電話。

meanings.definition: One who sneaks; one who moves stealthily to acquire an item or information.

examples.en: My little brother is such a sneak; yesterday I caught him trying to look through my diary.
examples.zh: 我的弟弟真是個鬼鬼祟祟的人；昨天我發現他試圖翻看我的日記。

meanings.definition: To creep or go stealthily; to come or go while trying to avoid detection, as a person who does not wish to be seen.

examples.en: He decided to sneak into the kitchen for a second cookie while his mom was on the phone.
examples.zh: 他決定趁媽媽打電話的時候溜進廚房吃第二塊餅乾。

meanings.definition: In advance; before release to the general public.

examples.en: The company gave us a sneak look at their new electronic devices.
examples.zh: 該公司讓我們先睹為快他們的新電子設備。

meanings.definition: The act of soaring.
To fly high with little effort, like a bird.

examples.en: The pump prices soared into new heights as the strike continued.
examples.zh: 隨著罷工的持續，汽油價格飆升至新高。

meanings.definition: A festive gathering to foster introductions.

examples.en: They organized a social at the dance club to get people to know each other.
examples.zh: 他們在舞蹈俱樂部組織了一次社交活動，讓人們互相認識。

meanings.definition: Being extroverted or outgoing.

examples.en: James is a very social guy; he knows lots of people.
examples.zh: 詹姆斯是一個非常善於交際的人。他認識很多人。

meanings.definition: A long-standing group of people sharing cultural aspects such as language, dress, norms of behavior and artistic forms.

examples.en: This society has been known for centuries for its colorful clothing and tight-knit family structure.
examples.zh: 幾個世紀以來，這個社會以其色彩繽紛的服裝和緊密的家庭結構而聞名。

meanings.definition: A knitted or woven covering for the foot.
A soft or foolish person; an idiot.
Easily giving way under pressure.

examples.en: My head sank easily into the soft pillow.
examples.zh: 我的頭輕易地就陷進了柔軟的枕頭裡。

meanings.definition: Softly; without roughness or harshness; gently; quietly.
Be quiet; hold; stop; not so fast.
Encoded computer instructions, usually modifiable (unless stored in some form of unalterable memory such as ROM).
A mixture of mineral particles and organic material, used to support plant growth.

examples.en: Kenyan soil
examples.zh: 肯亞土壤

meanings.definition: Of or pertaining to the sun; proceeding from the sun

examples.en: solar influence
examples.zh: 太陽影響

meanings.definition: (ditransitive) To transfer goods or provide services in exchange for money.

examples.en: I'll sell you all three for a hundred dollars.
examples.zh: 我會把三個都賣給你一百美元。

meanings.definition: A wooden band or yoke put around the neck of an ox or cow in the stall.
Alone; exclusively.

examples.en: The new chef was solely responsible for attending the grill.
examples.zh: 新廚師全權負責參加燒烤活動。

meanings.definition: Solicitation
To persistently endeavor to obtain an object, or bring about an event.

examples.en: to solicit alms, or a favour
examples.zh: 乞討施捨或幫忙

meanings.definition: A substance in the fundamental state of matter that retains its size and shape without need of a container (as opposed to a liquid or gas).

examples.en: I owe him; he did me a solid last year.
examples.zh: 我欠他的；去年他給了我很大的幫助。

meanings.definition: (of an object or substance) That can be picked up or held, having a texture, and usually firm. Unlike a liquid or a gas.

examples.en: Almost all metals are solid at room temperature.
examples.zh: 幾乎所有金屬在室溫下都是固態。

meanings.definition: Solidly.

examples.en: Many long-established compounds are set solid.
examples.zh: 許多歷史悠久的化合物已經固定下來。

meanings.definition: One who lives alone, or in solitude; an anchoret, hermit or recluse.

examples.en: The prisoners who started the riot were moved to solitary.
examples.zh: 引發騷亂的囚犯被轉移到單獨監禁的地方。

meanings.definition: Living or being by oneself; alone; having no companion present

examples.en: a solitary journey
examples.zh: 孤獨的旅程

meanings.definition: The condition of being soluble.
A homogeneous mixture, which may be liquid, gas or solid, formed by dissolving one or more substances.
To treat with a solution.
(chiefly law enforcement) A solution; an explanation.
To find an answer or solution to a problem or question; to work out.
A liquid that dissolves a solid, liquid, or gaseous solute, resulting in a solution.

examples.en: a solvent of mystery
examples.zh: 神秘的溶劑

meanings.definition: Able to pay all debts as they become due, and having no more liabilities than assets.
Sometime; at some unspecified time in the future.
In one way or another; in a way not yet known or explained; by some means

examples.en: This problem has to be tackled somehow.
examples.zh: 這個問題必須以某種方式解決。

meanings.definition: A partially specified but unnamed person.

examples.en: Do you need a gift for that special someone?
examples.zh: 您需要送給那個特別的人一份禮物嗎？

meanings.definition: Some person.

examples.en: Can someone help me, please?
examples.zh: 有人可以幫我嗎？

meanings.definition: An object whose nature is yet to be defined.
Applied to an action whose name is forgotten by, unknown or unimportant to the user, e.g. from words of a song.
Having a characteristic that the speaker cannot specify.
(degree) Somewhat; to a degree.

examples.en: The baby looks something like his father.
examples.zh: 這孩子看起來有點像他的父親。

meanings.definition: An uncertain or unspecified thing; one thing.

examples.en: I have a feeling something good is going to happen today.
examples.zh: 我預感今天會有好事發生。

meanings.definition: Former, erstwhile; at some previous time.

examples.en: my sometime friend and mentor
examples.zh: 我曾經的朋友和導師

meanings.definition: At an indefinite but stated time in the past or future.

examples.en: I'll see you at the pub sometime this evening.
examples.zh: 今晚某個時候我會在酒吧見你。

meanings.definition: More or less; a certain quantity or degree; a part, more or less; something.
(degree) To a limited extent or degree.

examples.en: The crowd was somewhat larger than expected, perhaps due to the good weather.
examples.zh: 可能是因為天氣好的原因，人潮比想像的還要多。

meanings.definition: Something.
Unspecified or unknown (unlocated) place or location.
In an uncertain or unspecified location.

examples.en: I must have left my glasses somewhere.
examples.zh: 我一定是把眼鏡落在什麼地方了。

meanings.definition: Short in length of time from the present.

examples.en: I need the soonest date you have available.
examples.zh: 我需要你能安排的最快日期。

meanings.definition: Immediately, instantly.
To restore to ease, comfort, or tranquility; relieve; calm; quiet; refresh.
To make less natural or innocent.
Having obtained worldly experience, and lacking naiveté; cosmopolitan.
A second-year undergraduate student in a college or university, or a second-year student in a four-year secondary school or high school.

examples.en: She was very mature for a sophomore and had several friends who were juniors or even seniors.
examples.zh: 作為一名大二學生，她已經很成熟了，並且有幾個大三甚至大四的朋友。

meanings.definition: The second in a series, especially, the second of an artist’s albums or the second of four years in a high school (tenth grade) or university.

examples.en: The band’s sophomore album built upon the success of their debut release, catapulting them to megastardom.
examples.zh: 樂團的第二張專輯建立在他們首張專輯的成功基礎上，使他們一躍成為巨星。

meanings.definition: An injured, infected, inflamed or diseased patch of skin.

examples.en: They put ointment and a bandage on the sore.
examples.zh: 他們在疼痛處塗上藥膏並用繃帶包紮。

meanings.definition: To mutilate the legs or feet of (a horse) in order to induce a particular gait.
Causing pain or discomfort; painfully sensitive.

examples.en: Her feet were sore from walking so far.
examples.zh: 走了這麼遠，她的腳已經酸痛了。

meanings.definition: Very, excessively, extremely (of something bad).
Unhappiness, woe

examples.en: Parting is such sweet sorrow.
examples.zh: 離別是多麼甜蜜的悲傷。

meanings.definition: To feel or express grief.
The act of saying sorry; an apology.
(of a person) Regretful for an action; grieved or saddened, especially by the loss of something or someone.

examples.en: I am sorry I stepped on your toes. It was an accident.
examples.zh: 對不起，我踩到你的腳趾了。這是一次意外。

meanings.definition: Expresses regret, remorse, or sorrow.

examples.en: Sorry! I didn't see that you were on the phone.
examples.zh: 對不起！我沒看到你在打電話。

meanings.definition: A general type.

examples.en: I had a sort of my cupboard.
examples.zh: 我有一個類似我的櫥櫃的東西。

meanings.definition: The spirit or essence of a person usually thought to consist of one's thoughts and personality. Often believed to live on after the person's death.

examples.en: Fifty souls were lost when the ship sank.
examples.zh: 船沉沒時，五十人喪生。

meanings.definition: To endow with a soul; to furnish with a soul or mind.
Healthy.

examples.en: He was safe and sound.
examples.zh: 他安然無恙。

meanings.definition: Soundly.
Yes; used to show agreement or understanding, generally without much enthusiasm.
Any of various dishes commonly made by combining liquids, such as water or stock with other ingredients, such as meat and vegetables, that contribute flavor and texture.

examples.en: Pho is a traditional Vietnamese soup.
examples.zh: 河粉是一種傳統的越南湯。

meanings.definition: To feed: to provide with soup or a meal.
The sensation of a sour taste.
To make sour.

examples.en: Too much lemon juice will sour the recipe.
examples.zh: 太多的檸檬汁會讓食譜變酸。

meanings.definition: Having an acidic, sharp or tangy taste.

examples.en: Lemons have a sour taste.
examples.zh: 檸檬有酸味。

meanings.definition: The person, place or thing from which something (information, goods, etc.) comes or is acquired.

examples.en: The accused refused to reveal the source of the illegal drugs she was selling.
examples.zh: 被告拒絕透露她所銷售的非法毒品的來源。

meanings.definition: To obtain or procure: used especially of a business resource.
One of the four major compass points, specifically 180°, directed toward the South Pole, and conventionally downwards on a map, abbreviated as S.
To turn or move toward the south; to veer toward the south.

examples.en: The moon souths at nine.
examples.zh: 九點月亮南行。

meanings.definition: Toward the south; southward.

examples.en: south highway 1
examples.zh: 南1號公路

meanings.definition: Toward the south; southward.
An item of sentimental value, to remember an event or location.
To take (an article) as a souvenir, especially illicitly, for example during wartime.
A monarch; the ruler of a country.
To rule over as a sovereign.
Exercising power of rule.

examples.en: sovereign nation
examples.zh: 主權國家

meanings.definition: (heading) Of time.
To roam, walk, wander.
Having plenty of space; roomy.

examples.en: The apartment has a spacious bedroom.
examples.zh: 公寓設有寬敞的臥室。

meanings.definition: The space from the thumb to the end of the little finger when extended; nine inches; an eighth of a fathom.

examples.en: He has a short attention span and gets bored within minutes.
examples.zh: 他的注意力持續時間很短，幾分鐘內就會感到無聊。

meanings.definition: The act of sparing; moderation; restraint.
Scant; not abundant or plentiful.

examples.en: a spare diet
examples.zh: 節食

meanings.definition: A small particle of glowing matter, either molten or on fire.
To trigger, kindle into activity (an argument, etc).
A little spark; a scintillation.

examples.en: the sparkle of a diamond
examples.zh: 鑽石的光芒

meanings.definition: The house sparrow, Passer domesticus; a small bird with a short bill, and brown, white and gray feathers.
A low class bar, a speakeasy.
To communicate with one's voice, to say words out loud.

examples.en: I was so surprised I couldn't speak.
examples.zh: 我驚訝得說不出話來。

meanings.definition: One who speaks.

examples.en: The company hired a motivational speaker to boost morale.
examples.zh: 該公司聘請了一位勵志演講者來鼓舞士氣。

meanings.definition: A long stick with a sharp tip used as a weapon for throwing or thrusting, or anything used to make a thrusting motion.

examples.en: asparagus and broccoli spears
examples.zh: 蘆筍和西蘭花矛

meanings.definition: To pierce with a spear.
Male.

examples.en: a spear counterpart
examples.zh: 矛對應物

meanings.definition: A reduction in consumer cost (usually for a limited time) for items or services rendered.

examples.en: We're running a special on turkey for Thanksgiving.
examples.zh: 我們正在為感恩節舉辦一場關於火雞的特別活動。

meanings.definition: (nursing) To supervise a patient one-on-one.
Distinguished by a unique or unusual quality.

examples.en: a special episode of a television series
examples.zh: 電視連續劇的特別集

meanings.definition: Someone who is an expert in, or devoted to, some specific branch of study or research.

examples.en: The tree Lepidothamnus laxifolius is a high alpine specialist found in high-altitude bog communities and in scrub.
examples.zh: Lepidothamnus laxifolius 樹是一種高山植物，生長於高海拔沼澤群落和灌木叢中。

meanings.definition: Specialised.
To make distinct or separate, particularly:
Type or kind. (Compare race.)

examples.en: the male species
examples.zh: 雄性物種

meanings.definition: A distinguishing attribute or quality.
Explicit or definite

examples.en: a highly specific test, specific and nonspecific symptoms
examples.zh: 高度特異性的測試、特異性和非特異性症狀

meanings.definition: To state explicitly, or in detail, or as a condition.
An individual instance that represents a class; an example.

examples.en: early specimens of the art of Picasso
examples.zh: 畢卡索藝術的早期標本

meanings.definition: An exciting or extraordinary scene, exhibition, performance etc.

examples.en: The horse race was a thrilling spectacle.
examples.zh: 賽馬真是一場驚心動魄的盛會。

meanings.definition: A spectacular display.
Amazing or worthy of special notice.

examples.en: The parachutists were spectacular.
examples.zh: 跳傘運動員的表演非常精彩。

meanings.definition: One who watches an event; especially, one held outdoors.

examples.en: The cheering spectators watched the fireworks.
examples.zh: 歡呼雀躍的觀眾觀賞了煙火表演。

meanings.definition: A range; a continuous, infinite, one-dimensional set, possibly bounded by extremes.
To think, meditate or reflect on a subject; to consider, to deliberate or cogitate.
The faculty of uttering articulate sounds or words; the ability to speak or to use vocalizations to communicate.

examples.en: It was hard to hear the sounds of his speech over the noise. He had a bad speech impediment.
examples.zh: 在喧鬧聲中很難聽清他的講話聲。他有嚴重的言語障礙。

meanings.definition: To make a speech; to harangue.
The state of moving quickly or the capacity for rapid motion; rapidity.

examples.en: How does Usain Bolt run at that speed?
examples.zh: 尤塞恩·博爾特如何跑得這麼快？

meanings.definition: Words or a formula supposed to have magical powers.

examples.en: He cast a spell to cure warts.
examples.zh: 他施了一個咒語來治癒疣。

meanings.definition: To put under the influence of a spell; to affect by a spell; to bewitch; to fascinate; to charm.
Amount of money spent (during a period); expenditure.

examples.en: I’m sorry, boss, but the advertising spend exceeded the budget again this month.
examples.zh: 對不起，老闆，這個月的廣告支出又超出預算了。

meanings.definition: To pay out (money).

examples.en: He spends far more on gambling than he does on living proper.
examples.zh: 他在賭博上的花費遠遠超過他在正常生活上的花費。

meanings.definition: A regular three-dimensional object in which every cross-section is a circle; the figure described by the revolution of a circle about its diameter .
To place in a sphere, or among the spheres; to ensphere.
Aromatic or pungent plant matter (usually dried) used to season or flavour food.
To add spice or spices to; season.
A mess of something that has been dropped.

examples.en: The bruise is from a bad spill he had last week.
examples.zh: 瘀傷是他上週發生的嚴重洩漏事故造成的。

meanings.definition: To drop something so that it spreads out or makes a mess; to accidentally pour.

examples.en: I spilled some sticky juice on the kitchen floor.
examples.zh: 我把一些黏稠的果汁灑在廚房地板上。

meanings.definition: Rapid circular motion.

examples.en: He put some spin on the cue ball.
examples.zh: 他使母球產生了一些旋轉。

meanings.definition: To rotate, revolve, gyrate (usually quickly); to partially or completely rotate to face another direction.

examples.en: I spun myself around a few times.
examples.zh: 我自己轉了好幾圈。

meanings.definition: The series of bones situated at the back from the head to the pelvis of a person, or from the head to the tail of an animal; backbone, vertebral column.
The soul of a person or other creature. What moves through experience into self-definition as souls purpose.

examples.en: A wandering spirit haunts the island.
examples.zh: 島上有一個遊蕩的靈魂。

meanings.definition: To carry off, especially in haste, secrecy, or mystery.

examples.en: Civil dissensions often spirit the ambition of private men.
examples.zh: 民間紛爭常激發私人的野心。

meanings.definition: A Christian religious song, especially one in an African-American style, or a similar non-religious song.

examples.en: He assigns supremacy to the pope in spirituals, and to the emperor in temporals. — Lowell.
examples.zh: 他賦予教宗在聖事上的至高無上權，以及皇帝在世俗上的至高無上權。 ——洛威爾。

meanings.definition: Of or pertaining to the spirit or the soul.

examples.en: Respect towards ancestors is an essential part of Thai spiritual practice.
examples.zh: 尊重祖先是泰國精神實踐的重要組成部分。

meanings.definition: A thin metal or wooden rod on which meat is skewered for cooking, often over a fire.
To impale on a spit; to pierce with a sharp object.

examples.en: to spit a loin of veal
examples.zh: 吐出小牛腰肉

meanings.definition: Ill will or hatred toward another, accompanied with the desire to irritate, annoy, or thwart; a want to disturb or put out another; mild malice

examples.en: He was so filled with spite for his ex-wife, he could not hold down a job.
examples.zh: 他對前妻充滿怨恨，以至於無法保住工作。

meanings.definition: To treat maliciously; to try to injure or thwart.

examples.en: She soon married again, to spite her ex-husband.
examples.zh: 為了報復她的前夫，她很快又結婚了。

meanings.definition: The sound made by an object hitting a liquid.

examples.en: I heard a splash when the rock landed in the pond.
examples.zh: 當石頭落入池塘時，我聽到撲通一聲。

meanings.definition: To hit or agitate liquid so that part of it separates from the principal liquid mass.

examples.en: sit and splash in the bathtub
examples.zh: 坐在浴缸裡嬉戲

meanings.definition: Possessing or displaying splendor; shining; very bright.

examples.en: a splendid sun
examples.zh: 燦爛的陽光

meanings.definition: A crack or longitudinal fissure.

examples.en: He’s got a nasty split.
examples.zh: 他的分裂很嚴重。

meanings.definition: Of something solid, to divide fully or partly along a more or less straight line.

examples.en: He has split his lip.
examples.zh: 他的嘴唇裂開了。

meanings.definition: Divided.

examples.en: Republicans appear split on the centerpiece of Mr. Obama's economic recovery plan.
examples.zh: 共和黨人在歐巴馬經濟復甦計畫的核心問題上似乎存在分歧。

meanings.definition: (Also in plural: spoils) Plunder taken from an enemy or victim.
To strip (someone who has been killed or defeated) of their arms or armour.

examples.en: Make sure you put the milk back in the fridge, otherwise it will spoil.
examples.zh: 確保將牛奶放回冰箱，否則會變質。

meanings.definition: One who speaks as the voice of a group of people.
A person or organisation with some sort of responsibility for another person or organisation, especially where the responsibility has a religious, legal, or financial aspect.

examples.en: He was my sponsor when I applied to join the club.
examples.zh: 當我申請加入社團時，他是我的贊助人。

meanings.definition: To be a sponsor for.
Self-generated; happening without any apparent external cause.

examples.en: He made a spontaneous offer of help.
examples.zh: 他自發性地提出提供協助。

meanings.definition: An implement for eating or serving; a scooped utensil whose long handle is straight, in contrast to a ladle.
To serve using a spoon; to transfer (something) with a spoon.

examples.en: Sarah spooned some apple sauce onto her plate.
examples.zh: 莎拉用湯匙舀了一些蘋果醬到她的盤子裡。

meanings.definition: Any activity that uses physical exertion or skills competitively under a set of rules that is not based on aesthetics.
