examples.en: You heard the new Rizzle Kicks song? — Tune!
examples.zh: 你聽過 Rizzle Kicks 的新歌嗎？ — 調！

meanings.definition: An underground or underwater passage.
To make a tunnel through or under something; to burrow.
Any of various rotary machines that use the kinetic energy of a continuous stream of fluid (a liquid or a gas) to turn a shaft.
Violently disturbed or agitated; tempestuous, tumultuous

examples.en: It is dangerous to sail in turbulent seas.
examples.zh: 在洶湧的大海中航行是危險的。

meanings.definition: The amount of money taken as sales transacted in a given period.

examples.en: The company had an annual turnover of $500,000.
examples.zh: 該公司的年營業額為 50 萬美元。

meanings.definition: Capable of being turned over; designed to be turned over.

examples.en: a turnover collar
examples.zh: 翻領

meanings.definition: One who teaches another (usually called a student, learner, or tutee) in a one-on-one or small-group interaction.

examples.en: He passed the difficult class with help from his tutor.
examples.zh: 在導師的幫助下，他通過了這門困難的課程。

meanings.definition: To instruct or teach, especially an individual or small group.

examples.en: To help pay her tuition, the college student began to tutor high school students in calculus and physics.
examples.zh: 為了支付學費，這位大學生開始輔導高中生微積分和物理。

meanings.definition: Either of two people (or, less commonly, animals) who shared the same uterus at the same time; one who was born at the same birth as a sibling.
(obsolete outside Scotland) To separate, divide.

examples.en: Coventry twinned with Dresden as an act of peace and reconciliation, both cities having been heavily bombed during the war.
examples.zh: 作為和平與和解的舉措，考文垂與德勒斯登結為友好城市，這兩個城市在戰爭期間都遭到了嚴重轟炸。

meanings.definition: A twisting force.

examples.en: Damascus twist
examples.zh: 大馬士革麻花

meanings.definition: To turn the ends of something, usually thread, rope etc., in opposite directions, often using force.

examples.en: Avarice twists itself into all human concerns.
examples.zh: 貪婪扭曲了人類所有的擔憂。

meanings.definition: A grouping based on shared characteristics; a class.

examples.en: This type of plane can handle rough weather more easily than that type of plane.
examples.zh: 這種類型的飛機比那種類型的飛機更容易應對惡劣的天氣。

meanings.definition: To put text on paper using a typewriter.

examples.en: The doctor ordered the lab to type the patient for a blood transfusion.
examples.zh: 醫生命令實驗室為病人進行輸血打字。

meanings.definition: Anything that is typical, normal, or standard.

examples.en: Among the moths, typicals were more common than melanics.
examples.zh: 在飛蛾中，典型飛蛾比黑色飛蛾更常見。

meanings.definition: Capturing the overall sense of a thing.
A person who types, a clerical worker who writes letters, etc., using a typewriter.
A government in which a single ruler (a tyrant) has absolute power; this system of government.
The most basic or fundamental of a set of things
To finish; to complete.
Final; last in a series.

examples.en: the ultimate disappointment
examples.zh: 最終的失望

meanings.definition: An ultraroyalist in France.
Extreme; far beyond the norm; fanatical; uncompromising.

examples.en: an ultra reformer; ultra measures
examples.zh: 超級改革者；極端措施

meanings.definition: Cloth-covered frame used for protection against rain or sun.

examples.en: Quick, grab that umbrella before you get rained on!
examples.zh: 快點，在被雨淋到之前，帶上雨傘吧！

meanings.definition: To cover or protect, as if by an umbrella.
The official who presides over a tennis game sat on a high chair.

examples.en: The umpire called the pitch a strike.
examples.zh: 裁判員稱本場比賽為罷工。

meanings.definition: To act as an umpire in a game.
Based on unanimity, assent or agreement.

examples.en: The debate went on for hours, but in the end the decision was unanimous.
examples.zh: 辯論持續了幾個小時，但最終一致決定。

meanings.definition: Not aware or informed; lacking knowledge.

examples.en: I'm unaware of the answer to that question.
examples.zh: 我不知道這個問題的答案。

meanings.definition: So unpleasant or painful as to be unendurable
Impartial or without bias or prejudice.
(with "the") Something uncertain.
Not certain; unsure.

examples.en: Tomorrow's weather is uncertain.
examples.zh: 明天的天氣不確定。

meanings.definition: The brother or brother-in-law of one’s parent.
To address somebody by the term uncle.
A cry used to indicate surrender.
Not comfortable; causing discomfort.

examples.en: The class squirmed and fidgeted in the uncomfortable new chairs.
examples.zh: 全班同學在不舒服的新椅子上蠕動不安。

meanings.definition: Unconscious mind
Not awake; having no awareness.

examples.en: After the anesthetist administered the general anesthetic the patient was unconscious.
examples.zh: 麻醉醫師給病人全身麻醉後，病人就失去了意識。

meanings.definition: Being lower; being beneath something.

examples.en: Ensure the patient is sufficiently under.
examples.zh: 確保患者處於足夠低的位置。

meanings.definition: In a way lower or less than.

examples.en: It took the hypnotist several minutes to make his subject go under.
examples.zh: 催眠師花了幾分鐘才讓他的對象陷入困境。

meanings.definition: In or at a lower level than.

examples.en: He served in World War II under General Omar Bradley.
examples.zh: 第二次世界大戰期間，他在奧馬爾·布拉德利將軍手下服役。

meanings.definition: An estimate that is too low.
To perceive (someone or something) as having a lower value, quantity, worth, etc., than what he/she/it actually has.
To go or move under or beneath.

examples.en: The project is undergoing great changes.
examples.zh: 該項目正在發生巨大變化。

meanings.definition: A student at a university who has not yet received a degree.
Of, relating to, or being an undergraduate.

examples.en: After completing my undergraduate studies, I embarked on a career in publishing.
examples.zh: 完成本科學業後，我開始了出版生涯。

meanings.definition: A line placed underneath a piece of text in order to provide emphasis or to indicate that it should be viewed in italics or (in electronic documents) that it acts as a hyperlink.
To draw a line underneath something, especially to add emphasis; to underscore

examples.en: underline the important words in the text
examples.zh: 在文中的重要單字下劃線

meanings.definition: Passing under a railway line.
To lie in a position directly beneath.

examples.en: A stratum of clay underlies the surface gravel.
examples.zh: 表面礫石下面有一層黏土。

meanings.definition: The entity from whose performance a derivative derives its value.
Lying underneath

examples.en: We dug down to the underlying rock.
examples.zh: 我們挖到了下面的岩石。

meanings.definition: To dig underneath (something), to make a passage for destructive or military purposes; to sap.

examples.en: The war efforts were undermined by the constant bickering between the allies.
examples.zh: 戰爭努力因盟友之間不斷的爭吵而受到破壞。

meanings.definition: To grasp a concept fully and thoroughly, especially (of words, statements, art, etc.) to be aware of the meaning of and (of people) to be aware of the intent of.
synonyms: pick up what someone is putting down

examples.en: I'm sorry. I don't understand.
examples.zh: 對不起。我不明白。

meanings.definition: To take upon oneself; to start, to embark on (a specific task etc.).

examples.en: He undertook to exercise more in future.
examples.zh: 他承諾今後要加強鍛鍊。

meanings.definition: Clothes worn next to the skin, underneath outer clothing.
To write below or under; subscribe.
An operation that reverses a previous action.

examples.en: How many undos does this program support?
examples.zh: 該程式支援多少次撤銷？

meanings.definition: To reverse the effects of an action.

examples.en: Fortunately, we can undo most of the damage to the system by the war.
examples.zh: 幸運的是，我們可以消除戰爭對系統造成的大部分損害。

meanings.definition: Excessive; going beyond that what is natural or sufficient.

examples.en: To individuals who despise killings in any form, death penalty is undue punishment.
examples.zh: 對於鄙視任何形式殺戮的人來說，死刑都是不適當的懲罰。

meanings.definition: The state of having no job; joblessness.

examples.en: Unemployment made Jack depressed.
examples.zh: 失業使傑克心情沮​​喪。

meanings.definition: To make uneven.
Not even
Not expected, anticipated or foreseen.
To make ugly
Not beautiful; uncomely; unattractive

examples.en: It was unfair for the boss to give larger bonuses to his friends.
examples.zh: 老闆給朋友多發獎金，這是不公平的。

meanings.definition: In functional programming, a kind of higher-order function that is the opposite of a fold.
To undo a folding.

examples.en: to unfold a map; to unfold a tablecloth; she unpacks the new dress and unfolds it carefully
examples.zh: 展開地圖；展開桌布；她打開新衣服的包裝並小心地展開

meanings.definition: An unlucky person; one who has fallen into bad circumstances.
Not favored by fortune
A distinctive outfit that serves to identify members of a group.
To clothe in a uniform.
Unvarying; all the same.
Cause to become one; make into a unit; consolidate; merge; combine.
The act of uniting or joining two or more things into one.
To combine sets using the union operation.
A thing without a like; something unequalled or unparallelled.
Being the only one of its kind; unequaled, unparalleled or unmatched.

examples.en: Every person has a unique life, therefore every person has a unique journey. ― Gary Cook
examples.zh: 每個人都有獨特的人生，因此每個人都有獨特的旅程。 ——加里·庫克

meanings.definition: A particular, minute unit of mass, defined differently for different substances, but so that varying substances of the same general type have the property that one international unit of the one has the same effect on the human body as one international unit of the other.
For each unit.

examples.en: We have to keep our unit costs down if we want to make a profit.
examples.zh: 如果我們想要獲利，就必須降低單位成本。

meanings.definition: A British gold coin worth 20 shillings, first produced during the reign of King James I, and bearing a legend indicating the king's intention of uniting the kingdoms of England and Scotland.
To bring together as one.

examples.en: I hope this song can unite people from all different cultures.
examples.zh: 我希望這首歌能團結來自不同文化的人們。

meanings.definition: A characteristic or property that particular things have in common.
Of or pertaining to the universe.

examples.en: She achieved universal fame.
examples.zh: 她獲得了舉世聞名。

meanings.definition: Our universe, the sum of everything that exists in the cosmos, including time and space itself.

examples.en: Powerful telescopes look far back into the distant reaches of the Universe.
examples.zh: 強大的望遠鏡可以遙望遙遠的宇宙。

meanings.definition: The sum of everything that exists in the cosmos, including time and space itself.

examples.en: I think that the universe was created by a life force rather than a deity.
examples.zh: 我認為宇宙是由生命力而不是神創造的。

meanings.definition: Institution of higher education (typically accepting students from the age of about 17 or 18, depending on country, but in some exceptional cases able to take younger students) where subjects are studied and researched in depth and degrees are offered.

examples.en: The only reason why I haven't gone to university is because I can't afford it.
examples.zh: 我沒有上大學的唯一原因是我負擔不起。

meanings.definition: Except on a specified condition; if not.

examples.en: I’m leaving unless I get a pay rise (AmE: raise).
examples.zh: 除非加薪（AmE：加薪），否則我會離開。

meanings.definition: Something that is not like something else; something different.
Not like; dissimilar (to); having no resemblance.

examples.en: The brothers are quite unlike each other.
examples.zh: 兄弟倆個性截然不同。

meanings.definition: Differently from; not in a like or similar manner.

examples.en: Being late is unlike him.
examples.zh: 遲到跟他不一樣。

meanings.definition: To remove the load or cargo from (a vehicle, etc.).

examples.en: to unload a ship; to unload a camel
examples.zh: 卸船；卸下駱駝

meanings.definition: The act of unlocking something.
To undo or open a lock or something locked by, for example, turning a key, or selecting a combination.

examples.en: I unlocked the door and walked in.
examples.zh: 我打開門鎖走了進去。

meanings.definition: Unfortunate, marked by misfortune.
To castrate; to remove the manhood of.

examples.en: His fear unmanned him.
examples.zh: 他的恐懼使他失去了控制。

meanings.definition: Not operated by a person or a crew

examples.en: an automated e-mail message sent from an unmanned mailbox
examples.zh: 從無人值守的郵箱發送的自動電子郵件

meanings.definition: Not needed or necessary.

examples.en: The automatic child-frightener made clowns unnecessary.
examples.zh: 自動兒童驚嚇器讓小丑不再存在。

meanings.definition: Not paid for.

examples.en: an unpaid bill
examples.zh: 未付帳單

meanings.definition: Not pleasant.
Never before seen, done, or experienced; without precedent.
Not qualified, ineligible, unfit for a position or task.

examples.en: His lack of a high school diploma renders him unqualified for the job.
examples.zh: 他沒有高中文憑，因此不具備這份工作的資格。

meanings.definition: Without the ability to reason; unreasoning.
Not connected or associated

examples.en: The holiday started with two unrelated mishaps.
examples.zh: 這個假期始於兩起無關的事故。

meanings.definition: Not reliable.
A state of trouble, confusion and turbulence, especially in a political context; a time of riots, demonstrations and protests.
Of a person or workforce: not having a skill or technical training.
To release (an animal) from a stable.
Having a strong tendency to change.
To render unsteady, removing balance.
Not held firmly in position, physically unstable.

examples.en: A slightly unsteady item of furniture.
examples.zh: 一件稍微不穩定的家具。

meanings.definition: Failed, not successful.

examples.en: His unsuccessful attempt earned him sympathy, condolences, and an occasional good-natured ribbing.
examples.zh: 他的失敗嘗試為他贏得了同情、哀悼，偶爾也有人善意地開玩笑。

meanings.definition: Up to the time of (something happening).

examples.en: If you can wait until after my meeting with her, we'll talk then.
examples.zh: 如果你能等到我和她見面之後我們再談。

meanings.definition: Up to the time that (a condition becomes true).
Something that is unusual; an anomaly.
Not usual, out of the ordinary

examples.en: Every once in a while, though, Idol comes across an unusual talent who just blows everyone away.
examples.zh: 不過，每隔一段時間，《偶像》就會遇到一位不尋常的天才，讓所有人驚嘆不已。

meanings.definition: Not willing; reluctant

examples.en: We are unwilling to restore his previous privileges after his betrayal of our trust.
examples.zh: 在他背叛了我們的信任後，我們不願意恢復他以前的特權。

meanings.definition: An advisement providing more up-to-date information than currently known.

examples.en: He gave me an update on the situation in New York.
examples.zh: 他向我介紹了紐約的最新情況。

meanings.definition: To bring (a thing) up to date.

examples.en: I need to update my records to take account of the most recent transaction.
examples.zh: 我需要更新我的記錄以考慮最近的交易。

meanings.definition: An upward grade or slope.

examples.en: With my phone company, I get a free upgrade every twelve months if I keep topping up 10 pounds a month.
examples.zh: 在我的電話公司，如果我每個月繼續充值 10 英鎊，那麼我每十二個月就可以獲得一次免費升級。

meanings.definition: To improve, usually applied to technology, generally by complete replacement of one or more components

examples.en: When you upgrade your standard of living, you become addicted to that standard of living.
examples.zh: 當你提高生活水平時，你就會沉迷於這種生活水平。

meanings.definition: Up a slope or grade.
To hold up; to lift on high; to elevate.
Being the target of an action.

examples.en: He was set upon by the agitated dogs
examples.zh: 他被那些激動的狗襲擊了

meanings.definition: Physically above and in contact with.

examples.en: Place the book upon the table.
examples.zh: 將書放在桌上。

meanings.definition: A stimulant, such as amphetamine, that increases energy and decreases appetite.
At a higher level, rank or position.
Any vertical part of a structure, especially one of the goal posts in sports.
To set upright or stand back up (something that has fallen).
Vertical; erect.

examples.en: I was standing upright, waiting for my orders.
examples.zh: 我站直了身子，等待我的命令。

meanings.definition: In or into an upright position
Disturbance or disruption.

examples.en: My late arrival caused the professor considerable upset.
examples.zh: 我遲到讓教授很不高興。

meanings.definition: To make (a person) angry, distressed, or unhappy.

examples.en: I’m sure the bad news will upset him, but he needs to know.
examples.zh: 我確信這個壞消息會讓他心煩意亂，但他需要知道。

meanings.definition: (of a person) Angry, distressed or unhappy.

examples.en: He was upset when she refused his friendship.
examples.zh: 當她拒絕他的友誼時，他很沮喪。

meanings.definition: An upper storey.
Located on a higher floor or level of a building.

examples.en: They can sleep in the upstairs bedroom.
examples.zh: 他們可以睡在樓上的臥室。

meanings.definition: Up the stairs; on a higher floor or level.

examples.en: I hate the people who live upstairs, and I especially hate their piano.
examples.zh: 我討厭住在樓上的人，尤其討厭他們的鋼琴。

meanings.definition: Part of the river towards the upstream direction.
To stream upward.

examples.en: I'd be more than happy to upstream your patch.
examples.zh: 我非常樂意將您的補丁上傳到上游。

meanings.definition: In a direction against the flow of a current or stream of fluid (typically water); upriver
Against the current.
The direction opposed to the pull of gravity.

examples.en: Up is a good way to go.
examples.zh: 向上是一個好方法。

meanings.definition: To increase or raise.

examples.en: If we up the volume, we may be able to hear what he says.
examples.zh: 如果我們把音量調大，也許就能聽到他說的話。

meanings.definition: Facing upwards.

examples.en: Put the notebook face up on the table.
examples.zh: 將筆記本正面朝上放在桌上。

meanings.definition: Away from the surface of the Earth or other planet; in opposite direction to the downward pull of gravity.

examples.en: I looked up and saw the airplane overhead.
examples.zh: 我抬起頭，看到頭頂上有一架飛機。

meanings.definition: Toward the top of.

examples.en: The cat climbed up the tree.
examples.zh: 那隻貓爬上了樹。

meanings.definition: Toward a closed, touching or engaging position.

examples.en: Please push the door to.
examples.zh: 請推門進去。

meanings.definition: Indicating destination: In the direction of, and arriving at.

examples.en: We are walking to the shop.
examples.zh: 我們正步行去商店。

meanings.definition: The fruit of the date palm, Phoenix dactylifera, somewhat in the shape of an olive, containing a soft, sweet pulp and enclosing a hard kernel.

examples.en: We made a nice cake from dates.
examples.zh: 我們用棗做了一個漂亮的蛋糕。

meanings.definition: Related to the (or any) city.
A strong desire; an itch to do something.
To press; to push; to drive; to impel; to force onward.

examples.en: to urge an argument; to urge the necessity of a case
examples.zh: 敦促爭論；敦促案件的必要性

meanings.definition: Requiring immediate attention.

examples.en: An urgent appeal was sent out for assistance.
examples.zh: 已發出緊急呼籲尋求援助。

meanings.definition: The manner or the amount of using; use.
Having a practical or beneficial use.
Without use or possibility to be used.

examples.en: This fork has prongs that are bent. It's useless now.
examples.zh: 這把叉子的叉齒是彎曲的。現在沒用了。

meanings.definition: One who uses or makes use of something, a consumer/client or an express or implied licensee (free user) or a trespasser.
A person, in a church, cinema etc., who escorts people to their seats.
To guide people to their seats.
The typical state of something, or something that is typical.

examples.en: I'll just have the usual.
examples.zh: 我就照常去吧。

meanings.definition: Most commonly occurring; typical.

examples.en: It is becoming more usual these days to rear children as bilingual.
examples.zh: 如今，以雙語方式撫養孩子變得越來越普遍。

meanings.definition: An instrument or device for domestic use, especially in the kitchen.

examples.en: We have convenient storage for all the kitchen utensils.
examples.zh: 我們為所有廚房用具提供便利的儲存空間。

meanings.definition: The state or condition of being useful; usefulness.

examples.en: I've bought a new disk utility that can recover deleted files.
examples.zh: 我購買了一個新的磁碟實用程序，可以恢復已刪除的檔案。

meanings.definition: Having to do with, or owned by, a service provider.

examples.en: utility line; utility bill
examples.zh: 公用事業線；水電費帳單

meanings.definition: To make use of; to use.
Maximum; greatest possible amount or quantity.
Situated at the most distant limit; farthest.

examples.en: the utmost limits of the land;  the utmost extent of human knowledge
examples.zh: 土地的最大界線；  人類知識的最大範圍

meanings.definition: Outer; furthest out, most remote.

examples.en: utter ruin; utter darkness
examples.zh: 徹底毀滅；完全黑暗

meanings.definition: An unoccupied position or job.
Not occupied; empty.

examples.en: a vacant stare
examples.zh: 空洞的凝視

meanings.definition: Freedom from some business or activity.

examples.en: The Conservative Party’s vacation of the centre ground gave an opportunity to its opponents.
examples.zh: 保守黨退出中間地帶，給了反對者可乘之機。

meanings.definition: To spend or take a vacation.

examples.en: This year, we’re vacationing in Mexico.
examples.zh: 今年，我們去墨西哥度假。

meanings.definition: Treat with a vaccine to produce immunity against a disease.
A region of space that contains no matter.

examples.en: a vacuum of 26 inches of mercury, or 13 pounds per square inch
examples.zh: 26 英吋汞柱或每平方英吋 13 磅的真空

meanings.definition: To clean (something) with a vacuum cleaner.
A wandering; a vagary.
To wander; to roam; to stray.
Not clearly expressed; stated in indefinite terms.

examples.en: a vague term of abuse
examples.zh: 模糊的濫用術語

meanings.definition: Overly proud of oneself, especially concerning appearance; having a high opinion of one's own accomplishments with slight reason.

examples.en: vain toil;  a vain attempt
examples.zh: 徒勞的勞動；  徒勞的嘗試

meanings.definition: Well grounded or justifiable, pertinent.

examples.en: I will believe him as soon as he offers a valid answer.
examples.zh: 只要他給出有效的答案，我就會相信他。

meanings.definition: To render valid.

examples.en: The data file cannot be imported because it doesn't validate.
examples.zh: 無法匯入資料文件，因為它未驗證。

meanings.definition: An elongated depression between hills or mountains, often with a river flowing through it.
A personal possession such as jewellery, of relatively great monetary value; — usually used in plural form.
Having a great value.

examples.en: valuable gemstones
examples.zh: 珍貴的寶石

meanings.definition: The quality (positive or negative) that renders something desirable or valuable.

examples.en: The Shakespearean Shylock is of dubious value in the modern world.
examples.zh: 莎士比亞筆下的夏洛克在現代世界的價值值得懷疑。

meanings.definition: To estimate the value of; judge the worth of something.

examples.en: I will have the family jewels valued by a professional.
examples.zh: 我將請專業人士對家庭珠寶進行估價。

meanings.definition: A device that controls the flow of a gas or fluid through a pipe.

examples.en: the ileocolic, mitral, and semilunar valves
examples.zh: 回結腸瓣、二尖瓣和半月瓣

meanings.definition: To control (flow) by means of a valve.
A covered vehicle used for carrying goods or people, usually roughly cuboid in shape, longer and higher than a car but smaller than a truck/lorry.

examples.en: The van sped down the road.
examples.zh: 貨車在路上飛馳。

meanings.definition: To transport in a van or similar vehicle (especially of horses).
The brief terminal part of a vowel or vocal element, differing more or less in quality from the main part.

examples.en: a as in ale ordinarily ends with a vanish of i as in ill.
examples.zh: ale 中的 a 通常以 ill 中的 i 消失而結束。

meanings.definition: To become invisible or to move out of view unnoticed.

examples.en: The function f(x)=x^2 vanishes at x=0.
examples.zh: 函數 f(x)=x^2 在 x=0 處消失。

meanings.definition: That which is vain, futile, or worthless; that which is of no value, use or profit.

examples.en: It is a vanity to say that if two stones are dropped from a tower, the heavier will experience the greater acceleration.
examples.zh: 說如果兩塊石頭從塔上落下，越重的加速度越大，這是一種虛榮心。

meanings.definition: Cloudy diffused matter such as mist, steam or fumes suspended in the air.
To become vapor; to be emitted or circulated as vapor.

examples.en: to vapor away a heated fluid
examples.zh: 蒸發掉加熱的液體

meanings.definition: Something that is variable.

examples.en: There are several variables to consider here.
examples.zh: 這裡有幾個變數要考慮。

meanings.definition: Able to vary or be varied.

examples.en: variable winds or seasons; a variable quantity; a variable resistor
examples.zh: 多變的風或季節；可變數量；可變電阻器

meanings.definition: The act of varying; a partial change in the form, position, state, or qualities of a thing.

examples.en: All of his soups are variations on a single recipe.
examples.zh: 他所有的湯都是同一個食譜的變體。

meanings.definition: The quality of being varied; diversity.

examples.en: Variety is the spice of life.
examples.zh: 多樣性是生活的調味料。

meanings.definition: Having a broad range (of different elements).

examples.en: The reasons are various.
examples.zh: 原因是多種多樣的。

meanings.definition: A type of paint with a solvent that evaporates to leave a hard, transparent, glossy film.
To apply varnish.
Alteration; change.
To change with time or a similar parameter.

examples.en: He varies his magic tricks so as to minimize the possibility that any given audience member will see the same trick twice.
examples.zh: 他會改變他的魔術，以盡量減少任何特定觀眾看到同一魔術兩次的可能性。

meanings.definition: A vast space.
Very large or wide (literally or figuratively).

examples.en: The Sahara desert is vast.
examples.zh: 撒哈拉沙漠廣闊無垠。

meanings.definition: An arched masonry structure supporting and forming a ceiling, whether freestanding or forming part of a larger building.

examples.en: Family members had been buried in the vault for centuries.
examples.zh: 幾個世紀以來，家族成員一直被埋在墓穴裡。

meanings.definition: To build as, or cover with a vault.
Any plant.
Of or relating to plants.
A conveyance; a device for carrying or transporting substances, objects or individuals.
Something hung up or spread out to hide or protect the face, or hide an object from view; usually of gauze, crepe, or similar diaphanous material.

examples.en: a nun's veil
examples.zh: 修女的面紗

meanings.definition: To dress in, or decorate with, a veil.

examples.en: The forest fire was veiled by smoke, but I could hear it clearly.
examples.zh: 森林大火被煙霧籠罩，但我聽得清清楚楚。

meanings.definition: A blood vessel that transports blood from the capillaries back to the heart.

examples.en: in the same vein
examples.zh: 同樣地

meanings.definition: To mark with veins or a vein-like pattern.
A vector quantity that denotes the rate of change of position with respect to time, or a speed with the directional component.
A closely woven fabric (originally of silk, now also of cotton or man-made fibres) with a thick short pile on one side.
To cover with velvet or with a covering of a similar texture.
Made of velvet.
A person or a company that vends or sells.
To bundle third-party dependencies with the source code for one's own program.

examples.en: I distributed my application with a vendored copy of Perl so that it wouldn't use the system copies of Perl where it is installed.
examples.zh: 我使用 Perl 的供應商副本分發了我的應用程序，這樣它就不會使用安裝它的 Perl 系統副本。

meanings.definition: A risky or daring undertaking or journey.
To undertake a risky or daring journey.

examples.en: Nothing ventured, nothing gained.
examples.zh: 沒有冒險，就沒有收穫。

meanings.definition: (grammar) A verb form which does not function as a predicate, or a word derived from a verb. In English, infinitives, participles and gerunds are verbals.
To induce into fabricating a confession.
Of or relating to words.

examples.en: a verbal contract
examples.zh: 口頭合約

meanings.definition: A decision on an issue of fact in a civil or criminal case or an inquest.

examples.en: The jury returned a “not guilty” verdict.
examples.zh: 陪審團做出「無罪」判決。

meanings.definition: A rod or staff of office, e.g. of a verger.
To substantiate or prove the truth of something
Capable of doing many things competently.

examples.en: 2004, "queenchaser1158", Horny Versatile TV/TS Wanted in Phoenix (on newsgroup alt.personals.gay)
examples.zh: 2004 年，“queenchaser1158”，鳳凰城通緝性慾旺盛的多功能電視/TS（在新聞群組 alt.personals.gay 上）

meanings.definition: A poetic form with regular meter and a fixed rhyme scheme.

examples.en: Restoration literature is well known for its carefully constructed verse.
examples.zh: 復闢文學以其精心建構的詩句而聞名。

meanings.definition: To compose verses.

examples.en: He versed us in the finer points of category theory.
examples.zh: 他讓我們精通範疇論的精髓。

meanings.definition: A specific form or variation of something.

examples.en: It's only in the King James Version of the Bible.
examples.zh: 它只存在於欽定本聖經中。

meanings.definition: To keep track of (a file, document, etc.) in a versioning system.
Against; in opposition to.

examples.en: It is the Packers versus the Steelers in the Super Bowl.
examples.zh: 這是超級盃比賽中包裝工隊對上鋼人隊的比賽。

meanings.definition: A vertex or zenith.

examples.en: We offer specialised accounting software targeting various verticals.
examples.zh: 我們提供針對各行業的專業會計軟體。

meanings.definition: Standing, pointing, or moving straight up or down; along the direction of a plumb line; perpendicular to something horizontal.
Any craft designed for transportation on water, such as a ship or boat.

examples.en: Blood and lymph vessels are found in humans; xylem and phloem vessels are found in plants.
examples.zh: 人體中存在血管和淋巴管；木質部和韌皮部導管存在於植物中。

meanings.definition: To put into a vessel.
A loose robe or outer garment worn historically by men in Arab or Middle Eastern countries.
To clothe with, or as with, a vestment, or garment; to dress; to robe; to cover, surround, or encompass closely.

examples.en: to vest a court with power to try cases of life and death
examples.zh: 賦予法院審理生死案件的權力

meanings.definition: A person with long experience of a particular activity.
Having had long experience, practice, or service.
A political right to disapprove of (and thereby stop) the process of a decision, a law etc.
To use a veto against.
A main road or highway, especially in ancient Rome. (Mainly used in set phrases, below.)
An organism that is able to live and develop.
Able to live on its own (as for a newborn).
The setting, on a portable electronic device, that causes it to vibrate rather than sound any (or most) needed alarms.

examples.en: Please put your cellphones on vibrate for the duration of the meeting.
examples.zh: 會議期間請將手機調至震動狀態。

meanings.definition: To shake with small, rapid movements to and fro.

examples.en: Her mind was vibrating with excitement.
examples.zh: 她的心因興奮而顫抖。

meanings.definition: A bad habit.

examples.en: Gluttony is a vice, not a virtue.
examples.zh: 暴食是一種惡習，而不是一種美德。

meanings.definition: Proximity; the state of being near.

examples.en: There was a crackling sound in the vicinity of my right ear.
examples.zh: 右耳附近傳來噼裡啪啦的聲音。

meanings.definition: Violent, destructive and cruel.
One that is harmed—killed, injured, subjected to oppression, deceived, or otherwise adversely affected—by someone or something, especially another person or event, force, or condition; in particular:

examples.en: the youngest victims of the brutal war
examples.zh: 殘酷戰爭中最年輕的受害者

meanings.definition: An instance of having won a competition or battle or succeeded in an effort.

examples.en: It was a great victory on the battlefield.
examples.zh: 這是戰場上的偉大勝利。

meanings.definition: To achieve a victory
Television, television show, movie.
To record using a video camera, to videotape
(physical) Visual perception.

examples.en: a fine view of Lake George
examples.zh: 喬治湖的美景

meanings.definition: To look at.

examples.en: The video was viewed by millions of people.
examples.zh: 該影片有數百萬人觀看。

meanings.definition: Someone who views a spectacle; an onlooker or spectator.
The position from which something is observed or considered; an angle, outlook or point of view.
Active strength or force of body or mind; capacity for exertion, physically, intellectually, or morally; energy.
A rural habitation of size between a hamlet and a town.

examples.en: There are 2 churches and 3 shops in our village.
examples.zh: 我們村有2座教堂和3家商店。

meanings.definition: The climbing plant that produces grapes.
A sour liquid formed by the fermentation of alcohol used as a condiment or preservative; a dilute solution of acetic acid.

examples.en: a range of herb-flavoured vinegars
examples.zh: 一系列香草醋

meanings.definition: To season or otherwise treat with vinegar.
To break or disregard (a rule or convention).

examples.en: Accessing unauthorized files violates security protocol.
examples.zh: 存取未經授權的文件違反了安全協議。

meanings.definition: Extreme force.

examples.en: The violence of the storm, fortunately, was more awesome than destructive.
examples.zh: 幸運的是，風暴的威力與其說是破壞性的，不如說是可怕的。

meanings.definition: To subject to violence.
An assailant.
To urge with violence.
Involving extreme force or motion.

examples.en: A violent wind ripped the branch from the tree.
examples.zh: 一陣猛烈的風把樹枝從樹上扯了下來。

meanings.definition: A virtual member function of a class.
In effect or essence, if not in fact or reality; imitated, simulated.

examples.en: In fact a defeat on the battlefield, Tet was a virtual victory for the North, owing to its effect on public opinion.
examples.zh: 事實上，由於其對公眾輿論的影響，春節對北韓來說實際上是戰場上的失敗。

meanings.definition: Accordance with moral principles; conformity of behaviour or thought with the strictures of morality; good moral conduct.

examples.en: Without virtue, there is no freedom.
examples.zh: 沒有美德，就沒有自由。

meanings.definition: A submicroscopic, non-cellular structure consisting of a core of DNA or RNA surrounded by a protein coat, that requires a living host cell to replicate, and often causes disease in the host organism.

examples.en: He caught a virus and had to stay home from school.
examples.zh: 他感染了病毒，只好待在家裡不上學。

meanings.definition: A permit to enter and leave a country, normally issued by the authorities of the country to be visited.
To endorse (a passport, etc.).
Able to be seen.

examples.en: When the sun rises, the world becomes visible.
examples.zh: 當太陽升起時，世界變得清晰可見。

meanings.definition: The sense or ability of sight.

examples.en: He tried drinking from the pool of water, but realized it was only a vision.
examples.zh: 他嘗試喝池裡的水，但意識到這只是幻象。

meanings.definition: To imagine something as if it were to be true.
A single act of visiting.
To habitually go to (someone in distress, sickness etc.) to comfort them. (Now generally merged into later senses, below.)
Someone who visits someone else; someone staying as a guest.
Any element of something that depends on sight.
Related to or affecting the vision.
Relating to, or characteristic of life.

examples.en: vital energies; vital functions; vital actions
examples.zh: 生命能量；重要功能；重要行動

meanings.definition: Any of a specific group of organic compounds essential in small quantities for healthy human growth, metabolism, development, and body function; found in minute amounts in plant and animal foods or sometimes produced synthetically; deficiencies of specific vitamins produce specific disorders.
A felt-tipped permanent marker.
(of perception) Clear, detailed or powerful.
A usually alphabetized and explained collection of words e.g. of a particular field, or prepared for a specific purpose, often for learning.

examples.en: My Russian vocabulary is very limited.
examples.zh: 我的俄語詞彙量非常有限。

meanings.definition: A vocal sound; specifically, a purely vocal element of speech, unmodified except by resonance; a vowel or a diphthong; a tonic element; a tonic; distinguished from a subvocal, and a nonvocal
Of or pertaining to the voice or speech

examples.en: vocal problems
examples.zh: 聲音問題

meanings.definition: Of or pertaining to a vocation.
Sound uttered by the mouth, especially by human beings in speech or song; sound thus uttered considered as possessing some special quality or character

examples.en: His low voice allowed him to become a bass in the choir.
examples.zh: 他低沉的聲音使他成為合唱團的貝斯手。

meanings.definition: An empty space; a vacuum.

examples.en: Nobody has crossed the void since one man died trying three hundred years ago; it's high time we had another go.
examples.zh: 自從三百年前有人嘗試穿越虛空以來，還沒有人穿越過虛空。現在是我們再試一次的時候了。

meanings.definition: To make invalid or worthless.

examples.en: He voided the check and returned it.
examples.zh: 他作廢了支票並將其退回。

meanings.definition: Containing nothing; empty; not occupied or filled.
A chemical or compound that changes into a gas easily.
Evaporating or vaporizing readily under normal conditions.

A vent or fissure on the surface of a planet (usually in a mountainous form) with a magma chamber attached to the mantle of a planet or moon, periodically erupting forth lava and volcanic gases onto the surface.

In the International System of Units, the derived unit of electrical potential and electromotive force (voltage); the potential difference across a conductor when a current of one ampere uses one watt of power. Symbol: V
The difference in electrostatic potential between two points in space, especially between live and neutral conductors or the earth.

examples.en: Be careful when opening high-voltage equipment.
examples.zh: 打開高壓設備時要小心。

meanings.definition: A three-dimensional measure of space that comprises a length, a width and a height. It is measured in units of cubic centimeters in metric, cubic inches or cubic feet in English measurement.

examples.en: The room is 9x12x8, so its volume is 864 cubic feet.
examples.zh: 該房間的尺寸為 9x12x8，因此其體積為 864 立方英尺。

meanings.definition: To be conveyed through the air, waft.
A short piece of music, often having improvisation, played on a solo instrument.
Done, given, or acting of one's own free will.

examples.en: If a man accidentally kills another by lopping a tree, it is not voluntary manslaughter.
examples.zh: 如果一個人砍伐一棵樹時意外殺死了另一個人，則不屬於故意殺人。

meanings.definition: Voluntarily.
One who enters into, or offers for, any service of his/her own free will, especially when done without pay.
To enlist oneself as a volunteer.

examples.en: to volunteer for doing the dishes
examples.zh: 自願洗碗

meanings.definition: A formalized choice on matters of administration or other democratic activities

examples.en: One occasion indicative votes were used was in 2003 when MPs were presented with seven different options on how to reform the House of Lords.
examples.zh: 一次使用指示性投票是在 2003 年，當時議員們就如何改革上議院提出了七種不同的選擇。

meanings.definition: To cast a vote; to assert a formalized choice in an election

examples.en: Did you vote last month?
examples.zh: 上個月你投票了嗎？

meanings.definition: Someone who votes.

examples.en: In your opinion, should we allow 16 and 17 year olds to become voters?
examples.zh: 您認為我們應該允許16歲和17歲的人成為選民嗎？

meanings.definition: A piece of paper that entitles the holder to a discount, or that can be exchanged for goods and services.
To establish the authenticity of; to vouch for.
A solemn promise to perform some act, or behave in a specified manner, especially a promise to live and act in accordance with the rules of a religious order.

examples.en: The old hermit, up in the mountains, took a vow of silence.
examples.zh: 老隱士在山上發誓保持沉默。

meanings.definition: To make a vow; to promise.

examples.en: The wronged woman vowed revenge.
examples.zh: 受委屈的女人發誓要報仇。

meanings.definition: A long journey, especially by ship.
To go on a long journey.
(classicism) A common, ordinary person.
Debased, uncouth, distasteful, obscene.

examples.en: The construction worker made a vulgar suggestion to the girls walking down the street.
examples.zh: 建築工人向街上行走的女孩提出了粗俗的建議。

meanings.definition: More or most likely to be exposed to the chance of being attacked or harmed, either physically or emotionally.

examples.en: It's okay to get vulnerable every now and again.
examples.zh: 時不時變得脆弱是沒關係的。

meanings.definition: An act of wading.

examples.en: We had to be careful during our dangerous wade across the river.}}
examples.zh: 我們在危險的涉水過河過程中必須小心。 }}

meanings.definition: To walk through water or something that impedes progress.

examples.en: to wade through a dull book
examples.zh: 費力地翻閱一本枯燥的書

meanings.definition: (often in plural) An amount of money paid to a worker for a specified quantity of work, usually calculated on an hourly basis and expressed in an amount of money per hour.

examples.en: Before her promotion, her wages were 20% less.
examples.zh: 升職前，她的薪水少了20%。

meanings.definition: A four-wheeled cart for hauling loads.
To load into a wagon in preparation for transportation; to transport by means of a wagon.
The part of the body between the pelvis and the stomach.
A delay.

examples.en: I had a very long wait at the airport security check.
examples.zh: 我在機場安檢處等了很久。

meanings.definition: To delay movement or action until the arrival or occurrence of; to await. (Now generally superseded by “wait for”.)

examples.en: Wait here until your car arrives.
examples.zh: 在這裡等待，直到你的車到達。

meanings.definition: A male or female attendant who serves customers at their tables in a restaurant, café or similar.

examples.en: Waiter! There's a fly in my soup.
examples.zh: 服務生!我的湯裡有一隻蒼蠅。

meanings.definition: A female attendant who serves customers in a restaurant, café, or similar.
To work as a waitress.
To relinquish (a right etc.); to give up claim to; to forego.

examples.en: If you waive the right to be silent, anything you say can be used against you in a court of law.
examples.zh: 如果您放棄保持沉默的權利，您所說的任何話都可能在法庭上對您不利。

meanings.definition: The act of waiving, or not insisting on, some right, claim, or privilege.

examples.en: I had to sign a waiver when I went skydiving, agreeing not to sue even if something went wrong.
examples.zh: 我去跳傘時必須簽署一份棄權書，同意即使有問題也不會起訴。

meanings.definition: To waive (to relinquish, to forego).
The act of waking, or state of being awake.
(often followed by up) To stop sleeping.

examples.en: I woke up at four o'clock this morning.
examples.zh: 今天早上四點我就醒了。

meanings.definition: To move on the feet by alternately setting each foot (or pair or group of feet, in the case of animals with four or more feet) forward, with at least one foot on the ground at all times. Compare run.

examples.en: To walk briskly for an hour every day is to keep fit.
examples.zh: 每天快走一小時是為了強身健體。

meanings.definition: A rampart of earth, stones etc. built up for defensive purposes.

examples.en: The town wall was surrounded by a moat.
examples.zh: 城牆周圍有護城河。

meanings.definition: To enclose with, or as if with, a wall or walls.

examples.en: He walled the study with books.
examples.zh: 他用書籍圍住了書房。

meanings.definition: A small case, often flat and often made of leather, for keeping money (especially paper money), credit cards, etc.

examples.en: The thief stole all the money and credit cards out of the old man's wallet.
examples.zh: 小偷偷走了老人錢包裡的所有錢和信用卡。

meanings.definition: The act or instance of wandering.

examples.en: to go for a wander in the park
examples.zh: 去公園散步

meanings.definition: To move without purpose or specified destination; often in search of livelihood.

examples.en: to wander over the fields
examples.zh: 漫步在田野上

meanings.definition: A desire, wish, longing.
To wish for or desire (something); to feel a need or desire for; to crave or demand.

examples.en: What do you want to eat?  I want you to leave.  I never wanted to go back to live with my mother.
examples.zh: 你想吃什麼？  我要你離開。  我從來不想回去和媽媽住在一起。

meanings.definition: A warden; a guard; a guardian or watchman.
A guard or watchman.

examples.en: the warden of a college
examples.zh: 學院院長

meanings.definition: To carry out the duties of a warden.
A room for keeping clothes and armor safe, particularly a dressing room or walk-in closet beside a bedroom.
To act as a wardrobe department, to provide clothing or sets of clothes.
The state of being aware; heed.
Aware.
A place for storing large amounts of products. In logistics, a place where products go to from the manufacturer before going to the retailer.
To store in a warehouse or similar.

examples.en: the warehousing of syndicated TV shows
examples.zh: 聯合電視節目的倉儲

meanings.definition: Having a temperature slightly higher than usual, but still pleasant; mildly hot.

examples.en: The tea is still warm.
examples.zh: 茶還是溫熱的。

meanings.definition: A moderate degree of heat; the sensation of being warm.
To make (someone) aware of (something impending); especially:

examples.en: Don't let me catch you running in the corridor again, I warn you.
examples.zh: 我警告你，別讓我再發現你在走廊上跑。

meanings.definition: To make (someone) aware of (something impending); especially:

examples.en: Don't let me catch you running in the corridor again, I warn you.
examples.zh: 我警告你，別讓我再發現你在走廊上跑。

meanings.definition: The action of the verb warn; an instance of warning someone.

examples.en: The boss gave him a warning that he would be fired if he did not desist from his behaviour.
examples.zh: 老闆警告他，如果他不停止這種行為，他就會被解僱。

meanings.definition: Used to warn of danger in signs and notices.
Authorization or certification; a sanction, as given by a superior.

examples.en: a warrant of authenticity; a warrant for success
examples.zh: 真實性保證；成功的保證

meanings.definition: To protect, keep safe (from danger).

examples.en: That tree is going to fall, I’ll warrant.
examples.zh: 我保證那棵樹會倒下。

meanings.definition: A guarantee that a certain outcome or obligation will be fulfilled; security.

examples.en: I made sure to check the terms of my warranty for my computer to ensure I was covered in case it broke down.
examples.zh: 我一定要檢查我的計算機的保固條款，以確保在計算機故障時我能得到保障。

meanings.definition: To warrant; to guarantee.
The process or an instance of washing or being washed by water or other liquid.

examples.en: I'm going to have a quick wash before coming to bed.
examples.zh: 睡前我要快速洗個澡。

meanings.definition: To clean with water.

examples.en: Dishwashers wash dishes much more efficiently than most humans.
examples.zh: 洗碗機比大多數人洗碗的效率高得多。

meanings.definition: Excess of material, useless by-products or damaged, unsaleable products; garbage; rubbish.

examples.en: The cage was littered with animal waste
examples.zh: 籠子裡堆滿了動物糞便

meanings.definition: A portable or wearable timepiece.

examples.en: More people today carry a watch on their wrists than in their pockets.
examples.zh: 如今，更多的人將手錶戴在手腕上，而不是放在口袋裡。

meanings.definition: A substance (of molecular formula H₂O) found at room temperature and pressure as a clear liquid; it is present naturally as rain, and found in rivers, lakes and seas; its solid form is ice and its gaseous form is steam.

examples.en: By the action of electricity, the water was resolved into its two parts, oxygen and hydrogen.
examples.zh: 在電的作用下，水被分解成氧氣和氫氣兩部分。

meanings.definition: To move back and forth repeatedly and somewhat loosely.

examples.en: The flag waved in the gentle breeze.
examples.zh: 旗幟在微風中飄揚。

meanings.definition: Beeswax.

examples.en: What role does the wax in your earhole fulfill?
examples.zh: 耳孔中的耳垢有什麼作用？

meanings.definition: Made of wax.
(heading) To do with a place or places.

examples.en: You're going about it the wrong way.  He's known for his quirky ways.  I don't like the way she looks at me.
examples.zh: 你的做法是錯誤的。  他以古怪的行事方式而聞名。  我不喜歡她看我的方式。

meanings.definition: To travel.
(only in reply to no way) yes; it is true; it is possible
Lacking in force (usually strength) or ability.

examples.en: The child was too weak to move the boulder.
examples.zh: 孩子太虛弱了，無法搬動巨石。

meanings.definition: Riches; valuable material possessions.

examples.en: She brings a wealth of knowledge to the project.
examples.zh: 她為該項目帶來了豐富的知識。

meanings.definition: An instrument of attack or defense in combat or hunting, e.g. most guns, missiles, or swords.

examples.en: The club that is now mostly used for golf was once a common weapon.
examples.zh: 現在主要用於高爾夫的球桿曾經是一種常見的武器。

meanings.definition: (in combination) clothing

examples.en: footwear; outdoor wear; maternity wear
examples.zh: 鞋類；戶外服裝；孕婦裝

meanings.definition: To carry or have equipped on or about one's body, as an item of clothing, equipment, decoration, etc.

examples.en: He's wearing some nice pants today.  She wore her medals with pride.  Please wear your seatbelt.  Can you wear makeup and sunscreen at the same time?  He was wearing his lunch after tripping and falling into the buffet.
examples.zh: 他今天穿了一條漂亮的褲子。  她自豪地戴著獎牌。  請繫好安全帶。  化妝和防曬可以同時進行嗎？  他絆倒並跌入自助餐後，穿著午餐。

meanings.definition: To make or to become weary.
Having the strength exhausted by toil or exertion; tired; fatigued.

examples.en: A weary traveller knocked at the door.
examples.zh: 一位疲憊的旅客敲了敲門。

meanings.definition: The short term state of the atmosphere at a specific time and place, including the temperature, relative humidity, cloud cover, precipitation, wind, etc.

examples.en: Wooden garden furniture must be well oiled as it is continuously exposed to weather.
examples.zh: 木製花園家具必須充分塗油，因為它不斷暴露在天氣中。

meanings.definition: To expose to the weather, or show the effects of such exposure, or to withstand such effects.

examples.en: to weather a cape; to weather another ship
examples.zh: 抵禦海角；抵禦另一艘船

meanings.definition: Facing towards the flow of a fluid, usually air.
The World Wide Web.

examples.en: Some of that content is now only available on the Web.
examples.zh: 其中一些內容現在只能在網路上取得。

meanings.definition: The silken structure which a spider builds using silk secreted from the spinnerets at the caudal tip of its abdomen; a spiderweb.

examples.en: The sunlight glistened in the dew on the web.
examples.zh: 陽光照在網路上的露珠上閃閃發光。

meanings.definition: To construct or form a web.
To perform the marriage ceremony for; to join in matrimony.

examples.en: The priest wed the couple.
examples.zh: 牧師為這對夫婦舉行了婚禮。

meanings.definition: Marriage ceremony; ritual officially celebrating the beginning of a marriage.

examples.en: Her announcement was quite a surprise, coming a month after she published the words "I hate weddings with a passion and a fury I can only partially explain rationally."
examples.zh: 她的聲明相當令人驚訝，一個月前她發表了“我討厭充滿激情和憤怒的婚禮，我只能部分理性地解釋。”

meanings.definition: One of the simple machines; a piece of material, such as metal or wood, thick at one edge and tapered to a thin edge at the other for insertion in a narrow crevice, used for splitting, tightening, securing, or levering.

examples.en: Stick a wedge under the door, will you? It keeps blowing shut.
examples.zh: 在門下面塞個楔子，可以嗎？它一直被風吹著關閉。

meanings.definition: To support or secure using a wedge.

examples.en: I wedged open the window with a screwdriver.
examples.zh: 我用螺絲起子撬開窗戶。

meanings.definition: Any plant regarded as unwanted at the place where, and at the time when it is growing.

examples.en: If it isn't in a straight line or marked with a label, it's a weed.
examples.zh: 如果它不是在一條直線上或沒有標記，那就是雜草。

meanings.definition: Any period of seven consecutive days.

examples.en: A 4-day week consists of Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday.
examples.zh: 每週 4 天包括星期一、星期二、星期三和星期四。

meanings.definition: A publication that is published once a week.
Of or relating to a week.

examples.en: He's going for his weekly check-up at the hospital.
examples.zh: 他要去醫院做每週檢查。

meanings.definition: Once every week.

examples.en: She visits her mother weekly.
examples.zh: 她每週去看望她的母親。

meanings.definition: A session of crying.

examples.en: Sometimes you just have to have a good weep.
examples.zh: 有時你只需要痛痛快快地哭一場。

meanings.definition: To cry; shed tears.

examples.en: a weeping spring, which discharges water slowly
examples.zh: 滴水泉水緩慢流出

meanings.definition: To determine the weight of an object.

examples.en: He weighed out two kilos of oranges for a client.
examples.zh: 他為一位客戶稱了兩公斤柳橙。

meanings.definition: (physical) Matter, material.
To add weight to something; to make something heavier.
Fate; destiny; luck.
To destine; doom; change by witchcraft or sorcery.
Having an unusually strange character or behaviour.

examples.en: There are lots of weird people in this place.
examples.zh: 這個地方有很多奇怪的人。

meanings.definition: The act of greeting someone’s arrival, especially by saying "Welcome!"; reception.

examples.en: We entered the house and found a ready welcome.
examples.zh: 我們走進房子，受到了熱烈的歡迎。

meanings.definition: To affirm or greet the arrival of someone, especially by saying "Welcome!".

examples.en: We welcome suggestions for improvement.
examples.zh: 我們歡迎提出改進建議。

meanings.definition: Whose arrival is a cause of joy; received with gladness; admitted willingly to the house, entertainment, or company.

examples.en: Refugees welcome in London!
examples.zh: 倫敦歡迎難民！

meanings.definition: Greeting given upon someone's arrival.
A herb (Reseda luteola) related to mignonette, growing in Europe, and to some extent in America, used to make a yellow dye.
Health, safety, happiness and prosperity; well-being in any respect.
To provide with welfare or aid.

examples.en: welfaring the poor
examples.zh: 造福窮人

meanings.definition: In good health.

examples.en: I had been sick, but now I'm well.
examples.zh: 我本來病過，但現在好了。

meanings.definition: (manner) Accurately, competently, satisfactorily.

examples.en: He does his job well.
examples.zh: 他的工作做得很好。

meanings.definition: Used to acknowledge a statement or situation.

examples.en: “I didn't like the music.” “Well, I thought it was good.”
examples.zh: “我不喜歡音樂。” “嗯，我覺得這樣很好。”

meanings.definition: One of the four principal compass points, specifically 270°, conventionally directed to the left on maps; the direction of the setting sun at an equinox, abbreviated as W.
To move to the west; (of the sun) to set.
Situated or lying in or toward the west; westward.
Towards the west; westwards.
Liquid or moisture.

examples.en: Don't go out in the wet.
examples.zh: 不要在潮濕的情況下外出。

meanings.definition: To cover or impregnate with liquid.

examples.en: Johnny wets the bed several times a week.
examples.zh: 約翰尼每週尿床幾次。

meanings.definition: Made up of liquid or moisture, usually (but not always) water.

examples.en: Water is wet.
examples.zh: 水是濕的。

meanings.definition: Any of several species of large sea mammals of the infraorder Cetacea.
To hunt for whales.
A man-made landing place for ships on a shore or river bank.
To secure by a wharf.
(Singlish) Used to contradict an underlying assumption held by the interlocutor.
Something; thing; stuff.
(interrogative) Which thing, event, circumstance, etc.: used interrogatively in asking for the specification of an identity, quantity, quality, etc.

examples.en: He knows what he wants.
examples.zh: 他知道自己想要什麼。

meanings.definition: An expression of surprise or disbelief.

examples.en: What? I'm busy.
examples.zh: 什麼？我很忙。

meanings.definition: Unexceptional or unimportant; blah.

examples.en: There is no point whatever in going on with this discussion.
examples.zh: 繼續這個討論沒有任何意義。

meanings.definition: No matter what; for any

examples.en: Whatever he does, he will still lose the game.
examples.zh: 無論他做什麼，他仍然會輸掉比賽。

meanings.definition: (dismissive) A holophrastic expression used discourteously to indicate that the speaker does not consider the matter worthy of further discussion.

examples.en: "For the last time, brush your teeth!" "Whatever!"
examples.zh: “最後一次，刷牙！” “任何！”

meanings.definition: Any of several cereal grains, of the genus Triticum, that yields flour as used in bakery.
Wheaten, of a light brown colour, like that of wheat.
A circular device capable of rotating on its axis, facilitating movement or transportation or performing labour in machines.

examples.en: the wheel of life
examples.zh: 生命之輪

meanings.definition: To roll along on wheels.

examples.en: Wheel that trolley over here, would you?
examples.zh: 把那輛手推車推過來，好嗎？

meanings.definition: (interrogative) When ever: emphatic form of when.

examples.en: Whenever will I find time to finish that jigsaw?
examples.zh: 我什麼時候才能抽出時間完成拼圖？

meanings.definition: At any time that.

examples.en: Visit whenever you want to.
examples.zh: 只要您願意，就可以隨時存取。

meanings.definition: A clause, as in legal documents, stating whereas.
Where (that).
In contrast; whilst on the contrary; although.

examples.en: He came first in the race, whereas his brother came last.
examples.zh: 他在比賽中獲得第一名，而他的兄弟則墊底。

meanings.definition: (interrogative) By what, in which direction; how.

examples.en: Whereby goest thou?
examples.zh: 你要去哪裡？

meanings.definition: An emphatic form of where.

examples.en: Wherever have you been all my life?
examples.zh: 我這輩子你都去哪了？

meanings.definition: In or to whatever place; anywhere.

examples.en: You can sit wherever you like.
examples.zh: 你可以坐在任何你喜歡的地方。

meanings.definition: Which of two.
Introducing a direct interrogative question (often with correlative or) which indicates doubt between alternatives.

examples.en: He chose the correct answer, but I don't know whether it was by luck or (whether it was) by skill.
examples.zh: 他選擇了正確的答案，但我不知道這是靠運氣還是（是否）靠技巧。

meanings.definition: (interrogative) What one or ones (of those mentioned or implied).

examples.en: By now, you must surely know which is which.
examples.zh: 到現在為止，您一定知道哪個是哪個。

meanings.definition: An uncertain duration of time, a period of time.

examples.en: He lectured for quite a long while.
examples.zh: 他講了很長一段時間。

meanings.definition: To pass (time) idly.
Until.
During the same time that.

examples.en: He was sleeping while I was singing.
examples.zh: 我唱歌時他正在睡覺。

meanings.definition: A lash; a pliant, flexible instrument, such as a rod (commonly of cane or rattan) or a plaited or braided rope or thong (commonly of leather) used to create a sharp "crack" sound for directing or herding animals.

examples.en: I had to use the whip to get the sheep's attention.
examples.zh: 我只好用鞭子來引起羊的注意。

meanings.definition: To hit with a whip.

examples.en: The rider whipped the horse.
examples.zh: 騎手鞭打馬。

meanings.definition: An act of whirling.

examples.en: She gave the top a whirl and it spun across the floor.
examples.zh: 她旋轉陀螺，它在地板上旋轉。

meanings.definition: To rotate, revolve, spin or turn rapidly.

examples.en: The dancer whirled across the stage, stopped, and whirled around to face the audience.
examples.zh: 舞者旋轉穿過舞台，停了下來，然後旋轉面向觀眾。

meanings.definition: The act of speaking in a quiet voice, especially, without vibration of the vocal cords.

examples.en: There are whispers of rebellion all around.
examples.zh: 周圍充滿了叛逆的聲音。

meanings.definition: To speak softly, or under the breath, so as to be heard only by one near at hand; to utter words without sonant breath; to talk without that vibration in the larynx which gives sonorous, or vocal, sound.
A device designed to be placed in the mouth and blown, or driven by steam or some other mechanism, to make a whistling sound.

examples.en: the whistle of the wind in the trees
examples.zh: 風在樹間的呼嘯聲

meanings.definition: To make a shrill, high-pitched sound by forcing air through the mouth. To produce a whistling sound, restrictions to the flow of air are created using the teeth, tongue and lips.

examples.en: Never whistle at a funeral.
examples.zh: 切勿在葬禮上吹口哨。

meanings.definition: The color/colour of snow or milk; the colour of light containing equal amounts of all visible wavelengths.
To make white; to whiten; to bleach.
Bright and colourless; reflecting equal quantities of all frequencies of visible light.

examples.en: Write in black ink on white paper.
examples.zh: 用黑色墨水在白紙上書寫。

meanings.definition: A person under discussion; a question of which person.
(interrogative) What person or people; which person or people; asks for the identity of someone. (used in a direct or indirect question)

examples.en: I don't know who it is. (indirect question)
examples.zh: 我不知道是誰。 （間接疑問句）

meanings.definition: (interrogative) Who ever: an emphatic form of who.

examples.en: Whoever thought up that stupid idea?
examples.zh: 誰想出了這個愚蠢的主意？

meanings.definition: Something complete, without any parts missing.

examples.en: This variety of fascinating details didn't fall together into an enjoyable, coherent whole.
examples.zh: 這些令人著迷的細節並沒有組合成一個令人愉悅、連貫的整體。

meanings.definition: Entire, undivided.

examples.en: I ate a whole fish.
examples.zh: 我吃了一整條魚。

meanings.definition: In entirety; entirely; wholly.

examples.en: I ate a fish whole!
examples.zh: 我吃了一整條魚！

meanings.definition: The sale of products, often in large quantities, to retailers or other merchants.
To sell at wholesale.
Of or relating to sale in large quantities, for resale.

examples.en: The bombing resulted in wholesale destruction.
examples.zh: 轟炸造成大規模破壞。

meanings.definition: In bulk or large quantity.
Completely and entirely; to the fullest extent.

examples.en: A creature wholly given to brawls and wine.
examples.zh: 一種完全沉迷於爭吵和酗酒的生物。

meanings.definition: (interrogative) What person or people; which person or people.

examples.en: He's a person with whom I work. (defining)
examples.zh: 他是和我一起工作的人。 （定義）

meanings.definition: (interrogative) That or those of whom or belonging to whom.

examples.en: Several people have lost their suitcases. Whose have you found?
examples.zh: 有幾個人丟了行李箱。你找到誰的了？

meanings.definition: A ball that passes so far from the batsman that the umpire deems it unplayable; the arm signal used by an umpire to signal a wide; the extra run added to the batting side's score
Having a large physical extent from side to side.

examples.en: We walked down a wide corridor.
examples.zh: 我們沿著一條寬闊的走廊走去。

meanings.definition: Extensively

examples.en: He travelled far and wide.

meanings.definition: To become wide or wider.

examples.en: His eyes widened as her negligee fell to the floor.
examples.zh: 當她的睡衣掉到地板上時，他睜大了眼睛。

meanings.definition: Affecting a large area (e.g. the entire land or body); broad in extent; widely diffused.
A woman whose spouse has died (and who has not remarried); feminine of widower.
To make a widow or widower of someone; to cause the death of the spouse of.
The state of being wide.

examples.en: Manchester United like to play with width.
examples.zh: 曼聯喜歡打寬度。

meanings.definition: A married woman, especially in relation to her spouse.

examples.en: A new wife for the gander is introduced into the pen.
examples.zh: 公鵝的新妻子被引入圍欄。

meanings.definition: (said of men) to marry
The undomesticated state of a wild animal

examples.en: After mending the lion's leg, we returned him to the wild.
examples.zh: 修好獅子的腿後，我們將他放回了野外。

meanings.definition: To commit random acts of assault, robbery, and rape in an urban setting, especially as a gang.
Untamed; not domesticated; specifically, in an unbroken line of undomesticated animals (as opposed to feral, referring to undomesticated animals whose ancestors were domesticated).

examples.en: Przewalski's horses are the only remaining wild horses.
examples.zh: 普氏野馬是僅存的野馬。

meanings.definition: Inaccurately; not on target.

examples.en: The javelin flew wild and struck a spectator, to the horror of all observing.
examples.zh: 標槍瘋狂飛翔，擊中了一名觀眾，令所有旁觀者感到恐懼。

meanings.definition: (now uncommon or literary) To wish, desire (something).

examples.en: Do what you will.
examples.zh: 做你想做的事。

meanings.definition: To wish, desire.

examples.en: All the fans were willing their team to win the game.
examples.zh: 所有球迷都希望自己的球隊能夠贏得比賽。

meanings.definition: The execution of a will.
Ready to do something that is not (can't be expected as) a matter of course.

examples.en: If my boyfriend isn't willing to change his drinking habits, I will split up with him.
examples.zh: 如果我的男友不願意改變他的飲酒習慣，我就會跟他分手。

meanings.definition: To conquer, defeat.

examples.en: to win the jackpot in a lottery;  to win a bottle of wine in a raffle
examples.zh: 贏得彩券大獎；  在抽獎中贏得一瓶葡萄酒

meanings.definition: Real or perceived movement of atmospheric air usually caused by convection or differences in air pressure.

examples.en: As they accelerated onto the motorway, the wind tore the plywood off the car's roof-rack.
examples.zh: 當他們加速駛上高速公路時，風把車頂行李架上的膠合板刮掉了。

meanings.definition: To blow air through a wind instrument or horn to make a sound.

examples.en: The boxer was winded during round two.
examples.zh: 拳擊手在第二回合氣喘吁籲。

meanings.definition: The inedible parts of a grain-producing plant.

examples.en: To separate out the chaff, early cultures tossed baskets of grain into the air and let the wind blow away the lighter chaff.
examples.zh: 為了分離出穀殼，早期文化將一籃穀物拋向空中，讓風吹走較輕的穀殼。

meanings.definition: To furnish with windows.
An alcoholic beverage made by fermenting the juice of grapes.

examples.en: She ordered some wine for the meal.
examples.zh: 她為這頓飯點了一些酒。

meanings.definition: To entertain with wine.
An appendage of an animal's (bird, bat, insect) body that enables it to fly; a similar fin at the side of a ray or similar fish

examples.en: to take wing
examples.zh: 展翅高飛

meanings.definition: To injure slightly (as with a gunshot), especially in the wing or arm.
An act of winking (a blinking of only one eye), or a message sent by winking.

examples.en: The film includes a wink to wartime rationing.
examples.zh: 這部電影對戰時配給眨眼。

meanings.definition: To close one's eyes in sleep.

examples.en: He winked at me. She winked her eye. He winked his assent.
examples.zh: 他對我眨了眨眼睛。 她眨了眨眼睛。 他眨了眨眼睛表示同意。

meanings.definition: One who has won or often wins.

Traditionally the fourth of the four seasons, typically regarded as being from December 23 to March 20 in continental regions of the Northern Hemisphere or the months of June, July and August in the Southern Hemisphere. It is the time when the sun is lowest in the sky, resulting in short days, and the time of year with the lowest atmospheric temperatures for the region.
To spend the winter (in a particular place).

examples.en: When they retired, they hoped to winter in Florida.
examples.zh: 當他們退休時，他們希望在佛羅裡達過冬。

meanings.definition: The act of wiping something.

examples.en: multiple wipes of a computer's hard disk
examples.zh: 多次清除電腦硬碟

meanings.definition: To move an object over, maintaining contact, with the intention of removing some substance from the surface. (Compare rub.)

examples.en: I wiped the sweat from my brow with the back of my hand.
examples.zh: 我用手背擦了擦額頭的汗。

meanings.definition: Metal formed into a thin, even thread, now usually by being drawn through a hole in a steel die.

examples.en: This election is going to go right to the wire
examples.zh: 這次選舉即將進入尾聲

meanings.definition: To fasten with wire, especially with reference to wine bottles, corks, or fencing.

examples.en: We need to wire that hole in the fence.
examples.zh: 我們需要用電線堵住柵欄上的那個洞。

meanings.definition: An element of personal character that enables one to distinguish the wise from the unwise.
To become wise.

examples.en: After Mo had a word with him, he wised up.
examples.zh: 莫跟他說了一番話後，他醒悟了。

meanings.definition: Showing good judgement or the benefit of experience.

examples.en: "It is a profitable thing, if one is wise, to seem foolish" - Aeschylus
examples.zh: 「如果一個人是明智的，那麼顯得愚蠢是一件有利可圖的事」——埃斯庫羅斯

meanings.definition: A desire, hope, or longing for something or for something to happen.

examples.en: My dearest wish is to see them happily married.
examples.zh: 我最大的願望就是看到他們幸福地結婚。

meanings.definition: To desire; to want.

examples.en: I'll come tomorrow, if you wish it.
examples.zh: 如果你願意的話我明天就來。

meanings.definition: (now usually in the plural) Sanity.

examples.en: He's gone completely out of his wits.
examples.zh: 他完全失去了理智。

meanings.definition: To pull (something) back, aside, or away.

examples.en: to withdraw false charges
examples.zh: 撤回虛假指控

meanings.definition: Receiving from someone's care what one has earlier entrusted to them. Usually refers to money.

examples.en: caffeine withdrawal
examples.zh: 咖啡因戒斷

meanings.definition: To shrivel, droop or dry up, especially from lack of water.
In the context of which the present document or ruling is made.

examples.en: the within appeal
examples.zh: 內部上訴

meanings.definition: In or into the interior; inside.
In the inner part, spatially; physically inside.

examples.en: within her studio
examples.zh: 在她的工作室裡

meanings.definition: Outside, externally. This is still used in the names of some civil parishes in England, e.g. St Cuthbert Without.

examples.en: Being from a large, poor family, he learned to live without.
examples.zh: 由於來自一個貧窮的大家庭，他學會瞭如何生活。

meanings.definition: Outside of, beyond.

examples.en: The snow was swirling without the cottage, but it was warm within.
examples.zh: 屋外雪花紛飛，屋內卻很溫暖。

meanings.definition: Unless, except (introducing a clause).
Attestation of a fact or event; testimony.

examples.en: She can bear witness, since she was there at the time.
examples.zh: 她可以作證，因為她當時就在場。

meanings.definition: To furnish proof of, to show.

examples.en: 1667: round he throws his baleful eyes / That witness'd huge affliction and dismay — John Milton, Paradise Lost, Book 1 ll. 56-7
examples.zh: 1667 年：他投來惡毒的眼睛/目睹了巨大的痛苦和沮喪——約翰·彌爾頓，《失樂園》，第 1 卷。 56-7

meanings.definition: Great sadness or distress; a misfortune causing such sadness.
Woeful; sorrowful
The gray wolf, specifically all subspecies of the gray wolf (Canis lupus) that are not dingoes or dogs.

examples.en: Check out my pet. He's a wolf.
examples.zh: 看看我的寵物。他是一頭狼。

meanings.definition: To devour; to gobble; to eat (something) voraciously.
An adult female human.
To staff with female labor.
Something that causes amazement or awe; a marvel.

examples.en: Wonders of the World seem to come in sevens.
examples.zh: 世界奇觀似乎接二連三。

meanings.definition: To be affected with surprise or admiration; to be struck with astonishment; to be amazed; to marvel; often followed by at.

examples.en: He wondered whether penguins could fly. She had wondered this herself sometimes.
examples.zh: 他想知道企鵝是否會飛。有時她自己也想知道這一點。

meanings.definition: Tending to excite wonder; surprising, extraordinary.

examples.en: They served a wonderful six-course meal.
examples.zh: 他們提供了一頓美味的六道菜餐。

meanings.definition: Exceedingly, to a great extent.
The substance making up the central part of the trunk and branches of a tree. Used as a material for construction, to manufacture various items, etc. or as fuel.

examples.en: There was lots of wood on the beach.
examples.zh: 海灘上有很多木頭。

meanings.definition: To cover or plant with trees.

examples.en: to wood a steamboat or a locomotive
examples.zh: 給汽船或機車裝木頭

meanings.definition: The hair of the sheep, llama and some other ruminants.
The smallest unit of language that has a particular meaning and can be expressed by itself; the smallest discrete, meaningful unit of language. (contrast morpheme.)

examples.en: mum's the word
examples.zh: 媽媽的話

meanings.definition: To say or write (something) using particular words; to phrase (something).

examples.en: I’m not sure how to word this letter to the council.
examples.zh: 我不知道如何向理事會寫這封信。

meanings.definition: Truth, indeed, that is the truth! The shortened form of the statement "My word is my bond."

examples.en: "Yo, that movie was epic!" / "Word?" ("You speak the truth?") / "Word." ("I speak the truth.")
examples.zh: "Yo, that movie was epic!" / “單字？” ("You speak the truth?") / "Word." （“我說的是實話。”）

meanings.definition: (heading) Employment.

examples.en: We don't have much time. Let's get to work piling up those sandbags.
examples.zh: 我們沒有太多時間。讓我們開始堆沙袋吧。

meanings.definition: A person who performs labor for a living, especially manual labor.

examples.en: This FTP client spawns a separate worker for each file to be uploaded.
examples.zh: 該 FTP 用戶端為每個要上傳的檔案產生一個單獨的工作程序。

meanings.definition: All the workers employed by a specific organization or state, or on a specific project
A room, especially one which is not particularly large, used for manufacturing or other light industrial work.
To help a playwright revise a draft of (a play) by rehearsing it with actors and critiquing the results.
(with "the") Human collective existence; existence in general.

examples.en: There will always be lovers, till the world’s end.
examples.zh: 總會有戀人的，直到世界的盡頭。

meanings.definition: To consider or cause to be considered from a global perspective; to consider as a global whole, rather than making or focussing on national or other distinctions; compare globalise.
A generally tubular invertebrate of the annelid phylum; an earthworm.

examples.en: Don't try to run away, you little worm!
examples.zh: 別想逃跑，你這個小蟲！

meanings.definition: To make (one's way) with a crawling motion.

examples.en: We wormed our way through the underbrush.
examples.zh: 我們在灌木叢中爬行。

meanings.definition: A strong feeling of anxiety.

examples.en: I'm afflicted by worry throughout the night.
examples.zh: 我整個晚上都被憂慮折磨著。

meanings.definition: To be troubled; to give way to mental anxiety or doubt.

examples.en: Stop worrying about your test, it’ll be fine.
examples.zh: 別再擔心你的考試了，一切都會好起來的。

meanings.definition: Unfavorable; negative; not good.

examples.en: Abducting children is bad for mothers.
examples.zh: 拐賣孩子對母親來說是不好的。

meanings.definition: In a bad manner.

examples.en: I want it so badly.
examples.zh: 我非常想要它。

meanings.definition: Loss; disadvantage; defeat.

examples.en: Do not think the worse of him for his enterprise.
examples.zh: 不要因為他的事業而對他抱持更壞的看法。

meanings.definition: To make worse; to put at disadvantage; to discomfit.
The condition of being worthy; honour, distinction.
To reverence (a deity, etc.) with supreme respect and veneration; to perform religious exercises in honour of.

examples.en: We worship at the church down the road.
examples.zh: 我們在路邊的教會做禮拜。

meanings.definition: Unfavorable; negative; not good.

examples.en: Abducting children is bad for mothers.
examples.zh: 拐賣孩子對母親來說是不好的。

meanings.definition: Something or someone that is the worst
To make worse.
Superlative of bad

examples.en: My sore leg hurts worst when it's cold and rainy.
examples.zh: 當寒冷和下雨天時，我的腿酸痛得最厲害。

meanings.definition: Value.

examples.en: I’ll have a dollar's worth of candy, please.
examples.zh: 請給我一美元的糖果。

meanings.definition: Having a value of; proper to be exchanged for.

examples.en: A painting worth thousands.
examples.zh: 一幅畫就價值上萬。

meanings.definition: Having no worth or use; without value; inconsequential.

examples.en: Don't be a worthless slouch! Go get yourself a job.
examples.zh: 別當個毫無價值的懶漢！給自己找份工作吧。

meanings.definition: Good and important enough to spend time, effort, or money on.

examples.en: Doing volunteer work to help others is truly worthwhile.
examples.zh: 做志工來幫助別人確實是值得的。

meanings.definition: A distinguished or eminent person
Having worth, merit or value
An injury, such as a cut, stab, or tear, to a (usually external) part of the body.

examples.en: It took a long time to get over the wound of that insult.
examples.zh: 我花了很長時間才從那次侮辱的創傷中恢復過來。

meanings.definition: To hurt or injure (someone) by cutting, piercing, or tearing the skin.

examples.en: The police officer wounded the suspect during the fight that ensued.
examples.zh: 警察在隨後的打架中打傷了嫌疑犯。

meanings.definition: To enclose (an object) completely in any flexible, thin material such as fabric or paper.

examples.en: A snake wraps itself around its prey.
examples.zh: 蛇將自己纏繞在獵物身上。

meanings.definition: Great anger.

examples.en: Homer relates an episode in the Trojan War that reveals the tragic consequences of the wrath of Achilles.
examples.zh: 荷馬講述了特洛伊戰爭中的一個插曲，揭示了阿喀琉斯憤怒所造成的悲慘後果。

meanings.definition: To anger; to enrage.
Wrathful; wroth; very angry.
Something twisted, intertwined, or curled.

examples.en: a wreath of smoke;  a wreath of clouds
examples.zh: 煙霧花環；  雲花環

meanings.definition: To place an entwined circle of flowers upon or around something.

examples.en: At the funeral, a circle of comrades wreathed the grave of the honored deceased.
examples.zh: 在葬禮上，一圈同志向尊敬的死者的墳墓敬獻了花圈。

meanings.definition: Something or someone that has been ruined.

examples.en: He was an emotional wreck after the death of his wife.
examples.zh: 妻子過世後，他的情緒崩潰了。

meanings.definition: To destroy violently; to cause severe damage to something, to a point where it no longer works, or is useless.

examples.en: He wrecked the car in a collision.
examples.zh: 他在一次碰撞中毀壞了汽車。

meanings.definition: The complex joint between forearm bones, carpus, and metacarpals where the hand is attached to the arm; the carpus in a narrow sense.
To hit a wrist shot
The act or style of writing.

examples.en: How many writes per second can this hard disk handle?
examples.zh: 該硬碟每秒可以處理幾次寫入？

meanings.definition: To form letters, words or symbols on a surface in order to communicate.

examples.en: The pupil wrote his name on the paper.
examples.zh: 學生在紙上寫下了自己的名字。

meanings.definition: A person who writes, or produces literary work.

examples.en: Has your girlfriend written you another letter already? She’s quite a writer!
examples.zh: 你女朋友已經寫過另一封信給你了嗎？她真是一位作家！

meanings.definition: Something that is immoral or not good.

examples.en: Injustice is a heinous wrong.
examples.zh: 不公正是令人髮指的錯誤。

meanings.definition: To treat unjustly; to injure or harm.
Incorrect or untrue.

examples.en: Some of your answers were correct, and some were wrong.
examples.zh: 您的有些答案是正確的，有些是錯誤的。

meanings.definition: In a way that isn't right; incorrectly, wrongly.

examples.en: I spelled several names wrong in my address book.
examples.zh: 我在通訊錄中拼錯了幾個名字。

meanings.definition: Intersex or non-binary (in passports and identification documents).
Used between the names of two characters to denote a ship, particularly in anime, manga, and video games.

examples.en: Taska Black x DROELOE, Steve Aoki x Lauren Jauregui, etc.
examples.zh: Taska Black x DROELOE、Steve Aoki x Lauren Jauregui 等

meanings.definition: A beam of light or radiation.

examples.en: I saw a ray of light through the clouds.
examples.zh: 我看到一絲光透過雲層。

meanings.definition: To emit something as if in rays.
A slick and light ship for making pleasure trips or racing on water, having sails but often motor-powered. At times used as a residence offshore on a dock.

examples.en: Would you like to go sailing on my uncle’s yacht?
examples.zh: 你想搭乘我叔叔的遊艇去航行嗎？

meanings.definition: To sail, voyage, or race in a yacht.
A small, usually uncultivated area adjoining or (now especially) within the precincts of a house or other building.
To confine to a yard.
The action of yawning; opening the mouth widely and taking a long, rather deep breath, often because one is tired or bored.

examples.en: The slideshow we sat through was such a yawn. I was glad when it finished.
examples.zh: 我們坐著看的幻燈片實在是太令人打哈欠了。當它完成時我很高興。

meanings.definition: To open the mouth widely and take a long, rather deep breath, often because one is tired or bored, and sometimes accompanied by pandiculation.

examples.en: I could see my students yawning, so I knew the lesson was boring them.
examples.zh: 我可以看到我的學生打哈欠，所以我知道這堂課讓他們感到無聊。

meanings.definition: A solar year, the time it takes the Earth to complete one revolution of the Sun (between 365.24 and 365.26 days depending on the point of reference).

examples.en: we moved to this town a year ago;  I quit smoking exactly one year ago
examples.zh: 我們一年前搬到這個小鎮；  我一年前就戒菸了

meanings.definition: Something that is published once a year.
Happening once every year.

examples.en: Christmas is a yearly celebration.
examples.zh: 聖誕節是一年一度的慶祝活動。

meanings.definition: Once a year.
To long, have a strong desire (for something).

examples.en: All I yearn for is a simple life.
examples.zh: 我嚮往的只是簡單的生活。

meanings.definition: An often humid, yellowish froth produced by fermenting malt worts, and used to brew beer, leaven bread, and also used in certain medicines.
To ferment.
A shout.
Shout; holler; make a loud sound with the voice.

examples.en: He yelled directions to the party from the car.
examples.zh: 他在車上大聲喊著去聚會的方向。

meanings.definition: The colour of gold, butter, or a lemon; the colour obtained by mixing green and red light, or by subtracting blue from white light.
To become yellow or more yellow.
Having yellow as its colour.

examples.en: 2012 March 2, Andrew Grice, "Yellow rebels take on Clegg over NHS 'betrayal'", The Independent
examples.zh: 2012 年 3 月 2 日，安德魯·格萊斯 (Andrew Grice)，“黃色叛軍因 NHS‘背叛’而攻擊克萊格”，《獨立報》

meanings.definition: The day immediately before today; one day ago.

examples.en: Today is the child of yesterday and the parent of tomorrow.
examples.zh: 今天是昨天的孩子，也是明天的父母。

meanings.definition: On the day before today.

examples.en: I started to watch the video yesterday, but could only finish it this evening.
examples.zh: 昨天開始看視頻，今天晚上才看完。

meanings.definition: (usually with negative) Thus far; up to the present; up to some specified time; still

examples.en: He has never yet been late for an appointment;   I’m not yet wise enough to answer that;   Have you finished yet?
examples.zh: 他約會時從來沒有遲到過。   我還沒有足夠的智慧來回答這個問題；   你完成了嗎？

meanings.definition: Nevertheless; however; but; despite that.

examples.en: I thought I knew you, yet how wrong I was.
examples.zh: 我以為我認識你，但我錯了。

meanings.definition: To pay, give in payment; repay, recompense; reward; requite.

examples.en: Yield the right of way to pedestrians.
examples.zh: 給行人讓路。

meanings.definition: Frame around the neck, and related senses.
To link or to join.
People who are young; young people, collectively; youth.

examples.en: The young of today are well-educated.
examples.zh: 現在的年輕人都受過良好的教育。

meanings.definition: To become or seem to become younger.
In the early part of growth or life; born not long ago.

examples.en: a lamb is a young sheep;  these picture books are for young readers
examples.zh: 羔羊是指小綿羊；  這些圖畫書適合年輕讀者

meanings.definition: The quality or state of being young.

examples.en: Her youth and beauty attracted him to her.
examples.zh: 她的青春和美麗吸引了他。

meanings.definition: The fervour or tireless devotion for a person, cause, or ideal and determination in its furtherance; diligent enthusiasm; powerful interest.

examples.en: She extols the virtues of veganism with missionary zeal.
examples.zh: 她以傳道者的熱情頌揚素食主義的美德。

meanings.definition: Full of zeal; ardent, fervent; exhibiting enthusiasm or strong passion.
The numeric symbol that represents the cardinal number zero.

examples.en: In unary and k-adic notation in general, zero is the empty string.
examples.zh: 一般來說，在一元和 k 進數表示法中，零是空字串。

meanings.definition: To set a measuring instrument to zero; to calibrate instrument scale to valid zero.

examples.en: Zero the fluorometer with the same solvent used in extraction.
examples.zh: 使用萃取中使用的相同溶劑將螢光計調零。

meanings.definition: No, not any

examples.en: She showed zero respect.
examples.zh: 她表現出零尊重。

meanings.definition: The cardinal number occurring before one and that denotes no quantity or amount at all, represented in Arabic numerals as 0.

examples.en: A cheque for zero dollars and zero cents crashed the computers on division by zero.
examples.zh: 一張零美元零美分的支票在除以零時使計算機崩潰。

meanings.definition: Each of the five regions of the earth's surface into which it was divided by climatic differences, namely the torrid zone (between the tropics), two temperate zones (between the tropics and the polar circles), and two frigid zones (within the polar circles).

examples.en: Files in the Internet zone are blocked by default, as a security measure.
examples.zh: 作為一項安全措施，Internet 區域中的檔案預設會被封鎖。

meanings.definition: To divide into or assign sections or areas.

examples.en: Please zone off our staging area, a section for each group.
examples.zh: 請劃分出我們的集合區域，每個小組都有一個區域。

meanings.definition: A park where live animals are exhibited.

examples.en: The shopping center was a zoo the week before Christmas.
examples.zh: 聖誕節前一周，購物中心變成了動物園。
